Novenário — side-by-side review

86 novenas

Each novena shows the Ember text (original, AI-composed for this app — English on top, Portuguese in italic) beside the traditional source on FishEaters and the modern source on PrayMoreNovenas. The fidelity note at the bottom of each card judges how closely the Ember version tracks the sources. Sources are English only; where a source is blank, none exists for that novena.

Mary, Mother of God mary-mother-of-god-novena

Dec 23–31
description & history

A nine-day novena honoring the Blessed Virgin Mary under her highest title, Mother of God, imploring her maternal protection.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra à Santíssima Virgem Maria sob o seu mais alto título, Mãe de Deus, implorando a sua proteção materna.

At the Council of Ephesus in 431, the Church solemnly proclaimed Mary 'Theotokos' — God-bearer, Mother of God — defending the truth that her Son is one divine Person. Because she gave human flesh to the eternal Word, she is truly the Mother of God, and from the Cross her Son gave her to us as our Mother. This novena draws on the ancient prayer Sub Tuum Praesidium, the oldest known prayer to Our Lady.

No Concílio de Éfeso, em 431, a Igreja proclamou solenemente Maria 'Theotokos' — aquela que gerou Deus, Mãe de Deus — defendendo a verdade de que o seu Filho é uma só Pessoa divina. Porque deu carne humana ao Verbo eterno, ela é verdadeiramente a Mãe de Deus, e do alto da Cruz o seu Filho a entregou a nós como nossa Mãe. Esta novena se inspira na antiga oração Sub Tuum Praesidium, a mais antiga súplica conhecida à Nossa Senhora.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Holy Mother of God, beneath your protection we take refuge. You who carried in your womb the eternal Word, look upon us with a mother's tenderness and lead us always to your Son. Amen.

Santa Mãe de Deus, sob a vossa proteção nos refugiamos. Vós que trouxestes em vosso seio o Verbo eterno, olhai para nós com ternura de mãe e conduzi-nos sempre ao vosso Filho. Amém.

Day 1: The Mother of God

Dia 1: A Mãe de Deus

At Ephesus the Church confessed that the child Mary bore is true God, and so she is rightly called Theotokos, Mother of God. This title does not exalt her above God, but proclaims the wonder that God willed to be born of a creature.

Em Éfeso, a Igreja confessou que o menino gerado por Maria é verdadeiro Deus, e por isso ela é justamente chamada Theotokos, Mãe de Deus. Esse título não a eleva acima de Deus, mas proclama a maravilha de que Deus quis nascer de uma criatura.

Intention

For a deeper faith in the mystery of the Incarnation.

Por uma fé mais profunda no mistério da Encarnação.

Day 2: Her humble fiat

Dia 2: O seu humilde fiat

When the angel announced God's will, Mary answered, 'Let it be done to me according to your word.' In that free consent she opened the door of salvation, teaching us to surrender our whole life to God.

Quando o anjo anunciou a vontade de Deus, Maria respondeu: 'Faça-se em mim segundo a vossa palavra.' Nesse consentimento livre, ela abriu a porta da salvação, ensinando-nos a entregar toda a nossa vida a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to say yes to God's will without reserve.

Pela graça de dizer sim à vontade de Deus sem reservas.

Day 3: Mother of our Redeemer

Dia 3: Mãe do nosso Redentor

Mary gave human flesh to the One who would redeem the world by His Blood. By cooperating in His coming, she stands closest to the work of our salvation, after Christ Himself.

Maria deu carne humana Àquele que redimiria o mundo pelo seu Sangue. Cooperando na sua vinda, ela está, depois de Cristo, mais próxima da obra da nossa salvação.

Intention

For gratitude for the redemption won by Christ.

Pela gratidão pela redenção conquistada por Cristo.

Day 4: Beneath your protection

Dia 4: Sob a vossa proteção

From the earliest centuries Christians fled to Mary's protection, praying the Sub Tuum Praesidium amid persecution. She is a sure refuge who shelters her children in every storm.

Desde os primeiros séculos, os cristãos buscaram refúgio na proteção de Maria, rezando o Sub Tuum Praesidium em meio às perseguições. Ela é refúgio seguro que abriga os seus filhos em toda tempestade.

Intention

For protection in times of trial and danger.

Pela proteção nos tempos de provação e perigo.

Day 5: The faithful heart

Dia 5: O coração fiel

Saint Luke tells us that Mary kept all these things, pondering them in her heart. She is the model of contemplation, treasuring the word of God in silence and love.

São Lucas nos diz que Maria guardava todas essas coisas, meditando-as em seu coração. Ela é o modelo da contemplação, guardando a palavra de Deus no silêncio e no amor.

Intention

For a recollected heart that treasures God's word.

Por um coração recolhido que guarda a palavra de Deus.

Day 6: Mother at the Cross

Dia 6: Mãe junto à Cruz

Mary stood faithfully beneath the Cross as her Son offered His life. There, in His words 'Behold your mother,' He gave her to the whole Church as our Mother.

Maria permaneceu fiel ao pé da Cruz, enquanto o seu Filho oferecia a vida. Ali, nas palavras 'Eis a tua mãe', Ele a entregou a toda a Igreja como nossa Mãe.

Intention

For perseverance in faith amid suffering.

Pela perseverança na fé em meio ao sofrimento.

Day 7: Our Mother

Dia 7: Nossa Mãe

Because she is Mother of the Head, Mary is Mother of all His members. She watches over each of her children with a love that never wearies and never forgets.

Porque é Mãe da Cabeça, Maria é Mãe de todos os seus membros. Ela vela por cada um de seus filhos com um amor que nunca se cansa e jamais esquece.

Intention

For trust in Mary's constant maternal care.

Pela confiança no constante cuidado materno de Maria.

Day 8: Queen of peace

Dia 8: Rainha da paz

She who gave us the Prince of Peace leads hearts and nations back to her Son. Mary intercedes that the peace of Christ may reign in our families and in the world.

Aquela que nos deu o Príncipe da Paz reconduz os corações e as nações ao seu Filho. Maria intercede para que a paz de Cristo reine nas nossas famílias e no mundo.

Intention

For peace in our homes and among the nations.

Pela paz nos nossos lares e entre as nações.

Day 9: Assumed into glory

Dia 9: Assunta à glória

Taken body and soul into heaven, Mary now reigns beside her Son as the first fruit of His victory. She is the sure hope of all who long to follow her into glory.

Elevada em corpo e alma ao céu, Maria reina agora ao lado do seu Filho como primícia da sua vitória. Ela é a esperança segura de todos os que anseiam por segui-la na glória.

Intention

For the grace of final perseverance and eternal life.

Pela graça da perseverança final e da vida eterna.

Closing prayer

O Mary, Mother of God and our Mother, despise not our petitions in our necessities, but deliver us always from every danger. Through your intercession may we one day behold the face of your Son in glory. Amen.

Ó Maria, Mãe de Deus e nossa Mãe, não desprezeis as nossas súplicas nas nossas necessidades, mas livrai-nos sempre de todo perigo. Por vossa intercessão, possamos um dia contemplar a face do vosso Filho na glória. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas, "Mary, Mother of God Novena." Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross and "Dear Lord, we thank You for the amazing gift that You have given us in Mary, Your Mother," then a day-specific petition to God and an invocation of Mary as "Mother of God" asking her intercession; each day closes "Mary, Mother of God, pray for us!" and the Sign of the Cross. The nine days are organized by petition intentions rather than by historical/theological meditations:
- Day 1: deeper devotion to Mary as our Mother through her role in salvation
- Day 2: Mary as model for all mothers in holiness and loving care
- Day 3: those who consecrate themselves to God through Mary
- Day 4: men and women religious who serve through Mary
- Day 5: those who do not yet know Mary's love as her children
- Day 6: submission to God's will after Mary's example
- Day 7: defense of the Church's teaching on Mary as Mother of God
- Day 8: strength and virtue for the Church's leaders
- Day 9: that the Church remain strong and vibrant

Fidelity note

Ember is not a translation of the PrayMoreNovenas text; it is an independent composition. Both honor Mary under the Theotokos/Mother-of-God title and share the daily Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be frame, but the substance diverges sharply: PMN structures the nine days as intercessory petitions (for mothers, religious, the Church, Church leaders), whereas Ember structures them as catechetical-Marian meditations following salvation history (Ephesus, the fiat, the Cross, the Assumption) and explicitly grounds itself in the ancient Sub Tuum Praesidium. Same feast and title, but different genre and day-by-day content; Ember is the more doctrinally/historically themed of the two.

Epiphany epiphany-novena

Dec 28–Jan 5
description & history

A nine-day novena celebrating the Epiphany, the manifestation of Christ to all nations in the coming of the Magi.

Uma novena de nove dias celebrando a Epifania, a manifestação de Cristo a todas as nações na vinda dos Magos.

The Epiphany — from a Greek word meaning 'manifestation' — celebrates the revealing of Christ to the world beyond Israel. Saint Matthew recounts how wise men from the East followed a star to Bethlehem and offered gold, frankincense, and myrrh to the newborn King. Their journey shows that Christ is the light of all peoples, and this novena prepares the heart to seek and adore Him.

A Epifania — de uma palavra grega que significa 'manifestação' — celebra a revelação de Cristo ao mundo para além de Israel. São Mateus narra como sábios do Oriente seguiram uma estrela até Belém e ofereceram ouro, incenso e mirra ao Rei recém-nascido. A sua jornada mostra que Cristo é a luz de todos os povos, e esta novena prepara o coração para buscá-Lo e adorá-Lo.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Lord Jesus, light of the nations, who revealed Yourself to the Magi by the brightness of a star, draw us also to Your manger. Open our eyes to seek You and our hearts to adore You as our King and our God. Amen.

Ó Senhor Jesus, luz das nações, que Vos revelastes aos Magos pelo brilho de uma estrela, atraí também a nós à vossa manjedoura. Abri os nossos olhos para Vos buscar e os nossos corações para Vos adorar como nosso Rei e nosso Deus. Amém.

Day 1: The light of the nations

Dia 1: A luz das nações

The Epiphany proclaims that Christ came not for one people alone but for the whole world. In the Magi, all the nations of the earth come to kneel before the Savior.

A Epifania proclama que Cristo veio não apenas para um povo, mas para o mundo inteiro. Nos Magos, todas as nações da terra vêm ajoelhar-se diante do Salvador.

Intention

For the spread of the Gospel to all peoples.

Pela difusão do Evangelho a todos os povos.

Day 2: The star that guides

Dia 2: A estrela que guia

God placed a star in the heavens to lead the wise men to His Son. He still guides every soul who watches for His signs and follows the light He gives.

Deus colocou uma estrela nos céus para conduzir os sábios até o seu Filho. Ele ainda guia toda alma que vigia os seus sinais e segue a luz que Ele concede.

Intention

For guidance in following God's call.

Pela orientação em seguir o chamado de Deus.

Day 3: The seeking of the wise

Dia 3: A busca dos sábios

The Magi left their land and crossed deserts to find the newborn King. Their long journey teaches us that true wisdom is restless until it rests in Christ.

Os Magos deixaram a sua terra e atravessaram desertos para encontrar o Rei recém-nascido. A sua longa jornada nos ensina que a verdadeira sabedoria permanece inquieta até descansar em Cristo.

Intention

For perseverance in the search for God.

Pela perseverança na busca de Deus.

Day 4: The gift of gold

Dia 4: O dom do ouro

The Magi offered gold to honor Christ as King. We too owe Him the homage of our lives, acknowledging His reign over all we possess.

Os Magos ofereceram ouro para honrar a Cristo como Rei. Também nós Lhe devemos a homenagem das nossas vidas, reconhecendo o seu reinado sobre tudo o que possuímos.

Intention

That Christ may truly reign as King of our lives.

Para que Cristo reine verdadeiramente como Rei das nossas vidas.

Day 5: The gift of frankincense

Dia 5: O dom do incenso

The frankincense, burned in worship, confessed the Child to be true God. It calls us to a life of adoration, lifting our prayer to heaven like rising incense.

O incenso, queimado na adoração, confessava ser o Menino verdadeiro Deus. Ele nos chama a uma vida de adoração, elevando a nossa oração ao céu como incenso que sobe.

Intention

For a spirit of true and constant adoration.

Por um espírito de adoração verdadeira e constante.

Day 6: The gift of myrrh

Dia 6: O dom da mirra

The myrrh, used to anoint the dead, foretold that this King would suffer and die for us. From the cradle, the shadow of the Cross already speaks of His redeeming love.

A mirra, usada para ungir os mortos, anunciava que este Rei sofreria e morreria por nós. Desde o berço, a sombra da Cruz já fala do seu amor redentor.

Intention

For the grace to embrace the Cross with love.

Pela graça de abraçar a Cruz com amor.

Day 7: They fell down and adored

Dia 7: Prostraram-se e adoraram

Finding the Child with Mary His mother, the Magi fell to their knees in worship. Greatness bows before the lowly manger, teaching us the humility of true faith.

Encontrando o Menino com Maria, sua mãe, os Magos prostraram-se em adoração. A grandeza se inclina diante da humilde manjedoura, ensinando-nos a humildade da verdadeira fé.

Intention

For the gift of humble, adoring faith.

Pelo dom de uma fé humilde e adoradora.

Day 8: Returning by another way

Dia 8: Voltando por outro caminho

Warned in a dream, the Magi went home by another road. Whoever truly meets Christ cannot return unchanged, but walks a new path of life.

Avisados em sonho, os Magos voltaram por outro caminho. Quem verdadeiramente encontra Cristo não pode voltar o mesmo, mas trilha um novo caminho de vida.

Intention

For true conversion of heart and life.

Pela verdadeira conversão do coração e da vida.

Day 9: Light to those in darkness

Dia 9: Luz aos que estão nas trevas

Christ the Epiphany is the dawn that scatters every darkness. He desires to shine in our own hearts and through us upon all who have not yet seen His light.

Cristo, a Epifania, é a aurora que dissipa toda treva. Ele deseja resplandecer nos nossos corações e, por meio de nós, sobre todos os que ainda não viram a sua luz.

Intention

That Christ's light may shine through us to others.

Para que a luz de Cristo brilhe por meio de nós aos outros.

Closing prayer

O Christ, manifested to the gentiles, let Your light shine upon all who walk in darkness. As the Magi returned home by another way, lead us by the path of faith until we behold You in the eternal day. Amen.

Ó Cristo, manifestado aos gentios, fazei brilhar a vossa luz sobre todos os que caminham nas trevas. Como os Magos voltaram por outro caminho, conduzi-nos pela senda da fé até Vos contemplarmos no dia eterno. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="magi">
Novena
to the Magi in anticipation of the Epiphany

This Novena is especially prayed beginning on 28
December (the Feast of the Holy Innocents) and ending on 5 January (the
Vigil of the Epiphany).

28 December:

O holy Magi! You were living in continual expectation of the rising of
the Star of Jacob, which would announce the birth of the true Sun of
justice; obtain for us an increase of faith and charity, and the grace
to live in continual hope of beholding one day the light of heavenly
glory and eternal joy. Glory be to the Father and to the Son and to the
Holy Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be,
world without end.

29 December:

O holy Magi! who at the first appearance of the wondrous star left your
native country to go and seek the newborn King of the Jews; obtain for
us the grace of corresponding with alacrity to every divine
inspiration. Glory be to the Father and to the Son and to the Holy
Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world
without end.

30 December:

O holy Magi! who regarded neither the severity of the season, nor the
inconveniences of the journey that you might find the newborn Messiah;
obtain for us the grace not to allow ourselves to be discouraged by any
of the difficulties which may meet us on the way of salvation. Glory be
to the Father and to the Son and to the Holy Ghost, as it was in the
beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end.

31 December:

O holy Magi, who, when deserted by the star in the city of Jerusalem,
sought humbly, and without human respect, from the rulers of the
Church, the place where you might discover the object of your journey;
obtain for us grace to have recourse, in faith and humility, in all our
doubts and perplexities to the counsel of our superiors, who hold the
place of God on earth. Glory be to the Father and to the Son and to the
Holy Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be,
world without end.

1 January:

O holy Magi, who were gladdened by the reappearance of the star which
led you to Bethlehem; obtain for us from God the grace, that, remaining
always faithful to Him in afflictions, we may be consoled in time by
His grace, and in eternity by His glory. Glory be to the Father and to
the Son and to the Holy Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is now, and
ever shall be, world without end.

2 January:

O holy Magi, who, entering full of faith into the stable of Bethlehem,
prostrated yourselves on the earth, to adore the newborn King of the
Jews, though he was surrounded only by signs of poverty and weakness;
obtain from the Lord for us a lively faith in the real presence of
Jesus in the blessed Sacrament, the true spirit of poverty, and a
Christ-like charity for the poor and suffering. Glory be to the Father
and to the Son and to the Holy Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is
now, and ever shall be, world without end.

3 January:

O holy Magi, who offered to Jesus Christ gold, incense, and myrrh,
thereby recognizing Him to be at once King, God, and Man; obtain from
the Lord for us the grace never to present ourselves before Him with
empty hands; but that we may continually offer to Him the gold of
charity, the incense of prayer, and the myrrh of penance and
mortification. Glory be to the Father and to the Son and to the Holy
Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world
without end.

4 January:

O holy Magi, who, when warned by an angel not to return to Herd,
traveled back to your country be another road; obtain for us from the
Lord, the grace that, after having found Him in true repentance, we may
avoid all danger of losing Him again. Glory be to the Father and to the
Son and to the Holy Ghost, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever
shall be, world without end.

5 January:

O holy Magi, who were first among the Gentiles called to the knowledge
of Jesus Christ, and who persevered in the faith till your

PrayMoreNovenas modern

— not on PrayMoreNovenas

Fidelity note

The two are essentially unrelated in wording. The FishEaters "Novena to the Magi" is a generic petition formula (the classic "ask and you shall receive" novena prayer through Mary's intercession), with no daily meditations on the Epiphany mystery. Ember instead composes nine original catechetical meditations following the Matthew narrative — the star, the journey, the three gifts (gold/frankincense/myrrh), the adoration, the return by another way — which is the traditional Epiphany devotional pattern, but the text is original, not drawn from FishEaters. Ember matches the feast faithfully and is far richer thematically; it does not reproduce the FishEaters source.

St. Anthony the Abbot st-anthony-abbot-novena

Jan 8–16
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Anthony the Abbot, father of monasticism, seeking his help in silence, prayer, and spiritual combat.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Antão, pai do monaquismo, pedindo o seu auxílio no silêncio, na oração e no combate espiritual.

St. Anthony the Abbot (c. 251–356) was a wealthy young Egyptian who, hearing the Gospel call 'sell what you have and follow Me,' gave away his goods and withdrew into the desert. There he battled fierce temptations and demons, persevering in prayer and penance, and became the father of Christian monasticism. His life, written by St. Athanasius, drew countless souls to the desert and still teaches us the way of inward combat.

Santo Antão (c. 251–356) foi um jovem egípcio rico que, ao ouvir o chamado do Evangelho 'vende o que tens e segue-Me', distribuiu os seus bens e retirou-se para o deserto. Ali enfrentou tentações e demônios ferozes, perseverando na oração e na penitência, e tornou-se o pai do monaquismo cristão. A sua vida, escrita por Santo Atanásio, atraiu inúmeras almas ao deserto e ainda nos ensina o caminho do combate interior.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glorious St. Anthony, who left all things to seek God alone in the silence of the desert, draw us away from the distractions that scatter our hearts. Teach us to thirst for God above every passing thing. Amen.

Ó glorioso Santo Antão, que tudo deixastes para buscar a Deus somente no silêncio do deserto, afastai-nos das distrações que dispersam o nosso coração. Ensinai-nos a ter sede de Deus acima de toda coisa passageira. Amém.

Day 1: The Gospel call

Dia 1: O chamado do Evangelho

Hearing the words 'sell what you have and give to the poor,' Anthony took them as spoken to himself and obeyed at once. He teaches us to receive the Word of God as a personal summons.

Ao ouvir as palavras 'vende o que tens e dá aos pobres', Antão tomou-as como dirigidas a si mesmo e obedeceu de imediato. Ele nos ensina a acolher a Palavra de Deus como um chamado pessoal.

Intention

For the grace to hear and obey God's call.

Pela graça de ouvir e obedecer ao chamado de Deus.

Day 2: Renouncing the world

Dia 2: Renunciar ao mundo

Anthony gave away his inheritance and embraced poverty for the kingdom of heaven. His detachment frees us from the grip of possessions and the fear of having too little.

Antão distribuiu a sua herança e abraçou a pobreza pelo Reino dos céus. O seu desapego nos liberta da escravidão dos bens e do medo de ter pouco.

Intention

For freedom from attachment to earthly goods.

Pela liberdade do apego aos bens terrenos.

Day 3: The desert and silence

Dia 3: O deserto e o silêncio

In the stillness of the desert Anthony learned to listen to God without the noise of the world. His example calls us to make room for silence, where God speaks to the heart.

Na quietude do deserto, Antão aprendeu a escutar a Deus sem o ruído do mundo. O seu exemplo nos convida a fazer espaço para o silêncio, onde Deus fala ao coração.

Intention

For the gift of silence and recollection in prayer.

Pelo dom do silêncio e do recolhimento na oração.

Day 4: Combat with the demons

Dia 4: Combate contra os demônios

Anthony was assailed by the devil with terrors and seductions, yet he stood firm by faith and the sign of the Cross. He shows us that no temptation can overcome a soul that clings to Christ.

Antão foi atacado pelo demônio com terrores e seduções, mas permaneceu firme pela fé e pelo sinal da Cruz. Ele nos mostra que nenhuma tentação vence a alma que se apega a Cristo.

Intention

For strength to resist temptation and the snares of evil.

Pela força de resistir à tentação e às ciladas do mal.

Day 5: Endurance in penance

Dia 5: Perseverança na penitência

Through fasting, vigils, and hard labor Anthony disciplined his body to serve the spirit. His ascesis teaches us that self-denial, joined to love, makes the soul strong.

Pelo jejum, pelas vigílias e pelo trabalho árduo, Antão disciplinou o corpo para servir o espírito. A sua ascese nos ensina que a renúncia, unida ao amor, fortalece a alma.

Intention

For the grace of self-denial and penance.

Pela graça da renúncia e da penitência.

Day 6: After the trial, consolation

Dia 6: Após a provação, a consolação

When the worst assaults were past, Anthony asked the Lord where He had been; Christ answered that He had been beside him all along. We learn that God never abandons us in our struggles, even when He seems hidden.

Quando os piores ataques cessaram, Antão perguntou ao Senhor onde Ele estivera; Cristo respondeu que estivera ao seu lado o tempo todo. Aprendemos que Deus nunca nos abandona nas lutas, ainda que pareça oculto.

Intention

For trust in God's nearness amid spiritual dryness.

Pela confiança na presença de Deus em meio à aridez espiritual.

Day 7: Father of monks

Dia 7: Pai dos monges

Many came to Anthony seeking the way of perfection, and he guided them with wisdom and gentleness. His fatherhood reminds us that holiness, hidden in God, bears fruit for many souls.

Muitos vinham a Antão buscando o caminho da perfeição, e ele os guiava com sabedoria e mansidão. A sua paternidade nos lembra que a santidade, oculta em Deus, dá fruto para muitas almas.

Intention

For wise spiritual guides in the Church.

Por sábios guias espirituais na Igreja.

Day 8: Humility of the saint

Dia 8: A humildade do santo

Though revered as a holy man, Anthony counted himself the least and feared pride above all. His lowliness teaches us that the greater the grace, the deeper must be our humility.

Embora venerado como homem santo, Antão considerava-se o menor e temia o orgulho acima de tudo. A sua pequenez nos ensina que, quanto maior a graça, mais profunda deve ser a nossa humildade.

Intention

For the grace of true humility of heart.

Pela graça da verdadeira humildade de coração.

Day 9: Faithful unto death

Dia 9: Fiel até a morte

Anthony lived to a great age and died in peace, having fought the good fight to the end. He intercedes that we too may persevere and finish our course in fidelity to Christ.

Antão alcançou idade avançada e morreu em paz, tendo combatido o bom combate até o fim. Ele intercede para que também nós perseveremos e cumpramos a nossa jornada na fidelidade a Cristo.

Intention

For the grace of perseverance to the end.

Pela graça da perseverança até o fim.

Closing prayer

Holy father Anthony, valiant soldier of Christ, obtain for us courage in temptation and constancy in prayer. May we, with you, fight the good fight to the end and rejoice forever in the presence of God. Amen.

Santo padre Antão, valente soldado de Cristo, alcançai-nos coragem na tentação e constância na oração. Possamos, convosco, combater o bom combate até o fim e alegrar-nos para sempre na presença de Deus. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

There is no FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas text for St. Anthony the Abbot to compare against, so the Ember novena stands as an original composition. Its content is nonetheless faithful to the saint's well-documented life as told in St. Athanasius's *Life of Antony* — the Gospel call ("sell what you have"), renunciation, the desert and silence, demonic combat, asceticism, Christ's "I was here all along" consolation, his role as father of monasticism, his humility, and his peaceful death at a great age. The historical anchors are accurate and well chosen; no factual divergence noted.

St. Francis de Sales st-francis-de-sales-novena

Jan 15–23
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Francis de Sales, Doctor of gentleness, seeking the grace of a devout life lived in God's will.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Francisco de Sales, Doutor da mansidão, pedindo a graça de uma vida devota vivida na vontade de Deus.

St. Francis de Sales (1567–1622), Bishop of Geneva and Doctor of the Church, won countless souls back to the faith through patience, charity, and gentle persuasion rather than harshness. In his Introduction to the Devout Life he taught that holiness is for everyone in every state of life, lived through small daily acts of love. His counsel of conformity to God's will — to ask for nothing and refuse nothing — remains a sure path to peace of soul.

São Francisco de Sales (1567–1622), bispo de Genebra e Doutor da Igreja, reconquistou inúmeras almas para a fé pela paciência, pela caridade e pela doce persuasão, em vez da dureza. Na sua Introdução à Vida Devota, ensinou que a santidade é para todos, em todo estado de vida, vivida em pequenos atos diários de amor. O seu conselho de conformidade à vontade de Deus — nada pedir e nada recusar — permanece caminho seguro para a paz da alma.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O gentle St. Francis de Sales, friend of every soul, draw us to the sweetness of God's love. Calm our restless hearts and teach us to serve the Lord with patience, charity, and unwavering trust. Amen.

Ó suave São Francisco de Sales, amigo de toda alma, atraí-nos à doçura do amor de Deus. Acalmai os nossos corações inquietos e ensinai-nos a servir o Senhor com paciência, caridade e confiança inabalável. Amém.

Day 1: Devotion for everyone

Dia 1: A devoção para todos

Francis taught that holiness is not reserved for cloisters but is meant for the soldier, the merchant, the parent, and the worker. He shows us that we can sanctify the very duties of our daily state.

Francisco ensinava que a santidade não está reservada aos claustros, mas é destinada ao soldado, ao comerciante, ao pai, à mãe e ao trabalhador. Ele nos mostra que podemos santificar os próprios deveres do nosso estado cotidiano.

Intention

For the grace to seek holiness in our daily life.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade na nossa vida cotidiana.

Day 2: Gentleness of heart

Dia 2: Mansidão de coração

Naturally inclined to anger, Francis labored for years to become meek, and he won more souls by sweetness than others by force. He teaches that a drop of honey draws more than a barrel of vinegar.

Naturalmente inclinado à ira, Francisco esforçou-se por anos para tornar-se manso, e conquistou mais almas pela doçura do que outros pela força. Ele ensina que uma gota de mel atrai mais do que um barril de vinagre.

Intention

For the grace of gentleness toward others.

Pela graça da mansidão para com os outros.

Day 3: Patience with ourselves

Dia 3: Paciência conosco mesmos

Francis counseled us to correct our faults gently, without bitter self-reproach, rising again with confidence after every fall. He teaches that even our patience with our own weakness must be charitable.

Francisco aconselhava a corrigir as nossas faltas com doçura, sem amarga censura, levantando-nos com confiança após cada queda. Ele ensina que mesmo a paciência com a própria fraqueza deve ser caridosa.

Intention

For patience and gentleness with our own faults.

Pela paciência e mansidão com as nossas próprias faltas.

Day 4: Conformity to God's will

Dia 4: Conformidade à vontade de Deus

Francis summed up the spiritual life in surrender: ask for nothing, refuse nothing. He teaches that perfect peace is found in wanting only what God wants for us.

Francisco resumia a vida espiritual no abandono: nada pedir, nada recusar. Ele ensina que a paz perfeita se encontra em querer somente o que Deus quer para nós.

Intention

For the grace to conform our will to God's.

Pela graça de conformar a nossa vontade à de Deus.

Day 5: Living charity

Dia 5: A caridade viva

For Francis, devotion is simply charity grown active and prompt. He teaches that true love of God overflows into kindness, service, and patience toward our neighbor.

Para Francisco, a devoção é simplesmente a caridade tornada ativa e pronta. Ele ensina que o verdadeiro amor de Deus transborda em bondade, serviço e paciência para com o próximo.

Intention

For a charity active in word and deed.

Por uma caridade ativa em palavras e obras.

Day 6: The little virtues

Dia 6: As pequenas virtudes

Francis prized humility, gentleness, and patience above showy heroics, calling them the virtues of everyday life. He teaches us to grow holy through small, hidden, faithful acts of love.

Francisco prezava a humildade, a mansidão e a paciência acima dos heroísmos espetaculares, chamando-as virtudes da vida comum. Ele nos ensina a crescer em santidade por meio de pequenos atos de amor, ocultos e fiéis.

Intention

For faithfulness in the small duties of each day.

Pela fidelidade nos pequenos deveres de cada dia.

Day 7: Confidence in God

Dia 7: Confiança em Deus

Amid anxieties and trials Francis counseled boundless trust in the Father's tender care. He teaches us never to lose heart, for the same God who guides the world holds our small life as well.

Em meio às ansiedades e provações, Francisco aconselhava confiança sem limites no terno cuidado do Pai. Ele nos ensina a nunca desanimar, pois o mesmo Deus que governa o mundo sustenta também a nossa pequena vida.

Intention

For confident trust in God's loving providence.

Pela confiança serena na amorosa providência de Deus.

Day 8: A zealous shepherd

Dia 8: Um pastor zeloso

As bishop, Francis traveled, preached, and wrote tirelessly to bring straying souls home, never wearying of patience. His zeal shows us that love of souls is the heart of the apostolate.

Como bispo, Francisco viajava, pregava e escrevia incansavelmente para reconduzir as almas extraviadas, sem jamais cansar-se da paciência. O seu zelo nos mostra que o amor às almas é o coração do apostolado.

Intention

For zeal in leading others closer to God.

Pelo zelo em conduzir os outros para mais perto de Deus.

Day 9: Live Jesus!

Dia 9: Viva Jesus!

Francis signed his letters 'Live Jesus,' that Christ might live and reign in every heart. He calls us to make Jesus the center and the love of our whole life.

Francisco assinava as suas cartas com 'Viva Jesus', para que Cristo vivesse e reinasse em cada coração. Ele nos chama a fazer de Jesus o centro e o amor de toda a nossa vida.

Intention

That Jesus may live and reign in our hearts.

Para que Jesus viva e reine nos nossos corações.

Closing prayer

O holy Bishop and Doctor, who sought in all things only the good pleasure of God, obtain for us a heart wholly conformed to His will. May we, asking nothing and refusing nothing, find true peace in loving surrender. Amen.

Ó santo Bispo e Doutor, que em todas as coisas buscastes apenas o beneplácito de Deus, alcançai-nos um coração inteiramente conforme à sua vontade. Possamos, nada pedindo e nada recusando, encontrar a verdadeira paz no abandono amoroso. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="desales">
Novena
to St. Francis de Sales

Pray especially beginning on January 20 and ending on
January
28, the eve of the Feast of St. Francis de Sales.

O great prelate, most blessed saint, model of bishops, the glory of the
Church. Thou wast and ever shall be revered on earth as a man according
to God's own heart, and a most perfect imitator of our adorable model,
Jesus Christ. Thou wast the champion of the Faith whose zeal extirpated
heresy; the ardent victim of charity and docile disciple of that
heavenly spirit Who spoke by thy pen, and rendered thy inspired
doctrine the treasure of the Church. O good and faithful servant, thou
hast now entered into the joy of thy Lord; the divine and Holy Object
of thy love on earth is now thy reward exceedingly great in heaven. O
amiable saint, whose tender, compassionate charity was always the
refuge of the miserable, despise not our supplications. Since thy
conduct on earth toward all thy fellow-creatures, particularly the
weakest, marked thee out, like thy beloved Master, as the friend of
sinners, show thyself such to us, by obtaining for us the intentions of
this novena.

Receive us, O great Master of solid perfection into the number of those
whom thou didst form according to the true spirit of the religious
state. Teach us those heavenly virtues which thy blessed life, no less
than thy word, so constantly inculcated. Thou wast one of the most
humble of men, though gifted with all that could exalt thee before God
and the world; O plant in our hearts that root of every virtue,
humility. Thou wast an angel of peace, whose presence alone gave
consolation to the most afflicted hearts, and whose heavenly meekness
succeeded in gaining the most obdurate sinners; obtain, then, for us,
also, that peace of God which surpasseth all understanding; a
condescending meekness in our conduct toward our neighbor. But
principally teach us thy own favorite lesson of perfect conformity to
the will of God, that we may repose tranquilly in the arms of His
adorable Providence; that we may cast all our solicitude on God, so as
"to ask for nothing and to refuse nothing;" at least to ask for nothing
more earnestly than the treasure of His divine love, and to refuse
nothing so resolutely as the misfortune of consenting to sin. Amen.<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas, "St. Francis de Sales Novena." Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross and follows a fixed format ending with a day-specific "Pray for me, that…" petition and "St. Francis de Sales, pray for us!" The nine days track his biography:
- Day 1: imitating his devotion to the priesthood — "that I may do all I can to serve you in my life"
- Day 2: submitting to God's will despite obstacles
- Day 3: missionary work converting Calvinists — "persevere against all difficulties to lead souls to God"
- Day 4: persevering through trials
- Day 5: growth during his episcopal service — "grow in my spiritual life each day"
- Day 6: his spiritual-direction work — "cultivate a close relationship with God"
- Day 7: his advocacy of lay holiness — "embrace the unique call to holiness of my vocation"
- Day 8: his Catholic writings — "strive for great holiness… each day"
- Day 9: his lifelong service — "always seek to serve God and His Church"

Fidelity note

Ember is an independent composition, not a copy of either source. It shares strong thematic overlap with the PrayMoreNovenas arc — universal call to holiness in everyday life (Day 1 in both), conformity to God's will (Day 4 / Day 2), zeal for souls, lay holiness — all genuinely Salesian. Ember additionally draws directly on de Sales's own well-known sayings and signatures ("a drop of honey," "ask for nothing, refuse nothing," "Live Jesus!"), which makes it arguably more faithful to the saint's *Introduction to the Devout Life* than the PMN petitions. No factual divergence; the two are parallel devotions of the same spirit rather than the same text.

St. Thomas Aquinas st-thomas-aquinas-novena

Jan 19–27
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Thomas Aquinas, the Angelic Doctor, seeking love of truth, holy study, and devotion to the Eucharist.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Tomás de Aquino, o Doutor Angélico, pedindo o amor à verdade, o estudo santo e a devoção à Eucaristia.

St. Thomas Aquinas (1225–1274), the Angelic Doctor, was a Dominican friar whose Summa Theologiae harmonized faith and reason in service of divine truth. A man of deep prayer, he composed the Eucharistic hymns Adoro te devote and the sequence Lauda Sion, and began his studies with the prayer 'Creator ineffabilis.' Near the end of his life, after a vision, he called his vast writings 'so much straw' compared to what he had seen of God.

Santo Tomás de Aquino (1225–1274), o Doutor Angélico, foi um frade dominicano cuja Suma Teológica harmonizou a fé e a razão a serviço da verdade divina. Homem de oração profunda, compôs os hinos eucarísticos Adoro te devote e a sequência Lauda Sion, e iniciava os seus estudos com a oração 'Creator ineffabilis'. Perto do fim da vida, após uma visão, chamou os seus vastos escritos de 'palha' diante do que vira de Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O ineffable Creator, who gave to St. Thomas Aquinas a burning love of truth, send forth Your light into our minds and Your love into our hearts. By his prayers grant us to seek, to find, and to rest in You, the Truth that never fails. Amen.

Ó inefável Criador, que destes a Santo Tomás de Aquino um ardente amor à verdade, enviai a vossa luz às nossas mentes e o vosso amor aos nossos corações. Por suas preces, concedei-nos buscar, encontrar e repousar em Vós, a Verdade que jamais falha. Amém.

Day 1: Love of truth

Dia 1: O amor à verdade

From his youth Thomas was consumed by one question: 'What is God?' His whole life was a search for the Truth who is God Himself, drawing all knowledge back to its divine source.

Desde a juventude, Tomás foi tomado por uma só pergunta: 'O que é Deus?' Toda a sua vida foi uma busca da Verdade que é o próprio Deus, reconduzindo todo conhecimento à sua fonte divina.

Intention

For a sincere love of truth above all opinions.

Por um amor sincero à verdade acima de todas as opiniões.

Day 2: Faith and reason

Dia 2: Fé e razão

Thomas showed that faith and reason are not enemies but two paths from the one God of truth. He teaches us to honor the mind God gave us while bowing before mysteries beyond it.

Tomás demonstrou que a fé e a razão não são inimigas, mas dois caminhos provindos do único Deus da verdade. Ele nos ensina a honrar a inteligência que Deus nos deu, inclinando-nos diante dos mistérios que a superam.

Intention

For harmony between our faith and our reason.

Pela harmonia entre a nossa fé e a nossa razão.

Day 3: Study as prayer

Dia 3: O estudo como oração

Thomas began his work with the prayer 'Creator ineffabilis,' asking light to learn and grace to teach. He shows us that study undertaken for God becomes itself a form of worship.

Tomás iniciava o seu trabalho com a oração 'Creator ineffabilis', pedindo luz para aprender e graça para ensinar. Ele nos mostra que o estudo empreendido por Deus torna-se ele mesmo uma forma de adoração.

Intention

That our learning and work may glorify God.

Para que o nosso estudo e trabalho glorifiquem a Deus.

Day 4: Adoring the hidden God

Dia 4: Adorando o Deus oculto

In the hymn Adoro te devote Thomas knelt before the Eucharist, where sight, touch, and taste fail but faith alone suffices. He teaches us to adore Christ truly present beneath the veils of bread and wine.

No hino Adoro te devote, Tomás prostrou-se diante da Eucaristia, onde a vista, o tato e o paladar falham, mas a fé sozinha basta. Ele nos ensina a adorar Cristo verdadeiramente presente sob os véus do pão e do vinho.

Intention

For a deep, adoring faith in the Holy Eucharist.

Por uma fé profunda e adoradora na Santa Eucaristia.

Day 5: The bread of angels

Dia 5: O pão dos anjos

In the sequence Lauda Sion Thomas sang of the Eucharist as the bread of angels given to the children of men. He teaches us to receive Holy Communion with reverence, wonder, and love.

Na sequência Lauda Sion, Tomás cantou a Eucaristia como o pão dos anjos dado aos filhos dos homens. Ele nos ensina a receber a Sagrada Comunhão com reverência, assombro e amor.

Intention

For greater reverence in receiving Holy Communion.

Por maior reverência ao receber a Sagrada Comunhão.

Day 6: Humility of the wise

Dia 6: A humildade dos sábios

Though the most learned of his age, Thomas was gentle, silent, and unassuming, mocked in youth as the 'dumb ox.' His humility teaches us that true wisdom never puffs up but bows before God.

Embora o mais douto do seu tempo, Tomás era manso, silencioso e despretensioso, ridicularizado na juventude como o 'boi mudo'. A sua humildade nos ensina que a verdadeira sabedoria jamais ensoberbece, mas se inclina diante de Deus.

Intention

For humility joined to whatever knowledge we possess.

Pela humildade unida a todo conhecimento que possuímos.

Day 7: Drawing wisdom from the Cross

Dia 7: A sabedoria da Cruz

When the crucified Lord asked what reward he desired, Thomas answered, 'Only Yourself, Lord.' He teaches that all learning is empty unless it leads us to love God alone.

Quando o Senhor crucificado lhe perguntou que recompensa desejava, Tomás respondeu: 'Apenas a Vós mesmo, Senhor.' Ele ensina que todo saber é vazio se não nos conduz a amar somente a Deus.

Intention

That we may desire God above every other gift.

Para que desejemos a Deus acima de todo outro dom.

Day 8: All is straw

Dia 8: Tudo é palha

After a profound vision, Thomas laid down his pen, saying that all he had written seemed like straw beside what he had glimpsed of God. He teaches us that no thought can contain the One we are made to behold.

Após uma profunda visão, Tomás depôs a pena, dizendo que tudo o que escrevera parecia palha diante do que vislumbrara de Deus. Ele nos ensina que nenhum pensamento pode conter Aquele que fomos feitos para contemplar.

Intention

For longing for the vision of God above all things.

Pelo anseio da visão de Deus acima de todas as coisas.

Day 9: Truth in love

Dia 9: A verdade no amor

Thomas died in peace, receiving Viaticum with words of fervent faith in the Sacrament he had so loved. He intercedes that we may seek the truth, live it in charity, and rest at last in God.

Tomás morreu em paz, recebendo o Viático com palavras de fervorosa fé no Sacramento que tanto amara. Ele intercede para que busquemos a verdade, a vivamos na caridade e, enfim, repousemos em Deus.

Intention

For the grace to seek and live the truth in charity.

Pela graça de buscar e viver a verdade na caridade.

Closing prayer

O Angelic Doctor, who adored the hidden God in the Blessed Sacrament and counted all learning as straw before His glory, obtain for us a humble and ardent faith. May we love truth for God's sake and adore Him with all our heart. Amen.

Ó Doutor Angélico, que adorastes o Deus oculto no Santíssimo Sacramento e tivestes todo saber por palha diante da sua glória, alcançai-nos uma fé humilde e ardente. Possamos amar a verdade por amor de Deus e adorá-Lo de todo o coração. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="aquinas">
Novena
to St. Thomas Aquinas
Pray especially beginning
on 26 February (27 February in leap years) and ending on March 6.

First day:

"He who loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and he
who loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me" (Matthew
10:37).

Ineffable Creator, Who out of the treasures of Your wisdom has
appointed three hierarchies of Angels and set them in admirable order
high above the heavens and has disposed the different portions of the
universe in such marvelous array, You Who are called the True Source of
Light and super-eminent Principle of Wisdom, be pleased to cast a beam
of Your radiance upon the darkness of my mind and dispel from me the
double darkness of sin and ignorance in which I have been born.

You Who makes eloquent the tongues of little children, fashion my words
and pour upon my lips the grace of Your benediction. Grant me
penetration to understand, capacity to retain, method and facility in
study, subtlety in interpretation and abundant grace of expression.

Order the beginning, direct the progress and perfect the achievement of
my work, You who are true God and Man and lives and reigns for ever and
ever. Amen.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be.

Blessed Thomas, pray for us; that we may be made worthy of the promises
of Christ. In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy
Spirit. Amen.

Second day:

“For God has not called us for uncleanness, but in holiness” (1
Thessalonians 4:7).

Ineffable Creator, Who out of the treasures of Your wisdom has
appointed three hierarchies of Angels and set them in admirable order
high above the heavens and has disposed the different portions of the
universe in such marvelous array, You Who are called the True Source of
Light and super-eminent Principle of Wisdom, be pleased to cast a beam
of Your radiance upon the darkness of my mind and dispel from me the
double darkness of sin and ignorance in which I have been born.

You Who makes eloquent the tongues of little children, fashion my words
and pour upon my lips the grace of Your benediction. Grant me
penetration to understand, capacity to retain, method and facility in
study, subtlety in interpretation and abundant grace of expression.

Order the beginning, direct the progress and perfect the achievement of
my work, You who are true God and Man and lives and reigns for ever and
ever. Amen.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be.

Blessed Thomas, pray for us; that we may be made worthy of the promises
of Christ. In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy
Spirit. Amen.

Third day:

“Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself
will be exalted” (Matthew 23:12).

Ineffable Creator, Who out of the treasures of Your wisdom has
appointed three hierarchies of Angels and set them in admirable order
high above the heavens and has disposed the different portions of the
universe in such marvelous array, You Who are called the True Source of
Light and super-eminent Principle of Wisdom, be pleased to cast a beam
of Your radiance upon the darkness of my mind and dispel from me the
double darkness of sin and ignorance in which I have been born.

You Who makes eloquent the tongues of little children, fashion my words
and pour upon my lips the grace of Your benediction. Grant me
penetration to understand, capacity to retain, method and facility in
study, subtlety in interpretation and abundant grace of expression.

Order the beginning, direct the progress and perfect the achievement of
my work, You who are true God and Man and lives and reigns for ever and
ever. Amen.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be.

Blessed Thomas, pray for us; that we may be made worthy of the promises
of Christ. In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy
Spirit. Amen.

Fourth day

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas, "St. Thomas Aquinas Novena." Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross and "Dear Lord, you chose St. Thomas Aquinas to write about Your Word and the Christian faith, help me to be educated by the faith that is revealed in his writings," then a day-specific intercession, closing with a Glory Be and the trinitarian blessing. The nine days are organized as petitions:
- Day 1: for students (those who learn easily and those who struggle), openness to truth
- Day 2: for the Church's growth, more priests and vocations, strength for the Pope, theologians, missionaries
- Day 3: deeper love and respect for the Holy Eucharist and Christ's presence in Communion
- Day 4: humility when facing insults and attacks on one's character
- Day 5: patience in discerning vocations (priests, religious, married, single)
- Day 6: greater reliance on prayer amid theological/personal difficulty and confusion
- Day 7: increased charity toward those who disagree with us
- Day 8: dedication to seeking and teaching truth; prayers for those lost in falsehood
- Day 9: protection from sin, chastity, and against abortion, abuse, pornography, infidelity

Fidelity note

Ember is an independent composition, not a copy of the PMN petitions. Both rightly center Thomas's love of truth, his Eucharistic devotion, and humility, and there is genuine overlap (Day 3/4 Eucharist; the humility theme). But Ember is markedly more biographical and devotional, weaving in authentic Thomistic detail the PMN version omits — the "What is God?" question, the *Creator ineffabilis* study-prayer, the *Adoro te devote* and *Lauda Sion* hymns, the "dumb ox" mockery, the crucifix's "Only Yourself, Lord," and the "all is straw" vision. These are accurate to the saint's life and writings, making Ember the fuller and more faithful portrait of the two. No factual divergence noted.

St. John Bosco st-john-bosco-novena

Jan 22–30
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. John Bosco, priest and tireless father of poor and abandoned youth.

Uma novena de nove dias a São João Bosco, sacerdote e incansável pai dos jovens pobres e abandonados.

St. John Bosco (1815–1888) gathered the street boys of Turin and raised them in faith and trade through what he called the Preventive System — reason, religion, and loving-kindness. Guided since childhood by prophetic dreams and entrusting all to Mary Help of Christians, he founded the Salesians and oratories that still shelter the young. This novena begs his fatherly intercession for the souls and needs entrusted to us.

São João Bosco (1815–1888) recolheu os meninos de rua de Turim e os formou na fé e no trabalho por meio do que chamou Sistema Preventivo — razão, religião e amabilidade. Guiado desde a infância por sonhos proféticos e confiando tudo a Maria Auxiliadora, fundou os Salesianos e os oratórios que ainda hoje acolhem os jovens. Esta novena implora sua intercessão paterna pelas almas e necessidades a nós confiadas.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glorious St. John Bosco, father and teacher of the young, you spent yourself wholly for souls and never turned away a child in need. Draw near to me now as you drew near to your boys, and present my prayer before the throne of God. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Bosco, pai e mestre da juventude, que vos consumistes inteiramente pelas almas e jamais afastastes um menino necessitado, aproximai-vos de mim como vos aproximáveis de vossos jovens e apresentai a minha súplica diante do trono de Deus. Amém.

Day 1: A heart for abandoned youth

Dia 1: Um coração pela juventude abandonada

In the streets and prisons of Turin, John Bosco saw boys with no one to love them and resolved to be their father. His first oratory began with a single orphan and grew into a refuge for hundreds.

Nas ruas e prisões de Turim, João Bosco via meninos a quem ninguém amava e decidiu ser-lhes pai. Seu primeiro oratório começou com um só órfão e cresceu até abrigar centenas.

Intention

For every child who feels unwanted, that they may find someone to love them in Christ.

Por toda criança que se sente rejeitada, para que encontre quem a ame em Cristo.

Day 2: The dream at nine years old

Dia 2: O sonho aos nove anos

At nine, John dreamed of unruly children he was told to win not with blows but with gentleness, and of a Lady who would be his teacher. He pondered that dream all his life and let it shape his mission.

Aos nove anos, João sonhou com crianças indisciplinadas que devia conquistar não com golpes, mas com mansidão, e com uma Senhora que seria sua mestra. Meditou esse sonho a vida inteira e deixou que moldasse sua missão.

Intention

For the grace to recognize and follow God's call in the events of my life.

Pela graça de reconhecer e seguir o chamado de Deus nos acontecimentos da minha vida.

Day 3: Reason

Dia 3: A razão

Don Bosco appealed first to the mind and conscience of the young, explaining rules patiently rather than imposing them by fear. He trusted that a boy who understands the good will more readily choose it.

Dom Bosco apelava primeiro à inteligência e à consciência dos jovens, explicando as regras com paciência em vez de impô-las pelo medo. Confiava em que o menino que compreende o bem mais prontamente o escolhe.

Intention

For teachers and parents, that they may guide the young with patience and reason.

Pelos educadores e pais, para que conduzam os jovens com paciência e razão.

Day 4: Religion

Dia 4: A religião

At the heart of his method stood frequent Confession, Communion, and devotion to Our Lady, for he knew no upbringing endures without God. He led his boys to the sacraments not as a duty but as a feast.

No centro de seu método estavam a Confissão e a Comunhão frequentes e a devoção a Nossa Senhora, pois sabia que nenhuma formação perdura sem Deus. Conduzia seus meninos aos sacramentos não como dever, mas como festa.

Intention

For a hunger for the sacraments in the hearts of the young.

Por uma fome dos sacramentos no coração dos jovens.

Day 5: Loving-kindness

Dia 5: A amabilidade

Don Bosco insisted that it is not enough to love the young; they must know they are loved. He played their games, learned their names, and made the playground a place where grace could take root.

Dom Bosco insistia que não basta amar os jovens; eles precisam saber que são amados. Participava de seus jogos, aprendia seus nomes e fazia do pátio um lugar onde a graça pudesse criar raízes.

Intention

That those I serve may truly know they are loved.

Para que aqueles a quem sirvo verdadeiramente saibam que são amados.

Day 6: Mary Help of Christians

Dia 6: Maria Auxiliadora

Don Bosco attributed every grace of his work to Our Lady and built a great basilica in her honor as Help of Christians. He told his sons to spread her devotion and watch the wonders she would work.

Dom Bosco atribuía toda graça de sua obra a Nossa Senhora e ergueu em sua honra uma grande basílica como Auxiliadora dos Cristãos. Dizia a seus filhos que difundissem essa devoção e vissem as maravilhas que ela operaria.

Intention

That I may entrust all my needs to Mary, Help of Christians.

Para que eu confie todas as minhas necessidades a Maria Auxiliadora.

Day 7: Tireless work for souls

Dia 7: Trabalho incansável pelas almas

He labored late into the night writing, teaching, and hearing confessions, with the motto 'Give me souls, take away the rest.' He spent his health without counting the cost, asking only that his boys reach heaven.

Trabalhava noite adentro escrevendo, ensinando e ouvindo confissões, com o lema 'Dai-me almas, levai o resto'. Gastou a saúde sem medir o custo, pedindo apenas que seus meninos alcançassem o céu.

Intention

For zeal in my own duties, offered for the good of souls.

Por zelo em meus próprios deveres, oferecidos pelo bem das almas.

Day 8: Holiness in cheerfulness

Dia 8: A santidade na alegria

Don Bosco taught his boys, as he taught young St. Dominic Savio, that sanctity consists in being always joyful and doing one's duty well. He banished gloom from his houses and made holiness attractive.

Dom Bosco ensinava a seus meninos, como ensinou ao jovem São Domingos Sávio, que a santidade consiste em estar sempre alegre e cumprir bem o seu dever. Baniu a tristeza de suas casas e tornou a santidade atraente.

Intention

For the grace to serve God with a joyful heart.

Pela graça de servir a Deus com coração alegre.

Day 9: A father even in death

Dia 9: Pai até na morte

As he lay dying in 1888, Don Bosco's last counsel to his sons was to love and care for the young and to trust in Mary. From heaven he still watches over the work he began on earth.

Ao morrer em 1888, o último conselho de Dom Bosco a seus filhos foi amar e cuidar dos jovens e confiar em Maria. Do céu ele ainda vela pela obra que iniciou na terra.

Intention

That, faithful to the end, I may one day rejoice with St. John Bosco in heaven.

Para que, fiel até o fim, eu me alegre um dia com São João Bosco no céu.

Closing prayer

St. John Bosco, who taught that holiness is found in cheerfulness and daily duty, obtain for me the grace I ask in this novena, if it be for God's glory and my salvation. Make me, like your boys, a friend of God, devoted to Our Lady and faithful in small things. Amen.

São João Bosco, que ensinastes que a santidade se encontra na alegria e no dever de cada dia, alcançai-me a graça que peço nesta novena, se for para a glória de Deus e a minha salvação. Fazei-me, como vossos meninos, amigo de Deus, devoto de Nossa Senhora e fiel nas pequenas coisas. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas, "St. John Bosco Novena." A single prayer repeated identically each of the nine days (no distinct daily meditations). Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross and prays:

"Oh holy patron of the youth, I come to you with sincere confidence in your closeness to Jesus. Saint John Bosco, I need your help, I need your prayers, I need your intercession to God for His grace to help me with…" (state intentions)

followed by the three-fold appeal "With your love of Mary, our Mother, pray for me! With your love for Jesus in the Blessed Sacrament, pray for me! With your love for all those who suffer, pray for me!" and the concluding petition "Don Bosco, please pray fervently for the grace that I now ask from God our Father… that I may have a sincere acceptance of the Will of God and a perfect trust in Him." Each day ends with the Our Father, Hail Mary, Glory Be, and the trinitarian closing.

Fidelity note

Ember is an independent composition and shares almost no wording with PrayMoreNovenas, whose text is a single repeated petition rather than daily meditations. Their common ground is structural (the Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be frame) and thematic (Don Bosco's closeness to youth, devotion to Mary Help of Christians, Eucharistic emphasis, acceptance of God's will). Ember is far richer and accurately biographical, organizing the nine days around the saint's real life and his Preventive System — reason, religion, loving-kindness (Days 3-5), the dream at nine, "Give me souls, take away the rest," St. Dominic Savio, and his death in 1888. All historical claims are sound; Ember is the fuller and more catechetical of the two.

Candlemas / Purification candlemas-novena

Jan 24–Feb 1
description & history

A nine-day novena for Candlemas, the Presentation of the Lord and the Purification of Our Lady.

Uma novena de nove dias para a festa das Candeias, a Apresentação do Senhor e a Purificação de Nossa Senhora.

Forty days after Christmas the Church keeps Candlemas, when Mary and Joseph carried the infant Christ to the Temple to present Him to the Father and fulfill the law of purification. There the aged Simeon took the Child in his arms and hailed Him as 'a light to enlighten the nations.' From his words comes the blessing of candles, signs of Christ the Light; this novena prepares the heart to receive that Light.

Quarenta dias após o Natal, a Igreja celebra as Candeias, quando Maria e José levaram o Menino Jesus ao Templo para apresentá-lo ao Pai e cumprir a lei da purificação. Ali o ancião Simeão tomou o Menino nos braços e o saudou como 'luz para iluminar as nações'. De suas palavras nasce a bênção das velas, sinais de Cristo Luz; esta novena prepara o coração para acolher essa Luz.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Lord Jesus Christ, presented in the Temple and received into the arms of just Simeon, you are the Light that no darkness can overcome. Enter the temple of my heart in these nine days and prepare me to welcome you. Amen.

Ó Senhor Jesus Cristo, apresentado no Templo e recebido nos braços do justo Simeão, vós sois a Luz que treva alguma pode vencer. Entrai no templo do meu coração nestes nove dias e preparai-me para vos acolher. Amém.

Day 1: The Law fulfilled

Dia 1: A Lei cumprida

Though the Lord of the Temple, Christ submitted to the law of presentation as a humble Hebrew child. He who owed nothing chose to fulfill all righteousness for our sake.

Embora Senhor do Templo, Cristo submeteu-se à lei da apresentação como um humilde menino hebreu. Aquele que nada devia quis cumprir toda a justiça por amor a nós.

Intention

For the grace of humble obedience to God's will.

Pela graça da humilde obediência à vontade de Deus.

Day 2: The Purification of Mary

Dia 2: A Purificação de Maria

The Immaculate Virgin, who needed no purifying, came to the Temple in obedience like any other mother. Her humility teaches us never to set ourselves above the lowly path of the law of God.

A Virgem Imaculada, que de purificação não precisava, veio ao Templo em obediência como qualquer outra mãe. Sua humildade nos ensina a nunca nos colocarmos acima do humilde caminho da lei de Deus.

Intention

For the grace of humility, after the example of Our Lady.

Pela graça da humildade, segundo o exemplo de Nossa Senhora.

Day 3: The offering of the poor

Dia 3: A oferta dos pobres

Mary and Joseph offered two turtledoves, the gift the law allowed to the poor. The King of kings was redeemed with the offering of the lowly, sanctifying poverty forever.

Maria e José ofereceram duas rolinhas, a oferta que a lei permitia aos pobres. O Rei dos reis foi resgatado com a oferta dos humildes, santificando para sempre a pobreza.

Intention

For the poor, and for a spirit of detachment in my own heart.

Pelos pobres e por um espírito de desapego no meu próprio coração.

Day 4: Simeon's long waiting

Dia 4: A longa espera de Simeão

Simeon had waited all his life for the consolation of Israel, faithful to the promise that he would not die before seeing the Christ. His patience reminds us that God keeps His word in His own hour.

Simeão esperou a vida inteira pela consolação de Israel, fiel à promessa de que não morreria sem ver o Cristo. Sua paciência nos lembra que Deus cumpre Sua palavra na hora que lhe apraz.

Intention

For perseverance and patience while awaiting God's promises.

Pela perseverança e paciência na espera das promessas de Deus.

Day 5: Nunc dimittis — now you may dismiss

Dia 5: Nunc dimittis — agora podeis despedir

Holding the Child, Simeon prayed that he might now depart in peace, for his eyes had seen salvation. Having beheld Christ, he asked nothing more of this world.

Tomando o Menino, Simeão rezou para que pudesse agora partir em paz, pois seus olhos haviam visto a salvação. Tendo contemplado a Cristo, nada mais pediu deste mundo.

Intention

For the grace of a peaceful and holy death.

Pela graça de uma morte serena e santa.

Day 6: A light to the nations

Dia 6: Luz das nações

Simeon proclaimed the Child a light of revelation to the Gentiles and the glory of Israel. In Christ the long night of the world gives way to an unfailing dawn.

Simeão proclamou o Menino luz para revelação dos gentios e glória de Israel. Em Cristo a longa noite do mundo cede lugar a uma aurora que não falha.

Intention

That the light of Christ may reach those still in darkness.

Para que a luz de Cristo alcance os que ainda estão nas trevas.

Day 7: The sword foretold

Dia 7: A espada anunciada

Simeon warned Mary that a sword would pierce her own soul, joining her even now to her Son's coming Passion. She received the prophecy in silence, treasuring it in her heart.

Simeão advertiu Maria de que uma espada lhe traspassaria a própria alma, unindo-a desde então à futura Paixão de seu Filho. Ela recebeu a profecia em silêncio, guardando-a no coração.

Intention

For the grace to unite my sorrows to those of Christ and His Mother.

Pela graça de unir minhas dores às de Cristo e de sua Mãe.

Day 8: Anna the prophetess

Dia 8: Ana, a profetisa

The aged widow Anna, who served God night and day in the Temple with fasting and prayer, came forward and spoke of the Child to all who awaited redemption. A life of prayer made her quick to recognize her Savior.

A anciã viúva Ana, que servia a Deus noite e dia no Templo com jejum e oração, aproximou-se e falava do Menino a todos os que esperavam a redenção. Uma vida de oração a tornou pronta para reconhecer o seu Salvador.

Intention

For perseverance in prayer and faithful worship.

Pela perseverança na oração e no culto fiel.

Day 9: The blessed candles

Dia 9: As velas benditas

On this feast the Church blesses candles and carries them in procession, for Christ Himself is the true Light they signify. As wax is consumed to give flame, so the Christian is called to be spent for love.

Nesta festa a Igreja abençoa as velas e as leva em procissão, pois o próprio Cristo é a verdadeira Luz que elas significam. Como a cera se consome para dar chama, assim o cristão é chamado a gastar-se por amor.

Intention

That my life may shine with the light of Christ for others.

Para que a minha vida brilhe com a luz de Cristo para os outros.

Closing prayer

O Christ, the Light of the world, as the blessed candle is consumed in giving light, so let my life be spent in your service. Through the prayers of the Virgin Mother who presented you in the Temple, grant me the grace I seek and a holy death in your peace. Amen.

Ó Cristo, Luz do mundo, assim como a vela bendita se consome ao dar luz, que a minha vida se gaste no vosso serviço. Pelas orações da Virgem Mãe que vos apresentou no Templo, concedei-me a graça que busco e uma santa morte na vossa paz. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas, "Presentation of the Lord Novena." Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross and "Dear Lord, we thank You and praise You for the mystery of the Presentation. In honor of this mystery, we ask You to hear our prayers," and closes "Lord, hear our prayers!" with the Sign of the Cross, plus a personalized "we especially ask in this novena for (mention your intentions here)." The nine days are organized as petitions:
- Day 1: blessing for all families to be full of holiness and love
- Day 2: God's blessing and protection for all children in the world
- Day 3: blessing and protection for all mothers
- Day 4: growth in submission to God's will
- Day 5: developing the humility necessary for spiritual growth
- Day 6: growth in holy poverty and detachment from material things
- Day 7: increased virtue of hope (referencing the witness of Simeon and Anna)
- Day 8: grace to accept suffering willingly as Mary did
- Day 9: deeper trust in God despite future hardships

Fidelity note

Ember is an independent composition, not a translation of the PrayMoreNovenas text. Both treat the same feast and overlap on several virtues — submission to God's will, humility, holy poverty/detachment, and the figures of Simeon and Anna. But the genres differ: PMN frames the days as intercessions (for families, children, mothers), while Ember walks through the Luke 2 narrative scene by scene — the fulfillment of the Law, Mary's Purification, the offering of the poor, Simeon's waiting, the Nunc dimittis, the light to the nations, the sword prophecy, Anna, and the blessing of candles. Ember is more scripturally and liturgically grounded (it alone develops the candle symbolism that names the feast). All content is doctrinally and historically faithful; the two are parallel devotions of the same feast rather than the same text.

Our Lady of Lourdes our-lady-of-lourdes-novena

Feb 2–10
description & history

A nine-day novena to Our Lady of Lourdes, the Immaculate Conception who appeared to St. Bernadette.

Uma novena de nove dias a Nossa Senhora de Lourdes, a Imaculada Conceição que apareceu a Santa Bernadette.

In 1858, at the grotto of Massabielle in Lourdes, the Blessed Virgin appeared eighteen times to a poor and unlettered girl, St. Bernadette Soubirous, calling herself 'the Immaculate Conception.' She asked for penance and prayer for sinners and revealed a spring whose waters have brought countless healings of body and soul. This novena draws near to Our Lady of Lourdes with the trust of a child seeking a Mother's help.

Em 1858, na gruta de Massabielle, em Lourdes, a Santíssima Virgem apareceu dezoito vezes a uma menina pobre e iletrada, Santa Bernadette Soubirous, chamando a si mesma de 'a Imaculada Conceição'. Pediu penitência e oração pelos pecadores e revelou uma fonte cujas águas trouxeram incontáveis curas do corpo e da alma. Esta novena aproxima-se de Nossa Senhora de Lourdes com a confiança de um filho que busca o auxílio de sua Mãe.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Immaculate Virgin Mary, who at Lourdes opened heaven to a humble child, turn your merciful eyes upon me as you turned them upon Bernadette. Receive the petition I bring to your grotto and present it to your Son. Amen.

Ó Virgem Imaculada Maria, que em Lourdes abristes o céu a uma humilde menina, voltai para mim vossos olhos misericordiosos como os voltastes para Bernadette. Recebei a súplica que trago à vossa gruta e apresentai-a a vosso Filho. Amém.

Day 1: The Lady appears

Dia 1: A aparição da Senhora

On a cold February day Bernadette saw a beautiful Lady in the rocky niche, holding a rosary, and instinctively reached for her own. Heaven chose a poor, asthmatic girl gathering firewood to be its messenger.

Num frio dia de fevereiro, Bernadette viu uma bela Senhora no nicho rochoso, segurando um rosário, e instintivamente buscou o seu. O céu escolheu uma menina pobre e asmática, que recolhia lenha, para ser sua mensageira.

Intention

For the grace to recognize God's presence in the humble and unexpected.

Pela graça de reconhecer a presença de Deus no humilde e no inesperado.

Day 2: 'I am the Immaculate Conception'

Dia 2: 'Eu sou a Imaculada Conceição'

When asked her name, the Lady joined her hands, lifted her eyes, and said, 'I am the Immaculate Conception.' Bernadette did not understand the words, yet they confirmed the dogma the Church had defined only four years before.

Ao ser perguntada por seu nome, a Senhora juntou as mãos, ergueu os olhos e disse: 'Eu sou a Imaculada Conceição'. Bernadette não entendeu as palavras, mas elas confirmavam o dogma que a Igreja definira apenas quatro anos antes.

Intention

In thanksgiving for Mary's Immaculate Conception and her sinless love.

Em ação de graças pela Imaculada Conceição de Maria e por seu amor sem mancha.

Day 3: The hidden spring

Dia 3: A fonte oculta

The Lady told Bernadette to drink and wash at a spring, and the child scratched the muddy ground until clear water welled up. From that small trickle has flowed a stream of healing for the world.

A Senhora disse a Bernadette que bebesse e se lavasse numa fonte, e a menina escavou o chão lodoso até brotar água límpida. Daquele pequeno fio jorrou para o mundo uma corrente de cura.

Intention

For all the sick who seek healing of body and soul.

Por todos os enfermos que buscam a cura do corpo e da alma.

Day 4: Penance, penance, penance

Dia 4: Penitência, penitência, penitência

The Lady's message was simple and grave: penance for one's own sins and for the sins of others. She invited not despair but a humble turning back to God.

A mensagem da Senhora foi simples e grave: penitência pelos próprios pecados e pelos pecados dos outros. Ela convidava não ao desespero, mas a uma humilde conversão a Deus.

Intention

For the grace of true penance and amendment of life.

Pela graça da verdadeira penitência e da emenda de vida.

Day 5: Prayer for sinners

Dia 5: Oração pelos pecadores

Again and again the Lady asked Bernadette to pray for the conversion of sinners. The Mother of Mercy weeps for her straying children and longs to bring them home.

Repetidas vezes a Senhora pediu a Bernadette que rezasse pela conversão dos pecadores. A Mãe de Misericórdia chora por seus filhos desgarrados e anseia por reconduzi-los ao lar.

Intention

For the conversion of sinners and of those far from God.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e dos que estão longe de Deus.

Day 6: Bernadette's faithfulness

Dia 6: A fidelidade de Bernadette

Mocked, questioned, and pressured by authorities, Bernadette never altered her account or sought any reward. Her simple steadfastness made her a fitting witness to the Queen of Heaven.

Zombada, interrogada e pressionada pelas autoridades, Bernadette jamais alterou seu relato nem buscou recompensa alguma. Sua firmeza simples fez dela uma digna testemunha da Rainha do Céu.

Intention

For courage to bear witness to the truth despite opposition.

Por coragem para testemunhar a verdade apesar da oposição.

Day 7: Health of the sick

Dia 7: Saúde dos enfermos

To Lourdes come the suffering of every land, carried to the grotto in hope. Many are healed in body; far more receive the deeper grace to bear their cross with Christ.

A Lourdes acorrem os sofredores de toda terra, levados à gruta na esperança. Muitos são curados no corpo; mais ainda recebem a graça mais profunda de carregar sua cruz com Cristo.

Intention

For those who suffer, that they may find strength and consolation.

Por aqueles que sofrem, para que encontrem força e consolação.

Day 8: The light of the candles

Dia 8: A luz das velas

Each night the faithful process at Lourdes with lighted candles, singing the Ave to Mary. The flame in each hand is a prayer made visible, joining countless hearts as one.

Cada noite os fiéis caminham em procissão em Lourdes com velas acesas, cantando a Ave a Maria. A chama em cada mão é uma oração tornada visível, unindo incontáveis corações em um só.

Intention

For perseverance in daily prayer to Our Lady.

Pela perseverança na oração diária a Nossa Senhora.

Day 9: Our Lady's enduring care

Dia 9: O cuidado perene de Nossa Senhora

What began at a poor grotto has drawn millions to conversion and trust for over a century and a half. Our Lady of Lourdes still bends down to her children with a Mother's tenderness.

O que começou numa pobre gruta atraiu milhões à conversão e à confiança por mais de um século e meio. Nossa Senhora de Lourdes ainda se inclina sobre seus filhos com a ternura de uma Mãe.

Intention

That I may entrust my whole life to the care of Mary.

Para que eu confie toda a minha vida ao cuidado de Maria.

Closing prayer

Our Lady of Lourdes, health of the sick and refuge of sinners, obtain for me the grace I ask in this novena, if it be God's will, and above all the conversion and healing of my soul. Help me to do penance and to pray, as you asked at the grotto. Amen.

Nossa Senhora de Lourdes, saúde dos enfermos e refúgio dos pecadores, alcançai-me a graça que peço nesta novena, se for da vontade de Deus, e sobretudo a conversão e a cura da minha alma. Ajudai-me a fazer penitência e a rezar, como pedistes na gruta. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="ollourdes">
Novena
to Our Lady of Lourdes

Opening Prayer To Be
Prayed Each Day

Be blessed, O most pure Virgin, for having vouchsafed to manifest thy
shining glory with life, sweetness and beauty, in the Grotto of
Lourdes, saying to the child, St. Bernadette: “I am the Immaculate
Conception.” A thousand times we praise thee for thine Immaculate
Conception. And now, O ever Immaculate Virgin, Mother of Mercy, Health
of the Infirm, Refuge of Sinners, Comforter of the Afflicted, thou
knowest our wants, our troubles, and our sufferings; deign to cast upon
us thy look of mercy.

By appearing in the grotto of Lourdes, thou wast pleased to make it a
privileged sanctuary, from whence thou dost dispense thy favors, and
already many have obtained the cure of their infirmities, both
spiritual and physical. We come, therefore, with the most unbounded
confidence to implore thy maternal affection, and intercession. Obtain
for us, O loving Mother, the request we beseech of thee&#8230;

(Mention your intentions here)

Through gratitude for thy favors, we will endeavor to imitate thy
virtues, that we may one day share thy glory. O Queen of Heaven.

O Mary Immaculate, O Mother of Christ, thine influence was
plentitudinous with thy Divine Son, while still upon earth. Do thou
shine forth thine influence ever present in Heaven. Pray for us, and
obtain for us from thy Divine Son our special request, if it be His
Will. Amen.

Our Lady of Lourdes, pray for us.

Saint Bernadette, pray for us.

First Day

O Mary Immaculate, O Sovereign Grace, Virgin and Mother, and Queen of
Heaven, chosen from all eternity to be the Mother of the Eternal Word
and by that virtue, preserved from Original Sin, we kneel before thee
as did little Bernadette at Lourdes and pray with childlike trust in
thee, that as we contemplate thy glorious appearance there, thou wilt
look with mercy on our present petition, and secure for us a favorable
answer to the request for which we offer this novena.

(Mention your intentions here)

O Brilliant star of purity, O Mary Immaculate, Our Lady of Lourdes,
glorious in thine Assumption, triumphant in thy Coronation, show unto
us thy mercy, O Virgin Mary, O Queen of Heaven and Our Mother, and be
our comfort, our hope, our strength, and our consolation. Amen.

Our Lady of Lourdes, pray for us.
