Saint Bernadette, pray for us.

Second Day

Blessed be thee, O most pure Virgin, for having vouchsafed to manifest
thyself shining with light, sweetness and beauty, in the Grotto of
Lourdes, saying to the child Saint Bernadette: “I am the Immaculate
Conception!” O Mary Immaculate, inflame our hearts with one ray of the
burning love of thy pure heart. Let them be consumed with love for
Jesus and for thee, in order that we may merit one day to enjoy thy
glorious eternity. O Mediatrix of All Graces here below, take into thy
keeping, and present to thy Divine Son, the intention with which we are
offer this novena.

(Mention your intentions here)

O Brilliant star of purity, O Mary Immaculate, Our Lady of Lourdes,
glorious in thine Assumption, triumphant in thy Coronation, show unto
us thy mercy, O Virgin Mary, O Queen of Heaven and Our Mother, and be
our comfort, our hope, our strength, and our consolation. Amen.

Our Lady of Lourdes, pray for us.

Saint Bernadette, pray for us.

Third Day

“Thou art all fair, O Mary, and there is in thee no stain of original
sin.” O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to
thee. O brilliant Star of Sanctity, as on that lovely day, upon the
rough hewn rocks at Lourdes, when thou didst speak to the child
Bernadette, and a fountain broke from the plain earth, and miracles
happened, and the great shrine of Lourdes began, do now, we beseech
thee, hear our fervent prayer and likewise obtain for us the fulfilment
of the intention we now so earnestly seek.

(Mention your intentions here)

O Brilliant star of purity, O Mary

PrayMoreNovenas modern

The PrayMoreNovenas Our Lady of Lourdes Novena opens each day with the Sign of the Cross, then a core daily prayer addressing Our Lady of Lourdes with childlike trust, acknowledging the sanctuary's spiritual and physical healing, a space to state intentions, and the Memorare ("Remember, O most gracious Virgin Mary, that never was it known that anyone who fled to thy protection..."). Each day adds a unique petition:
- Day 1: "Show us your mercy, O Mother of God"
- Day 2: "Comfort us in our affliction"
- Day 3: "Pray that we may have hope in times of trial"
- Day 4: "Pray for the grace of God to be our strength"
- Day 5: "Pray that we may reach the consolation of heaven"
- Day 6: "Manifest in us your sweetness and beauty"
- Day 7: "Shower us with the love of your pure heart"
- Day 8: "Fill our hearts with love for Jesus and for you"
- Day 9: "Give us what your motherly heart sees we need at this moment"

It closes each day with: "Our Lady of Lourdes, pray for us! Saint Bernadette, pray for us! Amen. In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen."

Fidelity note

Ember's novena shares the broad shape common to both sources — a daily opening prayer, nine days with meditation plus intention, and a closing prayer invoking Our Lady of Lourdes — but its prayer and meditation wording is an original composition, not a transcription of either source. It does not carry the distinctive fixed texts of either tradition (FishEaters' "O Brilliant star of purity" petition, or PrayMoreNovenas' Memorare and "Our Lady of Lourdes / Saint Bernadette, pray for us" refrain). Where the public sources repeat the same prayer body each day with only a varying petition, Ember instead gives each day a unique narrative meditation tracing the apparition story (the Lady's appearance, the Immaculate Conception, the spring, penance, prayer for sinners, Bernadette's witness, the sick, the candlelight procession, enduring care). It is faithful to the Lourdes devotion's themes and spirit but is best characterized as a freshly written novena rather than a reproduction of the FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas texts.

Annunciation annunciation-novena

Mar 16–24
description & history

A nine-day novena for the Annunciation, when the Word was made flesh in the womb of the Virgin.

Uma novena de nove dias para a Anunciação, quando o Verbo se fez carne no seio da Virgem.

At the Annunciation the angel Gabriel was sent to Nazareth to a virgin named Mary, announcing that she would conceive the Son of the Most High. By her humble 'Be it done unto me according to your word,' the eternal Word took flesh and dwelt among us. The Church recalls this mystery daily in the Angelus and in the ancient collect 'Pour forth, we beseech you'; this novena makes Mary's fiat our own.

Na Anunciação, o anjo Gabriel foi enviado a Nazaré, a uma virgem chamada Maria, anunciando que ela conceberia o Filho do Altíssimo. Por seu humilde 'Faça-se em mim segundo a vossa palavra', o Verbo eterno se fez carne e habitou entre nós. A Igreja recorda este mistério a cada dia no Angelus e na antiga coleta 'Infundi em nossas almas'; esta novena torna nosso o fiat de Maria.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Word of God, who at the angel's message became flesh in the most pure womb of the Virgin Mary, come and be born anew in my heart. As you found in her a willing dwelling, prepare in me a place to receive you. Amen.

Ó Verbo de Deus, que ao anúncio do anjo vos fizestes carne no puríssimo seio da Virgem Maria, vinde e nascei de novo no meu coração. Como encontrastes nela uma morada disposta, preparai em mim um lugar para vos acolher. Amém.

Day 1: The angel sent to Nazareth

Dia 1: O anjo enviado a Nazaré

God sent His messenger not to a palace but to an obscure town and a poor maiden betrothed to a carpenter. The greatest event of history began in hiddenness and silence.

Deus enviou seu mensageiro não a um palácio, mas a uma cidade obscura e a uma jovem pobre, prometida a um carpinteiro. O maior acontecimento da história começou no oculto e no silêncio.

Intention

For the grace to seek God in the hidden and the humble.

Pela graça de buscar a Deus no oculto e no humilde.

Day 2: Full of grace

Dia 2: Cheia de graça

Gabriel greeted Mary as 'full of grace,' the Lord already with her before she had spoken a word. Her whole soul had been prepared from the first moment to be God's dwelling.

Gabriel saudou Maria como 'cheia de graça', o Senhor já com ela antes mesmo de uma palavra. Toda a sua alma fora preparada desde o primeiro instante para ser morada de Deus.

Intention

That God's grace may take fuller possession of my heart.

Para que a graça de Deus tome posse mais plena do meu coração.

Day 3: She was troubled

Dia 3: Ela ficou perturbada

Mary was troubled at the greeting and pondered what it might mean, neither rushing nor refusing. Her thoughtful silence shows a soul attentive to the ways of God.

Maria perturbou-se com a saudação e ponderava o que aquilo significaria, sem se precipitar nem recusar. Seu silêncio pensativo revela uma alma atenta aos caminhos de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to ponder God's word before acting.

Pela graça de ponderar a palavra de Deus antes de agir.

Day 4: 'How can this be?'

Dia 4: 'Como acontecerá isto?'

Mary asked how this could be, not in doubt but in a faith that seeks understanding. She wished only to know God's will so that she might fulfill it perfectly.

Maria perguntou como aquilo aconteceria, não por dúvida, mas numa fé que busca compreender. Desejava apenas conhecer a vontade de Deus para cumpri-la perfeitamente.

Intention

For a faith that trusts even what it cannot understand.

Por uma fé que confia mesmo no que não pode compreender.

Day 5: The Holy Spirit overshadows her

Dia 5: O Espírito Santo a cobre com sua sombra

The angel promised that the Holy Spirit would come upon her and the power of the Most High overshadow her. What was impossible to nature, God would accomplish by His own power.

O anjo prometeu que o Espírito Santo viria sobre ela e a força do Altíssimo a cobriria com sua sombra. O que era impossível à natureza, Deus o realizaria por seu próprio poder.

Intention

That I may trust nothing is impossible to God.

Para que eu confie que nada é impossível a Deus.

Day 6: 'Behold the handmaid of the Lord'

Dia 6: 'Eis a serva do Senhor'

Mary called herself the handmaid of the Lord, claiming no title but service. In her, greatness and lowliness met, for the Mother of God wished only to be God's servant.

Maria chamou-se a serva do Senhor, sem reivindicar outro título senão o de serviço. Nela se encontraram grandeza e pequenez, pois a Mãe de Deus quis apenas ser serva de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to serve God without seeking honor.

Pela graça de servir a Deus sem buscar honra.

Day 7: The fiat

Dia 7: O fiat

With 'Be it done unto me according to your word,' Mary gave the consent for which all creation waited. Her free 'yes' undid the disobedience of Eve and opened the door of salvation.

Com o 'Faça-se em mim segundo a vossa palavra', Maria deu o consentimento que toda a criação aguardava. Seu 'sim' livre desfez a desobediência de Eva e abriu a porta da salvação.

Intention

For the courage to say my own 'yes' to God's will.

Pela coragem de dizer o meu próprio 'sim' à vontade de Deus.

Day 8: The Word made flesh

Dia 8: O Verbo feito carne

At her word the eternal Son took our flesh and made His home within her. Heaven came down to earth, and the Creator was carried by His creature.

À sua palavra, o Filho eterno assumiu a nossa carne e fez nela a sua morada. O céu desceu à terra, e o Criador foi carregado por sua criatura.

Intention

In adoration of the mystery of God-with-us.

Em adoração ao mistério do Deus-conosco.

Day 9: Mary's haste to serve

Dia 9: A pressa de Maria em servir

Bearing the Lord within her, Mary rose and went in haste to serve her cousin Elizabeth. The first act of the Incarnate Word's presence was a journey of charity.

Trazendo o Senhor dentro de si, Maria levantou-se e foi às pressas servir sua prima Isabel. O primeiro ato da presença do Verbo encarnado foi uma viagem de caridade.

Intention

That, carrying Christ within me, I may hasten to serve others.

Para que, trazendo Cristo em mim, eu me apresse a servir os outros.

Closing prayer

Pour forth, we beseech you, O Lord, your grace into my heart, that as I have known the Incarnation of your Son by the message of an angel, so by His Passion and Cross I may be brought to the glory of His resurrection. Through Mary's fiat, grant the grace I ask in this novena. Amen.

Infundi, Senhor, nós vos pedimos, a vossa graça em meu coração, para que, tendo conhecido pela mensagem do anjo a Encarnação de vosso Filho, por sua Paixão e Cruz eu seja conduzido à glória da ressurreição. Pelo fiat de Maria, concedei-me a graça que peço nesta novena. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="annunciation1">
Annunciation
Novena

This novena is prayed in anticipation of the Feast of
the Annunciation -- i.e., beginning on March 16 and ending on March 24,
the eve of the Annunciation

O most holy Virgin Mary, to whom God sent the Angel Gabriel to announce
that you should be the mother of his only-begotten Son, pray for us who
have recourse to you.

Holy, lovely Mary, we give our all to you, what is past and present,
and the future, too. Blessed be the holy and Immaculate Conception of
the most blessed Virgin Mary, Mother of God.

(Mention your intention here)

V. The Angel of the Lord declared unto Mary.

R. And she conceived of the Holy Spirit.

(Pray one Hail Mary)

V. Behold the handmaid of the Lord.

R. Be it done to me according to thy word.

(Pray one Hail Mary)

V. And the Word was made flesh.

R. And dwelt among us.

(Pray one Hail Mary)

V. Pray for us, O holy Mother of God.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Let us pray: Pour forth, we beseech Thee, O Lord, Thy grace into our
hearts, that we to whom the Incarnation of Christ Thy Son was made
known by the message of an angel, may by His Passion and Cross be
brought to the glory of His Resurrection. Through the same Christ Our
Lord. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

The same prayer is repeated each of the nine days (beginning March 16):

O most holy Virgin Mary, to whom God sent the Angel Gabriel to announce that you should be the mother of His Only-Begotten Son, pray for us who have recourse to you.

Holy, lovely Mary / We give our all to you / What is past and present, / And the future, too.

Blessed be the holy and Immaculate Conception of the most blessed Virgin Mary, Mother of God.

(State your intentions here…)

Closing prayer (the Memorare): Remember, O most gracious Virgin Mary, that never was it known that anyone who fled to thy protection… O Mother of the Word Incarnate, despise not my petitions, but in thy mercy hear and answer me. Amen.

In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

Fidelity note

Ember keeps the traditional frame of this novena — the March 16–24 dating and the closing collect "Pour forth, we beseech you, O Lord, your grace into our hearts," which both sources also use — so the structural anchor and the most distinctive prayer are faithful. The sources (FishEaters and PrayMoreNovenas) share a single fixed daily prayer built around the "O most holy Virgin Mary" invocation and the Angelus versicles; Ember instead replaces this with nine original day-by-day meditations and intentions tracing the Annunciation scene (Gabriel sent, full of grace, the fiat, the Visitation), plus its own composed opening prayer. This is a deliberate paraphrase/recomposition rather than a reproduction: the wording of the daily prayers is Ember's own, and the closing collect is lightly modernized ("your" for "Thy") and given a novena petition tail ("Through Mary's fiat, grant the grace I ask"). Faithful in spirit, dating, and the signature collect; substantially original in its meditative daily content.

St. Gemma Galgani st-gemma-galgani-novena

Apr 2–10
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Gemma Galgani, the young mystic of the Passion who bore the stigmata.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Gemma Galgani, a jovem mística da Paixão que recebeu os estigmas.

St. Gemma Galgani (1878–1903), the 'Flower of Lucca,' was a poor and sickly young woman who burned with love for the crucified Christ. Refused entry to the Passionist convent because of her illness, she lived as a hidden victim of love, bearing the stigmata each week and conversing intimately with her guardian angel. She died of tuberculosis on Holy Saturday at twenty-five; this novena commends our needs to her, the patroness of the suffering and the tempted.

Santa Gemma Galgani (1878–1903), a 'Flor de Lucca', foi uma jovem pobre e enferma que ardia de amor pelo Cristo crucificado. Impedida de entrar no convento das Passionistas por causa da doença, viveu como vítima oculta de amor, recebendo os estigmas a cada semana e conversando intimamente com seu anjo da guarda. Morreu de tuberculose num Sábado Santo, aos vinte e cinco anos; esta novena confia a ela, padroeira dos que sofrem e dos tentados, as nossas necessidades.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O St. Gemma Galgani, who loved the crucified Jesus with a heart on fire and gave your whole life to His Passion, look kindly upon me from heaven. Take my petition into your wounded hands and lay it at the feet of your beloved Lord. Amen.

Ó Santa Gemma Galgani, que amastes Jesus crucificado com o coração em chamas e destes toda a vida à sua Paixão, olhai por mim do céu com bondade. Tomai a minha súplica em vossas mãos chagadas e depositai-a aos pés de vosso amado Senhor. Amém.

Day 1: Orphaned and trusting

Dia 1: Órfã e confiante

Gemma lost her mother as a small child and her father in her teens, left poor and burdened with her younger siblings. In every loss she clung to God as her true and only Father.

Gemma perdeu a mãe ainda menina e o pai na adolescência, ficando pobre e sobrecarregada com os irmãos mais novos. Em cada perda, agarrou-se a Deus como seu verdadeiro e único Pai.

Intention

For orphans and all who have lost those they love.

Pelos órfãos e por todos os que perderam aqueles que amam.

Day 2: Love of the Crucified

Dia 2: Amor ao Crucificado

From her youth Gemma was drawn to the wounds of the crucified Jesus and could not look upon a crucifix without tears of love. The Passion was the book in which she read God's love for sinners.

Desde a juventude, Gemma era atraída pelas chagas de Jesus crucificado e não conseguia olhar um crucifixo sem lágrimas de amor. A Paixão era o livro no qual ela lia o amor de Deus pelos pecadores.

Intention

For the grace to love the crucified Christ more deeply.

Pela graça de amar mais profundamente o Cristo crucificado.

Day 3: The stigmata

Dia 3: Os estigmas

On the eve of the Sacred Heart in 1899, the wounds of Christ appeared in Gemma's hands, feet, and side, returning each Thursday and Friday. She bore in her body what she carried first in her heart: the Passion of her Lord.

Na véspera do Sagrado Coração, em 1899, as chagas de Cristo apareceram nas mãos, nos pés e no lado de Gemma, retornando a cada quinta e sexta-feira. Carregou no corpo o que já trazia primeiro no coração: a Paixão de seu Senhor.

Intention

That I may unite my pains to the wounds of Christ.

Para que eu una as minhas dores às chagas de Cristo.

Day 4: Victim of love

Dia 4: Vítima de amor

Gemma offered herself as a victim of love for the conversion of sinners and the souls in purgatory. She desired not suffering for its own sake but to console the wounded Heart of Jesus.

Gemma ofereceu-se como vítima de amor pela conversão dos pecadores e pelas almas do purgatório. Desejava não o sofrimento em si, mas consolar o Coração ferido de Jesus.

Intention

For the conversion of sinners and the holy souls in purgatory.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e pelas santas almas do purgatório.

Day 5: Friend of her guardian angel

Dia 5: Amiga de seu anjo da guarda

Gemma spoke with her guardian angel as with a familiar companion, who carried her messages and corrected her faults. Her trust reminds us that heaven is near to those who walk in childlike faith.

Gemma falava com seu anjo da guarda como com um companheiro íntimo, que levava seus recados e corrigia suas faltas. Sua confiança nos lembra que o céu está próximo dos que caminham em fé de criança.

Intention

For a deeper devotion to my own guardian angel.

Por uma devoção mais profunda ao meu próprio anjo da guarda.

Day 6: The closed convent door

Dia 6: A porta fechada do convento

Gemma longed to be a Passionist nun, yet her frail health closed that door to her again and again. She surrendered her dearest desire into God's hands, finding her cloister in His will.

Gemma ansiava ser religiosa Passionista, mas a saúde frágil lhe fechou essa porta repetidas vezes. Entregou seu mais caro desejo às mãos de Deus, encontrando seu claustro na vontade dele.

Intention

For the grace to surrender my dearest hopes to God's plan.

Pela graça de entregar minhas mais caras esperanças ao plano de Deus.

Day 7: Trust amid temptation

Dia 7: Confiança em meio à tentação

Gemma endured fierce assaults of the devil and dark trials of the soul, yet held fast to Jesus and Mary. She is a sure helper for all who are tempted and afflicted in spirit.

Gemma suportou violentos assaltos do demônio e sombrias provações da alma, mas manteve-se firme em Jesus e Maria. É auxílio seguro para todos os que são tentados e atribulados no espírito.

Intention

For all who are tempted, that they may not lose hope.

Por todos os que são tentados, para que não percam a esperança.

Day 8: Patience in sickness

Dia 8: Paciência na doença

Worn by tuberculosis, Gemma bore long months of pain with serene patience, asking only to do God's will. Her sickbed became an altar of loving sacrifice.

Consumida pela tuberculose, Gemma suportou longos meses de dor com serena paciência, pedindo apenas cumprir a vontade de Deus. Seu leito de enferma tornou-se altar de amoroso sacrifício.

Intention

For the sick and dying, that they may suffer with patience and hope.

Pelos doentes e moribundos, para que sofram com paciência e esperança.

Day 9: 'Jesus, I commend my soul'

Dia 9: 'Jesus, entrego-vos a minha alma'

On Holy Saturday of 1903, after long suffering, Gemma died at twenty-five with her eyes fixed on the crucifix. She passed into the embrace of the Lord she had loved through every wound.

No Sábado Santo de 1903, após longo sofrimento, Gemma morreu aos vinte e cinco anos com os olhos fixos no crucifixo. Passou ao abraço do Senhor que amara através de cada chaga.

Intention

For the grace of a holy death, with my heart fixed on Christ.

Pela graça de uma santa morte, com o coração fixo em Cristo.

Closing prayer

St. Gemma, victim of love and friend of the suffering, obtain for me the grace I seek in this novena, if it serve God's glory and my salvation. Above all, teach me to love the Passion of Christ and to trust His will in every cross. Amen.

Santa Gemma, vítima de amor e amiga dos que sofrem, alcançai-me a graça que busco nesta novena, se for para a glória de Deus e a minha salvação. Acima de tudo, ensinai-me a amar a Paixão de Cristo e a confiar em sua vontade em toda cruz. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="gemma">
Novena
to St. Gemma Galgani

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 2
April
and ending on 10 April .

First Day:

Preface: Oh most Divine Lord, we humbly prostrate ourselves before Thy
Infinite Majesty, and we adore Thee and dedicate to Thy glory the
devout prayers which we now present to Thee, as an act of devotion to
your servant, St Gemma Galgani, whose intercession we are now
imploring.

Most compassionate virgin, St. Gemma, during thy short life on earth,
you gave a most beautiful example of angelic innocence and seraphic
love and was found worthy to bear in thy flesh the marks of our Lord's
Passion. Have pity on us who are so much in need of God's Mercy, and
obtain for us through thy merits and intercession, the special favor
which we now fervently implore (mention your intention here) .

Pray one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be

Pray for us, Saint Gemma, that we may be made worthy of the promises of
Christ.

Let us pray. O God, Who fashioned thy servant Saint Gemma into a
likeness of Thy Crucified Son, grant us through her intercession the
favor that we humbly request, and through the Passion, Death and
Resurrection of Thy Son, may we be united with You for all eternity. We
ask this through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen

Second Day:

Preface: Oh most Divine Lord, we humbly prostrate ourselves before Thy
Infinite Majesty, and we adore Thee and dedicate to Thy glory the
devout prayers which we now present to Thee, as an act of devotion to
your servant, St Gemma Galgani, whose intercession we are now
imploring.

O worthy Spouse of the Lamb of God and faithful virgin St Gemma, you
preserved the innocence and splendor of virginity, giving to the world
a bright example of purity and the most exalted virtues. Look down with
pity from thy high place in heaven upon us who confide in thee, as we
implore thee for the favor we so ardently desire (mention your
intention here) .

Pray one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be

Pray for us, Saint Gemma, that we may be made worthy of the promises of
Christ.

Let us pray. O God, Who fashioned thy servant Saint Gemma into a
likeness of Thy Crucified Son, grant us through her intercession the
favor that we humbly request, and through the Passion, Death and
Resurrection of Thy Son, may we be united with You for all eternity. We
ask this through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen

Third Day:

Preface: Oh most Divine Lord, we humbly prostrate ourselves before Thy
Infinite Majesty, and we adore Thee and dedicate to Thy glory the
devout prayers which we now present to Thee, as an act of devotion to
your servant, St Gemma Galgani, whose intercession we are now
imploring.

O most loving virgin St. Gemma, with a fervent love for Jesus you
suffered immensely for the conversion of sinners as a victim for sin,
and you loved others intensely out of love for God. Do not forget us
then, who remain here on earth, and look down with kindness on those of
us who implore thee in the confident hope of receiving this favor
through thy loving intercession (mention your intention here)

Pray one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be

Pray for us, Saint Gemma, that we may be made worthy of the promises of
Christ.

Let us pray. O God, Who fashioned thy servant Saint Gemma into a
likeness of Thy Crucified Son, grant us through her intercession the
favor that we humbly request, and through the Passion, Death and
Resurrection of Thy Son, may we be united with You for all eternity. We
ask this through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen

Fourth Day:

Preface: Oh most Divine Lord, we humbly prostrate ourselves before Thy
Infinite Majesty, and we adore Thee and dedicate to Thy glory the
devout prayers which we now present to Thee, as an act of devotion to
your servant, St Gemma Galgani, whose intercession we are now
imploring.

Blessed St Gemma, you who by the will of God suffered the los

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a St. Gemma Galgani novena, but its structure differs from Ember's. Each day opens and closes with the Sign of the Cross ("In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.") and "St. Gemma Galgani, pray for us!" Every day follows the pattern: a thanksgiving to God ("Dear Lord, we thank You for giving us St. Gemma Galgani as an example of holiness. Help us to imitate the love of You she showed by remaining faithful amidst…"), an address to St. Gemma recalling an episode of her life, a request for her intercession, and space for a personal petition.

Its day-by-day themes are: Day 1 — perseverance through chronic health problems; Day 2 — faithfulness amid family loss; Day 3 — faith through grief; Day 4 — submission to God's will regarding her religious vocation; Day 5 — perseverance despite ridicule; Day 6 — devotion to Christ's Passion; Day 7 — patient endurance of suffering; Day 8 — charity toward the poor; Day 9 — wholehearted service to God.

Fidelity note

Ember's novena is not a transcription of any single published source: FishEaters has no St. Gemma novena at all, and the PrayMoreNovenas text uses different wording, a different opening/closing, and a different sequence of daily themes. Ember's prayers and nine meditations are original compositions, though they are faithful to St. Gemma's life and to the broad devotional themes (her love of the Passion, the stigmata, victimhood of love, illness, holy death) that the PrayMoreNovenas version also touches. This should be treated as an original, devotionally sound novena inspired by — not copied from — the common sources, with several meditation topics (orphanhood, the guardian angel, the closed convent door) that PrayMoreNovenas does not emphasize.

St. Gianna Beretta Molla st-gianna-novena

Apr 19–27
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Gianna Beretta Molla, physician, wife, and mother who gave her life for her unborn child, patroness of mothers and the unborn.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Gianna Beretta Molla, médica, esposa e mãe que deu a vida pelo filho que esperava, padroeira das mães e dos não nascidos.

St. Gianna Beretta Molla (1922–1962) was an Italian pediatrician, wife of Pietro Molla, and mother of four. During her fourth pregnancy she developed a fibroma on her uterus and, refusing both an abortion and a hysterectomy, chose to have only the fibroma removed to save her unborn child, dying a week after the child's birth. Canonized in 2004 with her husband present, she is honored as a model of holiness lived out in marriage, motherhood, and medicine. This novena entrusts our families and our work to her loving intercession.

Santa Gianna Beretta Molla (1922–1962) foi uma pediatra italiana, esposa de Pietro Molla e mãe de quatro filhos. Em sua quarta gravidez desenvolveu um fibroma no útero e, recusando tanto o aborto quanto a histerectomia, optou por remover apenas o fibroma para salvar o filho que esperava, vindo a falecer uma semana após o nascimento da menina. Canonizada em 2004 com seu esposo presente, é honrada como modelo de santidade vivida no matrimônio, na maternidade e na medicina. Esta novena confia nossas famílias e nosso trabalho à sua amorosa intercessão.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Gianna, faithful physician and devoted mother, you served Christ in the sick, the young, and the unborn with tender and competent love. Draw near to me now as I begin this novena, and teach me to make a gift of my whole life. Obtain for me from God the grace I seek and a heart wholly His. Amen.

Santa Gianna, médica fiel e mãe devotada, vós servistes a Cristo nos doentes, nas crianças e nos não nascidos com amor terno e competente. Aproximai-vos de mim agora, ao iniciar esta novena, e ensinai-me a fazer da minha vida inteira um dom. Alcançai-me de Deus a graça que peço e um coração todo Seu. Amém.

Day 1: Holiness in ordinary life

Dia 1: Santidade na vida comum

Gianna was not a cloistered mystic but a busy doctor, wife, and mother who found God in clinics, kitchens, and cradles. She shows us that the path to sanctity runs straight through our daily duties faithfully fulfilled.

Gianna não foi uma mística enclausurada, mas uma médica atarefada, esposa e mãe que encontrava Deus nos consultórios, nas cozinhas e nos berços. Ela nos mostra que o caminho da santidade passa direto pelos deveres cotidianos cumpridos com fidelidade.

Intention

For the grace to seek holiness within the ordinary tasks God gives me each day.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade dentro das tarefas comuns que Deus me confia a cada dia.

Day 2: Love of the sick

Dia 2: Amor aos doentes

As a pediatrician Gianna saw in every patient the suffering face of Christ, treating the poor with the same care as the rich. She teaches us that medicine and every honest profession can become an act of charity.

Como pediatra, Gianna via em cada paciente o rosto sofredor de Cristo, tratando os pobres com o mesmo cuidado que os ricos. Ela nos ensina que a medicina e toda profissão honesta podem tornar-se um ato de caridade.

Intention

For all physicians, nurses, and caregivers, that they may serve the sick with reverence.

Por todos os médicos, enfermeiros e cuidadores, para que sirvam os doentes com reverência.

Day 3: A vocation discerned in prayer

Dia 3: Uma vocação discernida na oração

Before her marriage Gianna prayed earnestly to know God's will, asking whether He called her to the missions, the consecrated life, or the home. She reminds us that every vocation is a holy answer to a divine invitation, discovered on our knees.

Antes de seu casamento, Gianna rezou intensamente para conhecer a vontade de Deus, perguntando se Ele a chamava às missões, à vida consagrada ou ao lar. Ela nos lembra que toda vocação é uma santa resposta a um convite divino, descoberta de joelhos.

Intention

For the grace to discern God's call for my life with patience and trust.

Pela graça de discernir o chamado de Deus para minha vida com paciência e confiança.

Day 4: Joyful married love

Dia 4: O amor conjugal alegre

Gianna's love for her husband Pietro was tender, faithful, and full of joy, expressed in letters that brimmed with affection and faith. She shows married couples that human love, lived in God, is a true path to heaven.

O amor de Gianna por seu esposo Pietro era terno, fiel e cheio de alegria, expresso em cartas transbordantes de afeto e fé. Ela mostra aos casais que o amor humano, vivido em Deus, é um verdadeiro caminho para o céu.

Intention

For married couples, that their love may be faithful, generous, and joyful.

Pelos casais, para que seu amor seja fiel, generoso e alegre.

Day 5: Reverence for the unborn

Dia 5: Reverência pelos não nascidos

Gianna believed that the life of a child is a sacred gift entrusted by God, never a possession to be discarded. Facing her own danger, she chose to protect the smallest and most defenseless of all.

Gianna acreditava que a vida de uma criança é um dom sagrado confiado por Deus, jamais uma posse a ser descartada. Diante do próprio perigo, escolheu proteger o menor e mais indefeso de todos.

Intention

For the protection of unborn children and the conversion of all hearts to the gospel of life.

Pela proteção das crianças não nascidas e pela conversão de todos os corações ao evangelho da vida.

Day 6: Courage in suffering

Dia 6: Coragem no sofrimento

When doctors revealed the tumor in her womb, Gianna calmly refused any remedy that would harm her child, accepting grave risk to herself. Her courage was not recklessness but a serene trust that God would not abandon her.

Quando os médicos revelaram o tumor em seu ventre, Gianna recusou serenamente todo remédio que prejudicasse seu filho, aceitando grave risco para si mesma. Sua coragem não era imprudência, mas uma serena confiança de que Deus não a abandonaria.

Intention

For courage to do what is right even when it costs me dearly.

Pela coragem de fazer o que é justo, mesmo quando isso me custa caro.

Day 7: The gift of self

Dia 7: O dom de si mesma

Knowing she might not survive the birth, Gianna asked that her child be saved if a choice had to be made. In laying down her life for her daughter, she imaged most perfectly the love of Christ, who lays down His life for His own.

Sabendo que poderia não sobreviver ao parto, Gianna pediu que sua filha fosse salva se uma escolha tivesse de ser feita. Ao entregar a vida por sua filha, ela espelhou de modo perfeito o amor de Cristo, que dá a vida pelos seus.

Intention

For the grace to love others with a generous and self-giving heart.

Pela graça de amar os outros com um coração generoso e doado.

Day 8: Trust in divine providence

Dia 8: Confiança na divina providência

Through her long final week of suffering, Gianna clung to Jesus and surrendered everything into His hands. She teaches us to entrust our fears, our families, and our futures to a Father whose providence never fails.

Ao longo de sua longa semana final de sofrimento, Gianna se apegou a Jesus e entregou tudo em Suas mãos. Ela nos ensina a confiar nossos medos, nossas famílias e nosso futuro a um Pai cuja providência jamais falha.

Intention

For deeper trust in God's providence amid uncertainty and trial.

Por uma confiança mais profunda na providência de Deus em meio à incerteza e à provação.

Day 9: A saint for our families

Dia 9: Uma santa para nossas famílias

Gianna died on Easter Saturday, 28 April 1962, and her daughter Gianna Emanuela lived to see her mother canonized. Her life proclaims that ordinary families can become forges of holiness when love is placed before self.

Gianna faleceu no Sábado de Páscoa, 28 de abril de 1962, e sua filha Gianna Emanuela viveu para ver a mãe canonizada. Sua vida proclama que famílias comuns podem tornar-se forjas de santidade quando o amor é posto acima de si mesmo.

Intention

For my family, that we may grow together in faith, love, and holiness.

Pela minha família, para que cresçamos juntos na fé, no amor e na santidade.

Closing prayer

St. Gianna, who in your final hour repeated, "If you must choose, save the baby," and offered your life without reserve, intercede for me before the throne of God. Help me to find Christ in the duties of each ordinary day and to love until the end. Through your prayers, may I one day join you in the joy of heaven. Amen.

Santa Gianna, que em vossa hora derradeira repetistes: "Se for preciso escolher, salvem a criança", e oferecestes a vida sem reservas, intercedei por mim diante do trono de Deus. Ajudai-me a encontrar Cristo nos deveres de cada dia comum e a amar até o fim. Por vossas orações, possa eu um dia unir-me a vós na alegria do céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

The PrayMoreNovenas novena uses a single recurring prayer prayed each day, with a unique daily petition inserted between the opening and closing sections.

Opening invocation (repeats daily): "Dear holy servant of God, Saint Gianna Beretta Molla, we pray to you because you were once the selfless and suffering mother who chose the life of her child over her own life. Your faithfulness to God brought you so close to God that you are now resting in His divine splendor. We humbly pray for your holy intercession."

Mid-prayer reflection (repeats daily): "In the midst of your suffering you continued your mission to care for the sick, needy and weak. And so we bring our needy hearts to you and ask that you pray for us to God our Father…"

Daily petition (varies by day): Day 1 — comfort for those experiencing infertility or miscarriage; Day 2 — that we may be filled with love for children; Day 3 — seeking God's will in all things; Day 4 — generosity toward those in need; Day 5 — patience in suffering; Day 6 — dignity in the medical professions; Day 7 — witnessing to the sanctity of life; Day 8 — an end to abortion; Day 9 — mothers as God's face within their families.

Closing petition (repeats daily): "Most holy Saint Gianna, you said 'yes' to the new life of your baby, generously sacrificing your own life. Show us how to say 'yes' as you did to God's will so that we may come to eternal rest with God in heaven." Each day concludes with a Hail Mary, a Glory Be, and "Saint Gianna Beretta Molla, pray for us!"

Fidelity note

Ember's text is an original composition, not a reproduction of the PrayMoreNovenas prayer; the two share no wording. Both are nine-day novenas to St. Gianna built around the same themes (her motherhood, her medical vocation, her sacrifice for her child, the sanctity of life), and several daily focuses line up closely — Ember's Day 2 (the sick), Day 5 (the unborn), and Day 7 (the gift of self) echo PMN's care for the sick, end to abortion, and "yes" to her child's life. Ember's version is more devotional-meditation in structure (a fixed opening prayer, a per-day meditation plus intention, then Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be, then a closing prayer), whereas PMN repeats one long invocation daily with a swapped petition. It is a faithful, theologically sound novena in spirit but should be regarded as Ember's own text rather than the PMN source.

St. Louis de Montfort st-louis-de-montfort-novena

Apr 19–27
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Louis Marie de Montfort, apostle of Total Consecration to Jesus through Mary and tireless preacher of the Holy Rosary.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Luís Maria de Montfort, apóstolo da Consagração Total a Jesus por Maria e incansável pregador do Santo Rosário.

St. Louis Marie Grignion de Montfort (1673–1716) was a French priest and missionary whose preaching set the countryside ablaze with the Rosary and a renewed love of Christ. In his classic "True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin" he taught the way of holy slavery of love: giving oneself entirely to Mary so as to belong wholly to Jesus. His motto, "To Jesus through Mary," has shaped saints and popes ever since. This novena follows his sure path of consecration into the heart of the Lord.

São Luís Maria Grignion de Montfort (1673–1716) foi um sacerdote e missionário francês cuja pregação incendiou os campos com o Rosário e um renovado amor a Cristo. Em sua obra clássica "Tratado da Verdadeira Devoção à Santíssima Virgem", ensinou o caminho da santa escravidão de amor: entregar-se inteiramente a Maria para pertencer todo a Jesus. Seu lema, "A Jesus por Maria", inspirou santos e papas desde então. Esta novena segue seu caminho seguro de consagração ao coração do Senhor.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Louis Marie de Montfort, faithful slave of Jesus in Mary, you spent your life leading souls to Christ through His holy Mother. Take me by the hand as I begin this novena and teach me to belong entirely to Jesus through Mary. Obtain for me the grace I now ask, that I may love God with an undivided heart. Amen.

São Luís Maria de Montfort, fiel escravo de Jesus em Maria, vós gastastes a vida conduzindo as almas a Cristo por meio de Sua santa Mãe. Tomai-me pela mão ao iniciar esta novena e ensinai-me a pertencer inteiramente a Jesus por Maria. Alcançai-me a graça que agora peço, para que eu ame a Deus com um coração indiviso. Amém.

Day 1: To Jesus through Mary

Dia 1: A Jesus por Maria

St. Louis taught that since Jesus came to us through Mary, it is through Mary that we most surely come to Him. He invites us to take the way the Eternal Word himself chose, trusting the path God has already blessed.

São Luís ensinava que, assim como Jesus veio a nós por Maria, é por Maria que mais seguramente vamos a Ele. Ele nos convida a tomar o caminho que o próprio Verbo Eterno escolheu, confiando na via que Deus já abençoou.

Intention

For the grace to come to Jesus through the hands of His holy Mother.

Pela graça de chegar a Jesus pelas mãos de Sua santa Mãe.

Day 2: Total Consecration

Dia 2: A Consagração Total

He counseled giving Mary our body and soul, our goods both interior and exterior, and even the value of all our good actions. This complete gift, he promised, loses nothing but is multiplied a hundredfold in the hands of the Mother of God.

Ele aconselhava entregar a Maria nosso corpo e nossa alma, nossos bens interiores e exteriores, e até o valor de todas as nossas boas ações. Esse dom completo, prometia ele, nada perde, mas é multiplicado cem vezes nas mãos da Mãe de Deus.

Intention

For the courage to give myself entirely to God, holding nothing back.

Pela coragem de entregar-me inteiramente a Deus, sem reter nada.

Day 3: Holy slavery of love

Dia 3: A santa escravidão de amor

St. Louis called the consecrated soul a willing slave of love, bound to Jesus in Mary not by force but by adoring freedom. This loving bondage, far from diminishing us, makes us truly free children of God.

São Luís chamava a alma consagrada de escrava voluntária de amor, ligada a Jesus em Maria não pela força, mas por uma liberdade que adora. Essa escravidão amorosa, longe de nos diminuir, faz de nós verdadeiros filhos livres de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to serve Christ freely, out of love rather than fear.

Pela graça de servir a Cristo livremente, por amor e não por medo.

Day 4: Through her, with her, in her

Dia 4: Por ela, com ela, nela

He taught us to do all our actions through Mary, with Mary, in Mary, and for Mary, so as to do them more perfectly through, with, in, and for Jesus Christ. Mary is never the destination but the swift and gentle way to her Son.

Ele nos ensinou a realizar todas as nossas ações por Maria, com Maria, em Maria e para Maria, a fim de fazê-las mais perfeitamente por, com, em e para Jesus Cristo. Maria nunca é o destino, mas o caminho rápido e suave até seu Filho.

Intention

For the grace to unite all my works to those of Mary and her Son.

Pela graça de unir todas as minhas obras às de Maria e de seu Filho.

Day 5: The Holy Rosary

Dia 5: O Santo Rosário

Montfort preached the Rosary as a treasure that contains the whole gospel, calling it a chain that binds heaven to earth. He saw in the daily decades a school of contemplation that gradually conforms us to Christ.

Montfort pregava o Rosário como um tesouro que contém todo o evangelho, chamando-o de corrente que liga o céu à terra. Ele via nas dezenas diárias uma escola de contemplação que pouco a pouco nos conforma a Cristo.

Intention

For the grace to pray the Rosary faithfully and to love its mysteries.

Pela graça de rezar o Rosário com fidelidade e de amar seus mistérios.

Day 6: Wisdom of the Cross

Dia 6: A sabedoria da Cruz

In his "Love of Eternal Wisdom" St. Louis taught that Christ crucified is the Wisdom of God, and that to possess Him we must embrace the Cross. He himself bore contradiction, poverty, and rejection without bitterness, kissing the Cross he carried.

Em seu "Amor pela Sabedoria Eterna", São Luís ensinou que Cristo crucificado é a Sabedoria de Deus, e que para possuí-Lo devemos abraçar a Cruz. Ele mesmo suportou contradição, pobreza e rejeição sem amargura, beijando a Cruz que carregava.

Intention

For the grace to embrace the cross in my life as the way to true wisdom.

Pela graça de abraçar a cruz em minha vida como caminho da verdadeira sabedoria.

Day 7: The zealous missionary

Dia 7: O missionário zeloso

Tireless and often opposed, St. Louis crisscrossed western France preaching parish missions, hearing confessions, and renewing whole towns. His burning zeal for souls reminds us that love of God overflows into love for our neighbor's salvation.

Incansável e muitas vezes contrariado, São Luís percorreu o oeste da França pregando missões paroquiais, ouvindo confissões e renovando cidades inteiras. Seu zelo ardente pelas almas nos recorda que o amor a Deus transborda em amor pela salvação do próximo.

Intention

For zeal to bring others closer to Christ by word and example.

Pelo zelo de aproximar os outros de Cristo pela palavra e pelo exemplo.

Day 8: Mary, mold of the saints

Dia 8: Maria, molde dos santos

St. Louis compared Mary to the mold of God, in which souls are gently and surely formed into living images of Jesus. To be cast in her is to be shaped quickly into holiness with far less labor and far greater fruit.

São Luís comparava Maria ao molde de Deus, no qual as almas são formadas, com suavidade e segurança, em imagens vivas de Jesus. Ser moldado nela é ser formado depressa na santidade, com muito menos esforço e muito maior fruto.

Intention

For the grace to be formed by Mary into the likeness of her Son.

Pela graça de ser formado por Maria à semelhança de seu Filho.

Day 9: Apostles of the latter times

Dia 9: Apóstolos dos últimos tempos

Montfort prophesied that God would raise up great saints, true children of Mary, to renew the Church in the last ages. He calls each of us to be one of these humble apostles, set on fire by Marian devotion for the glory of Christ.

Montfort profetizou que Deus suscitaria grandes santos, verdadeiros filhos de Maria, para renovar a Igreja nos últimos tempos. Ele convida cada um de nós a ser um desses humildes apóstolos, incendiados pela devoção mariana para a glória de Cristo.

Intention

For the grace to live my consecration faithfully for the renewal of the Church.

Pela graça de viver minha consagração com fidelidade para a renovação da Igreja.

Closing prayer

St. Louis Marie, who proclaimed that Mary is the surest, shortest, and most perfect way to Jesus, lead me along that royal road. Help me to consecrate myself wholly to Christ through her Immaculate Heart and to take up my Rosary each day. By your prayers, may I be among the apostles of the latter times whom you foretold. Amen.

São Luís Maria, que proclamastes ser Maria o caminho mais seguro, mais curto e mais perfeito para Jesus, conduzi-me por essa estrada real. Ajudai-me a consagrar-me inteiramente a Cristo por meio de seu Coração Imaculado e a tomar o Rosário cada dia. Por vossas orações, possa eu estar entre os apóstolos dos últimos tempos que vós anunciastes. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Opening (all days): "In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen."

Core prayer (repeated each day): "St. Louis de Montfort, you consecrated yourself to Jesus through Mary in your earthly life and are even more fully devoted to them now in Heaven. I ask that you present my petitions before our most blessed Mother, who will in turn place them at the feet of her Son."

(Space for personal petitions.)

Daily intercessions:
- Day 1: Request for growth in humility and self-denial through Mary's example
- Day 2: Request for invigorated and apparent faith
- Day 3: Request for becoming "a more immediately obedient child of God"
- Day 4: Request for endurance to "pray without ceasing"
- Day 5: Request to "become ever purer" through God's refining work
- Day 6: Request for "a more intense love for God and neighbor"
- Day 7: Request for growth in patience, "especially in trying situations"
- Day 8: Request to practice kindness "with all, especially those whom I find most difficult to love"
- Day 9: Request for attentiveness to "heavenly wisdom"

Closing (all days): "Amen. In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen."

Fidelity note

Ember's text is an original composition, not a transcription of any single published novena. It does not follow PrayMoreNovenas, which uses one fixed intercessory prayer with a generic per-day intention; Ember instead builds nine distinct catechetical meditations drawn faithfully from Montfort's own doctrine (Total Consecration, holy slavery of love, the "through, with, in, for Mary" formula, the Rosary, the Wisdom of the Cross, apostles of the latter times). The structural frame — Sign of the Cross, opening prayer, meditation, intention, Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be, closing prayer — is conventional and sound. As a Montfortian novena it is theologically accurate and substantive, but it should not be presented as matching the PrayMoreNovenas version, which it does not reproduce.

St. Catherine of Siena st-catherine-of-siena-novena

Apr 20–28
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Catherine of Siena, Doctor of the Church, mystic of the Precious Blood, and fearless counselor of popes.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Catarina de Sena, Doutora da Igreja, mística do Preciosíssimo Sangue e destemida conselheira dos papas.

St. Catherine of Siena (1347–1380) was a Dominican tertiary, mystic, and one of the first women declared a Doctor of the Church. Granted the grace of mystical espousal to Christ, she devoted herself to the sick, the poor, and the reform of a Church then torn by division. With burning love she wrote letters to princes and pleaded with the Pope to return from Avignon to Rome. This novena seeks her ardent intercession, that we too may become who God meant us to be.

Santa Catarina de Sena (1347–1380) foi terciária dominicana, mística e uma das primeiras mulheres declaradas Doutora da Igreja. Agraciada com o dom do desposório místico com Cristo, dedicou-se aos doentes, aos pobres e à reforma de uma Igreja então dilacerada por divisões. Com amor ardente escreveu a príncipes e suplicou ao Papa que voltasse de Avinhão para Roma. Esta novena busca sua intercessão ardente, para que também nós nos tornemos aquilo que Deus quis que fôssemos.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Catherine of Siena, bride of Christ and lover of His Precious Blood, you set the world on fire with the love that burned in your own heart. Be with me as I begin this novena and obtain for me a like ardor for God and His Church. Through your powerful intercession, grant me the grace I now humbly ask. Amen.

Santa Catarina de Sena, esposa de Cristo e amante de Seu Preciosíssimo Sangue, vós incendiastes o mundo com o amor que ardia em vosso próprio coração. Estai comigo ao iniciar esta novena e alcançai-me semelhante ardor por Deus e por Sua Igreja. Por vossa poderosa intercessão, concedei-me a graça que agora humildemente peço. Amém.

Day 1: The cell of self-knowledge

Dia 1: A cela do conhecimento de si

From childhood Catherine built within her soul an interior cell of self-knowledge, where she learned that she was nothing and God was all. There she found God dwelling within her and learned to abide with Him no matter where she went.

Desde a infância, Catarina construiu em sua alma uma cela interior de conhecimento de si, onde aprendeu que ela nada era e que Deus era tudo. Ali encontrou Deus habitando dentro dela e aprendeu a permanecer com Ele aonde quer que fosse.

Intention

For the grace of true self-knowledge that I may rest humbly in God.

Pela graça do verdadeiro conhecimento de si, para que eu repouse humildemente em Deus.

Day 2: Mystical espousal to Christ

Dia 2: O desposório místico com Cristo

Catherine received from the Lord the grace of a mystical marriage, a bond of pure and total love uniting her soul to Christ. She teaches us that the deepest vocation of every Christian is intimate friendship with Jesus.

Catarina recebeu do Senhor a graça de um matrimônio místico, um laço de amor puro e total que uniu sua alma a Cristo. Ela nos ensina que a vocação mais profunda de todo cristão é a amizade íntima com Jesus.

Intention

For the grace of an intimate and faithful friendship with Christ.

Pela graça de uma amizade íntima e fiel com Cristo.

Day 3: Devotion to the Precious Blood

Dia 3: Devoção ao Preciosíssimo Sangue

Catherine was consumed with love for the Blood of Christ, often closing her letters with the words, "in the Blood of Christ." She saw in that Blood the price of every soul and the unfailing fountain of mercy for sinners.

Catarina era consumida pelo amor ao Sangue de Cristo, encerrando muitas vezes suas cartas com as palavras "no Sangue de Cristo". Ela via naquele Sangue o preço de cada alma e a fonte inesgotável de misericórdia para os pecadores.

Intention

For a deeper love of the Precious Blood that has redeemed me.

Por um amor mais profundo ao Preciosíssimo Sangue que me redimiu.

Day 4: Charity to the poor and sick

Dia 4: A caridade aos pobres e doentes

Catherine nursed plague victims that others fled, embracing the most repulsive wounds for love of Christ in the suffering. Her contemplation never closed her in upon herself but poured out in fearless service.

Catarina cuidou de vítimas da peste de quem outros fugiam, abraçando as feridas mais repugnantes por amor a Cristo nos que sofriam. Sua contemplação jamais a fechou em si mesma, mas se derramou em serviço destemido.

Intention

For the grace to serve Christ in the poor and the suffering without fear.

Pela graça de servir a Cristo nos pobres e nos que sofrem, sem temor.

Day 5: Courageous love of the Church

Dia 5: O amor corajoso à Igreja

Catherine loved the Church as the Bride of Christ and grieved over her wounds without ever abandoning her. She pleaded for reform from within, with reverence for the Holy Father even as she begged him to act.

Catarina amava a Igreja como Esposa de Cristo e se entristecia com suas feridas, sem jamais abandoná-la. Suplicava por reforma de dentro, com reverência ao Santo Padre, mesmo ao implorar-lhe que agisse.

Intention

For the renewal of the Church and love for her amid her trials.

Pela renovação da Igreja e pelo amor a ela em meio às suas provações.

Day 6: Counsel to the Pope

Dia 6: O conselho ao Papa

A young laywoman with no power but holiness, Catherine urged Pope Gregory XI to be courageous and return the papacy from Avignon to Rome. She shows that humble fidelity to truth can move even the highest in the Church.

Jovem leiga sem outro poder além da santidade, Catarina exortou o Papa Gregório XI a ser corajoso e a devolver o papado de Avinhão para Roma. Ela mostra que a humilde fidelidade à verdade pode comover até os mais altos na Igreja.

Intention

For the Pope and all shepherds, that they may lead with courage and holiness.

Pelo Papa e por todos os pastores, para que conduzam com coragem e santidade.

Day 7: The Dialogue and divine truth

Dia 7: O Diálogo e a verdade divina

In her great work, The Dialogue, Catherine recorded God the Father's words about truth, mercy, and the bridge that is Christ crossing from earth to heaven. As a Doctor of the Church, she teaches that wisdom is born of love.

Em sua grande obra, o Diálogo, Catarina registrou as palavras de Deus Pai sobre a verdade, a misericórdia e a ponte que é Cristo, unindo a terra ao céu. Como Doutora da Igreja, ela ensina que a sabedoria nasce do amor.

Intention

For the grace to love divine truth and let it transform my life.

Pela graça de amar a verdade divina e deixar que ela transforme minha vida.

Day 8: Peacemaker amid division

Dia 8: Pacificadora em meio à divisão

Catherine traveled and wrote to reconcile warring cities and to heal the divisions tearing at Christendom. Her zeal for peace flowed from a heart that longed to see all souls united in the one love of God.

Catarina viajou e escreveu para reconciliar cidades em guerra e curar as divisões que dilaceravam a cristandade. Seu zelo pela paz brotava de um coração que ansiava ver todas as almas unidas no único amor de Deus.

Intention

For peace in our families, our communities, and our world.

Pela paz em nossas famílias, em nossas comunidades e em nosso mundo.

Day 9: Set the world on fire

Dia 9: Pôr fogo no mundo

Catherine wrote that if we become who we are meant to be, we will set the whole world ablaze. She died at thirty-three, spent for Christ and His Church, leaving the fire of her love to kindle generations after her.

Catarina escreveu que, se nos tornarmos aquilo que devemos ser, poremos fogo no mundo inteiro. Morreu aos trinta e três anos, gasta por Cristo e por Sua Igreja, deixando o fogo de seu amor a incendiar as gerações que vieram depois dela.

Intention

For the grace to become fully who God created me to be.

Pela graça de tornar-me plenamente aquilo que Deus me criou para ser.

Closing prayer

St. Catherine, who counseled, "Be who God meant you to be and you will set the world on fire," kindle that flame in me. Help me to seek truth, to love the Church, and to spend myself for the souls Christ redeemed with His Blood. By your prayers, may I serve God boldly in my own time. Amen.

Santa Catarina, que aconselhastes: "Sede aquilo que Deus quis que fôsseis, e poreis fogo no mundo", acendei em mim essa chama. Ajudai-me a buscar a verdade, a amar a Igreja e a gastar-me pelas almas que Cristo redimiu com Seu Sangue. Por vossas orações, possa eu servir a Deus com ousadia em meu próprio tempo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="siena">
Novena
to Saint Catherine of Siena

Pray this novena starting on 21 April and ending on 29
April, the eve of the Feast of St. Catherine of Siena on 30 April

Catherine, fairest and most glorious of the daughters of St. Dominic,
by that spirit of prayer, which was your delight from your infancy,
obtain for us the love and practice of prayer, and the grace so to
converse with God as to become daily more pleasing to Him.

Pray a Gloria

By that especial love which you, O great saint, bore to the virtue of
purity, consecrating yourself at eight years of age to the Lord by an
irrevocable vow, and afterwards by rejecting the most honorable offers
of marriage: obtain for us, we pray you, the grace to be always pure in
mind and heart, and to detest and abhor everything which could offend
in the smallest degree against a virtue so sublime that it raises men
to the rank of angels, and makes them most beloved by God.

Pray a Gloria

By that spirit of retirement which made you, O great saint, desire to
behold no one but your Jesus, Who when you were distracted by continual
employment in your family, taught you to build a solitude in your heart
and keep it at all times filled with thoughts of heaven: obtain for us,
we pray, the grace so to love solitude and retirement, however the
world may invite us to share its pleasures and its pomps, that our
hearts may always turn to God amidst the most dissipating cares which
may come upon us in our state of life.

Pray a Gloria

By the spirit of penance which taught you to inflict upon yourself,
even in your earliest years, the most painful mortifications: obtain
for us the grace to bear with patience whatever afflictions God may be
pleased to order for our good, and to mortify voluntarily all the
perverse inclinations of our hearts, and all the unruly desires of our
senses, that we may become, in some measure, like our crucified model,
Jesus.

Pray a Gloria

By that heroic charity which led you, O great saint, to attend and
minister with your own hands to the poor sick who had been abandoned by
all others in disgust, and for which you were repaid only by insult,
rudeness, and persecution: obtain of the Lord for us the grace to be,
at all times, equally ready to assist our neighbor in his necessities,
and to pardon him generously when he returns only insults for the
benefits we confer on him, that we may merit the blessedness promised
in this life and the next to meekness and true mercy.

Pray a Gloria

By that supernatural light with which you, O great saint, were
miraculously enabled to counsel the Roman Pontiff, who came in person
to consult you, when you obtained for him a reconciliation with his
adversaries, and his return to Rome: obtain for us of the Lord the
grace to know, in all our doubts, that which is most conformable to the
will of God, and most conducive to the salvation of souls, that in all
our actions we may promote the honor of God and the welfare of our
neighbor.

Pray a Gloria

By that especial devotion which you, O great saint, had to Jesus Christ
in the Blessed Sacrament, Who sometimes communicated you with His own
hands: obtain for us, we pray you, the grace to feel toward the Blessed
Sacrament the most fervent devotion, that we may rejoice to converse
with Jesus and receive Him into our bosoms to His honor and glory, and
for the salvation of our souls.

Pray a Gloria

St. Catherine, pray for us, that we may obtain what we desire through
this novena, if what we ask be pleasing to God and conducive to our
eternal salvation. May the will of God be done. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

This source uses a single prayer recited identically on each of the nine days.

Opening: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

Main Prayer: Heavenly Father, your glory is in your saints. We praise your glory in the life of the admirable St. Catherine of Siena, virgin and doctor of the Church. Her whole life was a noble sacrifice inspired by an ardent love of Jesus, your unblemished Lamb. In troubled times she strenuously upheld the rights of His beloved spouse, The Church. Father, honor her merits and hear her prayers for each of us, and for our whole parish family dedicated to her. Help us to pass unscathed through the corruption of this world, and to remain unshakably faithful to the church in word, deed, and example. Help us always to see in the Vicar of Christ an anchor in the storms of life, and a beacon of light to the harbor of your Love, in this dark night of your times and men's souls. Grant also to each of us our special petition . . . (State your own intentions) We ask this through Jesus, your Son, in the bond of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

Closing: St. Catherine of Siena, Pray for us. In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

Fidelity note

This is not a textual reproduction of either published source. FishEaters lists the novena in its table of contents but the text was not present on the fetched page. PrayMoreNovenas uses a single fixed daily prayer with no distinct daily meditations, whereas Ember's version is an original nine-day structure with a unique meditation and intention for each day drawn from Catherine's life (self-knowledge, mystical espousal, the Precious Blood, service, love of the Church, counsel to the Pope, the Dialogue, peacemaking, and "set the world on fire"). Ember's composition is faithful to the saint's authentic biography and spirituality and shares the same Church/papacy themes as the PMN prayer, but it stands as an independent devotional text rather than a copy of these sources.

St. Athanasius st-athanasius-novena

Apr 23–May 1
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Athanasius, Father and Doctor of the Church, fearless defender of the divinity of Christ against the Arian heresy.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Atanásio, Padre e Doutor da Igreja, destemido defensor da divindade de Cristo contra a heresia ariana.

St. Athanasius (c. 296–373) was Bishop of Alexandria and the great champion of the faith proclaimed at the Council of Nicaea. When much of the world embraced the Arian error that denied Christ's true divinity, he stood firm and was exiled five times for the truth. His phrase "Athanasius against the world" became a watchword of unshakable orthodoxy, and his treatise On the Incarnation remains a treasure of the faith. This novena begs his courage to confess Christ as true God.

Santo Atanásio (c. 296–373) foi bispo de Alexandria e o grande defensor da fé proclamada no Concílio de Niceia. Quando boa parte do mundo abraçou o erro ariano que negava a verdadeira divindade de Cristo, ele se manteve firme e foi exilado cinco vezes pela verdade. A expressão "Atanásio contra o mundo" tornou-se símbolo de uma ortodoxia inabalável, e seu tratado Sobre a Encarnação permanece um tesouro da fé. Esta novena implora sua coragem para confessar Cristo como verdadeiro Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Athanasius, intrepid bishop and pillar of the true faith, you confessed Christ as true God when nearly the whole world turned away. Strengthen me as I begin this novena, that I may hold fast to the truth without fear. Obtain for me from God the grace I now ask and a heart firmly fixed on Christ. Amen.

Santo Atanásio, bispo intrépido e coluna da verdadeira fé, vós confessastes Cristo como verdadeiro Deus quando quase o mundo inteiro Lhe deu as costas. Fortalecei-me ao iniciar esta novena, para que eu me apegue à verdade sem temor. Alcançai-me de Deus a graça que agora peço e um coração firmemente fixo em Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: Christ is true God

Dia 1: Cristo é verdadeiro Deus

Athanasius insisted that the Son is of one substance with the Father, true God from true God, not a creature however exalted. He saw clearly that our salvation depends on this truth, for only God can make us partakers of His own life.

Atanásio insistia que o Filho é da mesma substância do Pai, verdadeiro Deus de verdadeiro Deus, e não uma criatura, por mais elevada que fosse. Ele via com clareza que nossa salvação depende dessa verdade, pois só Deus pode tornar-nos participantes de Sua própria vida.

Intention

For the grace to adore Jesus Christ as true God and true man.

Pela graça de adorar Jesus Cristo como verdadeiro Deus e verdadeiro homem.

Day 2: On the Incarnation

Dia 2: Sobre a Encarnação

In his great treatise Athanasius wrote that God became man so that man might become a sharer in God. He marveled that the Word who upholds all things took a body to renew the image of God in us.

Em seu grande tratado, Atanásio escreveu que Deus se fez homem para que o homem se tornasse participante de Deus. Maravilhava-se de que o Verbo, que sustenta todas as coisas, tomasse um corpo para renovar em nós a imagem de Deus.

Intention

For wonder and gratitude before the mystery of the Word made flesh.

Por admiração e gratidão diante do mistério do Verbo feito carne.

Day 3: The Council of Nicaea

Dia 3: O Concílio de Niceia

As a young deacon, Athanasius assisted at the Council of Nicaea, where the Church confessed the Son as consubstantial with the Father. He spent the rest of his life defending what that council had so clearly proclaimed.

Ainda jovem diácono, Atanásio participou do Concílio de Niceia, onde a Igreja confessou o Filho como consubstancial ao Pai. Ele passou o resto da vida defendendo o que aquele concílio proclamara com tanta clareza.

Intention

For fidelity to the faith handed down by the Church through the ages.

Pela fidelidade à fé transmitida pela Igreja através dos séculos.

Day 4: Athanasius against the world

Dia 4: Atanásio contra o mundo

When emperors, bishops, and crowds embraced Arianism, Athanasius stood almost alone for the truth, earning the title "Athanasius against the world." He teaches us that truth is not decided by numbers, and that one faithful soul can hold the line.

Quando imperadores, bispos e multidões abraçaram o arianismo, Atanásio permaneceu quase sozinho em defesa da verdade, merecendo o título de "Atanásio contra o mundo". Ele nos ensina que a verdade não se decide pela maioria, e que uma só alma fiel pode sustentar a posição.

Intention

For courage to stand for the truth even when I stand nearly alone.

Pela coragem de defender a verdade mesmo quando eu estiver quase só.

Day 5: Patience in exile

Dia 5: Paciência no exílio

Five times Athanasius was driven from his see, spending long years in exile, hiding, and the desert. He bore each banishment without bitterness, trusting that God would vindicate the truth in His own time.

Cinco vezes Atanásio foi expulso de sua sé, passando longos anos no exílio, escondido e no deserto. Suportou cada banimento sem amargura, confiando que Deus haveria de fazer triunfar a verdade no tempo dEle.

Intention

For patience to endure injustice without bitterness or compromise.

Pela paciência de suportar a injustiça sem amargura nem concessões.

Day 6: Friend of the monks

Dia 6: Amigo dos monges

During his exiles Athanasius found refuge among the desert monks and wrote the famous Life of St. Antony, spreading the monastic ideal across the Church. He valued the hidden prayer of the desert as a wellspring of the Church's strength.

Durante seus exílios, Atanásio encontrou refúgio entre os monges do deserto e escreveu a célebre Vida de Santo Antão, difundindo o ideal monástico por toda a Igreja. Ele estimava a oração oculta do deserto como uma fonte da força da Igreja.

Intention

For all who seek God in prayer and hidden fidelity.

Por todos os que buscam a Deus na oração e na fidelidade oculta.

Day 7: Shepherd of his people

Dia 7: Pastor de seu povo

Even from hiding, Athanasius shepherded his flock by letters, instruction, and unfailing care for their faith. He never abandoned the people of Alexandria, who loved him and welcomed him home with joy after each return.

Mesmo escondido, Atanásio apascentava seu rebanho por meio de cartas, ensino e cuidado constante com a fé deles. Jamais abandonou o povo de Alexandria, que o amava e o recebia de volta com alegria a cada retorno.

Intention

For bishops and pastors, that they may guard their flocks with courage.

Pelos bispos e pastores, para que guardem seus rebanhos com coragem.

Day 8: Defender against error

Dia 8: Defensor contra o erro

Athanasius answered every subtle argument of the Arians with the plain witness of Scripture and the Church's worship of Christ. He shows us that sound doctrine guards the prayer and life of the faithful from being led astray.

Atanásio respondia a cada argumento sutil dos arianos com o testemunho claro da Escritura e o culto que a Igreja prestava a Cristo. Ele nos mostra que a sã doutrina protege a oração e a vida dos fiéis de serem desviadas.

Intention

For the grace to know my faith well and never be led into error.

Pela graça de conhecer bem minha fé e jamais ser levado ao erro.

Day 9: Perseverance to the end

Dia 9: Perseverança até o fim

After decades of struggle, Athanasius died at peace in Alexandria, having seen the tide begin to turn toward the truth he had defended. His life proclaims that perseverance in the faith, however costly, is never in vain.

Após décadas de luta, Atanásio morreu em paz em Alexandria, tendo visto a maré começar a virar em favor da verdade que defendera. Sua vida proclama que a perseverança na fé, por mais custosa que seja, jamais é em vão.

Intention

For the grace of final perseverance in the faith of Christ.

Pela graça da perseverança final na fé de Cristo.

Closing prayer

St. Athanasius, who endured exile and slander rather than betray the faith, teach me your steadfast love of the truth. Help me to adore the eternal Son, consubstantial with the Father, and to confess Him before the world. By your prayers, may I persevere in the faith of Nicaea until I see God face to face. Amen.

Santo Atanásio, que suportastes o exílio e a calúnia antes de trair a fé, ensinai-me vosso amor constante à verdade. Ajudai-me a adorar o Filho eterno, consubstancial ao Pai, e a confessá-Lo diante do mundo. Por vossas orações, possa eu perseverar na fé de Niceia até ver a Deus face a face. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas publishes a complete 9-day novena to St. Athanasius. Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross ("In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.") and a fixed thanksgiving: "Dear Lord, we thank You for giving us Your servant, St. Athanasius, as an example of holiness…" Each day then addresses St. Athanasius directly on a distinct theme of his life — Day 1: "St. Athanasius, you grew up with the benefit of devout Christian parents. They ensured that you received a strong education in the Faith, and you eagerly grew in your Faith and service to God." — followed by a petition ("Pray for me, that I may always seek ways to grow in holiness in my life…"), a space to mention personal intentions, the acclamation "St. Athanasius, pray for us!", and the closing Sign of the Cross.

The nine themes are: Day 1 — childhood and holiness; Day 2 — service as deacon; Day 3 — theological writings; Day 4 — preaching against heresy; Day 5 — holding fast through false accusations; Day 6 — perseverance through exile; Day 7 — fortitude amid trials; Day 8 — service as bishop; Day 9 — lifelong devotion to the Church.

Fidelity note

Ember's novena is an original composition, not a copy of the PrayMoreNovenas text — the wording of every opening, daily, and closing prayer differs entirely. Both follow the same nine-day arc through the saint's life and share several themes (Incarnation/writings, defending against heresy, exile, the episcopate, perseverance), so they are thematically close though structured differently. Ember separates meditation from a single short intention and folds in the Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be, whereas PMN embeds petitions in each day's prayer and repeats a fixed thanksgiving. This is a faithful devotional treatment of St. Athanasius rather than a reproduction of any specific published source.

Our Lady of Fatima our-lady-of-fatima-novena

May 4–12
description & history

A nine-day novena to Our Lady of Fatima, who appeared to three shepherd children in 1917 calling the world to prayer, penance, and the Rosary.

Uma novena de nove dias a Nossa Senhora de Fátima, que apareceu a três pastorinhos em 1917 convocando o mundo à oração, à penitência e ao Rosário.

In 1917, at Fatima in Portugal, the Blessed Virgin Mary appeared six times to three shepherd children, Lúcia, Francisco, and Jacinta. She called the world to pray the Rosary daily, to do penance for sinners, and to consecrate itself to her Immaculate Heart, confirming her message with the great miracle of the sun on 13 October. Her appeals to prayer and reparation remain as urgent today as a century ago. This novena entrusts our needs and the conversion of the world to her Immaculate Heart.

Em 1917, em Fátima, Portugal, a Santíssima Virgem Maria apareceu seis vezes a três pastorinhos: Lúcia, Francisco e Jacinta. Convocou o mundo a rezar o Rosário todos os dias, a fazer penitência pelos pecadores e a consagrar-se ao seu Imaculado Coração, confirmando sua mensagem com o grande milagre do sol em 13 de outubro. Seus apelos à oração e à reparação permanecem tão urgentes hoje quanto há um século. Esta novena confia nossas necessidades e a conversão do mundo ao seu Imaculado Coração.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Our Lady of Fatima, tender Mother who came to call your children back to God, I begin this novena trusting in your maternal love. Teach me to pray, to do penance, and to console the Heart of Jesus. Through your Immaculate Heart, present my petitions to your Son and obtain for me the grace I now ask. Amen.

Nossa Senhora de Fátima, terna Mãe que viestes chamar vossos filhos de volta a Deus, inicio esta novena confiando em vosso amor materno. Ensinai-me a rezar, a fazer penitência e a consolar o Coração de Jesus. Por vosso Imaculado Coração, apresentai minhas súplicas a vosso Filho e alcançai-me a graça que agora peço. Amém.

Day 1: The angel's call to prayer

Dia 1: O apelo do anjo à oração

Before Mary came, an angel appeared to the children at Fatima, teaching them to adore God and to pray for sinners. He prepared their hearts by leading them deep into prayer, showing that adoration is the soil in which every grace grows.

Antes da vinda de Maria, um anjo apareceu às crianças em Fátima, ensinando-as a adorar a Deus e a rezar pelos pecadores. Ele preparou seus corações conduzindo-as a uma oração profunda, mostrando que a adoração é o solo em que toda graça floresce.

Intention

For the grace to adore God with a humble and recollected heart.

Pela graça de adorar a Deus com um coração humilde e recolhido.

Day 2: The Lady from heaven

Dia 2: A Senhora vinda do céu

On 13 May 1917, a Lady brighter than the sun appeared above a small holm oak and asked the children to return on the thirteenth of each month. She came not to frighten but to draw her children gently back to her Son.

Em 13 de maio de 1917, uma Senhora mais brilhante que o sol apareceu sobre uma pequena azinheira e pediu às crianças que voltassem no dia treze de cada mês. Ela veio não para amedrontar, mas para conduzir com suavidade seus filhos de volta a seu Filho.

Intention

For the grace to welcome Mary's words into my own life.

Pela graça de acolher as palavras de Maria em minha própria vida.

Day 3: Pray the Rosary daily

Dia 3: Rezai o Rosário todos os dias

At every apparition Our Lady asked that the Rosary be prayed each day for peace and the conversion of sinners. She placed this simple, powerful prayer into the hands of children, and through them, into the hands of the whole world.

Em cada aparição, Nossa Senhora pediu que o Rosário fosse rezado todos os dias pela paz e pela conversão dos pecadores. Ela colocou essa oração simples e poderosa nas mãos de crianças e, por meio delas, nas mãos do mundo inteiro.

Intention

For the grace to pray the Rosary daily with faith and love.

Pela graça de rezar o Rosário todos os dias com fé e amor.

Day 4: Penance and reparation

Dia 4: Penitência e reparação

Mary asked the children to make sacrifices for sinners and to offer their daily hardships to God in reparation. The little shepherds embraced penance with generous love, teaching us that small offerings, given in love, console the Heart of God.

Maria pediu às crianças que fizessem sacrifícios pelos pecadores e oferecessem a Deus suas dificuldades diárias em reparação. Os pequenos pastores abraçaram a penitência com amor generoso, ensinando-nos que pequenas ofertas, feitas com amor, consolam o Coração de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to offer my daily sacrifices for the conversion of sinners.

Pela graça de oferecer meus sacrifícios diários pela conversão dos pecadores.

Day 5: The Immaculate Heart

Dia 5: O Imaculado Coração

Our Lady revealed her Immaculate Heart, surrounded by thorns from the sins of men, and asked for consecration and reparation. She promised that her Heart would be a refuge and the sure way leading souls to God.

Nossa Senhora revelou seu Imaculado Coração, cercado de espinhos pelos pecados dos homens, e pediu consagração e reparação. Ela prometeu que seu Coração seria um refúgio e o caminho seguro que conduz as almas a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to consecrate myself wholly to the Immaculate Heart of Mary.

Pela graça de consagrar-me inteiramente ao Imaculado Coração de Maria.

Day 6: The holiness of the children

Dia 6: A santidade dos pastorinhos

Francisco longed to console the hidden Jesus, while Jacinta burned with love for sinners and the Holy Father; both died young and were canonized as saints. They show that holiness is offered even to the smallest and the youngest.

Francisco ansiava por consolar o Jesus escondido, enquanto Jacinta ardia de amor pelos pecadores e pelo Santo Padre; ambos morreram cedo e foram canonizados. Eles mostram que a santidade é oferecida até aos menores e mais jovens.

Intention

For children and young people, that they may grow in holiness and love.
