Pelas crianças e pelos jovens, para que cresçam em santidade e amor.

Day 7: The miracle of the sun

Dia 7: O milagre do sol

On 13 October 1917, before a crowd of thousands, the sun seemed to dance and plunge toward the earth, confirming the children's testimony. God gave this sign so that all might believe and heed His Mother's appeal.

Em 13 de outubro de 1917, diante de uma multidão de milhares, o sol pareceu dançar e precipitar-se em direção à terra, confirmando o testemunho das crianças. Deus concedeu esse sinal para que todos cressem e atendessem ao apelo de Sua Mãe.

Intention

For the grace of firm faith in God's signs and His holy will.

Pela graça de uma fé firme nos sinais de Deus e em Sua santa vontade.

Day 8: Consecration of the world

Dia 8: A consagração do mundo

Our Lady asked for the consecration of the world to her Immaculate Heart and the devotion of the First Saturdays in reparation. She desires to gather all nations and all hearts and present them safely to her Son.

Nossa Senhora pediu a consagração do mundo ao seu Imaculado Coração e a devoção dos Primeiros Sábados em reparação. Ela deseja reunir todas as nações e todos os corações e apresentá-los em segurança a seu Filho.

Intention

For peace in the world and the consecration of all hearts to Mary.

Pela paz no mundo e pela consagração de todos os corações a Maria.

Day 9: The triumph of the Immaculate Heart

Dia 9: O triunfo do Imaculado Coração

Our Lady promised that in the end her Immaculate Heart would triumph and an era of peace would be granted to the world. This promise gives us hope, calling us to trust that no darkness can outlast the love of our Mother.

Nossa Senhora prometeu que, no fim, seu Imaculado Coração triunfaria e um tempo de paz seria concedido ao mundo. Essa promessa nos dá esperança, chamando-nos a confiar que treva alguma prevalece sobre o amor de nossa Mãe.

Intention

For unshakable hope in the final triumph of the Immaculate Heart.

Por uma esperança inabalável no triunfo final do Imaculado Coração.

Closing prayer

Our Lady of Fatima, who promised that your Immaculate Heart would triumph, keep me faithful to your requests of prayer and penance. Help me to pray the Rosary each day and to offer my sufferings for the conversion of sinners. May I one day rejoice with you and the shepherd children in the glory of heaven. Amen.

Nossa Senhora de Fátima, que prometestes o triunfo de vosso Imaculado Coração, guardai-me fiel aos vossos pedidos de oração e penitência. Ajudai-me a rezar o Rosário cada dia e a oferecer meus sofrimentos pela conversão dos pecadores. Possa eu um dia alegrar-me convosco e com os pastorinhos na glória do céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a Novena to Our Lady of Fatima (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/novena-our-lady-fatima). It opens each day with a Trinitarian blessing and a fixed invocation ("O Most Holy Virgin Mary, you came to Fatima to reveal the graces that come from praying the Holy Rosary..."), then gives a distinct daily meditation/intention, closing petitions to Our Lady of Fatima, Our Lady of the Rosary, and the Immaculate Heart, and a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes there: Day 1 overcoming doubts as Gospel messengers; Day 2 the oppressed and gratitude; Day 3 those suffering injustice; Day 4 humility/forgiveness/amendment; Day 5 offering daily actions in love; Day 6 trust amid fear; Day 7 constant prayer and listening; Day 8 peace through trustful faith; Day 9 thanksgiving and continued prayer and sacrifice.

Fidelity note

Ember is an independent composition, not a transcription of the PrayMoreNovenas text — none of the wording matches. Both share the same nine-day, fixed-opening + daily-meditation structure and the core Fatima themes (Rosary, penance/reparation, the Immaculate Heart, peace). Ember's days are more historically narrative (the angel, the May apparition, the miracle of the sun, the First Saturdays, the promised triumph), whereas PrayMoreNovenas leans toward generic petition themes. Faithful to the Fatima devotion in substance, but original in language.

St. Rita of Cascia st-rita-novena

May 11–20
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Rita of Cascia, advocate of desperate and impossible causes.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Rita de Cássia, advogada das causas desesperadas e impossíveis.

St. Rita of Cascia (1381–1457) endured a difficult marriage to a violent husband, whom she won to peace before his murder, then forgave his killers. Widowed and bereft of her sons, she entered the Augustinian convent at Cascia, where she received a wound from the crown of thorns of the crucified Christ. Honored as the saint of the impossible, she is invoked by those whose hopes seem beyond all remedy.

Santa Rita de Cássia (1381–1457) suportou um casamento difícil com um marido violento, a quem conduziu à paz antes de seu assassinato, e depois perdoou os assassinos. Viúva e privada dos filhos, entrou no convento agostiniano de Cássia, onde recebeu uma chaga da coroa de espinhos de Cristo crucificado. Venerada como a santa das causas impossíveis, é invocada por aqueles cujas esperanças parecem além de todo remédio.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glorious St. Rita, faithful spouse, devoted mother, and patient widow, you embraced every cross with love for the crucified Lord. As I begin this novena, obtain for me a heart that trusts God in the darkest hour. Amen.

Ó gloriosa Santa Rita, esposa fiel, mãe dedicada e viúva paciente, vós que abraçastes toda cruz com amor ao Senhor crucificado, ao iniciar esta novena alcançai-me um coração que confie em Deus na hora mais escura. Amém.

Day 1: Trust in God's plan

Dia 1: Confiança no plano de Deus

From childhood St. Rita longed for the cloister, yet her parents gave her in marriage; she bowed to God's will without bitterness. She teaches us that the path we do not choose can still lead straight to holiness.

Desde a infância Santa Rita desejava o claustro, mas seus pais a deram em casamento; ela se curvou à vontade de Deus sem amargura. Ela nos ensina que o caminho que não escolhemos pode conduzir reto à santidade.

Intention

For the grace to accept the life God has given me with trust.

Pela graça de aceitar com confiança a vida que Deus me deu.

Day 2: Patience in a hard marriage

Dia 2: Paciência num casamento difícil

For eighteen years St. Rita lived with a harsh and violent husband, answering his anger with gentleness and prayer. By her quiet constancy she softened his heart and won him at last to peace with God.

Por dezoito anos Santa Rita conviveu com um marido áspero e violento, respondendo à sua ira com mansidão e oração. Por sua constância silenciosa abrandou-lhe o coração e o conquistou enfim para a paz com Deus.

Intention

For peace and conversion within troubled households.

Pela paz e conversão nos lares em conflito.

Day 3: Forgiveness of enemies

Dia 3: Perdão dos inimigos

When her husband was murdered, St. Rita forgave his killers and begged God to spare her sons from vengeance. She shows us that the chain of hatred is broken only by mercy freely given.

Quando seu marido foi assassinado, Santa Rita perdoou os assassinos e suplicou a Deus que poupasse seus filhos da vingança. Ela nos mostra que a corrente do ódio só se rompe pela misericórdia oferecida com liberdade.

Intention

For the grace to forgive those who have wronged me.

Pela graça de perdoar aqueles que me ofenderam.

Day 4: Surrender of her sons

Dia 4: Entrega dos filhos

Fearing her sons would die avenging their father, St. Rita prayed they be taken rather than stain their souls; both died soon after, reconciled to God. Her sorrow teaches that we may entrust even our children wholly to Providence.

Temendo que seus filhos morressem vingando o pai, Santa Rita orou para que fossem levados antes que manchassem a alma; ambos morreram pouco depois, reconciliados com Deus. Sua dor nos ensina que podemos confiar até os filhos inteiramente à Providência.

Intention

For all parents anxious for the souls of their children.

Por todos os pais angustiados pela alma de seus filhos.

Day 5: Perseverance at the convent door

Dia 5: Perseverança à porta do convento

Refused three times by the Augustinian sisters because of her past, St. Rita knocked until heaven itself carried her within the walls. She teaches us never to abandon a holy desire that meets closed doors.

Recusada três vezes pelas irmãs agostinianas por causa de seu passado, Santa Rita insistiu até que o próprio céu a conduzisse para dentro dos muros. Ela nos ensina a nunca abandonar um santo desejo diante de portas fechadas.

Intention

For perseverance when my good desires seem refused.

Pela perseverança quando meus bons desejos parecem recusados.

Day 6: The wound of the thorn

Dia 6: A chaga do espinho

Praying before the crucifix, St. Rita asked to share Christ's suffering, and a thorn from His crown pierced her brow with a wound that remained until death. She invites us to love the Cross not from afar but in our own flesh.

Orando diante do crucifixo, Santa Rita pediu participar do sofrimento de Cristo, e um espinho de Sua coroa feriu-lhe a fronte com uma chaga que permaneceu até a morte. Ela nos convida a amar a Cruz não de longe, mas na própria carne.

Intention

For the grace to unite my pains to the sufferings of Christ.

Pela graça de unir minhas dores aos sofrimentos de Cristo.

Day 7: Hidden penance and prayer

Dia 7: Penitência e oração ocultas

For forty years in the cloister St. Rita lived in fasting, vigil, and constant prayer, bearing her hidden wound without complaint. She reminds us that the holiest lives are often the least seen.

Por quarenta anos no claustro Santa Rita viveu em jejum, vigília e oração constante, carregando sem queixa a chaga oculta. Ela nos recorda que as vidas mais santas são muitas vezes as menos vistas.

Intention

For fidelity in the small, unseen duties of each day.

Pela fidelidade nos pequenos deveres ocultos de cada dia.

Day 8: The rose in winter

Dia 8: A rosa no inverno

Near death, St. Rita asked for a rose from her family's garden, and one bloomed amid the January frost. The miracle shows that with God no situation is so barren that grace cannot make it flower.

Próxima da morte, Santa Rita pediu uma rosa do jardim de sua família, e uma floresceu em meio à geada de janeiro. O milagre mostra que com Deus nenhuma situação é tão estéril que a graça não possa fazê-la florescer.

Intention

For hope in causes that seem entirely lost.

Pela esperança nas causas que parecem totalmente perdidas.

Day 9: Advocate of the impossible

Dia 9: Advogada do impossível

In every trial St. Rita kept her gaze on the crucified Lord, and so the Church honors her as patroness of impossible causes. She assures us that no prayer offered in faith is ever truly beyond God's reach.

Em toda provação Santa Rita manteve o olhar no Senhor crucificado, e por isso a Igreja a honra como padroeira das causas impossíveis. Ela nos assegura que nenhuma oração feita com fé está verdadeiramente além do alcance de Deus.

Intention

For the particular grace I now place in St. Rita's hands.

Pela graça particular que agora coloco nas mãos de Santa Rita.

Closing prayer

O St. Rita, advocate of impossible causes, present my petitions before the throne of God. By the wound you bore from the crown of thorns, teach me to suffer with patience and to forgive without measure, until I rejoice with you in heaven. Amen.

Ó Santa Rita, advogada das causas impossíveis, apresentai os meus pedidos diante do trono de Deus. Pela chaga que recebestes da coroa de espinhos, ensinai-me a sofrer com paciência e a perdoar sem medida, até me alegrar convosco no céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="rita">
Novena
to St. Rita of Cascia

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 13
May and ending on 21 May, the eve of the Feast of St. Rita.

O holy protectress of those who art in greatest need, thou who shineth
as a star of hope in the midst of darkness, blessed Saint Rita, bright
mirror of God's grace, in patience and fortitude thou art a model of
all the states in life. I unite my will with the will of God through
the merits of my Savior Jesus Christ, and in particular through His
patient wearing of the crown of thorns, which with tender devotion thou
didst daily contemplate. Through the merits of the holy Virgin Mary and
thine own graces and virtues, I ask thee to obtain my earnest petition,
provided it be for the greater glory of God and my own sanctification.
Guide and purify my intention, O holy protectress and advocate, so that
I may obtain the pardon of all my sins and the grace to persevere
daily, as thou didst in walking with courage, generosity, and fidelity
down the path of life.

(Mention your intentions here)

Saint Rita, advocate of the impossible, pray for us.

Saint Rita, advocate of the helpless, pray for us.

Recite the Our Father,
Hail Mary, and Glory Be three times <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a St. Rita novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-rita-novena-2). Each day opens with a Trinitarian blessing, an appeal to the Heavenly Father referencing St. Rita's holiness and her stigmata wound from the crown of thorns, a request to understand the paschal mystery through her example, and a day-specific petition, then a fixed intercession ("St. Rita, you are the patron of the impossible...") and a daily closing invocation. Daily closing phrases there: Day 1 compassion for the suffering of Christ; Day 2 generous in forgiving; Day 3 heroic in virtue; Day 4 our protector; Day 5 example of patience; Day 6 close to God through prayer; Day 7 example of Christian love; Day 8 full of hope; Day 9 steadfast in faith.

Fidelity note

Ember's wording is original, not copied from PrayMoreNovenas, but the two are strongly aligned in substance: both build on St. Rita's biography (hard marriage, forgiveness, the thorn wound, patroness of the impossible) and use a fixed-opening + daily-theme structure. Ember's day themes track her life chronologically and capture the same pillars PrayMoreNovenas hits (forgiveness, patience, the wound of the thorn, hope in impossible causes), with Ember adding well-attested episodes (the convent door, the rose in winter). A faithful, well-grounded original composition; the FishEaters text could not be verified.

St. Philip Neri st-philip-neri-novena

May 17–25
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Philip Neri, the joyful Apostle of Rome and founder of the Oratory.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Filipe Néri, o alegre Apóstolo de Roma e fundador do Oratório.

St. Philip Neri (1515–1595) left Florence for Rome, where he won souls in the streets with disarming joy and holy humor. On the eve of Pentecost his heart was so enflamed with the love of God that it visibly swelled, breaking two ribs. He founded the Congregation of the Oratory and loved the gentle words 'Cor ad cor loquitur' — heart speaks to heart.

São Filipe Néri (1515–1595) deixou Florença por Roma, onde ganhou almas nas ruas com alegria cativante e santo bom humor. Na véspera de Pentecostes seu coração ardeu de tal modo no amor de Deus que dilatou visivelmente, quebrando duas costelas. Fundou a Congregação do Oratório e amava as doces palavras 'Cor ad cor loquitur' — coração fala ao coração.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glad St. Philip Neri, who set Rome on fire with the joy of the Gospel, look kindly on me as I begin this novena. Win for me a cheerful and humble heart, that I may serve God with gladness all my days. Amen.

Ó alegre São Filipe Néri, que incendiastes Roma com a alegria do Evangelho, olhai com bondade para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me um coração jovial e humilde, para que eu sirva a Deus com alegria todos os meus dias. Amém.

Day 1: Holy joy

Dia 1: A santa alegria

St. Philip was sure that a sad heart cannot serve God well, and his laughter drew the most hardened sinners to confession. He teaches us that true cheerfulness is a fruit of the Spirit, not a denial of the Cross.

São Filipe estava certo de que um coração triste não pode servir bem a Deus, e sua alegria atraía à confissão os pecadores mais endurecidos. Ele nos ensina que a verdadeira jovialidade é fruto do Espírito, não negação da Cruz.

Intention

For the grace to serve God with a glad and willing heart.

Pela graça de servir a Deus com coração alegre e disposto.

Day 2: Humility and self-mockery

Dia 2: Humildade e autocrítica jovial

St. Philip dreaded honors and would play the fool, walking out with half his beard shaved, lest men praise him. He shows us that humility can wear a smile and that holiness need not take itself too seriously.

São Filipe temia as honras e fazia-se de tolo, saindo com metade da barba raspada, para que os homens não o louvassem. Ele nos mostra que a humildade pode trazer um sorriso e que a santidade não precisa levar-se a sério demais.

Intention

For freedom from vanity and the love of human praise.

Pela libertação da vaidade e do amor ao louvor humano.

Day 3: The streets of Rome

Dia 3: As ruas de Roma

Before he was a priest, St. Philip walked the streets and shops of Rome, striking up conversations that turned hearts to Christ. He teaches us that the apostolate begins wherever people are, in the ordinary places of daily life.

Antes de ser sacerdote, São Filipe percorria as ruas e lojas de Roma, iniciando conversas que voltavam os corações a Cristo. Ele nos ensina que o apostolado começa onde estão as pessoas, nos lugares comuns da vida diária.

Intention

For courage to speak of God in everyday encounters.

Pela coragem de falar de Deus nos encontros cotidianos.

Day 4: The enflamed heart

Dia 4: O coração inflamado

Praying in the catacombs on the eve of Pentecost, St. Philip received a fire of divine love so great that it physically enlarged his heart. He invites us to ask boldly for a heart made wider by the Holy Spirit.

Orando nas catacumbas na véspera de Pentecostes, São Filipe recebeu um fogo de amor divino tão grande que dilatou fisicamente seu coração. Ele nos convida a pedir com ousadia um coração alargado pelo Espírito Santo.

Intention

For a heart set ablaze with love for God.

Por um coração incendiado de amor a Deus.

Day 5: Cor ad cor loquitur

Dia 5: Cor ad cor loquitur

St. Philip's motto, 'heart speaks to heart,' shaped his whole way of guiding souls with tenderness rather than force. He teaches that grace passes most freely through affection and patient friendship.

O lema de São Filipe, 'coração fala ao coração', moldou todo o seu modo de guiar as almas com ternura e não com força. Ele nos ensina que a graça flui mais livremente pela afeição e pela amizade paciente.

Intention

For the gift of speaking to others heart to heart.

Pelo dom de falar aos outros de coração para coração.

Day 6: The Oratory

Dia 6: O Oratório

St. Philip gathered friends for prayer, Scripture, song, and joyful conversation, and from this grew the Congregation of the Oratory. He shows us how holiness can flourish in community, lightly worn and shared in friendship.

São Filipe reunia amigos para a oração, a Escritura, o canto e a conversa alegre, e disso nasceu a Congregação do Oratório. Ele nos mostra como a santidade pode florescer em comunidade, vivida com leveza e partilhada na amizade.

Intention

For holy friendships that draw us closer to Christ.

Por santas amizades que nos aproximem de Cristo.

Day 7: Devotion to the Holy Spirit

Dia 7: Devoção ao Espírito Santo

St. Philip prayed often, 'Come, Holy Spirit,' and lived in constant dependence on God's grace for every word and work. He teaches us that fruitful action flows only from a soul docile to the Spirit.

São Filipe orava com frequência: 'Vinde, Espírito Santo', e vivia em constante dependência da graça de Deus para cada palavra e obra. Ele nos ensina que a ação fecunda só brota de uma alma dócil ao Espírito.

Intention

For openness to the guidance of the Holy Spirit.

Pela docilidade à condução do Espírito Santo.

Day 8: Distrust of self

Dia 8: Desconfiança de si mesmo

St. Philip began each day praying, 'Lord, keep your hand on Philip today, or he will betray you.' He reminds us that even the holiest soul stands only by the constant mercy of God.

São Filipe começava cada dia rezando: 'Senhor, guardai hoje vossa mão sobre Filipe, ou ele vos trairá.' Ele nos recorda que até a alma mais santa só se sustenta pela misericórdia constante de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to distrust myself and rely wholly on God.

Pela graça de desconfiar de mim e apoiar-me inteiramente em Deus.

Day 9: Apostle of Rome

Dia 9: Apóstolo de Roma

By a lifetime of joyful charity St. Philip renewed the faith of a whole city and earned the title Apostle of Rome. He shows us that one cheerful saint, faithful in small things, can rekindle the love of many.

Por uma vida inteira de caridade alegre, São Filipe renovou a fé de uma cidade inteira e mereceu o título de Apóstolo de Roma. Ele nos mostra que um santo alegre, fiel nas pequenas coisas, pode reacender o amor de muitos.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. Philip Neri.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São Filipe Néri.

Closing prayer

O St. Philip, whose heart was enlarged by the Holy Spirit, present my petitions to the Lord you loved so dearly. Teach me to delight in God and to lead others to Him by kindness rather than by harshness, until we meet in the joy of heaven. Amen.

Ó São Filipe, cujo coração foi dilatado pelo Espírito Santo, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor que tanto amastes. Ensinai-me a deleitar-me em Deus e a conduzir os outros a Ele pela bondade e não pela dureza, até nos encontrarmos na alegria do céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="neri">
Novena
to St. Philip Neri

This novena comes from the Oratorians in Toronto, who
say it has been adapted from the prayers and devotions of Cardinal
Newman. It is prayed especially beginning on May 17 and ending on May
25, the eve of the Feast of St. Philip Neri

Day One:

Philip, my glorious Patron, who didst count as dross the praise, and
even the good esteem of men, obtain for me also, from my Lord and
Savior, this fair virtue by thy prayers. How haughty are my thoughts,
how contemptuous are my words, how ambitious are my works. Gain for me
that low esteem of self with which thou wast gifted; obtain for me a
knowledge of my own nothingness, that I may rejoice when I am despised,
and ever seek to be great only in the eyes of my God and Judge. Through
Christ Our Lord. Amen.

Day Two:

Philip, my glorious Patron, gain for me a portion of that gift which
thou hadst so abundantly. Alas! thy heart was burning with love; mine
is all frozen towards God, and alive only for creatures. I love the
world, which can never make me happy; my highest desire is to be well
off here below. O my God, when shall I learn to love nothing else but
Thee? Gain for me, O Philip, a pure love, a strong love, and an
efficacious love, that, loving God here upon earth, I may enjoy the
sight of Him together with thee and all the saints, hereafter in
Heaven. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

Day Three:

Philip, my holy Patron, teach me by thy example, and gain for me by thy
intercessions, to seek my Lord and God at all times and in all places,
and to live in His presence and in sacred intercourse with Him. As the
children of this world look up to rich men or men in station for the
favor which they desire, so may I ever lift up my eyes and hands and
heart towards heaven, and betake myself to the source of all good for
those goods which I need. As the children of this world converse with
their friends and find their pleasure in them, so may I ever hold
communion with Saints and Angels, and with the Blessed Virgin, the
Mother of my Lord. Pray with me, O Philip, as thou didst pray with thy
penitents here below, and then prayer will become sweet to me as it did
to them. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

Day Four:

Philip, my glorious Patron, who didst ever keep unsullied the white
lily of thy purity, with such jealous care that the majesty of this
fair virtue beamed from thine eyes, shone in thy hands, and was
fragrant in thy breath, obtain for me that gift from the Holy Ghost,
that neither the words nor the example of sinners may ever make any
impression on my soul. And, since it is by avoiding occasions of sin,
by prayer, by keeping myself employed, and by frequent use of the
Sacraments that my dread enemy must be subdued, gain for me the grace
to persevere in these necessary observances. Through Christ our Lord.
Amen.

Day Five:

Philip, my glorious Advocate, teach me to look at all I see around me
after thy pattern as the creatures of God. Let me never forget that the
same God who made me made the whole world, and all men and all animals
that are in it. Gain for me the grace to love all God's works for His
sake, and all men for the sake of my Lord and Savior who has redeemed
them by the Cross. And especially let me be tender and compassionate
and loving towards all Christians, as my brethren in grace. And do
thou, who on earth wast so tender to all, be especially tender to us,
and feel for us, bear with us in all our troubles, and gain for us from
God, with whom thou dwellest in beatific light, all the aids necessary
for bringing us safely to Him and to thee. Through Christ our Lord.
Amen.

Day Six:

Philip, my glorious Advocate, who didst ever follow the precepts and
example of the Apostle Saint Paul in rejoicing always in all things,
gain for me the grace of perfect resignation to God's will, of
indifference to matters of this world, and a constant sight of Heaven;
so that I may never

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas reproduces the classic nine-day St. Philip Neri novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-philip-neri-novena), Trinitarian blessing opening and closing, with a unique daily prayer beginning "Philip, my glorious patron/advocate..." Daily themes: Day 1 humility; Day 2 devotion / divine love; Day 3 the exercise of prayer; Day 4 purity; Day 5 tenderness of heart; Day 6 cheerfulness; Day 7 patience; Day 8 care for the salvation of souls; Day 9 miraculous gifts.

Fidelity note

Ember's prayers are an original composition and do not reuse the "Philip, my glorious patron..." petitions of the classic Newman/PrayMoreNovenas text. The two overlap clearly on the saint's hallmark themes — joy/cheerfulness, humility, prayer, purity (Ember folds this into the wider "enflamed heart"/Spirit material), tenderness of heart ("cor ad cor loquitur"), and zeal for souls. Ember adds well-attested biographical days the source treats only lightly (the streets of Rome, the catacombs/enlarged heart, the Oratory, "Apostle of Rome"). Faithful to St. Philip's spirit and the traditional themes, but freshly worded.

St. José de Anchieta st-jose-de-anchieta-novena

May 31–Jun 8
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. José de Anchieta, the Jesuit missionary known as the Apostle of Brazil.

Uma novena de nove dias a São José de Anchieta, o missionário jesuíta conhecido como o Apóstolo do Brasil.

St. José de Anchieta (1534–1597), born in Tenerife, entered the Society of Jesus and sailed to Brazil in 1553. With Manuel da Nóbrega he helped found the mission of São Paulo de Piratininga and later Rio de Janeiro, learning the Tupi language and writing its first grammar. A poet and tireless catechist of the indigenous peoples, he was canonized by Pope Francis in 2014.

São José de Anchieta (1534–1597), nascido em Tenerife, entrou na Companhia de Jesus e partiu para o Brasil em 1553. Com Manuel da Nóbrega ajudou a fundar a missão de São Paulo de Piratininga e, mais tarde, o Rio de Janeiro, aprendendo a língua tupi e escrevendo sua primeira gramática. Poeta e incansável catequista dos povos indígenas, foi canonizado pelo Papa Francisco em 2014.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O holy José de Anchieta, missionary who carried Christ across the seas to a new land, look upon me as I begin this novena. Win for me your zeal for souls and your tender love for Our Lady, that I may labor for God wherever He sends me. Amen.

Ó santo José de Anchieta, missionário que levastes Cristo além dos mares a uma terra nova, olhai para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me o vosso zelo pelas almas e o vosso terno amor a Nossa Senhora, para que eu trabalhe por Deus onde quer que Ele me envie. Amém.

Day 1: The call across the sea

Dia 1: O chamado além-mar

Still a young Jesuit, José de Anchieta left the Canary Islands and crossed the ocean to a land he had never seen, trusting wholly in God. He teaches us that the Lord's call may lead far from home, and that love makes the journey light.

Ainda jovem jesuíta, José de Anchieta deixou as Ilhas Canárias e atravessou o oceano rumo a uma terra que nunca vira, confiando inteiramente em Deus. Ele nos ensina que o chamado do Senhor pode levar para longe de casa, e que o amor torna leve a jornada.

Intention

For courage to follow God's call wherever it leads.

Pela coragem de seguir o chamado de Deus aonde quer que ele conduza.

Day 2: Founder of São Paulo

Dia 2: Fundador de São Paulo

In 1554 Anchieta helped raise the mission school of São Paulo de Piratininga, laying the spiritual foundation of a future city. He shows us that great works of God often begin in poverty, in a humble hut on a high plain.

Em 1554 Anchieta ajudou a erguer o colégio missionário de São Paulo de Piratininga, lançando o alicerce espiritual de uma futura cidade. Ele nos mostra que as grandes obras de Deus costumam começar na pobreza, numa humilde choça sobre o planalto.

Intention

For all who build the Church in poor and forgotten places.

Por todos os que edificam a Igreja em lugares pobres e esquecidos.

Day 3: Learning the people's tongue

Dia 3: Aprender a língua do povo

Anchieta mastered the Tupi language and wrote its first grammar so the Gospel could be preached in the people's own words. He teaches us that true love takes the trouble to understand others as they are.

Anchieta dominou a língua tupi e escreveu sua primeira gramática para que o Evangelho fosse pregado nas próprias palavras do povo. Ele nos ensina que o verdadeiro amor se dá ao trabalho de compreender os outros como eles são.

Intention

For missionaries who labor to share the faith in every culture.

Pelos missionários que se esforçam por partilhar a fé em toda cultura.

Day 4: The poem in the sand

Dia 4: O poema na areia

Held as a hostage among the Tamoio, Anchieta composed a long Latin poem in praise of the Blessed Virgin, writing the verses each morning on the wet sand of the shore. He shows us that devotion to Mary can flower even in captivity and fear.

Mantido como refém entre os tamoios, Anchieta compôs um longo poema latino em louvor à Virgem Maria, escrevendo os versos a cada manhã na areia molhada da praia. Ele nos mostra que a devoção a Maria pode florescer mesmo no cativeiro e no medo.

Intention

For a deeper and more constant love for Our Lady.

Por um amor mais profundo e constante a Nossa Senhora.

Day 5: Peacemaker among nations

Dia 5: Pacificador entre os povos

During the conflict with the Tamoio, Anchieta risked his life as a peacemaker, offering himself for the safety of others. He teaches us that the servant of Christ is sent to reconcile, not to conquer.

Durante o conflito com os tamoios, Anchieta arriscou a vida como pacificador, oferecendo-se pela segurança dos demais. Ele nos ensina que o servo de Cristo é enviado para reconciliar, não para conquistar.

Intention

For peace among peoples divided by enmity.

Pela paz entre os povos divididos pela inimizade.

Day 6: Healer and comforter

Dia 6: Curador e consolador

Anchieta tended the sick with his own hands and was credited with many wonders of healing among the native peoples. He reminds us that the love of God is shown as much in care for the body as in care for the soul.

Anchieta cuidava dos doentes com as próprias mãos e a ele se atribuíram muitos prodígios de cura entre os indígenas. Ele nos recorda que o amor de Deus se manifesta tanto no cuidado do corpo quanto no cuidado da alma.

Intention

For the sick and for those who care for them.

Pelos doentes e por aqueles que deles cuidam.

Day 7: Teacher and dramatist

Dia 7: Mestre e dramaturgo

To teach the faith, Anchieta wrote songs, catechisms, and sacred plays performed in Tupi and Portuguese. He shows us that the truths of God may be taught with beauty, and that art can be put wholly at His service.

Para ensinar a fé, Anchieta escreveu cânticos, catecismos e autos sacros encenados em tupi e português. Ele nos mostra que as verdades de Deus podem ser ensinadas com beleza, e que a arte pode ser posta inteiramente a Seu serviço.

Intention

For teachers and for all who serve God through their gifts.

Pelos professores e por todos os que servem a Deus com seus dons.

Day 8: A life poured out

Dia 8: Uma vida derramada

For more than forty years Anchieta walked thousands of miles, often barefoot and ailing, never sparing himself for the souls entrusted to him. He teaches us that holiness is the patient gift of self, spent day after day.

Por mais de quarenta anos Anchieta percorreu milhares de léguas, muitas vezes descalço e enfermo, sem nunca se poupar pelas almas a ele confiadas. Ele nos ensina que a santidade é a doação paciente de si, gasta dia após dia.

Intention

For perseverance in serving others without counting the cost.

Pela perseverança em servir os outros sem medir o custo.

Day 9: Apostle of Brazil

Dia 9: Apóstolo do Brasil

At his death the people mourned the loss of a father, and the Church now honors him as the Apostle of Brazil. He assures us that a life given wholly to God leaves a harvest that endures for centuries.

Em sua morte o povo chorou a perda de um pai, e a Igreja agora o honra como o Apóstolo do Brasil. Ele nos assegura que uma vida inteiramente entregue a Deus deixa uma colheita que perdura por séculos.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. José de Anchieta.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São José de Anchieta.

Closing prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, Apostle of Brazil, present my petitions to the Lord for whom you spent your whole life. Teach me to love those of every tongue and people, and to seek God's glory above my own comfort, until I praise Him with you forever. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, Apóstolo do Brasil, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor por quem gastastes toda a vossa vida. Ensinai-me a amar os homens de toda língua e povo e a buscar a glória de Deus acima do meu conforto, até louvá-Lo convosco para sempre. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

St. José de Anchieta (the Jesuit "Apostle of Brazil," canonized 2014) has no English-language novena on FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas, so there is no comparison text. Ember's novena is an original composition that hews closely to the well-documented facts of his life: the voyage from the Canary Islands, the 1554 founding of São Paulo de Piratininga, his Tupi grammar, the Marian Latin poem composed on the sand during his Tamoio captivity, his peacemaking, healing, sacred plays, and the title Apostle of Brazil. The biography is accurate and the themes are devotionally apt; as an original work there is nothing to diverge from.

St. Aloysius Gonzaga st-aloysius-gonzaga-novena

Jun 12–20
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Aloysius Gonzaga, the angelic Jesuit youth and patron of the young.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Luís Gonzaga, o angélico jovem jesuíta e padroeiro da juventude.

St. Aloysius Gonzaga (1568–1591) was born to a noble Italian family destined for the court, yet from boyhood he gave himself wholly to God in prayer and purity. Renouncing his inheritance, he entered the Society of Jesus over his father's resistance. While caring for the sick in a plague hospital in Rome, he contracted the disease and died at twenty-three; he is the patron of youth and students.

São Luís Gonzaga (1568–1591) nasceu numa nobre família italiana destinada à corte, mas desde menino entregou-se inteiramente a Deus na oração e na pureza. Renunciando à herança, entrou na Companhia de Jesus apesar da resistência do pai. Cuidando dos doentes num hospital de peste em Roma, contraiu a doença e morreu aos vinte e três anos; é o padroeiro da juventude e dos estudantes.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O angelic St. Aloysius, who in your youth loved God with an undivided heart, look upon me as I begin this novena. Win for me purity of mind and body, and a love of God that nothing in this world can dim. Amen.

Ó angélico São Luís, que na juventude amastes a Deus com coração indiviso, olhai para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me pureza de mente e de corpo e um amor de Deus que nada neste mundo possa apagar. Amém.

Day 1: Early love of God

Dia 1: Amor precoce a Deus

While still a small child Aloysius began to seek God, and he made a private vow of perpetual chastity at the age of nine. He teaches us that the young heart is not too small to give itself wholly to the Lord.

Ainda criança pequena, Luís começou a buscar a Deus, e fez voto particular de castidade perpétua aos nove anos. Ele nos ensina que o coração jovem não é pequeno demais para se entregar inteiramente ao Senhor.

Intention

For all children, that they may know and love God early.

Por todas as crianças, para que conheçam e amem a Deus desde cedo.

Day 2: Purity of heart

Dia 2: Pureza de coração

Aloysius guarded his eyes and senses so faithfully that his innocence shone like that of an angel. He shows us that purity is not coldness but a heart kept undivided and free for God.

Luís guardava os olhos e os sentidos com tanta fidelidade que sua inocência brilhava como a de um anjo. Ele nos mostra que a pureza não é frieza, mas um coração mantido indiviso e livre para Deus.

Intention

For the grace of purity in thought, word, and deed.

Pela graça da pureza no pensamento, na palavra e na ação.

Day 3: Renouncing nobility

Dia 3: Renúncia à nobreza

Heir to a marquisate, Aloysius signed away his title and lands to his brother to follow Christ in poverty. He teaches us that no inheritance on earth is worth the loss of the kingdom of heaven.

Herdeiro de um marquesado, Luís abriu mão do título e das terras em favor do irmão para seguir Cristo na pobreza. Ele nos ensina que nenhuma herança na terra vale a perda do reino dos céus.

Intention

For detachment from riches and worldly ambition.

Pelo desapego das riquezas e da ambição mundana.

Day 4: Obedience over his father's will

Dia 4: Obediência apesar da vontade do pai

For years Aloysius patiently bore his father's anger and schemes to keep him from the Jesuits, never yielding his resolve. He shows us that we may honor our parents and still obey God's higher claim with steadfast love.

Por anos Luís suportou com paciência a ira e as artimanhas do pai para afastá-lo dos jesuítas, sem nunca ceder em seu propósito. Ele nos mostra que podemos honrar os pais e ainda assim obedecer ao chamado superior de Deus com amor firme.

Intention

For young people seeking to follow their vocation faithfully.

Pelos jovens que buscam seguir fielmente a sua vocação.

Day 5: A life of penance

Dia 5: Uma vida de penitência

Aloysius embraced fasting, long prayer, and hard mortification, so that his superiors had to restrain his fervor. He reminds us that love for God presses the soul to give, not to spare itself.

Luís abraçou o jejum, a oração prolongada e a mortificação severa, a ponto de seus superiores terem de moderar seu fervor. Ele nos recorda que o amor a Deus impele a alma a dar-se, não a poupar-se.

Intention

For the grace to deny myself for love of God.

Pela graça de renunciar a mim mesmo por amor a Deus.

Day 6: Devotion to the Eucharist

Dia 6: Devoção à Eucaristia

Aloysius prepared for Holy Communion with three days of thanksgiving and three of longing, so great was his love for the Blessed Sacrament. He teaches us to approach the altar with awe and burning desire.

Luís preparava-se para a Sagrada Comunhão com três dias de ação de graças e três de desejo, tão grande era seu amor ao Santíssimo Sacramento. Ele nos ensina a aproximar-nos do altar com reverência e ardente desejo.

Intention

For a deeper reverence and love for the Holy Eucharist.

Por uma reverência e um amor mais profundos à Santa Eucaristia.

Day 7: Serving the plague-stricken

Dia 7: Servir os atingidos pela peste

When plague swept through Rome, Aloysius begged to nurse the sick, carrying the dying on his own shoulders to the hospital. He shows us that charity counts no danger too great when a neighbor is in need.

Quando a peste assolou Roma, Luís suplicou para cuidar dos doentes, carregando os moribundos nos próprios ombros até o hospital. Ele nos mostra que a caridade não conta perigo algum como grande demais diante da necessidade do próximo.

Intention

For generous service to the sick and the suffering.

Pelo serviço generoso aos doentes e aos que sofrem.

Day 8: Joyful acceptance of death

Dia 8: Aceitação alegre da morte

Stricken by the very disease he tended, Aloysius welcomed his early death with peace, certain he was going to God. He teaches us to meet our last hour without fear, as the gateway to the One we have loved.

Atingido pela mesma doença que tratava, Luís acolheu sua morte precoce com paz, certo de que ia para Deus. Ele nos ensina a enfrentar a última hora sem medo, como a porta para Aquele que amamos.

Intention

For the grace of a holy and peaceful death.

Pela graça de uma morte santa e serena.

Day 9: Patron of youth

Dia 9: Padroeiro da juventude

In only twenty-three years Aloysius reached a holiness the Church now holds up to every young person. He assures us that sanctity is not measured by length of days but by the fullness of love poured out.

Em apenas vinte e três anos Luís alcançou uma santidade que a Igreja hoje propõe a todo jovem. Ele nos assegura que a santidade não se mede pela duração dos dias, mas pela plenitude do amor derramado.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. Aloysius.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São Luís Gonzaga.

Closing prayer

O St. Aloysius Gonzaga, patron of the young, present my petitions to the Lord you served so faithfully. Help me to flee what offends God and to spend my strength in His service, that with you I may one day behold His face. Amen.

Ó São Luís Gonzaga, padroeiro da juventude, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor que servistes com tanta fidelidade. Ajudai-me a fugir do que ofende a Deus e a gastar minhas forças em Seu serviço, para que convosco eu contemple um dia o Seu rosto. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a St. Aloysius Gonzaga novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-aloysius-gonzaga-novena). Each day opens with a Trinitarian blessing and a thanksgiving for St. Aloysius as an example, gives a daily meditation on his life and a "Pray for me..." petition, space for personal intentions, and closes "St. Aloysius Gonzaga, pray for us!" with a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes: Day 1 beginning holiness in youth (his turn to prayer in illness); Day 2 discerning God's will (drawn by Jesuit missionaries); Day 3 radical religious commitment despite family opposition; Day 4 renouncing his worldly inheritance; Day 5 persevering through opposition; Day 6 service through personal suffering / chronic illness; Day 7 courage in caring for plague victims; Day 8 patient endurance of suffering; Day 9 faithful service unto death.

Fidelity note

Ember's text is original and shares no wording with PrayMoreNovenas, but the two cover the same biography of the young Jesuit and overlap heavily in theme: youthful holiness, renouncing nobility, persevering against family opposition, serving the plague-stricken, and a holy early death. Ember adds emphases the source treats only lightly — purity/chastity (his vow at nine), penance, and Eucharistic devotion — which are central to Aloysius's traditional cult, so they strengthen rather than distort the fidelity. A faithful, well-grounded original composition closely parallel to the source in structure and substance.

St. Thomas More st-thomas-more-novena

Jun 13–21
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Thomas More, martyr for conscience and patron of statesmen and lawyers.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Tomás Moro, mártir da consciência e padroeiro dos estadistas e advogados.

St. Thomas More (1478–1535) was a brilliant lawyer, scholar, and devoted family man who rose to become Lord Chancellor of England under Henry VIII. When the king demanded he approve the royal supremacy over the Church, More refused, resigned his office, and was imprisoned in the Tower. He went to the scaffold with serene humor, declaring himself 'the King's good servant, but God's first,' and was beheaded for his fidelity to conscience and the Church.

São Tomás Moro (1478–1535) foi um brilhante advogado, erudito e dedicado pai de família que chegou a Lorde Chanceler da Inglaterra sob Henrique VIII. Quando o rei exigiu que aprovasse a supremacia real sobre a Igreja, Moro recusou, renunciou ao cargo e foi preso na Torre. Subiu ao cadafalso com serenidade e bom humor, declarando-se 'o bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus', e foi decapitado por sua fidelidade à consciência e à Igreja.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O steadfast St. Thomas More, who held fast to God's law amid the favor and threats of kings, look upon me as I begin this novena. Win for me a clear conscience and the courage to keep it, whatever the cost. Amen.

Ó firme São Tomás Moro, que vos mantivestes fiel à lei de Deus em meio ao favor e às ameaças dos reis, olhai para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me uma consciência reta e a coragem de mantê-la, custe o que custar. Amém.

Day 1: A holy life in the world

Dia 1: Vida santa no mundo

Thomas More once considered the cloister but discerned his call to serve God as a layman, husband, and public servant. He teaches us that the path to holiness runs through the ordinary duties of family and work.

Tomás Moro chegou a considerar o claustro, mas discerniu seu chamado a servir a Deus como leigo, esposo e servidor público. Ele nos ensina que o caminho da santidade passa pelos deveres comuns da família e do trabalho.

Intention

For the grace to seek holiness in my daily state of life.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade no meu estado de vida cotidiano.

Day 2: A man of prayer

Dia 2: Homem de oração

Amid the burdens of high office, More rose early for prayer and Scripture, wearing a hidden hair shirt beneath his fine robes. He shows us that the busiest life must be rooted in daily, hidden union with God.

Em meio aos encargos de um alto cargo, Moro levantava-se cedo para a oração e a Escritura, usando um cilício oculto sob suas finas vestes. Ele nos mostra que a vida mais ocupada deve enraizar-se na união diária e oculta com Deus.

Intention

For fidelity to daily prayer amid a busy life.

Pela fidelidade à oração diária em meio à vida atarefada.

Day 3: Devoted husband and father

Dia 3: Esposo e pai dedicado

More made his home a school of learning and faith, educating his daughters with the same care as his son. He teaches us that love of God begins in the patient, joyful love of one's own household.

Moro fez de seu lar uma escola de cultura e de fé, educando as filhas com o mesmo zelo que o filho. Ele nos ensina que o amor a Deus começa no amor paciente e alegre da própria família.

Intention

For Christian families and the education of children in the faith.

Pelas famílias cristãs e pela educação dos filhos na fé.

Day 4: Integrity in public office

Dia 4: Integridade no cargo público

As Lord Chancellor, More judged justly and refused the bribes that corrupted so many around him. He shows us that authority is a trust from God, to be exercised with honesty and the fear of the Lord.

Como Lorde Chanceler, Moro julgava com justiça e recusava os subornos que corrompiam tantos à sua volta. Ele nos mostra que a autoridade é um encargo confiado por Deus, a ser exercido com honestidade e temor do Senhor.

Intention

For honesty and justice among those who hold public office.

Pela honestidade e justiça entre os que exercem cargos públicos.

Day 5: The voice of conscience

Dia 5: A voz da consciência

When asked to swear against the truth, More chose silence and then refusal, unwilling to damn his soul to please a king. He teaches us that conscience formed by God's truth must never be sold, even under threat.

Quando lhe pediram que jurasse contra a verdade, Moro escolheu o silêncio e depois a recusa, não querendo condenar a alma para agradar a um rei. Ele nos ensina que a consciência formada pela verdade de Deus jamais deve ser vendida, mesmo sob ameaça.

Intention

For the courage to follow my conscience faithfully.

Pela coragem de seguir fielmente a minha consciência.

Day 6: Loss of all things

Dia 6: A perda de todas as coisas

More gave up his office, his wealth, and his liberty rather than betray the Church, leaving his beloved family to want. He shows us that no honor or comfort is worth purchasing at the price of faith.

Moro abriu mão do cargo, da riqueza e da liberdade antes de trair a Igreja, deixando a amada família na necessidade. Ele nos mostra que nenhuma honra ou conforto vale ser comprado ao preço da fé.

Intention

For detachment from honor, security, and human respect.

Pelo desapego da honra, da segurança e do respeito humano.

Day 7: Patience in prison

Dia 7: Paciência na prisão

During fifteen months in the Tower of London, More wrote tender letters and treatises on the Passion, finding consolation in Christ's own suffering. He reminds us that even a prison can become a place of peace when offered to God.

Durante quinze meses na Torre de Londres, Moro escreveu cartas ternas e tratados sobre a Paixão, encontrando consolo no próprio sofrimento de Cristo. Ele nos recorda que até uma prisão pode tornar-se lugar de paz quando oferecida a Deus.

Intention

For all who are imprisoned or persecuted for the faith.

Por todos os que estão presos ou perseguidos por causa da fé.

Day 8: Humor on the scaffold

Dia 8: Bom humor no cadafalso

Climbing the unsteady scaffold, More jested with his guard and asked help going up, promising to see to his own coming down. He teaches us that a soul anchored in God can meet even death with serenity and good cheer.

Subindo o cadafalso instável, Moro brincou com o guarda e pediu ajuda para subir, prometendo cuidar sozinho da descida. Ele nos ensina que uma alma ancorada em Deus pode enfrentar até a morte com serenidade e bom ânimo.

Intention

For peace and trust in the face of suffering and death.

Pela paz e confiança diante do sofrimento e da morte.

Day 9: The King's good servant, but God's first

Dia 9: Bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus

At the block More declared himself the king's good servant but God's first, then forgave his executioner and died in faith. He assures us that fidelity to God, though it cost everything, is never truly defeated.

No cepo, Moro declarou-se bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus, depois perdoou o carrasco e morreu na fé. Ele nos assegura que a fidelidade a Deus, ainda que custe tudo, nunca é verdadeiramente derrotada.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. Thomas More.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São Tomás Moro.

Closing prayer

O St. Thomas More, good servant of your king but first of God, present my petitions to the Lord for whom you laid down your life. Teach me to serve faithfully in the world without surrendering my soul, until I share your joy in heaven. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, bom servo do vosso rei, mas primeiro de Deus, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor por quem destes a vida. Ensinai-me a servir fielmente no mundo sem entregar a minha alma, até partilhar convosco a alegria do céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="thomasmore">
Novena to St.
Thomas More

Say once a day for nine days, especially starting on 30
June and ending on 8 July, the eve of the feast of St. Thomas More

First Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
prudence. You never thrust yourself rashly into any serious
undertaking; instead, you tested the strength of your powers and waited
on God's will in prayer and penance, then boldly carried it out without
hesitation. Through your prayers and intercession, obtain for me the
virtues of patience, prudence, wisdom and courage.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria

Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here) .

V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Second Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
diligence. You shunned procrastination, applied yourself with fervor to
your studies, and spared no effort in achieving mastery in any skill.
Through your prayers and intercession, obtain for me the virtue of
diligence and persistence in my preparations for all undertakings.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria

Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here) .

V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Third Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
industriousness. You threw yourself wholeheartedly into everything you
did, and you found enjoyment even in the most serious things. Through
your prayers and intercession, obtain for me the grace of always having
suitable employment, the grace to find interest in everything fitting,
and the fortitude always to pursue excellence in whatever task God
gives me to do.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria

Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here) .

V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Fourth Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a brilliant lawyer
and a just and compassionate judge. You attended to the smallest
details of your legal duties with the greatest care, and you were
unflagging in your pursuit of justice tempered by mercy. Through your
prayers and intercession, obtain for me the grace to overcome every
temptation to laxity, arrogance, and rash judgment in my duties.
Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria
Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here).
V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Fifth Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
humility. You never allowed pride to lead you to take on enterprises
beyond your abilities; even in th

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a St. Thomas More novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-thomas-more-novena). Each day opens with a Trinitarian blessing, thanks God for an aspect of More's witness, and adds a petition plus space for personal intentions, closing "through Christ Our Lord. Amen." with a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes: Day 1 holiness in everyday lay life; Day 2 submission to God's will (his discernment to serve God in the world); Day 3 virtue in professional life (lawyer/Parliament, resisting corruption); Day 4 holiness in family life; Day 5 integrity and honesty; Day 6 the sanctity of marriage (refusing the king's unlawful marriage); Day 7 fortitude; Day 8 courage and commitment to the Church / papal authority; Day 9 joy in suffering (his humor before execution).

Fidelity note

Ember's prayers are an original composition with no shared wording, yet they parallel the PrayMoreNovenas novena almost theme-for-theme: lay holiness, prayer/discernment, family life, integrity in office, conscience, fortitude/loss, fidelity to the Church, and joy/humor in death. Ember's Day 5–6 ("the voice of conscience," "loss of all things") and Day 8 ("humor on the scaffold") closely echo the source's conscience, sacrifice, and "joy in suffering" days. The biography (hair shirt, Tower imprisonment, "the king's good servant but God's first") is accurate. A faithful, closely parallel original; the FishEaters version could not be verified.

St. John the Baptist st-john-baptist-novena

Jun 15–23
description & history

Nine days with the Forerunner of Christ, who pointed to the Lamb of God and gave his life for the truth.

Nove dias com o Precursor de Cristo, que apontou para o Cordeiro de Deus e deu a vida pela verdade.

St. John the Baptist was the cousin and forerunner of Jesus, leaping in his mother's womb at the Visitation and later preaching repentance along the Jordan. He baptized the Lord, declared 'Behold the Lamb of God,' and was beheaded by Herod for condemning an unlawful marriage. This novena draws on his courage, humility, and burning zeal for God.

São João Batista foi primo e precursor de Jesus, exultando no ventre de sua mãe na Visitação e mais tarde pregando a penitência junto ao Jordão. Batizou o Senhor, proclamou 'Eis o Cordeiro de Deus' e foi decapitado por Herodes por condenar um casamento ilícito. Esta novena se inspira em sua coragem, humildade e ardente zelo por Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. John the Baptist, voice crying in the wilderness, you prepared the way of the Lord with fearless preaching and a pure heart. As I begin this novena, obtain for me a spirit of repentance and a love of the truth. Help me to point others always to Christ. Amen.

Glorioso São João Batista, voz que clama no deserto, vós preparastes o caminho do Senhor com pregação destemida e coração puro. Ao iniciar esta novena, alcançai-me um espírito de penitência e o amor da verdade. Ajudai-me a apontar sempre os outros para Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: The voice in the wilderness

Dia 1: A voz no deserto

St. John went out into the desert, eating locusts and wild honey, that nothing might dull his hearing of God. He teaches us that the soul must be emptied of noise before it can speak God's word.

São João retirou-se para o deserto, alimentando-se de gafanhotos e mel silvestre, para que nada embotasse sua escuta de Deus. Ele nos ensina que a alma precisa esvaziar-se do ruído antes de poder anunciar a palavra de Deus.

Intention

For the grace of silence and solitude in which to hear God's voice.

Pela graça do silêncio e da solidão em que se possa ouvir a voz de Deus.

Day 2: Preparing the way of the Lord

Dia 2: Preparar o caminho do Senhor

He called the people to make straight the paths, leveling the high places of pride and filling the valleys of despair. His whole life was to clear the road by which the Savior would come.

Ele chamava o povo a endireitar as veredas, abaixando os altos da soberba e enchendo os vales do desânimo. Sua vida inteira foi abrir o caminho por onde viria o Salvador.

Intention

That I may remove from my heart whatever blocks the coming of Christ.

Para que eu remova de meu coração tudo o que impede a vinda de Cristo.

Day 3: A call to repentance

Dia 3: O chamado à penitência

John baptized in the Jordan, urging hearts to turn from sin and bear worthy fruit. He reminds us that conversion is not a single moment but a daily turning back to God.

João batizava no Jordão, exortando os corações a abandonarem o pecado e a darem frutos dignos. Ele nos recorda que a conversão não é um só instante, mas um retorno diário a Deus.

Intention

For true contrition and a steadfast purpose of amendment.

Por uma contrição verdadeira e um firme propósito de emenda.

Day 4: Behold the Lamb of God

Dia 4: Eis o Cordeiro de Deus

Seeing Jesus approach, John cried, 'Behold the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world.' His joy was to disappear behind the One he announced.

Vendo Jesus aproximar-se, João exclamou: 'Eis o Cordeiro de Deus, que tira o pecado do mundo.' Sua alegria era desaparecer atrás d'Aquele que anunciava.

Intention

That I may recognize Christ present in the Eucharist and in the poor.

Para que eu reconheça Cristo presente na Eucaristia e nos pobres.

Day 5: He must increase, I must decrease

Dia 5: Importa que Ele cresça e eu diminua

When his own disciples grew jealous of Jesus, John answered serenely, 'He must increase, but I must decrease.' He shows us the freedom of the soul that desires only God's glory.

Quando seus próprios discípulos se inquietaram diante de Jesus, João respondeu sereno: 'Importa que Ele cresça e eu diminua.' Ele nos mostra a liberdade da alma que deseja apenas a glória de Deus.

Intention

For deliverance from vanity and the desire to be seen.

Pela libertação da vaidade e do desejo de aparecer.

Day 6: Humility before Christ

Dia 6: Humildade diante de Cristo

John confessed he was unworthy to loosen the strap of the Lord's sandal, though crowds came to him as a prophet. True greatness, he teaches, knows its place before God.

João confessou-se indigno de desatar a correia da sandália do Senhor, ainda que as multidões viessem a ele como a um profeta. A verdadeira grandeza, ele ensina, conhece seu lugar diante de Deus.

Intention

For a humble heart that takes the lowest place gladly.

Por um coração humilde que de bom grado tome o último lugar.

Day 7: Courage to speak the truth

Dia 7: Coragem para dizer a verdade

John did not flatter the powerful, but told Herod plainly that it was unlawful to take his brother's wife. He teaches that charity sometimes requires the courage to wound in order to heal.

João não adulou os poderosos, mas disse a Herodes claramente que não lhe era lícito tomar a mulher de seu irmão. Ele ensina que a caridade às vezes exige a coragem de ferir para curar.

Intention

For the courage to speak the truth with charity and without fear.

Pela coragem de dizer a verdade com caridade e sem temor.

Day 8: Faithful in prison

Dia 8: Fiel na prisão

Cast into Herod's dungeon, John sent his disciples to Jesus and waited in the darkness without bitterness. He teaches us to hold fast to faith even when God's purposes seem hidden.

Lançado no cárcere de Herodes, João enviou seus discípulos a Jesus e esperou nas trevas sem amargura. Ele nos ensina a permanecer firmes na fé mesmo quando os desígnios de Deus parecem ocultos.

Intention

For perseverance in trials and trust when God seems distant.

Pela perseverança nas provações e pela confiança quando Deus parece distante.

Day 9: Martyr for the truth

Dia 9: Mártir da verdade

John was beheaded for the sake of righteousness, sealing his witness with his blood. The Forerunner who prepared the way in life now leads the way of the martyrs to the throne of God.

João foi decapitado por causa da justiça, selando com o sangue o seu testemunho. O Precursor que preparou o caminho em vida agora conduz o caminho dos mártires até o trono de Deus.

Intention

For the grace of final perseverance and fidelity unto death.

Pela graça da perseverança final e da fidelidade até a morte.

Closing prayer

St. John the Baptist, who decreased that Christ might increase, teach me to seek not my own glory but His. Strengthen me to bear witness to the truth even at great cost, and to await the Lamb of God with a watchful heart. Intercede for me, that I may share one day in your joy. Amen.

São João Batista, que diminuístes para que Cristo crescesse, ensinai-me a buscar não a minha glória, mas a d'Ele. Fortalecei-me para testemunhar a verdade ainda que a grande custo, e a esperar o Cordeiro de Deus com coração vigilante. Intercedei por mim, para que um dia eu partilhe de vossa alegria. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="baptist">
Novena
to St. John the Baptist

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on
June 15 and ending on June 23, the eve of the Feast of St. John.

O glorious St. John the Baptist, of those born of women the greatest of
prophets; thou, though sanctified from thy mothers womb and most
innocent, didst nevertheless will to retire into the desert, there to
practise austerities and penance; obtain for us from our Lord the grace
to be detached, at least in the affections of our hearts, from all
earthly treasure, and to practise Christian mortification with interior
recollection and a spirit of holy prayer.

Pray one Pater, one Ave,
and one Gloria

O most zealous Apostle, who, without working any miracle on others, but
solely by the example of thy life of penance and the power of thy
words, didst draw after thee crowds, in order to prepare them to
worthily receive the Messiah, and listen to his heavenly teaching;
grant that it may be given to us also, by the example of a holy life
and the practice of good works, to lead many souls to God, and, above
all, those who have been enveloped in the darkness of error and
ignorance, and led astray by vice.

Pray one Pater, one Ave,
and one Gloria

Unconquered martyr, who for the honour of God and the salvation of
souls, didst, with heroic constancy, and at the cost of life itself,
with stand the impious Herod, reproving him openly for his bad

and dissolute life ; obtain for us a brave and generous heart, so that,
overcoming all human respect, we may boldly profess our faith, and
follow the teaching of our divine master Jesus Christ.

Pray one Pater, one Ave,
and one Gloria

V. Pray for us, St John the Baptist.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Let us pray. O God, Who hast made this day worthy of honour in the
nativity of St John, grant to Thy people the grace of spiritual joys,
and direct the minds of all the faithful into the way of eternal
salvation. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a Saint John the Baptist novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/saint-john-the-baptist-novena), Trinitarian opening, a unique daily meditation and intention, a closing petition ("Lord Jesus, Your cousin John prepared the way for Your coming..."), "St. John the Baptist, pray for us!", and a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes there lean on the infancy narrative and ministry: Day 1 miraculous conception; Day 2 recognition of Christ in the womb / protecting the unborn; Day 3 his naming and Zechariah's restored speech; Day 4 desert preparation and repentance; Day 5 Jesus's baptism and John's humility; Day 6 "Behold the Lamb of God"; Day 7 the forerunner / humble rejoicing in God's glory; Day 8 repeats Day 7; Day 9 the greatest prophet.

Fidelity note

Ember's text is an original composition, not a copy of either source. It is closest to the FishEaters novena in spirit — both stress the desert/penance, fearless rebuke of Herod, and martyrdom — though the wording is entirely Ember's and does not reproduce FishEaters' archaic "thou/didst" prayers. Against PrayMoreNovenas the structural match is strong (fixed opening + unique daily meditation + intention), but the day themes diverge: PrayMoreNovenas weights the infancy narrative, whereas Ember organizes around John's mission and Gospel sayings ("make straight the paths," "Behold the Lamb of God," "He must increase, I must decrease"). All meditations are scripturally accurate; a faithful, well-grounded original.

SS. Peter and Paul ss-peter-and-paul-novena

Jun 20–28
description & history

Nine days with the two great apostles, Peter the rock and Paul the teacher of the nations, pillars of the Church of Rome.

Nove dias com os dois grandes apóstolos, Pedro a pedra e Paulo o doutor das nações, colunas da Igreja de Roma.

St. Peter, a Galilean fisherman, was named by Christ the rock on which He would build His Church and given the keys of the kingdom. St. Paul, once a persecutor, was struck down on the road to Damascus and became the tireless apostle to the gentiles. Both crowned their labors with martyrdom in Rome and are honored together on June 29.

São Pedro, pescador da Galileia, foi chamado por Cristo de pedra sobre a qual edificaria a sua Igreja, recebendo as chaves do reino. São Paulo, outrora perseguidor, foi prostrado no caminho de Damasco e tornou-se o incansável apóstolo dos gentios. Ambos coroaram seus trabalhos com o martírio em Roma e são honrados juntos no dia 29 de junho.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Holy Apostles Peter and Paul, chosen pillars of Christ's Church, you laid down your lives in Rome for the faith you proclaimed. As I begin this novena, obtain for me a faith as firm as Peter's and a zeal as burning as Paul's. Watch over the Church you founded with your blood. Amen.

Santos Apóstolos Pedro e Paulo, colunas escolhidas da Igreja de Cristo, vós destes a vida em Roma pela fé que anunciastes. Ao iniciar esta novena, alcançai-me uma fé tão firme quanto a de Pedro e um zelo tão ardente quanto o de Paulo. Velai pela Igreja que fundastes com vosso sangue. Amém.

Day 1: Called from the nets

Dia 1: Chamado das redes

Peter left his boat at once when Jesus said, 'Follow me, and I will make you a fisher of men.' He teaches us to answer the Lord's call without delay or condition.

Pedro deixou de imediato a sua barca quando Jesus disse: 'Segue-me, e te farei pescador de homens.' Ele nos ensina a responder ao chamado do Senhor sem demora nem condição.

Intention

For the grace to follow Christ promptly wherever He leads.

Pela graça de seguir Cristo prontamente para onde quer que Ele conduza.

Day 2: Thou art the rock

Dia 2: Tu és a pedra

When Peter confessed Jesus as the Christ, the Son of the living God, the Lord named him the rock and gave him the keys of the kingdom. On this confession the unity of the Church still rests.

Quando Pedro confessou que Jesus é o Cristo, o Filho de Deus vivo, o Senhor o chamou de pedra e lhe entregou as chaves do reino. Sobre essa confissão repousa ainda a unidade da Igreja.

Intention

For unity in the Church around the successor of Peter.

Pela unidade da Igreja em torno do sucessor de Pedro.

Day 3: Peter's tears

Dia 3: As lágrimas de Pedro

Peter denied his Master three times, yet wept bitterly and was restored when the risen Lord asked, 'Do you love me?' He teaches us that no fall is final for the soul that loves and repents.

Pedro negou três vezes o seu Mestre, mas chorou amargamente e foi reerguido quando o Senhor ressuscitado lhe perguntou: 'Tu me amas?' Ele nos ensina que nenhuma queda é definitiva para a alma que ama e se arrepende.

Intention

For the grace of tears over my sins and confidence in God's mercy.

Pela graça das lágrimas pelos meus pecados e pela confiança na misericórdia de Deus.

Day 4: Paul on the Damascus road

Dia 4: Paulo no caminho de Damasco

Saul, breathing threats against the disciples, was thrown to the ground by a light from heaven and heard, 'Why do you persecute me?' In an instant the persecutor became the apostle, proof that grace can master any heart.

Saulo, que respirava ameaças contra os discípulos, foi lançado por terra por uma luz do céu e ouviu: 'Por que me persegues?' Num instante o perseguidor tornou-se apóstolo, prova de que a graça pode dominar qualquer coração.

Intention

For the conversion of sinners and of those who oppose the faith.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e dos que se opõem à fé.

Day 5: Apostle to the gentiles

Dia 5: Apóstolo dos gentios

Paul carried the Gospel across sea and land, becoming all things to all men that he might win some for Christ. His tireless travels remind us that the faith is meant for every nation.

Paulo levou o Evangelho por mar e por terra, fazendo-se tudo para todos a fim de ganhar alguns para Cristo. Suas viagens incansáveis nos recordam que a fé é destinada a todas as nações.

Intention

For missionaries and the spread of the Gospel to all peoples.

Pelos missionários e pela difusão do Evangelho a todos os povos.

Day 6: Strength in weakness

Dia 6: Força na fraqueza

Paul boasted of his weaknesses, for the Lord told him, 'My grace is sufficient for you; my power is made perfect in weakness.' He teaches that God works most when we rely least on ourselves.

Paulo gloriava-se de suas fraquezas, pois o Senhor lhe dissera: 'Basta-te a minha graça; a força se aperfeiçoa na fraqueza.' Ele ensina que Deus age mais quando menos confiamos em nós mesmos.

Intention

That I may trust God's grace in my own weakness and trials.

Para que eu confie na graça de Deus em minha própria fraqueza e provações.

Day 7: The charity of Paul

Dia 7: A caridade de Paulo

Paul wrote that without love he would be nothing, for love is patient and kind and bears all things. The Apostle places charity above every gift and every knowledge.

Paulo escreveu que sem amor nada seria, pois o amor é paciente e benigno e tudo suporta. O Apóstolo coloca a caridade acima de todo dom e de todo saber.

Intention

For the gift of genuine charity in all my relationships.

Pelo dom da caridade sincera em todas as minhas relações.

Day 8: Feed my sheep

Dia 8: Apascenta as minhas ovelhas

To Peter the risen Lord entrusted His flock, saying, 'Feed my sheep.' He teaches us that to love Christ is to care for His people and to serve in His name.

A Pedro o Senhor ressuscitado confiou o seu rebanho, dizendo: 'Apascenta as minhas ovelhas.' Ele nos ensina que amar a Cristo é cuidar do seu povo e servir em seu nome.

Intention

For the Pope, bishops, and all shepherds of souls.

Pelo Papa, pelos bispos e por todos os pastores de almas.

Day 9: The good fight finished

Dia 9: O bom combate consumado

Both apostles witnessed in Rome unto death, Peter crucified and Paul beheaded, sealing the faith with their blood. Paul could write at the end, 'I have fought the good fight, I have kept the faith.'

Ambos os apóstolos testemunharam em Roma até a morte, Pedro crucificado e Paulo decapitado, selando a fé com o sangue. Paulo pôde escrever no fim: 'Combati o bom combate, guardei a fé.'

Intention

For the grace of perseverance and a holy death.

Pela graça da perseverança e de uma santa morte.

Closing prayer

Glorious Apostles, Peter holding the keys and Paul wielding the sword of the Spirit, intercede for the Church and for the Holy Father who sits in Peter's chair. Keep me faithful to the apostolic faith you handed down. Lead me at last to the crown you now wear. Amen.

Gloriosos Apóstolos, Pedro com as chaves e Paulo com a espada do Espírito, intercedei pela Igreja e pelo Santo Padre que se assenta na cátedra de Pedro. Conservai-me fiel à fé apostólica que nos transmitistes. Conduzi-me enfim à coroa que agora ostentais. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a Novena to Saints Peter and Paul. It is structured as nine distinct days (not one repeated prayer): each day opens and closes with the Sign of the Cross, presents a unique reflection on the two apostles, voices petitions to the saints, and provides a space for the petitioner's own intentions ("St. Peter and St. Paul, please pray for my intentions [state your intentions here]."). The day themes run: (1) opposition and courage, (2) strengthening family faith, (3) repentance and amendment, (4) growth in faith and support for clergy, (5) evangelization using personal talents, (6) deepening relationship with Christ, (7) community service and spreading the Gospel, (8) listening to God's voice, (9) trust in God during difficulties.

Fidelity note

Ember and PMN share the same scaffold — nine distinct days, each a meditation plus a personal intention — which is the standard PMN shape, so the structural fidelity is high. The wording is wholly Ember's own: it is not a transcription of PMN's prayers but an independent composition built on the same Scripture-driven episodes of the apostles' lives. The thematic emphases also diverge: Ember organizes the nine days tightly around the biblical biography (call, confession, denial, Damascus, missions, weakness, charity, the keys, martyrdom), whereas PMN's themes lean more pastoral/applied (family, clergy, community service). Best read as an original Ember composition in the PMN format rather than a faithful copy of the PMN text.

St. Benedict st-benedict-novena

Jul 2–10
description & history

Nine days with the father of Western monasticism, whose Rule and holy medal have guarded souls in peace for fifteen centuries.

Nove dias com o pai do monaquismo ocidental, cuja Regra e medalha sagrada guardam as almas em paz há quinze séculos.

St. Benedict of Nursia, born around 480, fled the corruption of Rome to live as a hermit before founding the great monastery of Monte Cassino. His Rule, balancing prayer and work under the motto Ora et Labora, shaped the monasticism and culture of the West. The Medal and Cross of St. Benedict bear his prayers against evil, and he is invoked for peace, protection, and a holy death.

São Bento de Núrsia, nascido por volta de 480, fugiu da corrupção de Roma para viver como eremita antes de fundar o grande mosteiro de Monte Cassino. Sua Regra, equilibrando oração e trabalho sob o lema Ora et Labora, moldou o monaquismo e a cultura do Ocidente. A Medalha e a Cruz de São Bento trazem suas preces contra o mal, e ele é invocado pela paz, pela proteção e por uma santa morte.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. Benedict, father of monks and patron of a peaceful death, you sought God above all things in silence and labor. As I begin this novena, obtain for me a heart ordered to God and freed from the snares of the evil one. Teach me to seek nothing dearer than Christ. Amen.

Glorioso São Bento, pai dos monges e patrono de uma morte feliz, vós buscastes a Deus acima de todas as coisas no silêncio e no trabalho. Ao iniciar esta novena, alcançai-me um coração ordenado a Deus e livre das ciladas do maligno. Ensinai-me a nada preferir ao amor de Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: Seeking God alone

Dia 1: Buscar somente a Deus

As a young man Benedict left Rome's pleasures to live hidden in a cave at Subiaco, desiring God alone. He teaches us that holiness begins when we truly seek the one thing necessary.

Ainda jovem, Bento deixou os prazeres de Roma para viver escondido numa gruta em Subiaco, desejando somente a Deus. Ele nos ensina que a santidade começa quando buscamos de verdade a única coisa necessária.

Intention

For the grace to desire God above every earthly good.

Pela graça de desejar a Deus acima de todo bem terreno.

Day 2: Ora et Labora

Dia 2: Ora et Labora

Benedict ordered the monastic day around prayer and work, so that the hands labored while the heart adored. He teaches us that ordinary tasks, offered to God, become holy.
