Bento ordenou o dia monástico em torno da oração e do trabalho, para que as mãos trabalhassem enquanto o coração adorava. Ele nos ensina que as tarefas comuns, oferecidas a Deus, tornam-se santas.

Intention

That I may sanctify my daily work by offering it to God.

Para que eu santifique o meu trabalho diário oferecendo-o a Deus.

Day 3: Nothing dearer than Christ

Dia 3: Nada preferir a Cristo

The Rule commands the monk to prefer nothing whatever to Christ. Benedict shows us that every choice of the day is finally a choice for or against the Lord.

A Regra ordena ao monge nada absolutamente preferir a Cristo. Bento nos mostra que cada escolha do dia é, no fim, uma escolha a favor ou contra o Senhor.

Intention

For the grace to put Christ before all things, every day.

Pela graça de colocar Cristo antes de todas as coisas, cada dia.

Day 4: The school of humility

Dia 4: A escola da humildade

Benedict made humility the foundation of his Rule, charting twelve steps by which the soul climbs to God by descending into truth. He teaches that self-knowledge before God is the root of every virtue.

Bento fez da humildade o fundamento de sua Regra, traçando doze degraus pelos quais a alma sobe a Deus descendo à verdade. Ele ensina que o conhecimento de si diante de Deus é a raiz de toda virtude.

Intention

For a humble heart that knows itself before God.

Por um coração humilde que se conhece diante de Deus.

Day 5: Stability of heart

Dia 5: A estabilidade do coração

Benedict bound his monks to stability, that they might persevere in one place and one community rather than fleeing every difficulty. He teaches us that faithfulness is proven by staying.

Bento vinculou seus monges à estabilidade, para que perseverassem num só lugar e numa só comunidade, em vez de fugir de cada dificuldade. Ele nos ensina que a fidelidade se prova permanecendo.

Intention

For perseverance and stability in my commitments and vocation.

Pela perseverança e estabilidade em meus compromissos e em minha vocação.

Day 6: Welcoming Christ in the guest

Dia 6: Acolher Cristo no hóspede

The Rule commands that every guest be received as Christ Himself, and the poor and the pilgrim with special care. Benedict teaches us to see the Lord in everyone who knocks at our door.

A Regra ordena que todo hóspede seja recebido como o próprio Cristo, e os pobres e peregrinos com cuidado especial. Bento nos ensina a ver o Senhor em todos os que batem à nossa porta.

Intention

For a spirit of hospitality and charity toward strangers.

Por um espírito de hospitalidade e caridade para com os estrangeiros.

Day 7: The cross against evil

Dia 7: A cruz contra o mal

Tradition holds that Benedict made the sign of the cross over a poisoned cup, which shattered before it could harm him. His medal, marked 'May the Holy Cross be my light,' is a constant prayer for protection from evil.

A tradição relata que Bento fez o sinal da cruz sobre uma taça envenenada, que se partiu antes de feri-lo. Sua medalha, marcada 'Seja a Santa Cruz a minha luz', é uma prece constante de proteção contra o mal.

Intention

For protection against the snares and temptations of the evil one.

Pela proteção contra as ciladas e tentações do maligno.

Day 8: Peace, that none may be troubled

Dia 8: A paz, para que ninguém se perturbe

Benedict gave his order the motto Pax, peace, and ordered the monastery so that no one would be saddened or troubled in the house of God. He teaches us to be makers of peace in our homes and hearts.

Bento deu à sua ordem o lema Pax, a paz, e ordenou o mosteiro de modo que ninguém se entristecesse ou perturbasse na casa de Deus. Ele nos ensina a ser construtores de paz em nossos lares e corações.

Intention

For peace in my family, my community, and the world.

Pela paz em minha família, em minha comunidade e no mundo.

Day 9: A holy and watchful death

Dia 9: Uma morte santa e vigilante

Benedict died standing in the chapel, his arms upheld by his brethren, after receiving the Body of the Lord. Patron of a happy death, he teaches us to keep death daily before our eyes and to die in the Lord's embrace.

Bento morreu de pé na capela, com os braços amparados pelos irmãos, após receber o Corpo do Senhor. Patrono de uma morte feliz, ele nos ensina a ter cada dia a morte diante dos olhos e a morrer nos braços do Senhor.

Intention

For the grace of final perseverance and a happy death.

Pela graça da perseverança final e de uma morte feliz.

Closing prayer

St. Benedict, whose cross puts the enemy to flight, defend me against every temptation and lead me in the way of peace. May the balance of prayer and work order all my days, and may I persevere in the school of the Lord's service. Bring me at last to the kingdom to which your Rule points. Amen.

São Bento, cuja cruz põe em fuga o inimigo, defendei-me contra toda tentação e conduzi-me no caminho da paz. Que o equilíbrio entre a oração e o trabalho ordene todos os meus dias, e que eu persevere na escola do serviço do Senhor. Levai-me enfim ao reino para o qual a vossa Regra aponta. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="benedict">
Novena
to St. Benedict

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 12
March and ending on 20 March, the eve of the Feast of St. Benedict.

O glorious St. Benedict, sublime model of all virtues, pure vessel of
God's grace! Behold me, humbly kneeling at thy feet. I implore thy
loving heart to pray for me before the throne of God. To thee I have
recourse in all the dangers which daily surround me. Shield me against
my enemies, inspire me to imitate thee in all things. May thy blessing
be with me always, so that I may shun whatever God forbids and avoid
the occasions of sin.

Graciously obtain for me from God those favors and graces of which I
stand so much in need, in the trials, miseries and afflictions of life.
Thy heart was always so full of love, compassion, and mercy toward
those who were afflicted or troubled in any way. Thou didst never
dismiss without consolation and assistance anyone who had recourse to
thee. I therefore invoke thy powerful intercession, in the confident
hope that thou wilt hear my prayers and obtain for me the special grace
and favor I so earnestly implore (mention your intentions here) ,
if it be for the greater glory of God and the welfare of my soul.

Help me, O great St. Benedict, to live and die as a faithful child of
God, to be ever submissive to His holy will, and to attain the eternal
happiness of heaven. Amen.<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PMN's St. Benedict novena uses the same prayer text each of the nine days, framed by the Sign of the Cross. It opens "Holy St. Benedict, I ask that you pray with me now to our God, using your words for the intentions that are written on my heart," then quotes Benedict's own Rule ("What can be sweeter to us, dear ones, than this voice of the Lord inviting us?..." and the prologue's "let us ask the Lord that He supply by the help of His grace what is impossible to us by nature"), leaves a space for intentions, and dedicates each day to a different group (religious, professionals, parents, children, the poor, the imprisoned, the military, the enslaved, the infirm).

Fidelity note

Ember's St. Benedict novena does not reproduce either source's prayer text — neither FishEaters' single traditional invocation nor PMN's Rule-based daily prayer. Structurally Ember diverges from both: it uses nine distinct meditation-plus-intention days, where FishEaters repeats one prayer and PMN repeats one prayer with rotating beneficiaries. The content is faithful to Benedict's authentic spirituality and well-grounded (Subiaco, Ora et Labora, the twelve steps of humility, stability, hospitality, the medal and "Crux sacra sit mihi lux," Pax, a holy death), and Day 3's "prefer nothing whatever to Christ" and the closing echo the Rule much as PMN does. So it is themically faithful and draws on the same Benedictine sources, but it is an original composition rather than a transcription of either page.

SS. Louis & Zélie Martin ss-louis-zelie-martin-novena

Jul 3–11
description & history

Nine days with the first married couple canonized together, parents of St. Thérèse, who made holiness of an ordinary family life.

Nove dias com o primeiro casal canonizado em conjunto, pais de Santa Teresinha, que fizeram da vida familiar comum um caminho de santidade.

Louis Martin, a watchmaker, and Zélie Guérin, a skilled Alençon lacemaker, married in 1858 and raised their children in Normandy in deep faith. Of their nine children, five daughters survived, including St. Thérèse of Lisieux; four entered Carmel. Zélie died of breast cancer in 1877 and Louis after years of illness in 1894. Canonized together in 2015, they show that the path to holiness runs through marriage, work, and family.

Luís Martin, relojoeiro, e Zélia Guérin, hábil rendeira de Alençon, casaram-se em 1858 e criaram os filhos na Normandia numa fé profunda. Dos seus nove filhos sobreviveram cinco filhas, entre elas Santa Teresinha de Lisieux; quatro entraram no Carmelo. Zélia morreu de câncer de mama em 1877 e Luís após anos de enfermidade em 1894. Canonizados juntos em 2015, mostram que o caminho da santidade passa pelo matrimônio, pelo trabalho e pela família.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Holy spouses Louis and Zélie Martin, you made your home a school of faith and your marriage a path to heaven. As I begin this novena, obtain for me the grace to seek holiness in the duties of each ordinary day. Help me to love God in my family and my work. Amen.

Santos esposos Luís e Zélia Martin, vós fizestes de vosso lar uma escola de fé e de vosso matrimônio um caminho para o céu. Ao iniciar esta novena, alcançai-me a graça de buscar a santidade nos deveres de cada dia comum. Ajudai-me a amar a Deus em minha família e em meu trabalho. Amém.

Day 1: Marriage as a path to God

Dia 1: O matrimônio como caminho para Deus

Louis and Zélie married not for comfort alone but to walk together toward God and to raise saints for heaven. They teach us that the love of husband and wife is meant to lead both to holiness.

Luís e Zélia casaram-se não apenas em busca de conforto, mas para caminhar juntos rumo a Deus e gerar santos para o céu. Eles nos ensinam que o amor dos esposos é destinado a conduzir ambos à santidade.

Intention

For all married couples, that their love may lead them to God.

Por todos os casais, para que o seu amor os conduza a Deus.

Day 2: Faithfulness in daily work

Dia 2: Fidelidade no trabalho diário

Louis kept his watchmaker's shop and Zélie her lace business with honesty and care, supporting their home by patient labor. They teach us that work done well and offered to God is itself a prayer.

Luís mantinha sua relojoaria e Zélia o seu negócio de rendas com honestidade e zelo, sustentando o lar pelo trabalho paciente. Eles nos ensinam que o trabalho bem feito e oferecido a Deus é, em si mesmo, uma oração.

Intention

That I may do my daily work with honesty, diligence, and faith.

Para que eu realize meu trabalho diário com honestidade, diligência e fé.

Day 3: A home of prayer

Dia 3: Um lar de oração

The Martin home kept the rhythm of daily Mass, evening prayer, and the feasts of the Church. They teach us that children learn to love God first by seeing their parents kneel before Him.

O lar dos Martin guardava o ritmo da Missa diária, da oração da noite e das festas da Igreja. Eles nos ensinam que os filhos aprendem a amar a Deus primeiro ao verem os pais ajoelhados diante d'Ele.

Intention

That my home may be marked by prayer and the life of the Church.

Para que meu lar seja marcado pela oração e pela vida da Igreja.

Day 4: Trust in God's providence

Dia 4: Confiança na providência de Deus

Through illness, business worries, and an uncertain future, Zélie wrote that she wished only to accept whatever God sent. They teach us to lean on providence rather than on our own anxious plans.

Em meio à doença, às preocupações do negócio e a um futuro incerto, Zélia escreveu que desejava apenas aceitar tudo o que Deus enviasse. Eles nos ensinam a apoiar-nos na providência em vez de em nossos planos ansiosos.

Intention

For trust in God's providence amid the cares of life.

Pela confiança na providência de Deus em meio às inquietações da vida.

Day 5: Bearing the loss of children

Dia 5: Suportar a perda dos filhos

Four of their children died young, yet Louis and Zélie offered each one back to God in faith, trusting they had gained little intercessors in heaven. They teach grieving parents that no child given to God is ever truly lost.

Quatro de seus filhos morreram ainda pequenos, mas Luís e Zélia devolveram cada um a Deus na fé, confiando que ganhavam pequenos intercessores no céu. Eles ensinam aos pais enlutados que nenhum filho entregue a Deus está de fato perdido.

Intention

For parents who grieve the loss of a child.

Pelos pais que choram a perda de um filho.

Day 6: Raising children for heaven

Dia 6: Criar os filhos para o céu

Zélie said she wanted many children in order to raise them for heaven, and four of her daughters entered Carmel. They teach parents that their first task is to form souls for eternity, not merely for the world.

Zélia dizia que queria muitos filhos para criá-los para o céu, e quatro de suas filhas entraram no Carmelo. Eles ensinam aos pais que sua primeira tarefa é formar almas para a eternidade, e não apenas para o mundo.

Intention

For all parents, that they may raise their children in the faith.

Por todos os pais, para que criem os filhos na fé.

Day 7: Charity to the poor

Dia 7: Caridade para com os pobres

Louis and Zélie quietly sought out the poor and the dying, sharing their means and even bringing the destitute into their home. They teach us that a holy family looks outward to the needs of others.

Luís e Zélia procuravam discretamente os pobres e os moribundos, repartindo seus bens e até acolhendo os desamparados em casa. Eles nos ensinam que uma família santa volta o olhar para as necessidades dos outros.

Intention

For generosity toward the poor and the suffering.

Pela generosidade para com os pobres e os que sofrem.

Day 8: Suffering accepted in love

Dia 8: O sofrimento aceito no amor

Zélie bore her long fight with breast cancer without complaint, and Louis endured paralysis and confinement in his final years with serene faith. They teach us to unite our suffering to the Cross of Christ.

Zélia suportou sem queixas a longa luta contra o câncer de mama, e Luís enfrentou a paralisia e o internamento de seus últimos anos com fé serena. Eles nos ensinam a unir o nosso sofrimento à Cruz de Cristo.

Intention

For the grace to accept suffering and unite it to Christ's Cross.

Pela graça de aceitar o sofrimento e uni-lo à Cruz de Cristo.

Day 9: A family that becomes a garden of saints

Dia 9: Uma família que se torna jardim de santos

From their hidden, ordinary home came St. Thérèse of Lisieux, a Doctor of the Church. Louis and Zélie show that holiness is not reserved for the cloister but flourishes in the everyday faithfulness of family life.

De seu lar escondido e comum saiu Santa Teresinha de Lisieux, Doutora da Igreja. Luís e Zélia mostram que a santidade não está reservada ao claustro, mas floresce na fidelidade cotidiana da vida familiar.

Intention

That my family may become a path of holiness for all its members.

Para que minha família se torne um caminho de santidade para todos os seus membros.

Closing prayer

Saints Louis and Zélie, who trusted God through joy and sorrow and gave Him your children, intercede for all families and for mine. Teach husbands and wives to sanctify one another, and parents to raise their children for heaven. Obtain for us the grace to say in all things, 'God's will be done.' Amen.

Santos Luís e Zélia, que confiastes em Deus na alegria e na dor e Lhe entregastes os vossos filhos, intercedei por todas as famílias e pela minha. Ensinai aos esposos a santificarem-se mutuamente, e aos pais a criarem os filhos para o céu. Alcançai-nos a graça de dizer em tudo: 'Faça-se a vontade de Deus.' Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PMN has a Saints Louis and Zélie Martin novena structured as nine days in an identical format: the Sign of the Cross, an introductory invocation to God, an address to the saints, a petition section with space for personal intentions ("Please also pray for [mention your intention here]"), and the closing "Sts. Louis and Zelie Martin, pray for us!" followed by the Sign of the Cross. The day themes run: (1) openness to God's will and unexpected vocations, (2) holy and joyful family life, (3) virtue in daily family duties and spousal love, (4) faithfulness through the trials of parenthood and childbearing, (5) keeping faith through the loss of children, (6) fostering religious vocations, (7) sanctifying mundane daily work (the "Little Way"), (8) marriage and family as domestic church, (9) perseverance through severe trials including Zélie's terminal illness.

Fidelity note

Ember and PMN are closely aligned in both structure and content here — nine distinct days, each a reflection plus an intention, framed by the Sign of the Cross. The day themes overlap heavily: marriage as a path to holiness, daily work, family prayer, trust in providence, the loss of children, raising children for heaven, suffering/terminal illness all appear in both, in roughly parallel order. The wording is Ember's own (no verbatim borrowing), and the biographical detail is accurate (watchmaking and Alençon lace, four daughters in Carmel, Zélie's breast cancer, Louis's paralysis, St. Thérèse). Ember adds a "charity to the poor" day not foregrounded by PMN and frames Day 9 around the "garden of saints" rather than Zélie's illness. Overall a faithful, well-sourced treatment in the same devotional shape, paraphrased rather than copied.

St. Mary Magdalene st-mary-magdalene-novena

Jul 13–21
description & history

Nine days with the Apostle to the Apostles, who loved much, stood by the Cross, and first met the risen Lord.

Nove dias com a Apóstola dos Apóstolos, que muito amou, permaneceu junto à Cruz e foi a primeira a encontrar o Senhor ressuscitado.

St. Mary Magdalene, freed by Jesus from seven demons, followed Him faithfully and helped support His ministry. She stood near the Cross when others fled and went to the tomb at dawn, where the risen Lord called her by name and sent her to announce His resurrection. For this the Church honors her as the Apostle to the Apostles.

Santa Maria Madalena, libertada por Jesus de sete demônios, seguiu-O fielmente e ajudou a sustentar o seu ministério. Permaneceu junto à Cruz quando os outros fugiram e foi ao sepulcro de madrugada, onde o Senhor ressuscitado a chamou pelo nome e a enviou a anunciar a sua ressurreição. Por isso a Igreja a honra como Apóstola dos Apóstolos.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Mary Magdalene, faithful disciple who loved the Lord with an ardent and undivided heart, you sought Him in tears and found Him risen. As I begin this novena, kindle in me your love for Jesus and your longing to be near Him. Teach me to seek the Lord above all things. Amen.

Santa Maria Madalena, discípula fiel que amastes o Senhor com coração ardente e indiviso, vós O buscastes em lágrimas e O encontrastes ressuscitado. Ao iniciar esta novena, acendei em mim o vosso amor por Jesus e o vosso anseio de estar junto d'Ele. Ensinai-me a buscar o Senhor acima de todas as coisas. Amém.

Day 1: Healed and set free

Dia 1: Curada e libertada

The Gospel tells us that Jesus freed Mary Magdalene from seven demons, and from that hour she followed Him. She teaches us that the soul restored by Christ responds with grateful and faithful love.

O Evangelho nos diz que Jesus libertou Maria Madalena de sete demônios, e desde aquela hora ela O seguiu. Ela nos ensina que a alma restaurada por Cristo responde com amor grato e fiel.

Intention

For freedom from whatever binds my heart away from God.

Pela libertação de tudo o que prende meu coração para longe de Deus.

Day 2: Following the Lord

Dia 2: Seguir o Senhor

Mary Magdalene was among the women who followed Jesus and helped provide for Him out of their own means. She teaches us that discipleship is shown in faithful accompaniment and humble service.

Maria Madalena estava entre as mulheres que seguiam Jesus e O ajudavam com os próprios bens. Ela nos ensina que o discipulado se mostra no acompanhamento fiel e no serviço humilde.

Intention

For the grace to follow and serve Christ faithfully each day.

Pela graça de seguir e servir a Cristo fielmente cada dia.

Day 3: A heart that loves much

Dia 3: Um coração que ama muito

The Lord praised the woman who loved much because much had been forgiven her, and the Church reads this love in Mary Magdalene's devotion. She teaches us that grateful love is the fruit of mercy received.

O Senhor louvou a mulher que muito amou porque muito lhe fora perdoado, e a Igreja contempla esse amor na devoção de Maria Madalena. Ela nos ensina que o amor agradecido é fruto da misericórdia recebida.

Intention

That gratitude for God's mercy may set my heart on fire with love.

Para que a gratidão pela misericórdia de Deus inflame meu coração de amor.

Day 4: Sitting at the Lord's feet

Dia 4: Aos pés do Senhor

Tradition has long contemplated Mary at the feet of Jesus, listening to His word as the one thing necessary. She teaches us to choose the better part and to rest in His presence.

A tradição contempla há muito Maria aos pés de Jesus, escutando a sua palavra como a única coisa necessária. Ela nos ensina a escolher a melhor parte e a repousar em sua presença.

Intention

For the grace to sit at Christ's feet and listen to His word.

Pela graça de sentar-me aos pés de Cristo e escutar a sua palavra.

Day 5: Faithful at the Cross

Dia 5: Fiel junto à Cruz

When most of the disciples had fled, Mary Magdalene stood by the Cross of Jesus to the very end. She teaches us to remain near the Lord in His suffering and not to abandon Him in our own dark hours.

Quando a maioria dos discípulos havia fugido, Maria Madalena permaneceu junto à Cruz de Jesus até o fim. Ela nos ensina a ficar perto do Senhor em seu sofrimento e a não abandoná-Lo em nossas próprias horas escuras.

Intention

For fidelity to Christ in times of suffering and trial.

Pela fidelidade a Cristo nos tempos de sofrimento e provação.

Day 6: Seeking Him in tears

Dia 6: Buscá-Lo em lágrimas

At the empty tomb Mary wept, saying, 'They have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid Him.' She teaches us that holy longing keeps seeking even when consolation seems gone.

Diante do sepulcro vazio Maria chorava, dizendo: 'Levaram o meu Senhor, e não sei onde O puseram.' Ela nos ensina que o santo anseio continua a buscar mesmo quando a consolação parece ter-se ido.

Intention

That I may persevere in seeking God even in desolation.

Para que eu persevere na busca de Deus mesmo na desolação.

Day 7: He calls me by name

Dia 7: Ele me chama pelo nome

The risen Jesus said but one word, 'Mary,' and she knew Him at once, answering 'Rabboni,' Teacher. She teaches us that the Lord knows each of us personally and calls us by our own name.

Jesus ressuscitado disse apenas uma palavra, 'Maria', e ela O reconheceu de imediato, respondendo 'Rabôni', Mestre. Ela nos ensina que o Senhor conhece cada um de nós pessoalmente e nos chama pelo próprio nome.

Intention

For the grace to recognize the Lord's voice speaking to my heart.

Pela graça de reconhecer a voz do Senhor que fala ao meu coração.

Day 8: Apostle to the Apostles

Dia 8: Apóstola dos Apóstolos

Jesus sent Mary Magdalene to tell the apostles, 'I have seen the Lord,' and so she became the first herald of the Resurrection. She teaches us that those who encounter Christ are sent to proclaim Him.

Jesus enviou Maria Madalena a anunciar aos apóstolos: 'Eu vi o Senhor', e assim ela se tornou a primeira mensageira da Ressurreição. Ela nos ensina que quem encontra Cristo é enviado a anunciá-Lo.

Intention

For the courage to bear witness to the risen Christ.

Pela coragem de testemunhar Cristo ressuscitado.

Day 9: Do not cling, but go and announce

Dia 9: Não te apegues, mas vai e anuncia

The Lord told her not to cling to Him but to go to His brethren, for love must become mission. Mary Magdalene teaches us that true devotion overflows from the tomb into the joyful proclamation of the Gospel.

O Senhor lhe disse para não se apegar a Ele, mas ir aos seus irmãos, pois o amor deve tornar-se missão. Maria Madalena nos ensina que a verdadeira devoção transborda do sepulcro para o anúncio gozoso do Evangelho.

Intention

That my love for Christ may overflow into joyful witness to others.

Para que meu amor a Cristo transborde em testemunho gozoso aos outros.

Closing prayer

St. Mary Magdalene, first witness of the Resurrection and herald of the risen Christ, obtain for me a heart that loves much and clings to the Lord. May I, like you, seek Him faithfully in this life and rejoice to hear Him call my name. Lead me to the joy of Easter that never ends. Amen.

Santa Maria Madalena, primeira testemunha da Ressurreição e arauto de Cristo ressuscitado, alcançai-me um coração que ame muito e se apegue ao Senhor. Que eu, como vós, O busque fielmente nesta vida e me alegre ao ouvi-Lo chamar o meu nome. Conduzi-me à alegria da Páscoa que não tem fim. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="magdalen">

Novena
to St. Mary Magdalen

Pray once a day, especially starting on July 13 and
ending on July 21, the eve of the Feast of St. Mary Magdalen

O Most merciful Jesus, I give Thee thanks for that work of piety which
the blessed Mary Magdalen wrought on Thee when she washed Thy feet with
her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head, and kissed them
and anointed them with fragrant ointment; whereby she obtained from
Thee such signal grace that Thou didst pour into her heart and soul so
great love of Thee that she could love nothing apart from Thee:
beseeching Thee that by her merits and intercessions Thou wouldst
vouchsafe to grant me tears of true repentance, and pour into my heart
Thy divine love. Amen.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary and one Glory be
<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PMN's St. Mary Magdalene novena runs nine days in a fixed format: Sign of the Cross; a thanksgiving prayer to God for Mary Magdalene as an example; a direct address to her on a day-specific aspect of her life; the petition "Please bring my petitions before [Christ/God]"; a meditation; personal intercessory requests; space for intentions ("Please also pray for [mention your intentions here]"); and the close "St. Mary Magdalene, pray for us!" with the Sign of the Cross. Day themes: (1) repentance, (2) devotion / leaving the old life, (3) embracing God's mercy, (4) witnessing to others, (5) friendship with Jesus, (6) faith and devotion, (7) courageous proclamation, (8) faithful service in solitude, (9) steadfast service throughout life.

Fidelity note

Ember does not reuse FishEaters' single traditional prayer (which centers on the washing/anointing of Christ's feet and the gift of tears) — in fact Ember consciously builds on the modern Gospel portrait (Luke 8's seven demons, John 20's "Mary"/"Rabboni" and "do not cling"), and treats the foot-washing only obliquely via "loved much." Against PMN it is structurally similar (nine meditation-plus-intention days framed by the Sign of the Cross) and overlaps in themes (mercy/forgiveness, witness, faithful service), but Ember's day-by-day arc is more tightly chronological and Scripture-anchored (healing → discipleship → love → Bethany → Cross → tomb → recognition → mission → "go and announce"). The wording throughout is Ember's own. Faithful to the saint and to the genre; an original composition rather than a copy of either source.

SS. Anne & Joachim ss-anne-joachim-novena

Jul 17–25
description & history

A nine-day novena to Saints Anne and Joachim, the parents of the Blessed Virgin Mary and grandparents of Our Lord, patrons of families, grandparents, and expectant mothers, who waited many years in faith for the child God promised.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Ana e São Joaquim, pais da Bem-Aventurada Virgem Maria e avós de Nosso Senhor, padroeiros das famílias, dos avós e das gestantes, que esperaram longos anos, na fé, o filho que Deus lhes prometeu.

Tradition, drawn from early Christian writings, names Anne and Joachim as the parents of the Virgin Mary. Long childless and aged, they begged God for a child and, in answer, were given the Mother of the Redeemer. Devotion to them spread widely in both East and West, and they are honored as the holy grandparents of Jesus and protectors of the family.

A tradição, recolhida de antigos escritos cristãos, dá os nomes de Ana e Joaquim aos pais da Virgem Maria. Já idosos e por muito tempo sem filhos, suplicaram a Deus uma criança e receberam, em resposta, a Mãe do Redentor. A devoção a eles difundiu-se amplamente no Oriente e no Ocidente, e são honrados como os santos avós de Jesus e protetores da família.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious Saints Anne and Joachim, blessed parents of the Mother of God, you raised in your home the one who would bear the Savior of the world. Look kindly upon my family and obtain for us the grace of a household rooted in faith, patience, and love. Amen.

Gloriosos Santa Ana e São Joaquim, bem-aventurados pais da Mãe de Deus, em vosso lar criastes aquela que haveria de gerar o Salvador do mundo. Olhai com bondade para a minha família e alcançai-nos a graça de um lar enraizado na fé, na paciência e no amor. Amém.

Day 1: Faithful waiting

Dia 1: A espera fiel

Anne and Joachim grew old without the child their hearts desired, yet they never ceased to trust in God. Their long waiting teaches us that God's gifts ripen in His own time, not ours.

Ana e Joaquim envelheceram sem o filho que seus corações desejavam, mas nunca deixaram de confiar em Deus. A sua longa espera nos ensina que os dons de Deus amadurecem no tempo d'Ele, e não no nosso.

Intention

For the patience to wait upon God without losing hope in His promises.

Pela paciência de esperar em Deus sem perder a esperança em Suas promessas.

Day 2: Persevering prayer

Dia 2: A oração perseverante

Year after year this holy couple lifted their prayer to heaven, refusing to grow bitter or discouraged. They show us that perseverance in prayer is itself a kind of faith.

Ano após ano, este santo casal elevava sua oração ao céu, recusando-se a ceder à amargura ou ao desânimo. Eles nos mostram que a perseverança na oração é, em si mesma, uma forma de fé.

Intention

For the grace to pray steadfastly even when no answer seems near.

Pela graça de orar com firmeza mesmo quando nenhuma resposta parece próxima.

Day 3: A holy marriage

Dia 3: Um santo matrimônio

Anne and Joachim loved one another in God and bore their sorrow together as one heart. Their union reminds us that marriage is meant to be a shared road toward holiness.

Ana e Joaquim amaram-se em Deus e carregaram juntos a sua tristeza, como um só coração. A sua união nos lembra que o matrimônio é feito para ser um caminho partilhado rumo à santidade.

Intention

For all married couples, that they may grow holy together in good times and in trial.

Por todos os casais, para que cresçam juntos na santidade, na alegria e na provação.

Day 4: The gift of Mary

Dia 4: O dom de Maria

When God at last gave them a daughter, He gave them the purest creature ever to live. Anne and Joachim received Mary as a treasure entrusted to their care.

Quando Deus enfim lhes deu uma filha, deu-lhes a mais pura criatura que jamais viveu. Ana e Joaquim acolheram Maria como um tesouro confiado aos seus cuidados.

Intention

For parents awaiting a child, that they may receive new life as a gift from God.

Pelos pais que esperam um filho, para que recebam a nova vida como um dom de Deus.

Day 5: Raising a child for God

Dia 5: Educar um filho para Deus

In their home Mary first learned to pray, to serve, and to love the Lord. Anne and Joachim teach us that the first school of holiness is the family hearth.

Em seu lar, Maria aprendeu primeiro a rezar, a servir e a amar ao Senhor. Ana e Joaquim nos ensinam que a primeira escola de santidade é o lar da família.

Intention

For parents, that they may form their children first of all in the love of God.

Pelos pais, para que formem seus filhos, antes de tudo, no amor de Deus.

Day 6: The blessing of grandparents

Dia 6: A bênção dos avós

As grandparents of Jesus, Anne and Joachim stand for all who pass on the faith to a new generation. Their memory honors the quiet wisdom and prayers of the old.

Como avós de Jesus, Ana e Joaquim representam todos os que transmitem a fé a uma nova geração. A sua memória honra a sabedoria silenciosa e as orações dos mais velhos.

Intention

For grandparents, that their faith and prayers may bless their families for generations.

Pelos avós, para que sua fé e suas orações abençoem suas famílias por gerações.

Day 7: Trust in God's providence

Dia 7: Confiança na providência de Deus

Their barren years gave way to a joy beyond all they had dared to ask. Anne and Joachim assure us that God's providence often works most wondrously in our seasons of poverty.

Os seus anos de esterilidade deram lugar a uma alegria maior do que tudo o que ousaram pedir. Ana e Joaquim nos garantem que a providência de Deus muitas vezes age de modo mais admirável em nossos tempos de pobreza.

Intention

For trust in God's providence when our hopes seem long unfulfilled.

Pela confiança na providência de Deus quando nossas esperanças parecem demorar a se cumprir.

Day 8: Holiness in ordinary life

Dia 8: A santidade na vida comum

Anne and Joachim became great saints not by extraordinary deeds but by faithfulness in a humble, ordinary life. They remind us that sanctity is woven from the daily duties done in love.

Ana e Joaquim tornaram-se grandes santos não por feitos extraordinários, mas pela fidelidade numa vida humilde e comum. Eles nos lembram que a santidade é tecida com os deveres diários cumpridos por amor.

Intention

For the grace to seek holiness in the ordinary duties of each day.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade nos deveres comuns de cada dia.

Day 9: Intercessors for the family

Dia 9: Intercessores da família

From heaven, the grandparents of Jesus watch over every household that calls upon them. They love to present our family needs to their daughter Mary and to her divine Son.

Do céu, os avós de Jesus velam por todo lar que os invoca. Comprazem-se em apresentar as necessidades de nossas famílias à sua filha Maria e ao seu Filho divino.

Intention

For the protection of my whole family under the care of Saints Anne and Joachim.

Pela proteção de toda a minha família sob os cuidados de Santa Ana e São Joaquim.

Closing prayer

Holy grandparents of Jesus, you who trusted God through long years of waiting, teach us to hope when His answer is slow in coming. Present my petitions to your daughter Mary, that through her they may reach the throne of God. Amen.

Santos avós de Jesus, vós que confiastes em Deus durante longos anos de espera, ensinai-nos a esperar quando Sua resposta tarda a chegar. Apresentai os meus pedidos à vossa filha Maria, para que por ela cheguem ao trono de Deus. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="Ann">
Novena
to St. Anne

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 17
July and ending on 25 July, the eve of the Feast of St. Anne.

O glorious St. Anne, filled with compassion for those who invoke thee
and with love for those who suffer, heavily laden with the weight of my
troubles, I cast myself at thy feet and humbly beg of thee to take
under thy special protection the present affair which I commend to
thee.

{Mention your intentions here)

Be pleased to commend it to thy daughter, the Blessed Virgin Mary, and
lay it before the throne of Jesus, so that He may bring it to a happy
outcome. Cease not to intercede for me until my request is granted.
Above all, obtain for me the grace of one day beholding my God face to
face, and, with thee and Mary and all the Saints, of praising and
blessing Him for all eternity. Amen.

Good St. Anne, mother of her who is our life, our sweetness and our
hope, pray to her for us and obtain our request.

Good St. Anne, mother of her who is our life, our sweetness and our
hope, pray to her for us and obtain our request.

Good St. Anne, mother of her who is our life, our sweetness and our
hope, pray to her for us and obtain our request.

Good St. Anne, pray for us. Jesus, Mary, Anne. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PMN has a combined Saints Joachim and Anne novena, nine days in one fixed format: opening Sign of the Cross; gratitude to God for the saints as examples; an address to them on a day-specific virtue; "Please bring my petitions before God"; a reflection; a petition for grace mirroring their virtue; space for intentions; and the close "Sts. Joachim and Anne, pray for us!" with the Sign of the Cross. Day themes: (1) faithfulness in marriage, (2) perseverance through the suffering of infertility, (3) hope despite prolonged childlessness, (4) faithful parenting and holy example, (5) deep trust in God's plan, (6) holy family life centered on God, (7) gratitude for answered prayer, (8) family as gift, (9) humility/virtue in obscure lives.

Fidelity note

Ember combines Anne and Joachim into one novena (like PMN), rather than FishEaters' two separate single-prayer novenas, and uses nine distinct meditation-plus-intention days framed by the Sign of the Cross — structurally a very close match to PMN. The themes track PMN tightly: waiting, persevering prayer, holy marriage, the gift of the child, raising a child for God, providence, holiness in ordinary/obscure life, and family intercession all appear in both, in similar order; Ember adds an explicit "blessing of grandparents" day. Wording is Ember's own (no verbatim borrowing from either source), and the closing — petitions presented "to your daughter Mary" so they reach God — echoes the FishEaters Anne prayer's distinctive "commend it to thy daughter, the Blessed Virgin Mary, and lay it before the throne of Jesus." Faithful and well-grounded; an original composition in the PMN combined-novena format with a nod to the FishEaters Anne text.

St. Ignatius of Loyola st-ignatius-loyola-novena

Jul 22–30
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Ignatius of Loyola, soldier turned saint, founder of the Society of Jesus, author of the Spiritual Exercises, who taught the Church to seek the greater glory of God in all things.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Inácio de Loyola, soldado convertido em santo, fundador da Companhia de Jesus, autor dos Exercícios Espirituais, que ensinou a Igreja a buscar em tudo a maior glória de Deus.

Born in 1491 in the Basque country, Ignatius was a worldly soldier until a cannonball shattered his leg at Pamplona in 1521. During his recovery he read the life of Christ and the saints, and a deeper ambition was born in him. After a long conversion at Manresa he composed the Spiritual Exercises, founded the Jesuits in 1540, and sent his sons across the world for the salvation of souls.

Nascido em 1491 no País Basco, Inácio foi um soldado mundano até que uma bala de canhão lhe estilhaçou a perna em Pamplona, em 1521. Durante a convalescença leu a vida de Cristo e dos santos, e nasceu nele uma ambição mais profunda. Após uma longa conversão em Manresa, compôs os Exercícios Espirituais, fundou os Jesuítas em 1540 e enviou seus filhos pelo mundo para a salvação das almas.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. Ignatius, who once sought the glory of earthly kings and then gave your whole life to the King of Heaven, obtain for me a generous heart. Teach me to desire nothing but the greater glory of God and the salvation of souls. Amen.

Glorioso Santo Inácio, que buscastes outrora a glória dos reis da terra e depois destes toda a vossa vida ao Rei do Céu, alcançai-me um coração generoso. Ensinai-me a nada desejar senão a maior glória de Deus e a salvação das almas. Amém.

Day 1: From soldier to saint

Dia 1: De soldado a santo

A cannonball at Pamplona ended Ignatius's dreams of military glory and began God's work in his soul. He teaches us that God can turn our greatest defeats into the doorway of grace.

Uma bala de canhão em Pamplona pôs fim aos sonhos de glória militar de Inácio e deu início à obra de Deus em sua alma. Ele nos ensina que Deus pode transformar nossas maiores derrotas na porta de entrada da graça.

Intention

For the grace to find God's call hidden within my failures and wounds.

Pela graça de encontrar o chamado de Deus escondido em meus fracassos e feridas.

Day 2: Holy reading

Dia 2: A leitura santa

Confined to his sickbed, Ignatius read the life of Christ and the saints, and felt a lasting peace that worldly daydreams never gave him. He shows us how the right books can quietly reshape a soul.

Preso ao leito de doente, Inácio leu a vida de Cristo e dos santos, e sentiu uma paz duradoura que os devaneios mundanos jamais lhe davam. Ele nos mostra como as boas leituras podem, em silêncio, remodelar uma alma.

Intention

For a love of spiritual reading that draws my heart toward Christ.

Pelo amor à leitura espiritual que atraia meu coração para Cristo.

Day 3: Discernment of spirits

Dia 3: O discernimento dos espíritos

Ignatius learned to notice the movements of his heart, distinguishing the consolation that comes from God from the false promises of the enemy. He teaches us to read our inner life with honesty and prayer.

Inácio aprendeu a perceber os movimentos de seu coração, distinguindo a consolação que vem de Deus das falsas promessas do inimigo. Ele nos ensina a ler nossa vida interior com honestidade e oração.

Intention

For the wisdom to discern God's voice amid the many voices within me.

Pela sabedoria de discernir a voz de Deus em meio às muitas vozes dentro de mim.

Day 4: The Spiritual Exercises

Dia 4: Os Exercícios Espirituais

At Manresa, Ignatius composed the Exercises to lead souls to know, love, and follow Christ more closely. He reminds us that the soul, like the body, must be trained through disciplined prayer.

Em Manresa, Inácio compôs os Exercícios para conduzir as almas a conhecer, amar e seguir Cristo mais de perto. Ele nos lembra que a alma, como o corpo, deve ser exercitada por uma oração disciplinada.

Intention

For the discipline to give myself regularly to serious prayer.

Pela disciplina de me entregar regularmente a uma oração séria.

Day 5: The First Principle and Foundation

Dia 5: O Princípio e Fundamento

Ignatius taught that we are created to praise, reverence, and serve God, and that all else is given to help us toward that end. He frees us by teaching holy indifference to everything but God's will.

Inácio ensinou que fomos criados para louvar, reverenciar e servir a Deus, e que tudo o mais nos é dado para nos ajudar a alcançar esse fim. Ele nos liberta ao ensinar a santa indiferença a tudo, exceto à vontade de Deus.

Intention

For freedom of heart to desire only what leads me to God.

Pela liberdade de coração para desejar somente o que me conduz a Deus.

Day 6: For the greater glory of God

Dia 6: Para a maior glória de Deus

Ad maiorem Dei gloriam was the motto by which Ignatius measured every choice and labor. He challenges us never to settle for the merely good, but to seek the greater good for God.

Ad maiorem Dei gloriam era o lema pelo qual Inácio media cada escolha e cada trabalho. Ele nos desafia a nunca nos contentarmos com o que é apenas bom, mas a buscar o maior bem para Deus.

Intention

For the desire to do all things for the greater glory of God.

Pelo desejo de fazer todas as coisas para a maior glória de Deus.

Day 7: Finding God in all things

Dia 7: Encontrar Deus em todas as coisas

Ignatius saw the Creator present and laboring in every creature and circumstance of his day. He teaches us to meet God not only in church but in our work, our duties, and the people before us.

Inácio via o Criador presente e operante em cada criatura e circunstância de seu dia. Ele nos ensina a encontrar Deus não só na igreja, mas em nosso trabalho, em nossos deveres e nas pessoas diante de nós.

Intention

For the grace to recognize God present in every moment of my day.

Pela graça de reconhecer Deus presente em cada momento do meu dia.

Day 8: Generous service

Dia 8: O serviço generoso

Ignatius prayed to give and not to count the cost, to labor and not to seek reward except that of knowing he did God's will. His generosity rebukes every half-hearted following of Christ.

Inácio rezava para dar e não contar o custo, para trabalhar e não buscar recompensa senão a de saber que fazia a vontade de Deus. A sua generosidade repreende todo seguimento tíbio de Cristo.

Intention

For a generous heart that serves God without counting the cost.

Por um coração generoso que sirva a Deus sem contar o custo.

Day 9: The Suscipe — total surrender

Dia 9: O Suscipe — a entrega total

At the summit of the Exercises, Ignatius surrenders his memory, understanding, and will, asking only for God's love and grace. He shows us that the highest prayer is to give God everything we are.

No ápice dos Exercícios, Inácio entrega sua memória, seu entendimento e sua vontade, pedindo apenas o amor e a graça de Deus. Ele nos mostra que a oração mais alta é dar a Deus tudo o que somos.

Intention

For the grace to surrender my whole life into the hands of God.

Pela graça de entregar toda a minha vida nas mãos de Deus.

Closing prayer

St. Ignatius, faithful soldier of Christ, help me to set the world rightly in my heart, choosing always what leads me closer to God. With you I pray: take, Lord, all that I have, for You have given it; give me only Your love and Your grace, and that is enough for me. Amen.

Santo Inácio, fiel soldado de Cristo, ajudai-me a ordenar retamente o mundo em meu coração, escolhendo sempre o que me conduz mais perto de Deus. Convosco rezo: tomai, Senhor, tudo o que tenho, pois Vós mo destes; dai-me somente o Vosso amor e a Vossa graça, e isto me basta. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PMN's St. Ignatius of Loyola novena is nine distinct days framed by the Sign of the Cross, each with a day-specific theme, petition, and space for personal intentions, closing "St. Ignatius of Loyola, pray for us!" Day themes: (1) deeper conversion ("help me fall deeper in love with Christ"), (2) discernment of God's voice amid the world's noise, (3) evangelization and zeal for souls, (4) the Spiritual Exercises and discipline in prayer, (5) learning and intellectual growth / wisdom, (6) humility and obedience, servant leadership, (7) divine providence and surrendering fears, (8) total self-offering ("Take, Lord, and receive"), (9) the greater glory of God (Ad Majorem Dei Gloriam).

Fidelity note

Ember and PMN match closely in structure (nine themed days, each with a meditation and intention, framed by the Sign of the Cross) and in several core Ignatian themes that both hit: conversion/the cannonball, discernment, the Spiritual Exercises, providence/surrender, the Suscipe ("Take, Lord, and receive" / Ember's closing "take, Lord, all that I have... give me only Your love and Your grace"), and the AMDG motto on the climactic day. Ember's day arc is more explicitly tied to the Ignatian corpus — adding "holy reading," the First Principle and Foundation, "finding God in all things," and the Suscipe as the ninth day's centerpiece — where PMN substitutes more general themes (evangelization, learning, humility). Wording is Ember's own; the closing paraphrases the Suscipe rather than quoting any PMN line. Faithful and richly Ignatian; an original composition in the PMN format that, if anything, leans harder into Ignatius's own writings.

St. Alphonsus Liguori st-alphonsus-liguori-novena

Jul 23–31
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Alphonsus Liguori, Doctor of the Church, founder of the Redemptorists, tender shepherd of sinners, whose writings on Mary, the Eucharist, and prayer have led countless souls to God.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Afonso Maria de Ligório, Doutor da Igreja, fundador dos Redentoristas, terno pastor dos pecadores, cujos escritos sobre Maria, a Eucaristia e a oração conduziram inumeráveis almas a Deus.

Born near Naples in 1696, Alphonsus was a brilliant lawyer who abandoned the courts after a painful defeat to give himself wholly to Christ. Ordained a priest, he founded the Redemptorists in 1732 to preach to the poor and abandoned of the countryside. A master moral theologian and prolific writer, he is honored as a Doctor of the Church and patron of confessors and moral theologians.

Nascido perto de Nápoles em 1696, Afonso foi um brilhante advogado que abandonou os tribunais após uma derrota dolorosa, para entregar-se inteiramente a Cristo. Ordenado sacerdote, fundou os Redentoristas em 1732 para pregar aos pobres e abandonados dos campos. Mestre da teologia moral e escritor fecundo, é honrado como Doutor da Igreja e padroeiro dos confessores e dos moralistas.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. Alphonsus, who left the honors of the world to serve the souls most forgotten, obtain for me a heart on fire with love of God. Teach me, by your tenderness toward sinners, never to despair of God's mercy. Amen.

Glorioso Santo Afonso, que deixastes as honras do mundo para servir as almas mais esquecidas, alcançai-me um coração ardente de amor a Deus. Ensinai-me, por vossa ternura para com os pecadores, a nunca desesperar da misericórdia divina. Amém.

Day 1: Leaving the world for Christ

Dia 1: Deixar o mundo por Cristo

A celebrated young lawyer, Alphonsus laid his sword upon Our Lady's altar and turned from worldly success to the priesthood. He teaches us that the loss of earthly honor can be the gateway to a far greater calling.

Jovem advogado célebre, Afonso depôs a sua espada sobre o altar de Nossa Senhora e deixou o sucesso mundano pelo sacerdócio. Ele nos ensina que a perda da honra terrena pode ser a porta de um chamado muito maior.

Intention

For the courage to renounce vain ambitions for the sake of Christ.

Pela coragem de renunciar às ambições vãs por amor a Cristo.

Day 2: Love for the poor and abandoned

Dia 2: O amor aos pobres e abandonados

Alphonsus founded the Redemptorists to carry the Gospel to the goatherds and country folk whom no one else reached. He shows us that Christ's love seeks out those whom the world overlooks.

Afonso fundou os Redentoristas para levar o Evangelho aos cabreiros e camponeses que ninguém mais alcançava. Ele nos mostra que o amor de Cristo busca aqueles a quem o mundo despreza.

Intention

For a heart that reaches out to the poor and the forgotten.

Por um coração que se volte para os pobres e os esquecidos.

Day 3: Tenderness toward sinners

Dia 3: A ternura para com os pecadores

In the confessional Alphonsus was gentle and patient, drawing souls back to God by kindness rather than fear. He teaches us that mercy, not harshness, is the surest road to conversion.

No confessionário, Afonso era manso e paciente, atraindo as almas de volta a Deus pela bondade, e não pelo medo. Ele nos ensina que a misericórdia, e não a dureza, é o caminho mais seguro para a conversão.

Intention

For confessors and for sinners, that mercy may lead them home to God.

Pelos confessores e pelos pecadores, para que a misericórdia os conduza de volta a Deus.

Day 4: The Glories of Mary

Dia 4: As Glórias de Maria

Alphonsus poured out his love for the Mother of God in writings that proclaim her as the refuge of sinners and gate of heaven. He teaches us to fly to Mary with the confidence of a child.

Afonso derramou seu amor pela Mãe de Deus em escritos que a proclamam refúgio dos pecadores e porta do céu. Ele nos ensina a recorrer a Maria com a confiança de uma criança.

Intention

For a deeper, childlike devotion to Mary, our refuge and hope.

Por uma devoção mais profunda e filial a Maria, nosso refúgio e esperança.

Day 5: Love of the Eucharist

Dia 5: O amor à Eucaristia

Alphonsus called the visit to the Blessed Sacrament the dearest of all devotions and spent long hours before the tabernacle. He shows us that no friend awaits us so faithfully as Jesus truly present in the Eucharist.

Afonso chamava a visita ao Santíssimo Sacramento a mais cara de todas as devoções e passava longas horas diante do sacrário. Ele nos mostra que nenhum amigo nos espera com tanta fidelidade como Jesus realmente presente na Eucaristia.

Intention

For a burning love for Jesus present in the Blessed Sacrament.

Por um amor ardente a Jesus presente no Santíssimo Sacramento.

Day 6: Prayer, the great means of salvation

Dia 6: A oração, grande meio de salvação

Alphonsus taught that those who pray are surely saved and those who do not are surely lost. He urges us to ask God constantly, for the Lord has promised to grant what we humbly beg.

Afonso ensinava que os que rezam certamente se salvam e os que não rezam certamente se perdem. Ele nos exorta a pedir sempre a Deus, pois o Senhor prometeu conceder o que humildemente Lhe suplicamos.

Intention

For the grace to pray faithfully and never to neglect it.

Pela graça de rezar com fidelidade e nunca descuidar da oração.

Day 7: Conformity to God's will

Dia 7: A conformidade com a vontade de Deus

Through years of illness and trial, Alphonsus taught that all holiness consists in loving and embracing God's will. He shows us that peace is found not in changing our lot but in surrendering it to God.

Através de anos de doença e provação, Afonso ensinou que toda a santidade consiste em amar e abraçar a vontade de Deus. Ele nos mostra que a paz não se encontra em mudar nossa sorte, mas em entregá-la a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to accept God's will in joy and in suffering alike.

Pela graça de aceitar a vontade de Deus tanto na alegria quanto no sofrimento.

Day 8: Constancy in suffering

Dia 8: A constância no sofrimento

In old age Alphonsus bore great physical pain and even inner darkness without losing his trust in God. He teaches us that fidelity in the long night of suffering is itself a hidden victory.

Na velhice, Afonso suportou grandes dores físicas e até a escuridão interior sem perder a confiança em Deus. Ele nos ensina que a fidelidade na longa noite do sofrimento é, em si mesma, uma vitória oculta.

Intention

For perseverance and trust in seasons of pain and spiritual darkness.

Pela perseverança e confiança nos tempos de dor e de escuridão espiritual.

Day 9: Final perseverance

Dia 9: A perseverança final

Alphonsus taught his people to beg unceasingly for the grace of a holy death, which crowns every other grace. He reminds us that to die in the friendship of God is the one thing truly necessary.

Afonso ensinava seu povo a suplicar sem cessar a graça de uma morte santa, que coroa todas as outras graças. Ele nos lembra que morrer na amizade de Deus é a única coisa verdadeiramente necessária.

Intention

For the grace of a holy death in the friendship of God.

Pela graça de uma morte santa na amizade de Deus.

Closing prayer

St. Alphonsus, doctor of prayer and herald of Mary, lead me ever closer to Jesus in the Blessed Sacrament and to His Mother, our hope. Obtain for me the grace I now ask, and above all the grace of final perseverance. Amen.

Santo Afonso, doutor da oração e arauto de Maria, conduzi-me sempre mais perto de Jesus no Santíssimo Sacramento e de sua Mãe, nossa esperança. Alcançai-me a graça que agora vos peço e, acima de tudo, a graça da perseverança final. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="alphonsus">
Novena
to St. Alphonsus Liguori

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 24
July and ending on 1 August (Lammas), the eve of the Feast of St.
Alphonsus Liguori on 2 August.

O my glorious and well-beloved patron, Saint Alphonsus, thou who didst
toil and suffer so abundantly to assure to men the fruits of the
Redemption, behold the miseries of my poor soul and have pity on me. By
thy powerful intercession with Jesus and Mary, obtain for me true
repentance for my sins together with their pardon and remission, a deep
hatred of sin, and strength evermore to resist all temptations. Share
with me, I pray, at least a spark of that fire of love wherewith thy
heart did ever burn; and grant that, following thy example, I may make
the will of God the only rule of my life. Obtain for me likewise a
fervent and lasting love of Jesus, and a tender and childlike devotion
to Mary, together with the grace to pray without ceasing and to
persevere in the service of God even to the end of my life, that so I
may finally be united with thee in praising God and most holy Mary
through all eternity. Amen

The Litany of St. Alphonsus Liguori

Lord, have pity on us.

Christ, have pity on us.

Lord, have pity on us.

Christ, hear us:

Christ, graciously hear us.

God the Father, from heaven, have pity on us, hear us.

God the Son, Redeemer of the world, hear us.

God the Holy Ghost, hear us.

Holy Trinity, Who art one only God, hear us.

Holy Mary, Virgin Immaculate, pray for us.

Saint Alphonsus, model of piety from tenderest youth, pray for us.

Saint Alphonsus, preserved even till death from mortal sin, pray for us.

Despiser of the riches and vanities of the world, pray for us.

Always subject to the voice of Divine Providence, pray for us.

Rich in the treasures of Christian poverty, pray for us.

Model of patience in pains and afflictions, pray for us.

Model of meekness and of resignation in contradictions, pray for us.

Burning with a holy zeal for the salvation of souls, pray for us.

Scourge of heresies, pray for us.

Defender of the Catholic Faith, pray for us.

Always occupied in evangelizing the poor, pray for us.

Tender comforter of the afflicted. pray for us.

Instructed in the divine art of converting sinners, pray for us.

Enlightened guide in the path of perfection, pray for us.

Who became all things to all men, to gain all to Jesus Christ, pray for
us.

New ornament of religion, pray for us.

Bold champion of ecclesiastical discipline, pray for us.

Model of submission and devotion to the Sovereign Pontiff, pray for us.

Who watched unceasingly over the flock committed to you, pray for us.

Full of solicitude to procure the common good of the Church, pray for
us.

Glory of the Priesthood and of the Episcopate, pray for us.

Shining mirror of all virtues, pray for us.

Full of tenderest love for the Infant Jesus, pray for us.

Inflamed with divine heat in offering the Holy Sacrifice, pray for us.

Fervent worshipper of Jesus Christ in the Holy Eucharist, pray for us.

Penetrated with lively grief at the meditation of the sufferings of our
Divine Saviour, pray for us.

Specially devoted to the veneration of Mary, pray for us.

Honored by the apparition of the Blessed Virgin, while preaching in her
honor, pray for us.

Of angelic life and purity, pray for us.

True Patriarch in your paternal solicitude for the people of God, pray
for us.

Endowed with the gift of prophecy and miracles, pray for us.

Apostle by the extent and fruit of your labors, pray for us.

Martyr through your unheard of austerities, pray for us.

Confessor by your writings full of the Spirit of God, pray for us.

Virgin by purity of Body and soul, pray for us.

Founder of the Order of the Most Holy Redeemer, pray for us.

Model of Missionaries, pray for us.

Our tender father and powerful protector, pray for us.

Lamb of God, Who takest away the sins of the world,
Pardon us O Lord.

Lamb of God, Who takest away

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PMN's St. Alphonsus novena is nine days in an identical format (Sign of the Cross open/close, thematic content, "Please bring my petitions before God" with space for intentions, "St. Alphonsus, pray for us!"). Its day themes are organized around his biography: (1) faithfulness to vocation despite physical limitations, (2) balancing worldly success with the spiritual calling, (3) openness to God's will despite setbacks, (4) devotion through priesthood and perseverance, (5) service to the poor, (6) effective preaching, (7) missionary work, (8) renouncing material wealth, (9) theological study and writing.

Fidelity note

Ember does not transcribe the FishEaters prayer, but its themes are strikingly faithful to it: the FishEaters text's "fire of love," "the will of God the only rule of my life," "fervent and lasting love of Jesus," "tender and childlike devotion to Mary," "grace to pray without ceasing," and "persevere... to the end" map almost one-for-one onto Ember's days on the Eucharist, conformity to God's will, devotion to Mary, prayer, and final perseverance — so it reads as a faithful expansion of that single prayer into nine meditations. Against PMN it shares the nine-day meditation format and overlaps on leaving the world, the poor, and God's will, but Ember weights the days toward Alphonsus's writings and spiritual doctrine (The Glories of Mary, the Eucharist, "those who pray are saved," final perseverance) rather than PMN's biographical/ministry sequence. Wording is Ember's own throughout. Faithful, well-sourced, and notably close in spirit to the traditional FishEaters prayer; an original composition.

St. John Vianney st-john-vianney-novena

Jul 26–Aug 3
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. John Vianney, the Curé of Ars, patron of parish priests, whose austerity, zeal for souls, and long hours in the confessional drew sinners from across France back to God.

Uma novena de nove dias a São João Maria Vianney, o Cura d'Ars, padroeiro dos párocos, cuja austeridade, zelo pelas almas e longas horas no confessionário trouxeram de volta a Deus pecadores de toda a França.

Born near Lyon in 1786, John Vianney struggled greatly with his studies and was ordained only with difficulty. Sent in 1818 to the small, indifferent village of Ars, he transformed it by prayer, penance, and tireless preaching. In his last years he heard confessions for up to sixteen hours a day; pilgrims came from everywhere to seek him, and he is honored as the patron of priests.

Nascido perto de Lyon em 1786, João Maria Vianney teve grande dificuldade nos estudos e foi ordenado a custo. Enviado em 1818 ao pequeno e indiferente povoado de Ars, transformou-o pela oração, pela penitência e pela pregação incansável. Em seus últimos anos confessava até dezesseis horas por dia; peregrinos vinham de toda parte buscá-lo, e é honrado como padroeiro dos sacerdotes.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. John Vianney, humble priest of Ars, who spent yourself wholly for the salvation of souls, obtain for me a like hunger for God. By your prayers, draw me away from sin and closer to the heart of Christ. Amen.

Glorioso São João Maria Vianney, humilde sacerdote de Ars, que vos consumistes inteiramente pela salvação das almas, alcançai-me uma fome semelhante de Deus. Por vossas orações, afastai-me do pecado e aproximai-me do coração de Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: Perseverance against all odds

Dia 1: A perseverança contra tudo

John Vianney struggled bitterly with Latin and study, yet he never gave up his hope of the priesthood. He teaches us that holiness depends not on talent but on a will that refuses to surrender.

João Maria Vianney lutou amargamente com o latim e os estudos, mas nunca abandonou a esperança do sacerdócio. Ele nos ensina que a santidade não depende do talento, mas de uma vontade que se recusa a desistir.

Intention

For perseverance in pursuing what God asks despite my own weakness.

Pela perseverança em buscar o que Deus me pede, apesar da minha fraqueza.

Day 2: Zeal for souls

Dia 2: O zelo pelas almas

The Curé wept and pleaded with God for the salvation of his cold, indifferent parish. He shows us that true love for our neighbor begins with longing for their eternal salvation.

O Cura chorava e suplicava a Deus pela salvação de sua paróquia fria e indiferente. Ele nos mostra que o verdadeiro amor ao próximo começa pelo desejo de sua salvação eterna.

Intention

For zeal to desire and work for the salvation of those around me.

Pelo zelo de desejar e trabalhar pela salvação dos que me cercam.

Day 3: The ministry of the confessional

Dia 3: O ministério do confessionário

For up to sixteen hours a day the Curé sat in his confessional, reading hearts and pouring out God's pardon. He teaches us the immense power of the Sacrament of Penance to remake a soul.

Por até dezesseis horas diárias, o Cura permanecia em seu confessionário, lendo os corações e derramando o perdão de Deus. Ele nos ensina o imenso poder do Sacramento da Penitência para refazer uma alma.

Intention

For a sincere love of confession and the grace of true repentance.

Pelo amor sincero à confissão e pela graça do verdadeiro arrependimento.

Day 4: Penance and austerity

Dia 4: A penitência e a austeridade

The Curé lived on little more than boiled potatoes and fasted that sinners might be converted. He shows us that self-denial offered in love becomes a quiet power for the good of others.

O Cura vivia de pouco mais que batatas cozidas e jejuava para que os pecadores se convertessem. Ele nos mostra que a renúncia oferecida com amor torna-se uma força silenciosa para o bem dos outros.

Intention

For the grace to embrace small sacrifices for the love of God and souls.

Pela graça de abraçar pequenos sacrifícios pelo amor de Deus e das almas.

Day 5: Love of the Real Presence

Dia 5: O amor à Presença Real

The Curé spent long hours before the tabernacle and once described prayer as simply looking at God while God looks at us. He teaches us that the heart of every priest and every Christian is found before the Eucharist.

O Cura passava longas horas diante do sacrário e descreveu certa vez a oração como simplesmente olhar para Deus enquanto Deus olha para nós. Ele nos ensina que o coração de todo sacerdote e de todo cristão se encontra diante da Eucaristia.

Intention

For a deep love of Jesus truly present in the Blessed Sacrament.

Por um amor profundo a Jesus verdadeiramente presente no Santíssimo Sacramento.

Day 6: Humility

Dia 6: A humildade

Honored by all of France, the Curé thought himself unworthy and longed only to hide in prayer. He teaches us that the greater God raises a soul, the more deeply that soul bows before Him.

Honrado por toda a França, o Cura julgava-se indigno e desejava apenas esconder-se na oração. Ele nos ensina que, quanto mais Deus eleva uma alma, mais profundamente ela se inclina diante d'Ele.

Intention

For true humility that gives all glory to God and none to myself.

Pela verdadeira humildade que dá toda a glória a Deus e nenhuma a mim mesmo.

Day 7: Trust in St. Philomena

Dia 7: A confiança em Santa Filomena

The Curé attributed the cures and conversions at Ars to his beloved patroness, St. Philomena, the little saint. He teaches us to honor the saints and to trust the power of their intercession.

O Cura atribuía as curas e conversões de Ars à sua amada padroeira, Santa Filomena, a pequena santa. Ele nos ensina a honrar os santos e a confiar no poder de sua intercessão.

Intention

For confidence in the intercession of the saints in my needs.

Pela confiança na intercessão dos santos em minhas necessidades.

Day 8: Spiritual combat

Dia 8: O combate espiritual

The Curé endured years of torment from the devil, whom he wryly called the grappin, yet he was never shaken from his work. He shows us that the closer a soul draws to God, the more bravely it must fight.

O Cura suportou anos de tormento por parte do demônio, a quem chamava jocosamente de grappin, mas nunca se deixou afastar de sua obra. Ele nos mostra que, quanto mais a alma se aproxima de Deus, com mais coragem deve lutar.

Intention

For courage and steadfastness in the daily battle against temptation.

Pela coragem e firmeza no combate diário contra a tentação.

Day 9: A heart spent for God

Dia 9: Um coração consumido por Deus

Worn out by labor and love, the Curé died with the simple confidence that it is good to die when one has lived for God. He leaves us his great desire: to love God and to bring as many souls as possible to Him.

Esgotado pelo trabalho e pelo amor, o Cura morreu com a simples confiança de que é bom morrer quando se viveu para Deus. Ele nos deixa o seu grande desejo: amar a Deus e conduzir a Ele o maior número possível de almas.

Intention

For all priests, and for the grace to spend my life for the love of God.

Por todos os sacerdotes e pela graça de gastar minha vida pelo amor de Deus.

Closing prayer

Holy Curé of Ars, friend of sinners and lover of the Eucharist, intercede for all priests and for the conversion of poor sinners. Obtain for me the grace I now implore, and a heart that loves God as you loved Him. Amen.

Santo Cura d'Ars, amigo dos pecadores e amante da Eucaristia, intercedei por todos os sacerdotes e pela conversão dos pobres pecadores. Alcançai-me a graça que agora vos imploro e um coração que ame a Deus como vós O amastes. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts a St. John Vianney novena. Each day is wrapped in the Sign of the Cross and uses a fixed template: address to the saint touching that day's virtue, a petition for specific graces, then "Believing in the power of your kind intercession, I humbly ask you to pray for me…" (with space for a personal intention), and the refrain "St. John Vianney, Priest of Ars, pray for our priests, and pray for us." Its daily themes run: (1) courage/faith despite obstacles, (2) charitable service, (3) determination/perseverance, (4) moral renewal and compassion for sinners, (5) trust in God's will, (6) power of prayer and contemplation, (7) worthiness to receive the sacraments, (8) spiritual over material wealth, (9) zeal to share God's love.

Fidelity note

Ember is not a copy of the PrayMoreNovenas text but an independent composition drawn from the same well-known biography of the Curé. Both open and close by addressing the saint and both frame nine daily virtue-meditations, but Ember's wording is wholly its own and its day-by-day themes are richer and more specific (confessional, austerity, Eucharist, Philomena, spiritual combat) where PMN's are broader. The shared structure (Sign of the Cross, daily virtue + intention, Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be) reflects the common novena form rather than borrowing. This is a faithful original devotion, accurate to the saint's life, not a paraphrase of the source.

St. Philomena st-philomena-novena

Aug 1–9
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Philomena, the young virgin and martyr known as the Wonderworker, beloved patroness of the Curé of Ars, model of purity and courage, and powerful intercessor in difficult causes.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Filomena, a jovem virgem e mártir conhecida como Taumaturga, amada padroeira do Cura d'Ars, modelo de pureza e coragem, e poderosa intercessora nas causas difíceis.

In 1802 a tomb was found in the Catacomb of Priscilla in Rome bearing the name Filumena and the symbols of virginity and martyrdom, with a vial of dried blood beside the bones. Tradition holds she was a young girl who consecrated her virginity to Christ and died rather than betray it, around the early fourth century. After her relics were brought to Mugnano in Italy, many miracles followed, and St. John Vianney made her his celestial patroness.

Em 1802, foi encontrado na Catacumba de Priscila, em Roma, um túmulo com o nome Filumena e os símbolos da virgindade e do martírio, com um frasco de sangue seco junto aos ossos. A tradição diz que era uma jovem que consagrou sua virgindade a Cristo e preferiu morrer a traí-la, por volta do início do século IV. Levadas suas relíquias a Mugnano, na Itália, sucederam-se muitos milagres, e São João Maria Vianney fez dela a sua padroeira celeste.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. Philomena, young virgin and martyr powerful with God, you gave your life rather than surrender your love for Christ. Look upon me from heaven and obtain for me the courage to remain faithful to God in every trial. Amen.

Gloriosa Santa Filomena, jovem virgem e mártir poderosa junto de Deus, vós destes a vida em vez de renunciar ao vosso amor por Cristo. Olhai para mim do céu e alcançai-me a coragem de permanecer fiel a Deus em toda provação. Amém.

Day 1: Consecrated to Christ

Dia 1: Consagrada a Cristo

While still young, Philomena vowed her virginity entirely to Christ as her only spouse. She teaches us that the heart given wholly to God finds a love greater than any the world can offer.

Ainda jovem, Filomena consagrou sua virgindade inteiramente a Cristo, como seu único esposo. Ela nos ensina que o coração dado por inteiro a Deus encontra um amor maior do que qualquer outro que o mundo possa oferecer.

Intention

For the grace to give my heart wholly and undividedly to God.

Pela graça de dar meu coração por inteiro e sem reservas a Deus.

Day 2: Purity of heart

Dia 2: A pureza de coração

Philomena guarded her purity as a treasure dearer to her than life itself. She shows us that chastity is not a cold restraint but a radiant fidelity to the One we love.

Filomena guardou sua pureza como um tesouro mais precioso do que a própria vida. Ela nos mostra que a castidade não é uma fria privação, mas uma fidelidade luminosa Àquele que amamos.

Intention

For the grace of purity in mind, heart, and body.

Pela graça da pureza na mente, no coração e no corpo.

Day 3: Courage before power

Dia 3: A coragem diante do poder

Tradition tells that Philomena, though only a girl, refused the demands of an emperor who sought her hand. She teaches us that fidelity to God can make even the weakest soul unshakable.

A tradição relata que Filomena, ainda menina, recusou as exigências de um imperador que a queria por esposa. Ela nos ensina que a fidelidade a Deus pode tornar inabalável até a alma mais frágil.

Intention

For courage to stand for the truth even before the powerful.

Pela coragem de defender a verdade mesmo diante dos poderosos.

Day 4: Fidelity in suffering

Dia 4: A fidelidade no sofrimento

The symbols on her tomb — the arrows, the scourge, the anchor — speak of cruel torments borne for love of Christ. She teaches us that suffering accepted in faith binds us more closely to the Crucified.

Os símbolos de seu túmulo — as flechas, o açoite, a âncora — falam dos cruéis tormentos suportados por amor a Cristo. Ela nos ensina que o sofrimento aceito na fé nos une mais estreitamente ao Crucificado.

Intention

For strength to remain faithful to God in pain and adversity.

Pela força de permanecer fiel a Deus na dor e na adversidade.

Day 5: The crown of martyrdom

Dia 5: A coroa do martírio

At the last, Philomena was beheaded, sealing her witness with her blood. She reminds us that to lose one's life for Christ is to gain it forever in the glory of heaven.

Por fim, Filomena foi decapitada, selando com seu sangue o seu testemunho. Ela nos lembra que perder a vida por Cristo é ganhá-la para sempre na glória do céu.

Intention

For the grace to value heaven above every earthly comfort.

Pela graça de prezar o céu acima de todo conforto terreno.

Day 6: The hidden treasure unearthed

Dia 6: O tesouro oculto desenterrado

For centuries Philomena lay forgotten in the catacombs until God brought her to light in His own time. She reminds us that no act of faithful love is ever truly lost before God.

Por séculos, Filomena jazeu esquecida nas catacumbas, até que Deus a trouxe à luz no tempo d'Ele. Ela nos lembra que nenhum ato de amor fiel se perde verdadeiramente diante de Deus.

Intention

For trust that God sees and remembers every hidden act of love.

Pela confiança de que Deus vê e recorda cada ato oculto de amor.

Day 7: Powerful with God

Dia 7: Poderosa junto de Deus

After her relics were honored at Mugnano, countless miracles flowed through her intercession. She assures us that the prayers of the saints are mighty before the throne of God.

Depois que suas relíquias foram honradas em Mugnano, inúmeros milagres brotaram de sua intercessão. Ela nos assegura que as orações dos santos são poderosas diante do trono de Deus.

Intention

For confidence to bring my hardest needs to St. Philomena's intercession.

Pela confiança de apresentar minhas necessidades mais difíceis à intercessão de Santa Filomena.

Day 8: Friend of the Curé of Ars

Dia 8: Amiga do Cura d'Ars

St. John Vianney loved Philomena tenderly and credited to her the wonders worked at Ars. Their friendship shows us how the saints in heaven labor together for the souls on earth.

São João Maria Vianney amava Filomena com ternura e atribuía a ela as maravilhas operadas em Ars. A sua amizade nos mostra como os santos do céu trabalham juntos pelas almas da terra.

Intention

For the grace to seek the friendship and help of the saints.

Pela graça de buscar a amizade e o auxílio dos santos.

Day 9: Model for the young

Dia 9: Modelo para os jovens

Though young in years, Philomena was rich in faith, courage, and love. She inspires the young to give their lives to God now, without waiting, and to fear nothing but offending Him.

Embora jovem em anos, Filomena foi rica em fé, coragem e amor. Ela inspira os jovens a entregar sua vida a Deus desde já, sem esperar, e a nada temer senão ofendê-Lo.

Intention

For young people, that they may give their lives to God in courage and purity.

Pelos jovens, para que entreguem sua vida a Deus com coragem e pureza.

Closing prayer

St. Philomena, wonderworker and friend of the saints, by the power God has given you, present my petitions before His throne. Obtain for me the grace I now ask, if it be for God's glory and the good of my soul. Amen.

Santa Filomena, taumaturga e amiga dos santos, pelo poder que Deus vos concedeu, apresentai os meus pedidos diante de Seu trono. Alcançai-me a graça que agora vos peço, se for para a glória de Deus e o bem da minha alma. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="philomena">
Novena
to St. Philomena

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 2
August and ending on 10 August, the eve of the Feast of St. Philomena.

O faithful virgin and glorious martyr, St. Philomena, who works so many
miracles on behalf of the poor and sorrowing, have pity on me. Thou
knowest the multitude and diversity of my needs. Behold me at thy feet,
full of misery, but full of hope. I entreat thy charity , O great
Saint! Graciously hear me and obtain from God a favorable answer to the
request which I now humbly lay before thee.

(Mention your intentions here)

I am firmly convinced that through thy merits, through
the scorn, the sufferings and the death thou didst endure, united to
the merits of the Passion and death of Jesus, thy Spouse, I shall
obtain what I ask of thee, and in the joy of my heart I will bless God,
Who is admirable in His Saints. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts a St. Philomena novena. It is bracketed by the Sign of the Cross and uses one repeated prayer for all nine days, with no distinct daily meditations: "Dearest St. Philomena, your prayers are powerful because you are so close to Our Lord. Your child-like faith and devotion are an inspiration to all of the faithful." After a space for personal intentions it continues: "St. Philomena, at such a young age, you gave everything to Jesus to become a consecrated virgin for the Kingdom. Giving even more, you gave your life as you suffered a martyr's death to preserve your gift to our Lord.
