Pray for me, that I might have the same faith and willingness to accept God's will no matter the cost."

Fidelity note

Ember's text is an original composition, not drawn from PMN, which uses a single repeated prayer rather than nine themed days. The two share the same core facts of Philomena's traditional story — her childhood consecration of virginity, her martyrdom, and her power as intercessor ("wonderworker"/"powerful because so close to Our Lord") — and Ember even devotes Day 8 to her bond with the Curé of Ars. Where PMN gives one undifferentiated petition, Ember builds a fuller nine-day arc (consecration, purity, courage, suffering, martyrdom, hidden relics, intercession, friendship, youth). It is faithful to the devotion and to the tradition without paraphrasing any source.

St. Clare of Assisi st-clare-novena

Aug 2–10
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Clare of Assisi, the first Franciscan woman, who embraced Lady Poverty, founded the Poor Clares, and repelled invaders from San Damiano by holding aloft the Blessed Sacrament.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Clara de Assis, a primeira mulher franciscana, que abraçou a Senhora Pobreza, fundou as Clarissas e repeliu os invasores de São Damião erguendo o Santíssimo Sacramento.

Born to a noble family of Assisi in 1194, Clare heard St. Francis preach and fled her home on Palm Sunday night in 1212 to consecrate herself to Christ. At San Damiano she founded the Order of Poor Ladies, defending its radical poverty against popes who wished to soften her Rule. She is patroness of television, having seen a distant Mass upon her cell wall, and her body remains incorrupt in the basilica that bears her name.

Nascida em família nobre de Assis em 1194, Clara ouviu São Francisco pregar e fugiu de casa na noite de Domingo de Ramos de 1212 para consagrar-se a Cristo. Em São Damião fundou a Ordem das Pobres Damas, defendendo sua pobreza radical contra papas que desejavam suavizar sua Regra. É padroeira da televisão, por ter visto uma Missa distante projetada na parede de sua cela, e seu corpo permanece incorrupto na basílica que leva seu nome.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glorious St. Clare, mirror of holy poverty and bride of Christ, you left all things to follow the poor and crucified Lord. Draw my heart away from the passing things that weigh it down, and teach me to gaze, as you did, upon the face of Jesus. Amen.

Ó gloriosa Santa Clara, espelho da santa pobreza e esposa de Cristo, vós deixastes tudo para seguir o Senhor pobre e crucificado. Afastai o meu coração das coisas passageiras que o oprimem e ensinai-me a contemplar, como vós, o rosto de Jesus. Amém.

Day 1: Leaving all for Christ

Dia 1: Deixar tudo por Cristo

On Palm Sunday night, Clare slipped from her noble home through a sealed door and let Francis cut her hair, exchanging silk for a rough habit. She shows us that following Christ begins with a decisive break from whatever holds us back.

Na noite de Domingo de Ramos, Clara saiu de sua casa nobre por uma porta lacrada e deixou que Francisco cortasse seus cabelos, trocando a seda por um hábito grosseiro. Ela nos mostra que seguir a Cristo começa com uma ruptura decidida de tudo o que nos retém.

Intention

For the courage to renounce whatever keeps me from belonging fully to God.

Pela coragem de renunciar a tudo o que me impede de pertencer inteiramente a Deus.

Day 2: Love of Lady Poverty

Dia 2: Amor à Senhora Pobreza

Clare so loved poverty that she begged the Pope for the 'privilege of poverty,' the right to own nothing at all. She teaches that the soul empty of possessions has room for the riches of God alone.

Clara amava tanto a pobreza que suplicou ao Papa o 'privilégio da pobreza', o direito de nada possuir. Ela ensina que a alma vazia de bens tem espaço apenas para as riquezas de Deus.

Intention

For freedom from greed and a spirit content with God alone.

Pela libertação da ganância e por um espírito contente somente com Deus.

Day 3: The contemplative gaze

Dia 3: O olhar contemplativo

In her letters to Agnes of Prague, Clare urged her to gaze upon Christ, consider Him, contemplate Him, and desire to imitate Him. Her whole spirituality flows from this loving look fixed upon the Crucified.

Em suas cartas a Inês de Praga, Clara a exortava a olhar para Cristo, considerá-Lo, contemplá-Lo e desejar imitá-Lo. Toda a sua espiritualidade nasce desse olhar amoroso fixo no Crucificado.

Intention

For the grace to fix my eyes on Jesus amid the noise of the day.

Pela graça de fixar meus olhos em Jesus em meio ao ruído do dia.

Day 4: Devotion to the Eucharist

Dia 4: Devoção à Eucaristia

When Saracen soldiers scaled the walls of San Damiano, Clare, though gravely ill, rose and carried the monstrance to the threshold, and the attackers fled. She trusted that her true defense was the living presence of her Lord.

Quando soldados sarracenos escalaram os muros de São Damião, Clara, ainda que gravemente enferma, levantou-se e levou a custódia até o limiar, e os atacantes fugiram. Ela confiava que sua verdadeira defesa era a presença viva de seu Senhor.

Intention

For deeper reverence and trust before the Blessed Sacrament.

Por maior reverência e confiança diante do Santíssimo Sacramento.

Day 5: A mother to her sisters

Dia 5: Mãe de suas irmãs

As abbess Clare washed the feet of her sisters and served the sick with her own hands, ruling not by command but by example. She teaches that authority in the Church is the willingness to stoop and serve.

Como abadessa, Clara lavava os pés de suas irmãs e servia as enfermas com as próprias mãos, governando não pela ordem, mas pelo exemplo. Ela ensina que a autoridade na Igreja é a disposição de abaixar-se e servir.

Intention

For a humble, serving heart toward those entrusted to me.

Por um coração humilde e servidor para com aqueles confiados a mim.

Day 6: Patience in long suffering

Dia 6: Paciência no longo sofrimento

For twenty-eight years Clare bore illness without complaint, often unable to rise, yet still spinning cloth for altar linens from her bed. She shows that a hidden, patient suffering offered in love bears more fruit than great deeds.

Por vinte e oito anos Clara suportou a enfermidade sem queixas, muitas vezes sem poder levantar-se, ainda assim fiando tecido para as toalhas do altar de sua cama. Ela mostra que um sofrimento oculto e paciente, oferecido por amor, dá mais fruto que grandes feitos.

Intention

For patience to bear my own crosses in union with Christ.

Pela paciência de carregar minhas próprias cruzes em união com Cristo.

Day 7: Defending the Rule

Dia 7: Defendendo a Regra

Two days before her death, Clare received papal approval of her own Rule, the first written by a woman for women. Her steadfastness teaches us to guard with holy firmness the gift God has given us to keep.

Dois dias antes de sua morte, Clara recebeu a aprovação papal de sua própria Regra, a primeira escrita por uma mulher para mulheres. Sua perseverança nos ensina a guardar com santa firmeza o dom que Deus nos confiou.

Intention

For fidelity to my vocation and the duties of my state in life.

Pela fidelidade à minha vocação e aos deveres do meu estado de vida.

Day 8: Joy in simplicity

Dia 8: Alegria na simplicidade

Though her life was austere, Clare radiated a deep gladness, calling herself the 'little plant' of Francis and rejoicing in the smallest gifts of God. She teaches that true joy is not found in abundance but in gratitude.

Embora sua vida fosse austera, Clara irradiava uma alegria profunda, chamando-se a 'plantinha' de Francisco e regozijando-se nos menores dons de Deus. Ela ensina que a verdadeira alegria não está na abundância, mas na gratidão.

Intention

For a grateful heart that rejoices in God's small daily mercies.

Por um coração agradecido que se alegra nas pequenas misericórdias diárias de Deus.

Day 9: A holy death

Dia 9: Uma santa morte

As she lay dying Clare was heard to bless her own soul, saying, 'Go forth in peace, for you have followed the good road.' She invites us to so live that death may be only the threshold of the vision we have longed for.

Ao morrer, ouviu-se Clara abençoar a própria alma, dizendo: 'Vai em paz, pois seguiste o bom caminho.' Ela nos convida a viver de tal modo que a morte seja apenas o limiar da visão que tanto desejamos.

Intention

For the grace of a peaceful death in the friendship of Christ.

Pela graça de uma morte serena na amizade de Cristo.

Closing prayer

St. Clare, who held the Blessed Sacrament before the enemy and saw your sisters delivered, hold me before your Lord and shield me in every danger of soul and body. Obtain for me the grace I now implore, and a heart wholly given to Christ as yours was. Amen.

Santa Clara, que erguestes o Santíssimo Sacramento diante do inimigo e vistes vossas irmãs libertadas, erguei-me diante de vosso Senhor e protegei-me em todo perigo da alma e do corpo. Alcançai-me a graça que agora vos imploro e um coração inteiramente entregue a Cristo, como foi o vosso. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="clare">
Novena
to St. Clare of Assisi

Say once a day for nine days, especially beginning on 3
August and ending
on 11 August, the eve of the Feast of St. Clare.

First Day:

O most Holy Trinity, Father, Son and Holy Spirit, we praise Thy Holy
Name
and the wonders of grace Thou hast worked in Thy servant, Saint Clare.
Through
her powerful intercession grant us the favours we beg in this novena,
above
all the grace to live and die as she did in Thy most Holy Love.
Amen.

O Seraphic Saint Clare, first disciple of the Poor Man of Assisi, who
hast
abandoned all riches and honours for a life of sacrifice and of highest
poverty,
obtain from God for us the grace we ask (mention your intentions
here) , that
of always submitting to the Divine Will and of living confidently in
the
providence of our Heavenly Father. Amen.

V. Pray for us, Saint Clare.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Let us Pray. We Pray Thee, Lord, Grant us Thy servants who celebrate
the
festival of blessed Clare Thy Virgin, by her intercession, to be
partakers
of the joys of heaven and coheirs with Thine only-begotten Son, Who
being
God, lives and reigns forever and ever. Amen.

Second Day:

O most Holy Trinity, Father, Son and Holy Spirit, we praise Thy Holy
Name
and the wonders of grace Thou hast worked in Thy servant, Saint Clare.
Through
her powerful intercession grant us the favours we beg in this novena,
above
all the grace to live and die as she did in Thy most Holy Love.
Amen.

O Seraphic Saint Clare who, notwithstanding living separated from the
world
hast not forgotten the poor and the afflicted, but hast become a mother
to
them, sacrificing for them your riches and working for them innumerable
miracles;
obtain from God for us the grace we implore (mention your
intentions here) ,
Christian charity towards our brethren in all their spiritual and
temporal
needs. Amen.

V. Pray for us, Saint Clare.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Let us Pray. We Pray Thee, Lord, Grant us Thy servants who celebrate
the
festival of blessed Clare Thy Virgin, by her intercession, to be
partakers
of the joys of heaven and coheirs with Thine only-begotten Son, Who
being
God, lives and reigns forever and ever. Amen.

Third Day:

O most Holy Trinity, Father, Son and Holy Spirit, we praise Thy Holy
Name
and the wonders of grace Thou hast worked in Thy servant, Saint Clare.
Through
her powerful intercession grant us the favours we beg in this novena,
above
all the grace to live and die as she did in Thy most Holy Love.
Amen.

O Seraphic Saint Clare, light of your country, who hast delivered Italy
from
barbarous invaders; obtain from God for us the grace we implore (mention
your intentions here) , that of overcoming all attacks of the world
against
faith and morals thus preserving in our families true Christian peace
with
a holy fear of God and a devotion to the Blessed Sacrament. Amen.

V. Pray for us, Saint Clare.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Let us Pray. We Pray Thee, Lord, Grant us Thy servants who celebrate
the
festival of blessed Clare Thy Virgin, by her intercession, to be
partakers
of the joys of heaven and coheirs with Thine only-begotten Son, Who
being
God, lives and reigns forever and ever. Amen.

Fourth Day:

O most Holy Trinity, Father, Son and Holy Spirit, we praise Thy Holy
Name
and the wonders of grace Thou hast worked in Thy servant, Saint Clare.
Through
her powerful intercession grant us the favours we beg in this novena,
above
all the grace to live and die as she did in Thy most Holy Love.
Amen.

Blessed Saint Clare, whose very name means light, illumine the darkness
of
our minds and hearts so that we might see what God wishes us to do and
perform
it with a willing and joyful heart. Before your birth, a Heavenly voice
foretold
that you would be a light illuminating the world. Be a light to us in
the
sorrows and anxieties of this e

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts a St. Clare novena. Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross and a repeated address to the Trinity: "Dearest Holy Trinity, Father, Son and Holy Spirit. We praise you and the fruits of your grace that manifested themselves in your poor and gracious servant, Saint Clare." Each day then adds a short reflection on a distinct virtue — its themes run virtue, poverty, light, happiness, contemplation, peace, fearlessness, love, and prayerfulness — with a space for intentions and the refrain "Saint Clare, Pray for us! That we may be worthy of the promises of Christ," closed by the Sign of the Cross.

Fidelity note

Both are nine-day, virtue-per-day novenas, and the two overlap on real Clare themes — poverty, contemplative gazing on Christ, fearlessness before the Saracens, joy/happiness — but Ember's prose and selection are its own. Ember's opening directs its prayer to Clare herself rather than PMN's address to the Trinity, and Ember anchors several days in concrete biography (the Palm Sunday flight, the letters to Agnes of Prague, the monstrance at San Damiano, the twenty-eight years of illness, the deathbed words) that PMN states more abstractly. It is a faithful, biographically accurate original composition that parallels the source's structure without copying its wording.

St. Maximilian Kolbe st-maximilian-kolbe-novena

Aug 5–13
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Maximilian Maria Kolbe, the Franciscan friar and martyr of charity who gave his life for a stranger at Auschwitz, founded the Militia of the Immaculate, and lived a total consecration to Mary.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Maximiliano Maria Kolbe, o frade franciscano e mártir da caridade que deu a vida por um estranho em Auschwitz, fundou a Milícia da Imaculada e viveu uma consagração total a Maria.

Born Raymund Kolbe in Poland in 1894, as a boy he received a vision of the Virgin offering two crowns, white for purity and red for martyrdom, and he chose both. As a friar he founded the Militia Immaculatae and the great evangelizing centers of Niepokalanów and Nagasaki. Arrested by the Nazis, he volunteered to die in place of a married father in the starvation bunker of Auschwitz, and was put to death by lethal injection on August 14, 1941.

Nascido Raimundo Kolbe na Polônia em 1894, ainda menino recebeu uma visão da Virgem oferecendo-lhe duas coroas, a branca da pureza e a vermelha do martírio, e escolheu ambas. Como frade fundou a Milícia da Imaculada e os grandes centros de evangelização de Niepokalanów e Nagasaki. Preso pelos nazistas, ofereceu-se para morrer no lugar de um pai de família no bunker da fome de Auschwitz, e foi morto por injeção letal em 14 de agosto de 1941.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O St. Maximilian Kolbe, knight of the Immaculate and martyr of love, you laid down your life so that a father might return to his family. Kindle in me your fearless charity and your boundless devotion to Mary, that I too may live and die for others. Amen.

Ó São Maximiliano Kolbe, cavaleiro da Imaculada e mártir do amor, vós destes a vida para que um pai pudesse voltar à sua família. Acendei em mim a vossa caridade destemida e a vossa ilimitada devoção a Maria, para que eu também viva e morra pelos outros. Amém.

Day 1: The two crowns

Dia 1: As duas coroas

As a child Maximilian saw Our Lady hold out a white crown of purity and a red crown of martyrdom, and he answered that he would have them both. He teaches us to desire holiness boldly and to hold nothing back from God.

Ainda criança, Maximiliano viu Nossa Senhora estender-lhe uma coroa branca da pureza e uma coroa vermelha do martírio, e respondeu que queria as duas. Ele nos ensina a desejar a santidade com ousadia e a nada negar a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to desire holiness without fear or reserve.

Pela graça de desejar a santidade sem medo nem reservas.

Day 2: Total consecration to Mary

Dia 2: Consagração total a Maria

Maximilian gave himself entirely to the Immaculate, calling himself her property and her instrument in all things. He shows that to belong wholly to Mary is the surest road to belonging wholly to Christ.

Maximiliano entregou-se inteiramente à Imaculada, chamando-se propriedade e instrumento dela em todas as coisas. Ele mostra que pertencer totalmente a Maria é o caminho mais seguro para pertencer totalmente a Cristo.

Intention

For the grace to entrust my whole life to Our Lady.

Pela graça de confiar toda a minha vida a Nossa Senhora.

Day 3: The Militia of the Immaculate

Dia 3: A Milícia da Imaculada

He founded the Militia Immaculatae to win the whole world for Christ through Mary, using every modern means, from printing presses to radio. He teaches us to be zealous and inventive in spreading the Kingdom of God.

Ele fundou a Milícia da Imaculada para conquistar o mundo inteiro para Cristo por meio de Maria, valendo-se de todos os meios modernos, da imprensa ao rádio. Ele nos ensina a ser zelosos e criativos na difusão do Reino de Deus.

Intention

For zeal to spread the faith by every honest means within my reach.

Por zelo em difundir a fé por todos os meios honestos ao meu alcance.

Day 4: A builder of community

Dia 4: Construtor de comunidade

At Niepokalanów he gathered hundreds of friars into a 'City of the Immaculate' devoted to prayer, work, and the printed word. He shows that great works for God are built on humble obedience and shared life.

Em Niepokalanów reuniu centenas de frades numa 'Cidade da Imaculada' dedicada à oração, ao trabalho e à palavra impressa. Ele mostra que as grandes obras de Deus se edificam sobre a obediência humilde e a vida em comum.

Intention

For the grace to build up rather than tear down those around me.

Pela graça de edificar, e não destruir, aqueles que me cercam.

Day 5: Missionary to the East

Dia 5: Missionário no Oriente

Though in fragile health, Maximilian sailed to Japan and founded a friary on a hillside in Nagasaki that survived the atomic blast unharmed. He teaches us that love of souls overcomes weakness, distance, and fear.

Apesar da saúde frágil, Maximiliano partiu para o Japão e fundou um convento numa encosta de Nagasaki que sobreviveu ileso à bomba atômica. Ele nos ensina que o amor às almas vence a fraqueza, a distância e o medo.

Intention

For generosity to answer God's call beyond my comfort.

Pela generosidade de responder ao chamado de Deus para além do meu conforto.

Day 6: Charity behind the wire

Dia 6: Caridade atrás do arame

In the Auschwitz prison Maximilian shared his meager rations, heard confessions in secret, and comforted the despairing. He shows that even in the darkest place, a heart full of God becomes a light for others.

Na prisão de Auschwitz, Maximiliano partilhava sua escassa ração, ouvia confissões em segredo e consolava os desesperados. Ele mostra que mesmo no lugar mais sombrio, um coração cheio de Deus se torna luz para os outros.

Intention

For the grace to be kind to others in my own hardships.

Pela graça de ser bondoso com os outros em minhas próprias dificuldades.

Day 7: Greater love has no one

Dia 7: Ninguém tem maior amor

When a prisoner was condemned to starve and cried out for his wife and children, Maximilian stepped forward and said, 'I am a Catholic priest; let me take his place.' He fulfilled in his own flesh the words of Christ about laying down one's life for a friend.

Quando um prisioneiro foi condenado a morrer de fome e gritou pela esposa e pelos filhos, Maximiliano deu um passo à frente e disse: 'Sou sacerdote católico; deixai-me tomar o seu lugar.' Cumpriu na própria carne as palavras de Cristo sobre dar a vida por um amigo.

Intention

For a love willing to sacrifice itself for the good of another.

Por um amor disposto a sacrificar-se pelo bem do próximo.

Day 8: Singing in the bunker

Dia 8: Cantando no bunker

In the starvation cell Maximilian led the condemned in hymns and prayers to the Virgin until their voices fell silent one by one. He teaches that faith can transform even agony into worship.

Na cela da fome, Maximiliano conduzia os condenados em hinos e orações à Virgem até que suas vozes se calassem uma a uma. Ele ensina que a fé pode transformar até a agonia em adoração.

Intention

For trust and peace in the face of suffering and death.

Por confiança e paz diante do sofrimento e da morte.

Day 9: Martyr of love

Dia 9: Mártir do amor

Last to remain alive, Maximilian raised his arm in peace to receive the lethal injection on the vigil of the Assumption, going to the Mother he had served. St. John Paul II called him the patron of our difficult century, a witness that love is stronger than hate.

Último a permanecer vivo, Maximiliano ergueu o braço em paz para receber a injeção letal na vigília da Assunção, indo ao encontro da Mãe que servira. São João Paulo II o chamou padroeiro de nosso difícil século, testemunha de que o amor é mais forte que o ódio.

Intention

For the grace to overcome evil with love in my own life.

Pela graça de vencer o mal com o amor em minha própria vida.

Closing prayer

St. Maximilian, who turned a death camp into a place of song and hope, intercede for me before the Immaculate to whom you gave yourself wholly. Win for me the grace I now ask and a love so great that it counts no cost. Amen.

São Maximiliano, que transformastes um campo de morte em lugar de cânticos e esperança, intercedei por mim junto à Imaculada a quem vos entregastes inteiramente. Alcançai-me a graça que agora vos peço e um amor tão grande que não conte os sacrifícios. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="Max">
Novena
to St. Maximilian Kolbe

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 5
August and ending on 13 August, the eve of the Feast of St. Maximilian.

O Lord Jesus Christ, Who said, "greater love than this no man has that
a man lay down his life for his friends," through the intercession of
Saint Maximilian Kolbe whose life illustrated such love, we beseech
Thee to grant us our petitions.

(Mention your intentions here)

Through the Militia Immaculata movement, which Maximilian founded, he
spread a fervent devotion to Our Lady throughout the world. He gave up
his life for a total stranger and loved his persecutors, giving us an
example of unselfish love for all men -- a love that was inspired by
true devotion to Mary.

Grant, O Lord Jesus, that we too may give ourselves entirely without
reserve to the love and service of our Heavenly Queen in order to
better love and serve our fellow man in imitation of Thy humble
servant, Saint Maximilian. Amen.

Recite three Hail Marys
and a Glory Be <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts a St. Maximilian Kolbe novena. It is bracketed by the Sign of the Cross and uses one repeated prayer for all nine days rather than distinct daily meditations. The core prayer opens on Christ's words — "O Lord Jesus Christ, who said, 'greater love than this no man has that a man lay down his life for his friends'" — recalls that Maximilian founded the Militia Immaculata and laid down his life for a stranger while forgiving his persecutors, and petitions: "Grant, O Lord Jesus, that we too may give ourselves entirely without reserve to the love and service of our Heavenly Queen." Each day closes with three Hail Marys, a Glory Be, and the Sign of the Cross.

Fidelity note

Ember and PMN converge on the same two pillars of Kolbe's life — his total consecration to the Immaculate and his "greater love has no one" sacrifice for a fellow prisoner — but the texts are independent. PMN is a single repeated Christ-addressed prayer; Ember addresses the saint and unfolds nine distinct, biographically dense days (the two crowns, the Militia, Niepokalanów, Nagasaki, the Auschwitz bunker, the hymn-singing, the lethal injection on the Assumption vigil). Ember's added detail is historically accurate and the shared Marian/self-gift themes reflect the saint's own spirituality rather than borrowing. A faithful original composition.

Assumption of Mary assumption-novena

Aug 6–14
description & history

A nine-day novena in honor of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary, taken up body and soul into heavenly glory, crowned as Queen, and given to us as the sure hope of our own resurrection.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra à Assunção da Bem-aventurada Virgem Maria, elevada em corpo e alma à glória celeste, coroada como Rainha e dada a nós como segura esperança da nossa própria ressurreição.

From the earliest centuries the Church has believed that Mary, preserved from sin, did not suffer the corruption of the tomb but was taken up into heaven at the end of her earthly life. This ancient faith was solemnly defined as dogma by Pope Pius XII in 1950. The feast on August 15 celebrates the woman clothed with the sun of the Book of Revelation, crowned Queen of heaven and earth, the first of the redeemed to share fully in her Son's victory over death.

Desde os primeiros séculos a Igreja crê que Maria, preservada do pecado, não sofreu a corrupção do túmulo, mas foi elevada ao céu ao fim de sua vida terrena. Essa antiga fé foi solenemente definida como dogma pelo Papa Pio XII em 1950. A festa de 15 de agosto celebra a mulher vestida de sol do livro do Apocalipse, coroada Rainha do céu e da terra, a primeira dos remidos a participar plenamente da vitória do Filho sobre a morte.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Mary, assumed into heaven and crowned Queen of all creation, you are the first fruits of those who belong to Christ. Turn your merciful eyes upon me from your throne of glory, and draw me along the road that you have walked before me. Amen.

Ó Maria, assunta ao céu e coroada Rainha de toda a criação, vós sois as primícias dos que pertencem a Cristo. Voltai para mim os vossos olhos misericordiosos do vosso trono de glória e conduzi-me pelo caminho que percorrestes antes de mim. Amém.

Day 1: The lowly handmaid

Dia 1: A humilde serva

She who was exalted above all creatures first called herself the handmaid of the Lord and rejoiced that God had looked upon her lowliness. Mary teaches us that the way up to glory passes through humility.

Aquela que foi exaltada acima de todas as criaturas chamou-se primeiro a serva do Senhor e alegrou-se porque Deus olhou para a sua pequenez. Maria nos ensina que o caminho para a glória passa pela humildade.

Intention

For a humble heart that magnifies the Lord in all things.

Por um coração humilde que engrandece o Senhor em todas as coisas.

Day 2: Preserved from all sin

Dia 2: Preservada de todo pecado

Because Mary was kept free from the stain of sin, her body did not see corruption but was taken up whole into glory. Her purity reminds us that holiness is the seed of immortal glory.

Por ter sido preservada da mancha do pecado, seu corpo não conheceu a corrupção, mas foi elevado íntegro à glória. Sua pureza nos lembra que a santidade é a semente da glória imortal.

Intention

For purity of heart and freedom from the bondage of sin.

Pela pureza de coração e pela libertação da escravidão do pecado.

Day 3: Faithful to the foot of the Cross

Dia 3: Fiel até o pé da Cruz

Mary stood beside her dying Son when all but a few had fled, sharing his agony with unbroken faith. She who suffered most with Christ now shares most fully in his triumph.

Maria permaneceu junto ao Filho moribundo quando quase todos haviam fugido, partilhando a agonia com fé inabalável. Aquela que mais sofreu com Cristo participa agora mais plenamente do seu triunfo.

Intention

For perseverance in faith through every trial and sorrow.

Pela perseverança na fé em meio a toda provação e tristeza.

Day 4: The Woman clothed with the sun

Dia 4: A Mulher vestida de sol

St. John beheld a great sign in heaven, a woman clothed with the sun, the moon beneath her feet, and a crown of twelve stars. The Church sees in her image Mary glorified, victorious over the ancient serpent.

São João contemplou um grande sinal no céu, uma mulher vestida de sol, a lua sob seus pés e uma coroa de doze estrelas. A Igreja vê nessa imagem Maria glorificada, vitoriosa sobre a antiga serpente.

Intention

For confidence in Mary's victory over evil in my own struggles.

Pela confiança na vitória de Maria sobre o mal em minhas próprias lutas.

Day 5: Taken up in body and soul

Dia 5: Elevada em corpo e alma

At the end of her earthly course Mary was assumed whole and entire, body and soul, into the glory of heaven. Her Assumption proclaims that the body too is made for resurrection and eternal life.

Ao fim de sua jornada terrena, Maria foi elevada inteira, em corpo e alma, à glória do céu. Sua Assunção proclama que também o corpo é feito para a ressurreição e a vida eterna.

Intention

For reverence toward my body as a temple destined for glory.

Pela reverência ao meu corpo como templo destinado à glória.

Day 6: Crowned Queen of heaven

Dia 6: Coroada Rainha do céu

Christ the King welcomed his Mother to his side and set upon her a crown above all the saints and angels. Her queenship is not power for herself but mercy poured out for her children on earth.

Cristo Rei acolheu sua Mãe ao seu lado e colocou sobre ela uma coroa acima de todos os santos e anjos. Sua realeza não é poder para si mesma, mas misericórdia derramada sobre seus filhos na terra.

Intention

For trust in Mary's queenly intercession for my needs.

Pela confiança na régia intercessão de Maria por minhas necessidades.

Day 7: Our hope of resurrection

Dia 7: Nossa esperança de ressurreição

In Mary assumed into heaven we behold the destiny promised to all the faithful, body and soul reunited in glory. She goes before us as a sign of sure hope and comfort to a pilgrim people.

Em Maria assunta ao céu contemplamos o destino prometido a todos os fiéis, corpo e alma reunidos na glória. Ela nos precede como sinal de esperança segura e consolo para um povo peregrino.

Intention

For unshakable hope in the resurrection of the dead.

Pela esperança inabalável na ressurreição dos mortos.

Day 8: Mother of the pilgrim Church

Dia 8: Mãe da Igreja peregrina

From her place in glory Mary does not forget the Church still journeying through trial, but watches over us as a tender mother. She intercedes without ceasing until all her children are gathered home.

De seu lugar na glória, Maria não esquece a Igreja que ainda peregrina em meio às provações, mas vela por nós como mãe terna. Ela intercede sem cessar até que todos os seus filhos sejam reunidos na pátria.

Intention

For the whole Church on earth and the souls in purgatory.

Por toda a Igreja na terra e pelas almas do purgatório.

Day 9: To Jesus through Mary

Dia 9: A Jesus por Maria

Every glory given to Mary leads back to her Son, for she is the first and finest fruit of his redemption. She invites us to let her carry us, as she carried him, into the heart of God.

Toda glória dada a Maria conduz de volta ao seu Filho, pois ela é o primeiro e mais belo fruto de sua redenção. Ela nos convida a deixar que nos leve, como o levou, ao coração de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to follow Mary all the way to her Son in heaven.

Pela graça de seguir Maria até o seu Filho no céu.

Closing prayer

O glorious Queen of heaven, who shares now in body and soul the joy of your risen Son, obtain for me the grace I implore in this novena. Keep me faithful unto death, that at the last I may rejoice with you in the glory you already possess. Amen.

Ó gloriosa Rainha do céu, que partilhais agora em corpo e alma da alegria do vosso Filho ressuscitado, alcançai-me a graça que vos imploro nesta novena. Guardai-me fiel até a morte, para que ao fim eu me alegre convosco na glória que já possuís. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts an Assumption novena. Each day is bracketed by the Sign of the Cross and opens with the same fixed prayer (this is in fact the Collect of the feast): "Lord Jesus Christ, You have destroyed the power of death and given the hope of eternal life in body and soul. You granted your Mother a special place in your glory, and did not allow decay to touch her body. As we rejoice in the Assumption of Mary, give to us a renewed confidence in the victory of life over death. You live and reign forever and ever. Amen." Each day then adds a distinct intercessory prayer to Mary, a space for petitions, and the refrain "O Queen Assumed into Heaven, pray for us." Day 9 ends: "When our hour of death has come, lead us safely to the presence of Jesus to enjoy the vision of God for all eternity together with you."

Fidelity note

Ember and PMN share the doctrinal core of the feast — Mary taken up body and soul, preserved from corruption, crowned Queen, the pledge of our own resurrection and hope at the hour of death — and Ember's closing ("Keep me faithful unto death… rejoice with you in the glory you already possess") echoes the same note as PMN's Day 9, though in Ember's own words. The structures differ: PMN's fixed daily prayer is the liturgical Collect addressed to Christ, whereas Ember addresses Mary throughout and organizes nine themed Marian meditations (handmaid, Immaculate, Calvary, Apocalypse 12, Assumption, Coronation, resurrection-hope, pilgrim Church, "to Jesus through Mary"). The result is a doctrinally faithful original composition that parallels the source's themes without reproducing its text.

St. Bernard of Clairvaux st-bernard-novena

Aug 11–19
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Bernard of Clairvaux, the 'mellifluous Doctor' of the Church, Cistercian reformer, and tireless lover of Jesus and Mary, whose words on loving God still set hearts aflame.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Bernardo de Claraval, o 'Doutor melífluo' da Igreja, reformador cisterciense e incansável amante de Jesus e de Maria, cujas palavras sobre o amor a Deus ainda incendeiam corações.

Born near Dijon in 1090, Bernard entered the struggling monastery of Cîteaux at twenty-two, bringing thirty companions with him, and was soon sent to found Clairvaux. From his cloister he reformed monastic life, counseled popes and kings, and preached across Europe, earning the title 'Doctor Mellifluus,' the honey-sweet teacher. A burning lover of the Holy Name and of the Virgin Mary, he wrote 'On Loving God' and many sermons on the Song of Songs, and died at Clairvaux in 1153.

Nascido perto de Dijon em 1090, Bernardo entrou no combalido mosteiro de Cister aos vinte e dois anos, levando consigo trinta companheiros, e logo foi enviado a fundar Claraval. De seu claustro reformou a vida monástica, aconselhou papas e reis e pregou por toda a Europa, merecendo o título de 'Doutor melífluo', o mestre doce como o mel. Ardente amante do Santo Nome e da Virgem Maria, escreveu 'O Amor de Deus' e muitos sermões sobre o Cântico dos Cânticos, e morreu em Claraval em 1153.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O St. Bernard, mellifluous Doctor and faithful servant of Mary, your every word breathed love for Jesus Christ. Teach me to love God for his own sake, and to find in his Holy Name the sweetness that drew your heart heavenward. Amen.

Ó São Bernardo, Doutor melífluo e fiel servo de Maria, cada palavra vossa respirava amor a Jesus Cristo. Ensinai-me a amar a Deus por Ele mesmo e a encontrar no seu Santo Nome a doçura que elevava o vosso coração ao céu. Amém.

Day 1: Leaving the world

Dia 1: Deixar o mundo

The young Bernard renounced wealth and a brilliant future to enter the poor and rigorous monastery of Cîteaux, persuading even his brothers to follow. He teaches us that the love of God is worth every earthly sacrifice.

O jovem Bernardo renunciou às riquezas e a um futuro brilhante para entrar no pobre e rigoroso mosteiro de Cister, persuadindo até os próprios irmãos a segui-lo. Ele nos ensina que o amor de Deus vale todo sacrifício terreno.

Intention

For the courage to seek God above worldly success.

Pela coragem de buscar a Deus acima do êxito mundano.

Day 2: Loving God for His own sake

Dia 2: Amar a Deus por Ele mesmo

In his treatise 'On Loving God,' Bernard asked why we should love God and answered that God himself is the reason, and the measure is to love without measure. He calls us beyond fear and reward to a love that seeks God alone.

Em seu tratado 'O Amor de Deus', Bernardo perguntou por que devemos amar a Deus e respondeu que o próprio Deus é a razão, e a medida é amar sem medida. Ele nos chama para além do temor e da recompensa, a um amor que busca somente a Deus.

Intention

For a love of God that asks for nothing but Himself.

Por um amor a Deus que nada peça senão Ele mesmo.

Day 3: The sweetness of the Holy Name

Dia 3: A doçura do Santo Nome

Bernard wrote that the name of Jesus is honey in the mouth, melody in the ear, and gladness in the heart. He teaches us to call upon that Name often, as a remedy in temptation and a comfort in sorrow.

Bernardo escreveu que o nome de Jesus é mel na boca, melodia no ouvido e alegria no coração. Ele nos ensina a invocar esse Nome com frequência, como remédio na tentação e consolo na tristeza.

Intention

For the habit of calling on the name of Jesus throughout the day.

Pelo hábito de invocar o nome de Jesus ao longo do dia.

Day 4: Devotion to Our Lady

Dia 4: Devoção a Nossa Senhora

Bernard called Mary the aqueduct through which every grace flows down to us, and counseled, 'If the winds of temptation arise, look to the star, call upon Mary.' His tender confidence in her is the root of the Memorare tradition.

Bernardo chamou Maria de aqueduto pelo qual toda graça desce até nós, e aconselhou: 'Se sopram os ventos da tentação, olha para a estrela, invoca Maria.' Sua terna confiança nela é a raiz da tradição da Memorare.

Intention

For confident recourse to Mary in every need.

Pelo recurso confiante a Maria em toda necessidade.

Day 5: Never despair of Mary's help

Dia 5: Nunca desesperar do auxílio de Maria

From Bernard's teaching springs the prayer that no one who fled to Mary's protection was ever left unaided. He assures the most discouraged sinner that the Mother of Mercy turns no one away.

Do ensinamento de Bernardo nasce a oração de que jamais se ouviu dizer que alguém que recorreu à proteção de Maria tenha sido por ela desamparado. Ele assegura ao pecador mais desanimado que a Mãe de Misericórdia a ninguém rejeita.

Intention

For hope and trust that Our Lady never abandons her children.

Pela esperança e confiança de que Nossa Senhora jamais abandona seus filhos.

Day 6: Zeal for the Church

Dia 6: Zelo pela Igreja

From his quiet cloister Bernard was drawn into the great affairs of his age, healing a papal schism and counseling rulers with fearless honesty. He shows that the contemplative life bears fruit in love and service of the whole Church.

De seu claustro silencioso, Bernardo foi chamado aos grandes assuntos de seu tempo, curando um cisma papal e aconselhando governantes com destemida franqueza. Ele mostra que a vida contemplativa frutifica no amor e no serviço de toda a Igreja.

Intention

For courage to serve the truth even when it is unwelcome.

Pela coragem de servir à verdade mesmo quando é mal recebida.

Day 7: The bride and the Bridegroom

Dia 7: A esposa e o Esposo

In his sermons on the Song of Songs, Bernard described the soul as a bride longing for union with Christ her Bridegroom. He teaches that prayer is not mere duty but the ardent friendship of love.

Em seus sermões sobre o Cântico dos Cânticos, Bernardo descreveu a alma como uma esposa que anseia pela união com Cristo, seu Esposo. Ele ensina que a oração não é mero dever, mas a ardente amizade do amor.

Intention

For a prayer life enkindled by love rather than habit alone.

Por uma vida de oração inflamada pelo amor, e não só pelo hábito.

Day 8: Humility, the true foundation

Dia 8: A humildade, verdadeiro alicerce

Bernard wrote at length on the degrees of humility and pride, knowing that no virtue stands without a lowly heart. He teaches us that self-knowledge is the doorway to the love of God.

Bernardo escreveu longamente sobre os graus da humildade e do orgulho, sabendo que nenhuma virtude se sustenta sem um coração humilde. Ele nos ensina que o conhecimento de si é a porta para o amor de Deus.

Intention

For honest self-knowledge and a truly humble heart.

Pelo sincero conhecimento de si e por um coração verdadeiramente humilde.

Day 9: Longing for the vision of God

Dia 9: O anseio pela visão de Deus

All Bernard's labors flowed from one desire: to see and to enjoy God forever, the final degree of love when the soul loves itself only for God's sake. He invites us to live each day reaching toward that blessed vision.

Todo o labor de Bernardo brotava de um único desejo: ver e gozar de Deus para sempre, o último grau do amor, quando a alma só se ama por amor de Deus. Ele nos convida a viver cada dia estendendo-nos para essa bem-aventurada visão.

Intention

For the grace to long above all for the vision of God in heaven.

Pela graça de anelar acima de tudo pela visão de Deus no céu.

Closing prayer

St. Bernard, who taught us never to despair of Mary's help, present to her the petition I bring in this novena. Obtain for me a heart on fire with love of God, and lead me at last to contemplate forever the One whom you so longed to see. Amen.

São Bernardo, que nos ensinastes a jamais desesperar do auxílio de Maria, apresentai a ela o pedido que trago nesta novena. Alcançai-me um coração ardente de amor a Deus e conduzi-me enfim a contemplar para sempre Aquele que tanto desejastes ver. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts a St. Bernard of Clairvaux novena. Each day is bracketed by the Sign of the Cross and follows a fixed template: thanksgiving to God for giving Bernard as an example, an address to the saint touching one aspect of his life, the request "Please bring my petitions before God…", a petition for the reader's own spiritual growth (e.g., "Pray for me, that I may always seek to serve God worthily in my life, as you did. Pray that I may be eager to give my life to serving Him and His Church"), a space for personal intentions, and the refrain "St. Bernard of Clairvaux, pray for us!"

Fidelity note

Both are nine-day novenas built on episodes of Bernard's life, but the wording is independent. PMN keeps its daily focus fairly general (service to God and the Church); Ember draws far more directly on Bernard's own writings and reputation — "On Loving God" and its "love without measure," the Holy Name as "honey in the mouth," Mary as the aqueduct and the "look to the star, call upon Mary" counsel behind the Memorare, the Song of Songs sermons, and the degrees of humility. These are accurate to the Doctor's corpus, making Ember the richer and more sourced text. It is a faithful original composition that shares only the common novena scaffolding with the PMN source.

St. Louis IX of France st-louis-ix-novena

Aug 16–24
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Louis IX, King of France, the holy monarch who ruled with justice, loved the poor, led the Crusades, and enshrined the Crown of Thorns in the Sainte-Chapelle.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Luís IX, Rei de França, o santo monarca que governou com justiça, amou os pobres, conduziu as Cruzadas e abrigou a Coroa de Espinhos na Sainte-Chapelle.

Born in 1214 and raised by his devout mother Blanche of Castile, Louis became King of France at twelve and reigned for over forty years with rare integrity. He administered justice fairly, even beneath an oak at Vincennes, fed the poor at his own table, and built hospitals and abbeys. He ransomed the Crown of Thorns from Constantinople and raised the Sainte-Chapelle to house it, led two Crusades, and died of plague near Tunis in 1270; he is the only canonized king of France.

Nascido em 1214 e criado por sua piedosa mãe Branca de Castela, Luís tornou-se Rei de França aos doze anos e reinou por mais de quarenta anos com rara integridade. Administrava a justiça com equidade, mesmo sob um carvalho em Vincennes, alimentava os pobres à sua própria mesa e construiu hospitais e abadias. Resgatou a Coroa de Espinhos de Constantinopla e ergueu a Sainte-Chapelle para abrigá-la, conduziu duas Cruzadas e morreu de peste perto de Túnis em 1270; é o único rei de França canonizado.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O St. Louis, holy king and faithful father, you wore an earthly crown without losing sight of the heavenly one. Teach me to serve God in the duties of my state, to love justice, and to seek first the Kingdom that does not pass away. Amen.

Ó São Luís, santo rei e pai fiel, vós portastes uma coroa terrena sem perder de vista a celeste. Ensinai-me a servir a Deus nos deveres do meu estado, a amar a justiça e a buscar primeiro o Reino que não passa. Amém.

Day 1: Formed in the faith

Dia 1: Formado na fé

Louis often repeated his mother's words that she would rather see him dead than guilty of a single mortal sin. He shows that holiness is sown in childhood and that a parent's faith shapes a life.

Luís repetia muitas vezes as palavras de sua mãe, de que preferia vê-lo morto a vê-lo culpado de um só pecado mortal. Ele mostra que a santidade se semeia na infância e que a fé dos pais molda uma vida.

Intention

For families, that they may raise their children in the love of God.

Pelas famílias, para que criem seus filhos no amor de Deus.

Day 2: The dignity of baptism

Dia 2: A dignidade do batismo

Louis loved best the parish church where he was baptized, saying he prized that place above his coronation, for there he was made a child of God. He teaches us that our deepest identity is found not in rank but in grace.

Luís amava sobretudo a igreja paroquial onde fora batizado, dizendo que estimava aquele lugar mais que a sua coroação, pois ali fora feito filho de Deus. Ele nos ensina que a nossa identidade mais profunda não está na posição, mas na graça.

Intention

For the grace to live worthily of my baptismal calling.

Pela graça de viver dignamente o meu chamado batismal.

Day 3: Justice for all

Dia 3: Justiça para todos

Louis sat beneath an oak at Vincennes to hear the pleas of the humblest subjects, refusing to let power excuse injustice. He teaches that authority is a trust to be exercised for the good of others, never for oneself.

Luís sentava-se sob um carvalho em Vincennes para ouvir as causas dos mais humildes súditos, recusando que o poder servisse de desculpa para a injustiça. Ele ensina que a autoridade é um encargo a ser exercido para o bem dos outros, jamais para si mesmo.

Intention

For all who hold authority, that they may govern with justice.

Por todos os que exercem autoridade, para que governem com justiça.

Day 4: Love of the poor

Dia 4: Amor aos pobres

The king fed hundreds of the poor daily, served them with his own hands, and even tended lepers whom others shunned. He shows that the greatness of a soul is measured by its tenderness toward the least.

O rei alimentava diariamente centenas de pobres, servindo-os com as próprias mãos, e cuidava até de leprosos que os outros evitavam. Ele mostra que a grandeza de uma alma se mede pela ternura para com os menores.

Intention

For generosity and compassion toward the poor and suffering.

Pela generosidade e compaixão para com os pobres e sofredores.

Day 5: Penance and self-mastery

Dia 5: Penitência e domínio de si

Beneath his royal robes Louis wore a hair shirt and practiced fasting, ruling his own passions before he ruled a kingdom. He teaches that no one governs others well who has not first learned to govern himself.

Sob as vestes reais, Luís usava um cilício e praticava o jejum, dominando as próprias paixões antes de governar um reino. Ele ensina que ninguém governa bem os outros sem antes ter aprendido a governar a si mesmo.

Intention

For self-discipline and the spirit of penance in my daily life.

Pelo domínio de si e pelo espírito de penitência na minha vida diária.

Day 6: Honoring the Passion of Christ

Dia 6: Honrar a Paixão de Cristo

Louis received the Crown of Thorns walking barefoot in penance, and built the radiant Sainte-Chapelle as a worthy shrine for the relics of the Passion. He teaches us to reverence the sufferings by which Christ redeemed us.

Luís recebeu a Coroa de Espinhos caminhando descalço em penitência, e ergueu a radiante Sainte-Chapelle como digno santuário para as relíquias da Paixão. Ele nos ensina a venerar os sofrimentos pelos quais Cristo nos redimiu.

Intention

For a deeper love of the Cross and Passion of Our Lord.

Por um amor mais profundo à Cruz e à Paixão de Nosso Senhor.

Day 7: A devoted husband and father

Dia 7: Esposo e pai dedicado

Louis was a faithful husband to Queen Margaret and a careful father who wrote his son a famous letter urging him above all to love God and shun sin. He shows that holiness begins within the home and its daily duties.

Luís foi esposo fiel à rainha Margarida e pai zeloso, que escreveu ao filho uma célebre carta exortando-o, acima de tudo, a amar a Deus e a fugir do pecado. Ele mostra que a santidade começa dentro do lar e em seus deveres cotidianos.

Intention

For holiness lived faithfully in the duties of family and work.

Pela santidade vivida com fidelidade nos deveres da família e do trabalho.

Day 8: Courage in the Crusade

Dia 8: Coragem na Cruzada

Louis bore captivity, defeat, and disease on Crusade with unshaken faith, refusing to abandon his men or his trust in God. He teaches us to persevere with courage when our best efforts seem to fail.

Luís suportou o cativeiro, a derrota e a doença na Cruzada com fé inabalável, recusando-se a abandonar seus homens ou a confiança em Deus. Ele nos ensina a perseverar com coragem quando os nossos melhores esforços parecem fracassar.

Intention

For perseverance and trust in God amid failure and hardship.

Pela perseverança e confiança em Deus em meio ao fracasso e à adversidade.

Day 9: Faithful unto death

Dia 9: Fiel até a morte

Dying of plague near Tunis, Louis asked to be laid on a bed of ashes and breathed his last with the words, 'Father, into your hands.' He invites us to make our whole life a preparation for that final surrender to God.

Morrendo de peste perto de Túnis, Luís pediu para ser deitado sobre um leito de cinzas e expirou com as palavras: 'Pai, nas vossas mãos.' Ele nos convida a fazer de toda a nossa vida uma preparação para essa entrega final a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to surrender my life to God at the hour of death.

Pela graça de entregar a minha vida a Deus na hora da morte.

Closing prayer

St. Louis, who counted your baptism a greater honor than your throne, intercede for me before the King of kings whom you served so faithfully. Obtain the grace I seek in this novena, and a heart that rules its own desires for the love of God. Amen.

São Luís, que tínheis o vosso batismo por honra maior que o vosso trono, intercedei por mim junto ao Rei dos reis a quem servistes tão fielmente. Alcançai-me a graça que busco nesta novena e um coração que domine os próprios desejos por amor a Deus. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="louisix">
Novena
to King St. Louis IX

Pray especially starting on August 16 and ending on
August 24,
the eve of the Feast of St. St. Louis

O
God, the Being of all beings, Who art my God, prostrate before Thy
divine Majesty, I adore Thee and offer Thee my most humble homage. I
thank Thee for all the graces Thou hast bestowed upon Thy holy servant,
King Saint Louis, and I beseech Thee, through his merits and
intercession, to grant me the grace I ask of you in this novena.


(Mention
your intentions here)


Pray
one Pater and one Ave


Glorious
Saint Louis, pray for us, that we may become worthy of the promises of
Jesus Christ.

<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas hosts a St. Louis King of France novena. Each day is bracketed by the Sign of the Cross and follows a fixed template: thanksgiving to God for Louis as an example, an address to him recalling a specific virtue or event, the request "Please bring my petitions before God…", a "Pray for me, that I may…" petition, a space for intentions ("Please also pray for…"), and the refrain "St. Louis King of France, pray for us!" Its daily themes run: (1) upbringing in faith, (2) using temporal power to glorify God, (3) marriage and fatherhood, (4) promoting peace as arbiter, (5) judicial reform and justice, (6) penitential practices, (7) charity toward the poor and sick, (8) reverence for holy things, (9) overall devotion to God and Church.

Fidelity note

Ember and PMN draw on the same biography and overlap strongly on themes — formation in the faith, justice, charity to the poor, penance, family life, reverence for holy things — though Ember orders and frames them differently and adds vivid, accurate detail (Blanche of Castile's words, prizing baptism over the coronation, the oak at Vincennes, the hair shirt, the barefoot reception of the Crown of Thorns and the Sainte-Chapelle, the letter to his son, the "Father, into your hands" death). PMN's "promoting peace as arbiter" day has no direct Ember counterpart, and Ember's Day 9 deathbed scene is fuller. The wording is wholly Ember's own. A faithful, well-sourced original composition that parallels rather than copies the available source.

St. Monica st-monica-novena

Aug 18–26
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Monica, mother of St. Augustine, patroness of mothers, wives, and all who pray with tears for the conversion of those they love.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Mônica, mãe de Santo Agostinho, padroeira das mães, das esposas e de todos os que oram com lágrimas pela conversão dos que amam.

St. Monica (331–387) was a Christian mother in Roman North Africa who wept and prayed for seventeen years over the dissolute life of her brilliant son Augustine and the harshness of her pagan husband Patricius. Her perseverance won both their souls: Augustine became one of the Church's greatest saints, and a bishop assured her that 'the child of so many tears could never be lost.' She is honored as the model of the praying mother.

Santa Mônica (331–387) foi uma mãe cristã na África romana que chorou e orou por dezessete anos pela vida dissoluta de seu filho brilhante, Agostinho, e pela dureza de seu marido pagão, Patrício. Sua perseverança conquistou ambas as almas: Agostinho tornou-se um dos maiores santos da Igreja, e um bispo a confortou dizendo que 'o filho de tantas lágrimas não poderia se perder'. É venerada como o modelo da mãe que reza.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glorious St. Monica, mother of tears and of unfailing hope, you never ceased to pray for those entrusted to your love. Obtain for me a heart that does not grow weary in prayer, and present my petitions before the throne of God. Amen.

Ó gloriosa Santa Mônica, mãe das lágrimas e da esperança que não falha, vós nunca cessastes de orar pelos que vos foram confiados. Alcançai-me um coração que não se canse na oração e apresentai os meus pedidos diante do trono de Deus. Amém.

Day 1: Perseverance in prayer

Dia 1: Perseverança na oração

For seventeen years St. Monica prayed without seeing any fruit, yet she never abandoned her son to despair. She teaches us that God's delays are not refusals, and that love prays on through the long silence.

Por dezessete anos Santa Mônica orou sem ver nenhum fruto, e mesmo assim jamais entregou o filho ao desespero. Ela nos ensina que as demoras de Deus não são recusas, e que o amor continua a orar através do longo silêncio.

Intention

For the grace never to abandon prayer for those who have strayed from God.

Pela graça de nunca abandonar a oração por aqueles que se afastaram de Deus.

Day 2: Patience with a difficult husband

Dia 2: Paciência com um marido difícil

Married to the hot-tempered pagan Patricius, Monica bore his anger without bitterness and won him to baptism by her quiet goodness. She shows us that holiness lived steadily within the home can convert even a hardened heart.

Casada com o pagão de gênio violento Patrício, Mônica suportou suas iras sem amargura e o conquistou ao batismo por sua bondade silenciosa. Ela nos mostra que a santidade vivida com firmeza dentro do lar pode converter até um coração endurecido.

Intention

For peace and conversion within troubled marriages and families.

Pela paz e pela conversão dentro dos casamentos e das famílias atribuladas.

Day 3: Tears that pierced heaven

Dia 3: Lágrimas que tocaram o céu

Monica wept so long for Augustine that a bishop told her, 'It is impossible that the child of so many tears should perish.' Her sorrow was not weakness but a prayer the body offered when words ran out.

Mônica chorou tanto tempo por Agostinho que um bispo lhe disse: 'É impossível que o filho de tantas lágrimas se perca'. Sua tristeza não era fraqueza, mas uma oração que o corpo oferecia quando faltavam as palavras.

Intention

For all parents who weep for the faith of their children.

Por todos os pais que choram pela fé de seus filhos.

Day 4: Following her son in love

Dia 4: Seguir o filho com amor

When Augustine fled by ship to Italy to escape her, Monica followed him across the sea rather than let him go. Her love was relentless yet never controlling, pursuing him only toward God.

Quando Agostinho fugiu de navio para a Itália para escapar dela, Mônica o seguiu pelo mar antes que o deixasse ir. Seu amor era incansável, porém nunca dominador, perseguindo-o apenas em direção a Deus.

Intention

For the courage to keep loving those who pull away from us.

Pela coragem de continuar amando aqueles que se afastam de nós.

Day 5: Docility to wise counsel

Dia 5: Docilidade ao bom conselho

Monica sought out holy bishops and humbly accepted their guidance, even when told simply to wait and pray. She teaches us that perseverance is wedded to humility, trusting the Church to direct our zeal.

Mônica procurava santos bispos e aceitava humildemente sua orientação, mesmo quando lhe diziam apenas que esperasse e orasse. Ela nos ensina que a perseverança caminha unida à humildade, confiando à Igreja a direção do nosso zelo.

Intention

For humility to seek and follow holy counsel in our trials.

Pela humildade de buscar e seguir o santo conselho em nossas provações.

Day 6: Hope against all evidence

Dia 6: Esperança contra toda evidência

Through years of Augustine's heresy and pride, Monica clung to hope when every sign said her prayers had failed. She shows us that Christian hope is not optimism but a refusal to let God's mercy be smaller than our fears.

Atravessando anos da heresia e do orgulho de Agostinho, Mônica agarrou-se à esperança quando tudo dizia que suas orações haviam falhado. Ela nos mostra que a esperança cristã não é otimismo, mas a recusa de deixar a misericórdia de Deus menor que os nossos medos.

Intention

For hope to endure when conversion seems impossible.

Pela esperança que persevera quando a conversão parece impossível.

Day 7: The conversion of Augustine

Dia 7: A conversão de Agostinho

At last in Milan, under the preaching of St. Ambrose, Augustine surrendered to grace and Monica's long labor bore its fruit. She reminds us that God works in His own hour, and the harvest, when it comes, exceeds the sowing.

Por fim, em Milão, sob a pregação de Santo Ambrósio, Agostinho rendeu-se à graça e o longo trabalho de Mônica deu fruto. Ela nos lembra que Deus age em Sua própria hora, e a colheita, quando chega, supera a semeadura.

Intention

For one soul, named in our heart, to return to God.

Por uma alma, que trazemos no coração, para que volte a Deus.

Day 8: Resting in God's will

Dia 8: Repousar na vontade de Deus

Near death at Ostia, Monica told Augustine she no longer cared where her body lay, for nothing was far from God. Having received the one thing she had begged for, she was ready to go home in peace.

Próxima da morte, em Óstia, Mônica disse a Agostinho que já não se importava onde repousasse seu corpo, pois nada está longe de Deus. Tendo recebido a única coisa que suplicara, estava pronta para partir em paz.

Intention

For the grace to surrender our desires into the hands of God.

Pela graça de entregar os nossos desejos nas mãos de Deus.

Day 9: Patroness of mothers and families

Dia 9: Padroeira das mães e das famílias

Monica's only request of her sons was to be remembered at the altar of the Lord wherever they might be. She intercedes now for every family, drawing scattered hearts back toward the Mass and toward home.

O único pedido de Mônica a seus filhos foi que se lembrassem dela no altar do Senhor, onde quer que estivessem. Ela intercede agora por toda família, atraindo os corações dispersos de volta à Missa e ao lar.

Intention

For all families, that they may be reunited around the table of the Lord.

Por todas as famílias, para que se reúnam ao redor da mesa do Senhor.

Closing prayer

St. Monica, who saw your prayers answered beyond all your hope, intercede for me and for all I carry in my heart. Teach me to wait upon God with patience and to trust that no tear shed for love of souls is ever wasted. Amen.

Santa Mônica, que vistes as vossas orações atendidas além de toda a vossa esperança, intercedei por mim e por todos os que levo no coração. Ensinai-me a esperar em Deus com paciência e a confiar que nenhuma lágrima derramada por amor às almas se perde. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="monica">
Novena
to St. Monica

Say once a day for nine days, especially beginning on 25
April and ending
on 3 May, the eve of the Feast of St. Monica.

Exemplary mother of the great Augustine, thou didst perserveringly
pursue thy wayward son, not with wild threats but with prayerful cries
to heaven. Intercede for all mothers in our day so that they may learn
to draw their children to God. Teach them how to remain close to their
children, even the prodigal sons and daughters who have sadly gone
astray.

Dear St Monica, troubled wife and mother, many sorrows pierced thy
heart during thy lifetime. Yet thou didst never despair or lose faith.
With confidence, persistence and profound faith, thou didst pray daily
for the conversion of thy beloved husband, Patricius, and thy beloved
son, Augustine.

Grant me that same fortitude, patience and trust in the Lord. Intercede
for me, dear St. Monica, that God may favorably hear my plea for

(Mention your intentions here)

And grant me the grace to accept His will in all things, through Jesus
Christ, our Lord, in the unity of the Holy Spirit, one God forever and
ever. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

A 9-day novena that repeats one identical prayer every day (no separate opening prayer, no per-day intentions or meditations). The daily prayer reads: "Dear Saint Monica, you were once the mournful mother of a prodigal son. Your faithfulness to prayer brought you and your son so close to God that you are now with him in eternity. Please take my request to God with the same fervor and persistence with which you prayed for your own son. (Mention your intentions here) ... Above all, dear Saint Monica, pray for me, that I may, like your son, turn from my sin and become a great saint for the glory of God." Framed around her 17-year intercession for Augustine; begins Aug 18.

Fidelity note

Ember's version is an original composition, not a transcription of either source. It shares the universal St. Monica themes — 17 years of tears, perseverance in prayer, the bishop's "child of so many tears" line (which Ember quotes directly in Day 3), and her patronage of mothers — but PrayMoreNovenas uses a single repeated prayer with no daily structure, whereas Ember builds nine distinct biographical meditations. The wording is Ember's own, well-grounded in Monica's life from the Confessions. The match is thematic and structural-genre, not textual.

St. Augustine st-augustine-novena

Aug 19–27
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Augustine of Hippo, Doctor of Grace, for all who seek God through restless hearts, intellectual struggle, and the long road of conversion.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Agostinho de Hipona, Doutor da Graça, por todos os que buscam a Deus através de corações inquietos, da luta intelectual e do longo caminho da conversão.

St. Augustine (354–430), bishop of Hippo in North Africa, was one of the most influential thinkers in the history of the Church. After years of worldly ambition, a son born out of wedlock, and adherence to the Manichaean heresy, he was converted in a Milan garden at the words 'Take up and read.' His Confessions and City of God shaped Western theology, and his cry, 'You have made us for Yourself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in You,' remains the prayer of every seeking soul.

Santo Agostinho (354–430), bispo de Hipona, na África do Norte, foi um dos pensadores mais influentes da história da Igreja. Após anos de ambição mundana, um filho fora do matrimônio e adesão à heresia maniqueísta, converteu-se em um jardim de Milão ao ouvir as palavras 'Toma e lê'. Suas Confissões e A Cidade de Deus moldaram a teologia ocidental, e seu clamor, 'Fizestes-nos para Vós, Senhor, e inquieto está o nosso coração enquanto não repousa em Vós', permanece a oração de toda alma que busca.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O God who made the heart for Yourself, hear me through the intercession of St. Augustine, who sought You long and far before he found You within. Grant me the courage of his conversion and the fire of his love, that my restless heart may come to rest in You. Amen.

Ó Deus que fizestes o coração para Vós, ouvi-me pela intercessão de Santo Agostinho, que Vos buscou longa e distantemente antes de Vos encontrar em si mesmo. Concedei-me a coragem de sua conversão e o fogo de seu amor, para que o meu coração inquieto venha a repousar em Vós. Amém.

Day 1: The restless heart

Dia 1: O coração inquieto

Augustine confessed that he chased pleasures, honors, and false philosophies, yet found no rest in any of them. He teaches us that every restlessness is secretly a hunger for God, who alone can fill the heart He made.

Agostinho confessou ter buscado prazeres, honras e falsas filosofias, sem encontrar repouso em nenhum deles. Ele nos ensina que toda inquietude é, secretamente, uma fome de Deus, o único que pode saciar o coração que Ele fez.

Intention

For all who feel an emptiness no created thing can fill.

Por todos os que sentem um vazio que nenhuma coisa criada pode preencher.

Day 2: Honesty about sin

Dia 2: Honestidade diante do pecado

In the Confessions Augustine laid bare his sins with searching honesty, even the boyish theft of pears done only for the thrill of doing wrong. He shows us that conversion begins when we stop excusing ourselves before God.

Nas Confissões, Agostinho expôs seus pecados com honestidade penetrante, até mesmo o roubo juvenil das peras, feito só pelo prazer de fazer o mal. Ele nos mostra que a conversão começa quando deixamos de nos desculpar diante de Deus.

Intention

For the grace of an honest and contrite confession.

Pela graça de uma confissão honesta e contrita.

Day 3: Seeking truth with the mind

Dia 3: Buscar a verdade com a inteligência

Augustine wrestled for years with the Manichees and the philosophers before the truth of Christ overcame him. He reminds us that faith does not fear reason, and the searching intellect is itself a gift drawing us toward God.

Agostinho lutou por anos com os maniqueus e os filósofos antes que a verdade de Cristo o vencesse. Ele nos recorda que a fé não teme a razão, e que a inteligência que busca é, ela mesma, um dom que nos atrai para Deus.

Intention

For all who seek God through study and honest questioning.

Por todos os que buscam a Deus através do estudo e do questionamento honesto.

Day 4: Take up and read

Dia 4: Toma e lê

Weeping in a Milan garden, Augustine heard a child's voice chanting 'Take up and read,' opened St. Paul, and was finally freed from his old self. He teaches us that grace can break through in a single moment after years of resistance.

Chorando em um jardim de Milão, Agostinho ouviu a voz de uma criança a cantar 'Toma e lê', abriu São Paulo e foi enfim libertado do homem velho. Ele nos ensina que a graça pode irromper em um único instante, depois de anos de resistência.

Intention

For the grace to recognize and obey God's call when it comes.

Pela graça de reconhecer e obedecer ao chamado de Deus quando ele vier.

Day 5: The power of a mother's prayer

Dia 5: O poder da oração de uma mãe

Augustine owed his conversion in great part to the tears and prayers of his mother Monica, who never gave up on him. He shows us that no one is saved alone, and intercession is a true labor of love.

Agostinho deveu sua conversão, em grande parte, às lágrimas e orações de sua mãe Mônica, que nunca desistiu dele. Ele nos mostra que ninguém se salva sozinho, e que a intercessão é um verdadeiro trabalho de amor.

Intention

In thanksgiving for those who have prayed us toward God.

Em ação de graças por aqueles que oraram por nós para que chegássemos a Deus.

Day 6: Late have I loved You

Dia 6: Tarde Vos amei

Augustine grieved that he had loved God so late, having sought His beauty everywhere but within. He teaches us that it is never too late to begin, and that God has been near all along, waiting to be found.

Agostinho lamentou ter amado a Deus tão tarde, tendo buscado Sua beleza por toda parte, menos dentro de si. Ele nos ensina que nunca é tarde para começar, e que Deus esteve perto o tempo todo, esperando ser encontrado.

Intention

For all who think themselves too far gone to return to God.

Por todos os que se julgam demasiado perdidos para voltar a Deus.

Day 7: Shepherd of his people

Dia 7: Pastor do seu povo

As bishop of Hippo, Augustine preached, judged disputes, and fed the poor for thirty-five years, spending himself for his flock. He reminds us that holiness is proven not in feeling but in the daily service of others.

Como bispo de Hipona, Agostinho pregou, julgou disputas e alimentou os pobres por trinta e cinco anos, gastando-se por seu rebanho. Ele nos recorda que a santidade não se prova no sentimento, mas no serviço diário aos outros.

Intention

For bishops, priests, and all who shepherd God's people.

Pelos bispos, sacerdotes e por todos os que apascentam o povo de Deus.

Day 8: Defender of grace

Dia 8: Defensor da graça

Against those who claimed man could save himself by his own strength, Augustine taught that every good begins in the free gift of God's grace. He shows us to glory not in our own merit but in the mercy that lifts us.

Contra os que afirmavam que o homem podia salvar-se por suas próprias forças, Agostinho ensinou que todo bem começa no dom gratuito da graça de Deus. Ele nos mostra a não nos gloriarmos no próprio mérito, mas na misericórdia que nos ergue.

Intention

For the humility to depend wholly on God's grace.

Pela humildade de depender inteiramente da graça de Deus.

Day 9: Resting in God at last

Dia 9: Repousar enfim em Deus

Augustine died as the Vandals besieged his city, the penitential psalms pinned to his wall, his heart finally at rest in the God he had so long sought. He teaches us that the whole of life is a homeward journey into the peace of God.

Agostinho morreu enquanto os vândalos sitiavam sua cidade, com os salmos penitenciais afixados na parede, o coração enfim em repouso no Deus que tanto buscara. Ele nos ensina que toda a vida é uma viagem de regresso à paz de Deus.

Intention

For the grace of final perseverance and a holy death.

Pela graça da perseverança final e de uma santa morte.

Closing prayer

St. Augustine, who turned from the love of the world to the love of God, obtain for me the grace I now implore and a heart wholly given to the Truth. Late may I have loved You, O Beauty ever ancient and ever new, but let me love You without end. Amen.

Santo Agostinho, que vos voltastes do amor do mundo para o amor de Deus, alcançai-me a graça que agora imploro e um coração inteiramente entregue à Verdade. Tarde Vos amei, ó Beleza tão antiga e tão nova, mas concedei que Vos ame sem fim. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="augustine">
Novena
to St. Augustine

Say once a day for nine days, especially beginning on 19
August and ending
on 27 August, the eve of the Feast of St. Augutine.

First Day:

O holy Saint Augustine, who has famously declared that “Our hearts were
made for you, O Lord, and they are restless until they rest in you.”,
aid me in my own search for our Lord that through your intercession I
may be granted the wisdom to determine the purpose God has planned for
me. Pray that I be blessed with the courage to follow God’s will even
at times when I do not understand. Ask our Lord to lead me to a life
worthy of His love, that I may one day share the riches of His kingdom.
Petition our Lord and Savior to ease the burden of my problems and
grant my special intention, and I will honor you all of my days.

Beloved Saint Augustine the miracles you have performed for the greater
glory of God have caused people to seek your intercession for their
most pressing concerns. Hear my cries as I invoke your name to petition
God for an increased faith and to aid me in my present distress.

(Mention your intentions here)

Glorious Saint Augustine I boldly ask for your intercession confident
in your boundless wisdom and compassion. May this devotion lead me to a
life dedicated to the fulfilling the will of God that I may one day be
deemed worthy of sharing His Kingdom with you and all the saints for
all eternity.

Say one Pater, one Ave, and one Gloria

St. Augustine, pray for us! Amen.

Second Day:

O holy Saint Augustine, who has famously declared that “Our hearts were
made for you, O Lord, and they are restless until they rest in you.”,
aid me in my own search for our Lord that through your intercession I
may be granted the wisdom to determine the purpose God has planned for
me. Pray that I be blessed with the courage to follow God’s will even
at times when I do not understand. Ask our Lord to lead me to a life
worthy of His love, that I may one day share the riches of His kingdom.
Petition our Lord and Savior to ease the burden of my problems and
grant my special intention, and I will honor you all of my days.

Holy Saint Augustine, you have been plucked from a life of iniquity
through the incessant prayers of your mother Saint Monica to become a
staunch defender of the faith. Pray for me now and turn not from my
petitions, that I may also be blessed with God’s grace and guidance
through this present predicament into a life of complete subservience
to His will.

(Mention your intentions here)

Glorious Saint Augustine I boldly ask for your intercession confident
in your boundless wisdom and compassion. May this devotion lead me to a
life dedicated to the fulfilling the will of God that I may one day be
deemed worthy of sharing His Kingdom with you and all the saints for
all eternity.

Say one Pater, one Ave, and one Gloria

St. Augustine, pray for us! Amen.

Third Day:

O holy Saint Augustine, who has famously declared that “Our hearts were
made for you, O Lord, and they are restless until they rest in you.”,
aid me in my own search for our Lord that through your intercession I
may be granted the wisdom to determine the purpose God has planned for
me. Pray that I be blessed with the courage to follow God’s will even
at times when I do not understand. Ask our Lord to lead me to a life
worthy of His love, that I may one day share the riches of His kingdom.
Petition our Lord and Savior to ease the burden of my problems and
grant my special intention, and I will honor you all of my days.

Glorious Saint Augustine you are renowned in the Christian world for
your kindness, compassion and powerful intercession. Emboldened by
this, I humbly come before you to invoke your aid in asking for God’s
all-powerful assistance to see me through my most pressing concern.

(Mention your intentions here)

Glorious Saint Augustine I boldly ask for your intercession confident
in your boun

PrayMoreNovenas modern

A 9-day novena. Each day opens with the Sign of the Cross, then an identical core prayer — "O holy Saint Augustine, who has famously declared that 'Our hearts were made for you, O Lord, and they are restless until they rest in you'" — asking for wisdom, courage, and relief from burdens. Each day adds a unique daily meditation/intention, an invitation to "State the nature of your problem or the special favor you seek," then Our Father, Hail Mary, Glory Be, "St. Augustine, Pray For Us!", and a closing Sign of the Cross.

Fidelity note

Ember's text is an original composition, but it is the closest in spirit of the set: it shares PMN's signature framing device — Augustine's "restless heart" (Confessions I.1) — building the entire opening prayer around it, and echoes the "Late have I loved You, O Beauty ever ancient and ever new" line in both Day 6 and the closing prayer. Structurally Ember matches PMN's pattern of one fixed prayer plus nine distinct daily meditations, though all wording is Ember's own and the nine meditations follow a tighter biographical arc than PMN's looser daily themes.

Exaltation of the Holy Cross exaltation-holy-cross-novena

Sep 5–13
description & history

A nine-day novena for the Exaltation of the Holy Cross, adoring the wood of our redemption and learning to glory in the Cross of Christ.

Uma novena de nove dias pela Exaltação da Santa Cruz, adorando o lenho da nossa redenção e aprendendo a gloriar-nos na Cruz de Cristo.

The Feast of the Exaltation of the Holy Cross, kept on September 14, honors the Cross by which Christ redeemed the world. Tradition holds that St. Helena, mother of Constantine, discovered the True Cross in Jerusalem around 326, and a basilica was built on Calvary. What was an instrument of shameful death became the throne of glory, the sign in which the Church finds her boast and her hope.

A Festa da Exaltação da Santa Cruz, celebrada em 14 de setembro, honra a Cruz pela qual Cristo redimiu o mundo. A tradição diz que Santa Helena, mãe de Constantino, descobriu a Verdadeira Cruz em Jerusalém por volta de 326, e uma basílica foi erguida no Calvário. O que era instrumento de morte vergonhosa tornou-se o trono de glória, o sinal em que a Igreja encontra sua glória e sua esperança.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

We adore You, O Christ, and we bless You, because by Your holy Cross You have redeemed the world. Through this sacred sign of our salvation, hear the prayers I bring before You, and let the Cross be my strength, my refuge, and my glory. Amen.
