St. Faustina, faithful secretary of Divine Mercy, who hid the splendor of God's love beneath a poor religious habit, draw near to me now. Teach me to trust Jesus as you did, holding nothing back, and obtain for me the grace I seek in this novena. Amen.

Santa Faustina, fiel secretária da Divina Misericórdia, que escondestes o esplendor do amor de Deus sob um pobre hábito religioso, aproximai-vos de mim agora. Ensinai-me a confiar em Jesus como vós confiastes, sem nada reter, e alcançai-me a graça que peço nesta novena. Amém.

Day 1: Hidden holiness

Dia 1: Santidade escondida

St. Faustina spent her days in convent kitchens and gardens, unknown to the world, yet there she received the greatest of revelations. She teaches us that sanctity is not found in grand deeds but in humble fidelity to ordinary duties done for love of God.

Santa Faustina passou seus dias nas cozinhas e jardins do convento, desconhecida do mundo, e ali recebeu as maiores revelações. Ela nos ensina que a santidade não está em grandes feitos, mas na fidelidade humilde aos deveres comuns realizados por amor de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to serve God faithfully in the small, hidden duties of each day.

Pela graça de servir a Deus com fidelidade nos pequenos e ocultos deveres de cada dia.

Day 2: Total trust in Jesus

Dia 2: Confiança total em Jesus

At Christ's command, St. Faustina had the words 'Jesus, I trust in You' inscribed beneath His Image. She lived these words in the darkest hours, surrendering her fears, her illness, and her future entirely into His hands.

Por ordem de Cristo, Santa Faustina mandou inscrever as palavras 'Jesus, eu confio em Vós' sob a Sua Imagem. Ela viveu essas palavras nas horas mais escuras, entregando seus medos, sua doença e seu futuro inteiramente nas mãos d'Ele.

Intention

For the grace to abandon all my anxieties into the merciful hands of Jesus.

Pela graça de abandonar todas as minhas angústias nas mãos misericordiosas de Jesus.

Day 3: The Diary of mercy

Dia 3: O Diário da misericórdia

Out of obedience, St. Faustina recorded in her Diary the words and graces Jesus gave her, though she felt unworthy of so great a task. Through those pages the Lord still speaks to countless souls of His unfathomable mercy.

Por obediência, Santa Faustina registrou em seu Diário as palavras e graças que Jesus lhe dava, embora se sentisse indigna de tão grande missão. Por aquelas páginas o Senhor ainda fala a inúmeras almas de Sua insondável misericórdia.

Intention

For docility to God's will, even when it asks more than we feel able to give.

Pela docilidade à vontade de Deus, mesmo quando ela nos pede mais do que nos sentimos capazes de dar.

Day 4: Apostle of Divine Mercy

Dia 4: Apóstola da Divina Misericórdia

Jesus called St. Faustina the secretary and apostle of His mercy, charged to make it known to the whole world. She accepted this mission with joy, longing for every sinner to discover the ocean of mercy that awaits the contrite heart.

Jesus chamou Santa Faustina de secretária e apóstola de Sua misericórdia, encarregada de torná-la conhecida no mundo inteiro. Ela aceitou essa missão com alegria, desejando que cada pecador descobrisse o oceano de misericórdia que espera o coração contrito.

Intention

For the zeal to make God's mercy known to those who have lost hope.

Pelo zelo de fazer conhecida a misericórdia de Deus àqueles que perderam a esperança.

Day 5: Love for the suffering

Dia 5: Amor pelos que sofrem

St. Faustina bore the long agony of tuberculosis with patience, offering her pain for the conversion of sinners. She saw in suffering not a curse but a hidden treasure united to the Passion of her Lord.

Santa Faustina suportou a longa agonia da tuberculose com paciência, oferecendo sua dor pela conversão dos pecadores. Ela via no sofrimento não uma maldição, mas um tesouro oculto unido à Paixão de seu Senhor.

Intention

For the grace to bear my sufferings patiently and offer them for the salvation of souls.

Pela graça de suportar com paciência os meus sofrimentos e oferecê-los pela salvação das almas.

Day 6: Mercy in deed and word

Dia 6: Misericórdia em obras e palavras

Jesus asked St. Faustina to show mercy in deed, in word, and in prayer, withholding it from no one. She understood that to receive God's mercy we must first become merciful toward others.

Jesus pediu a Santa Faustina que praticasse a misericórdia por obras, por palavras e por orações, não a negando a ninguém. Ela compreendeu que, para receber a misericórdia de Deus, devemos primeiro tornar-nos misericordiosos para com os outros.

Intention

For a heart that is merciful to all I meet, in action, in speech, and in prayer.

Por um coração misericordioso para com todos que encontro, em ações, em palavras e em orações.

Day 7: Childlike simplicity

Dia 7: Simplicidade de criança

St. Faustina approached God with the confidence of a little child, certain of His tenderness toward the lowly. She teaches us that the soul most pleasing to Jesus is the one that trusts simply, without calculation or pride.

Santa Faustina aproximava-se de Deus com a confiança de uma pequena criança, certa de Sua ternura para com os pequeninos. Ela nos ensina que a alma mais agradável a Jesus é aquela que confia com simplicidade, sem cálculo nem orgulho.

Intention

For the grace of a simple, trusting heart before our heavenly Father.

Pela graça de um coração simples e confiante diante do nosso Pai celestial.

Day 8: Devotion to the Eucharist

Dia 8: Devoção à Eucaristia

St. Faustina drew all her strength from Jesus present in the Blessed Sacrament, where the same mercy flows from His Heart. Before the tabernacle she found the courage to fulfill the great mission entrusted to her.

Santa Faustina haurava toda a sua força de Jesus presente no Santíssimo Sacramento, de onde brota a mesma misericórdia de Seu Coração. Diante do sacrário encontrava a coragem para cumprir a grande missão que lhe fora confiada.

Intention

For a deeper love of Jesus truly present in the Holy Eucharist.

Por um amor mais profundo a Jesus realmente presente na Santíssima Eucaristia.

Day 9: The hour of mercy

Dia 9: A hora da misericórdia

Jesus revealed to St. Faustina the three o'clock hour as the hour of His death and of great mercy, when grace flows for the whole world. She invites us to pause in that hour and immerse our needs in the merciful Heart of the Savior.

Jesus revelou a Santa Faustina a hora das três como a hora de Sua morte e de grande misericórdia, quando a graça jorra para o mundo inteiro. Ela nos convida a deter-nos nessa hora e a mergulhar as nossas necessidades no Coração misericordioso do Salvador.

Intention

For perseverance in trusting God's mercy until my last breath.

Pela perseverança em confiar na misericórdia de Deus até o meu último suspiro.

Closing prayer

St. Faustina, apostle of the merciful Heart of Jesus, who promised that nothing is too great for the soul that trusts, intercede for me before the throne of mercy. May I, like you, repeat in every joy and every sorrow, 'Jesus, I trust in You,' until His mercy is my eternal home. Amen.

Santa Faustina, apóstola do Coração misericordioso de Jesus, que prometestes que nada é grande demais para a alma que confia, intercedei por mim diante do trono da misericórdia. Que eu, como vós, repita em toda alegria e em toda dor: 'Jesus, eu confio em Vós', até que a Sua misericórdia seja a minha morada eterna. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

With no FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas counterpart, there is nothing to measure verbal fidelity against. The content is doctrinally sound and faithful to St. Faustina's biography and spirituality as recorded in her Diary — "Jesus, I trust in You," the apostolate of mercy, the Image, the Hour of Mercy, her hidden convent life and death at thirty-three. Themes deliberately echo motifs of the canonical Divine Mercy devotion without copying its fixed daily intentions. Treat as an original, sourced-by-spirituality composition rather than a transcription.

Our Lady of the Rosary our-lady-of-the-rosary-novena

Sep 28–Oct 6
description & history

A nine-day novena to Our Lady of the Rosary, who through her beads leads us into the mysteries of Christ's life. In Mary's school of contemplation we learn to pray the Gospel and to entrust our battles to her victorious intercession.

Uma novena de nove dias a Nossa Senhora do Rosário, que por suas contas nos conduz aos mistérios da vida de Cristo. Na escola de contemplação de Maria aprendemos a rezar o Evangelho e a confiar as nossas batalhas à sua intercessão vitoriosa.

The Holy Rosary, woven through centuries of Christian devotion and long associated with St. Dominic and the Order of Preachers, is the Gospel itself prayed on beads, leading the soul through the joyful, sorrowful, and glorious mysteries of Christ. The feast of Our Lady of the Rosary commemorates the victory of the Christian fleet at Lepanto on October 7, 1571, which Pope St. Pius V attributed to the Rosary prayed by the faithful throughout Christendom. In gratitude the Pope honored Our Lady under the title of Victory, later renamed the Rosary by Pope Gregory XIII. Through countless apparitions, including Fatima, Our Lady has continued to call her children to this prayer as a remedy for the world's ills.

O Santo Rosário, tecido ao longo de séculos de devoção cristã e há muito associado a São Domingos e à Ordem dos Pregadores, é o próprio Evangelho rezado em contas, conduzindo a alma pelos mistérios gozosos, dolorosos e gloriosos de Cristo. A festa de Nossa Senhora do Rosário comemora a vitória da frota cristã em Lepanto, em 7 de outubro de 1571, que o Papa São Pio V atribuiu ao Rosário rezado pelos fiéis em toda a cristandade. Em gratidão, o Papa honrou Nossa Senhora sob o título da Vitória, mais tarde renomeado como do Rosário pelo Papa Gregório XIII. Por inúmeras aparições, entre elas Fátima, Nossa Senhora continuou a chamar seus filhos a esta oração como remédio para os males do mundo.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Queen of the Holy Rosary, who delights to lead your children through the mysteries of your Son, gather me now beneath your mantle. Teach me to pray the Gospel with your own loving heart, and obtain for me the grace I implore in this novena. Amen.

Ó Rainha do Santíssimo Rosário, que vos comprazeis em conduzir vossos filhos pelos mistérios de vosso Filho, recolhei-me agora sob o vosso manto. Ensinai-me a rezar o Evangelho com o vosso próprio coração amoroso, e alcançai-me a graça que imploro nesta novena. Amém.

Day 1: The Gospel in prayer

Dia 1: O Evangelho em oração

The Rosary is the whole Gospel gathered into a garland of prayer, where we walk with Christ from Nazareth to Calvary to the glory of heaven. Mary teaches us that to pray the Rosary is to learn Jesus from the one who knew Him best.

O Rosário é o Evangelho inteiro recolhido numa coroa de oração, na qual caminhamos com Cristo de Nazaré ao Calvário e à glória do céu. Maria nos ensina que rezar o Rosário é aprender Jesus daquela que melhor O conheceu.

Intention

For the grace to contemplate the life of Christ through Mary's eyes.

Pela graça de contemplar a vida de Cristo pelos olhos de Maria.

Day 2: The joyful mysteries

Dia 2: Os mistérios gozosos

In the joyful mysteries Mary welcomes the angel, visits Elizabeth, gives birth in poverty, and finds her Son in the temple. She shows us how to receive God's coming with humble joy amid the ordinary events of life.

Nos mistérios gozosos Maria acolhe o anjo, visita Isabel, dá à luz na pobreza e reencontra seu Filho no templo. Ela nos mostra como receber a vinda de Deus com humilde alegria em meio aos acontecimentos comuns da vida.

Intention

For joy in welcoming Christ into the daily moments of our lives.

Pela alegria de acolher Cristo nos momentos cotidianos de nossas vidas.

Day 3: The luminous mysteries

Dia 3: Os mistérios luminosos

The luminous mysteries shine upon Christ's public ministry, from His baptism to the gift of the Eucharist. At Cana, Mary's words still echo: 'Do whatever He tells you,' guiding us to obey her Son in all things.

Os mistérios luminosos iluminam o ministério público de Cristo, desde o batismo até o dom da Eucaristia. Em Caná, as palavras de Maria ainda ressoam: 'Fazei tudo o que Ele vos disser', conduzindo-nos a obedecer ao seu Filho em tudo.

Intention

For the grace to listen to Jesus and do whatever He tells us.

Pela graça de escutar Jesus e fazer tudo o que Ele nos disser.

Day 4: The sorrowful mysteries

Dia 4: Os mistérios dolorosos

In the sorrowful mysteries we kneel with Mary at the foot of the Cross, sharing the agony, the scourging, and the death of her Son. She teaches us to unite our own sufferings to His and to find in them the price of our redemption.

Nos mistérios dolorosos ajoelhamos com Maria ao pé da Cruz, partilhando a agonia, a flagelação e a morte de seu Filho. Ela nos ensina a unir os nossos sofrimentos aos d'Ele e a neles encontrar o preço da nossa redenção.

Intention

For the grace to carry our crosses united to the Passion of Christ.

Pela graça de carregar nossas cruzes unidos à Paixão de Cristo.

Day 5: The glorious mysteries

Dia 5: Os mistérios gloriosos

The glorious mysteries lift our hearts to the Resurrection, the Ascension, and Mary's own crowning in heaven. They remind us that sorrow is never the final word, and that the same glory is promised to those who persevere.

Os mistérios gloriosos elevam nossos corações à Ressurreição, à Ascensão e à própria coroação de Maria no céu. Eles nos lembram que a dor nunca é a palavra final e que a mesma glória é prometida aos que perseveram.

Intention

For unwavering hope in the resurrection and the glory to come.

Pela esperança inabalável na ressurreição e na glória futura.

Day 6: The victory of Lepanto

Dia 6: A vitória de Lepanto

When Christendom faced ruin at Lepanto, Pope St. Pius V summoned the faithful to the Rosary, and the victory that followed was credited to Our Lady. She shows us that prayer is a true power that turns the course of history.

Quando a cristandade enfrentava a ruína em Lepanto, o Papa São Pio V convocou os fiéis ao Rosário, e a vitória que se seguiu foi atribuída a Nossa Senhora. Ela nos mostra que a oração é uma verdadeira força que muda o curso da história.

Intention

For confidence in the Rosary as a weapon against every evil.

Pela confiança no Rosário como arma contra todo mal.

Day 7: Mary's school of contemplation

Dia 7: A escola de contemplação de Maria

Scripture says that Mary kept all these things, pondering them in her heart, and in the Rosary she shares that contemplation with us. Bead by bead she draws us into the silence where the soul gazes upon the face of Christ.

A Escritura diz que Maria guardava todas estas coisas, meditando-as em seu coração, e no Rosário ela partilha conosco essa contemplação. Conta por conta ela nos atrai ao silêncio em que a alma contempla o rosto de Cristo.

Intention

For a contemplative heart that ponders the mysteries of God in silence.

Por um coração contemplativo que medita em silêncio os mistérios de Deus.

Day 8: A remedy for the world

Dia 8: Um remédio para o mundo

At Fatima and elsewhere, Our Lady pleaded with her children to pray the Rosary daily for peace and the conversion of sinners. She offers this humble prayer as a gentle remedy for a wounded and restless world.

Em Fátima e em outros lugares, Nossa Senhora suplicou a seus filhos que rezassem o Rosário diariamente pela paz e pela conversão dos pecadores. Ela oferece esta humilde oração como suave remédio para um mundo ferido e inquieto.

Intention

For peace in our world and the conversion of all sinners.

Pela paz no mundo e pela conversão de todos os pecadores.

Day 9: Our Lady's faithful child

Dia 9: Filho fiel de Nossa Senhora

To take up the Rosary daily is to place one's hand in Mary's and let her lead the way to Jesus. She promises her constant protection to all who honor her through this faithful and persevering prayer.

Tomar o Rosário cada dia é colocar a própria mão na de Maria e deixar que ela conduza o caminho até Jesus. Ela promete sua proteção constante a todos os que a honram por meio desta oração fiel e perseverante.

Intention

For the grace of fidelity to the daily Rosary all our lives.

Pela graça da fidelidade ao Rosário diário por toda a vida.

Closing prayer

O Queen of the Holy Rosary, terror of demons and help of Christians, who at Lepanto turned defeat into triumph, fight now for me in every spiritual battle. Bind me ever closer to your Son through your most holy beads, that I may share at last in the glory of His mysteries. Amen.

Ó Rainha do Santíssimo Rosário, terror dos demônios e auxílio dos cristãos, que em Lepanto transformastes a derrota em triunfo, combatei agora por mim em toda batalha espiritual. Uni-me cada vez mais ao vosso Filho por vossas santíssimas contas, para que eu participe enfim da glória de Seus mistérios. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

Novena to Our Lady of Victory

``Where
the Bishop is, there let the multitude of believers be;

even as where Jesus is, there is the Catholic Church'' Ignatius of
Antioch, 1st c. A.D

Novena to Our Lady of Victory

V. In the Name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.

R. Amen.

Prayer to Our Lady,
Help of Christians

V: O Virgin most powerful, loving helper of the Christian people,

R: How great thanks do we not owe thee for the assistance thou didst
give our fathers, who, when they were threatened by the Turkish
Infidels at Lepanto, invoked thy maternal help by the devout recitation
of thy Rosary!

V: From heaven thou didst see their deadly peril;

R: Thou didst hear the voices imploring thy compassion;

V: And their humble prayers, enjoined by the great Pope, St. Pius V,
were acceptable unto thee;

R: And thou camest quickly to deliver them.

V: Grant, dear Mother, that in like manner the prolonged sighs of the
holy Bride of Christ in these our days may come to thy throne and
engage thy pity;

R: Do thou, moved anew to compassion for her, rise once again to
deliver her from the many foes who encompass her on every side.

V: Even now from the four quarters of the earth there arises to thy
throne that loved prayer, the Holy Rosary,

R: To win thy mercy in these troubled times, even as of old. Yet
unhappily our sins hinder, or at least, retard its effect.

V: Wherefore, dear Mother, obtain for us true sorrow for our sins,

R: And a firm resolution to face death itself rather than return to our
former iniquities; we are sore distressed that through our fault thy
help, of which we stand in such extreme need, should be denied or come
too late.

V: Rise, then, O Mary, incline thyself to hear the prayers of the whole
Catholic world,

R: And beat flat to the ground the pride of those wretched men, who in
their insolence blaspheme Almighty God and would destroy His Church,
against which, according to the infallible words of Christ, the gates
of hell shall never prevail.

V: Let it be seen once more,

R: That when thou dost arise to protect the Church, her victory is
sure. Amen.

V: Blessed Mother, Help of Christians,

R: Pray for us.

Prayer to Our Lady
of the Rosary

V: O Queen of the Most Holy Rosary,

R: In these times of such brazen impiety, manifest thy power with the
signs of thine ancient victories, and from thy throne, whence thou dost
dispense pardon and graces, mercifully regard the Church of thy Son,
His Vicar on earth, and every order of clergy and laity who are sore
oppressed in the mighty conflict.

V: Do thou, who are the powerful vanquisher of all heresies, hasten the
hour of mercy,

R: Even though the hour of God’s justice is every day provoked by the
countless sins of men. For me who am the least of men, kneeling
before thee in supplication, do thou obtain the grace I need to live
righteously upon earth and to reign among the just in heaven; the while
in company with all the faithful Christians throughout the world, I
salute thee and acclaim thee as Queen of the Most Holy Rosary.

V: Queen of the Most Holy Rosary,

R: Pray for us.

Prayer to Our Lady
of Victory

V: O Mary, powerful Virgin, thou art the mighty and glorious protector
of the Church;

R: Thou art the marvelous help of Christians,

V: Thou art terrible as an army in battle array;

R: Thou alone hast destroyed every heresy in the whole world.

V: In the midst of our anguish, our struggles and our distress,

R: Defend us from the power of the enemy and at the hour of our death
receive our souls in paradise. Amen.

V. Our Lady of Victory, we beseech thy intercession before the throne
of thy Son, that ( state your
intentions here )

V. If it be for God’s glory and honor and our good.

R. Amen.

V. Our Lady of Victo

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross and a fixed thanksgiving ("Dear Lord, we thank You for giving us Your mother as Our Lady of the Rosary. Help us to turn to her as our mother in all of our needs"), with a distinct daily meditation, a personal-intentions line, and the refrain "Our Lady of the Rosary, pray for us!" Daily themes: 1–2 growth in devotion to Mary and the Rosary, 3 faith, 4 perseverance in prayer, 5 persecuted Christians, 6 freedom to practice the faith, 7 peace in the world, 8 conversion of those hostile to Christianity, 9 the Church. Lepanto frames the historical reflections throughout.

Fidelity note

Ember's novena is an original composition with a different organizing principle: it walks through the Rosary itself (Gospel-in-prayer, then the joyful/luminous/sorrowful/glorious mysteries on days 2–5) before turning to Lepanto, Mary's school of contemplation, Fatima as a remedy for the world, and fidelity to the daily Rosary. It shares the Lepanto/Pius V history and the petitions for peace and conversion of sinners with both sources, but the mystery-by-mystery structure is its own and is catechetically richer than PrayMore's theme list. The FishEaters page is not a true match in form. Wording is Ember's own throughout.

St. Teresa of Ávila st-teresa-of-avila-novena

Oct 6–14
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Teresa of Ávila, Doctor of the Church and reformer of Carmel, who taught that prayer is friendship with the Christ who loves us. Through her example we learn to seek God within the interior castle of the soul.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Teresa de Jesus, Doutora da Igreja e reformadora do Carmelo, que ensinou que a oração é amizade com o Cristo que nos ama. Por seu exemplo aprendemos a buscar a Deus no castelo interior da alma.

Teresa de Cepeda y Ahumada was born in Ávila, Spain, in 1515, and entered the Carmelite convent of the Incarnation as a young woman. After years of mediocrity she experienced a profound conversion before an image of the wounded Christ, and was drawn into a deep life of mystical prayer, including the famous transverberation in which an angel pierced her heart with a fiery dart. Despite poor health and fierce opposition, she founded seventeen reformed convents of the Discalced Carmelites, often aided by St. John of the Cross. Her writings, especially the Interior Castle and the Way of Perfection, became treasures of Christian mysticism; she died in 1582, was canonized in 1622, and was proclaimed the first woman Doctor of the Church by Pope St. Paul VI in 1970.

Teresa de Cepeda y Ahumada nasceu em Ávila, Espanha, em 1515, e entrou ainda jovem no convento carmelita da Encarnação. Após anos de mediocridade, experimentou uma profunda conversão diante de uma imagem de Cristo chagado e foi introduzida numa vida intensa de oração mística, incluindo a célebre transverberação, em que um anjo lhe traspassou o coração com um dardo de fogo. Apesar da saúde frágil e da feroz oposição, fundou dezessete conventos reformados das Carmelitas Descalças, muitas vezes auxiliada por São João da Cruz. Seus escritos, sobretudo o Castelo Interior e o Caminho de Perfeição, tornaram-se tesouros da mística cristã; morreu em 1582, foi canonizada em 1622 e proclamada a primeira mulher Doutora da Igreja pelo Papa São Paulo VI em 1970.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Teresa of Jesus, valiant daughter of the Church and mistress of prayer, who found God dwelling within the castle of your soul, turn your gaze upon me. Teach me to converse with the Christ who loves me, and obtain for me the grace I ask in this novena. Amen.

Santa Teresa de Jesus, filha valorosa da Igreja e mestra da oração, que encontrastes Deus habitando no castelo de vossa alma, voltai o vosso olhar para mim. Ensinai-me a conversar com o Cristo que me ama, e alcançai-me a graça que peço nesta novena. Amém.

Day 1: Prayer as friendship with Christ

Dia 1: A oração como amizade com Cristo

St. Teresa described mental prayer as nothing other than spending time alone with the One we know loves us. She teaches us that prayer is not a duty to perform but a friendship to cultivate with the living God.

Santa Teresa descreveu a oração mental como nada mais que tratar de amizade, estando muitas vezes a sós com Aquele que sabemos que nos ama. Ela nos ensina que a oração não é um dever a cumprir, mas uma amizade a cultivar com o Deus vivo.

Intention

For the grace to make prayer a daily friendship with Jesus.

Pela graça de fazer da oração uma amizade diária com Jesus.

Day 2: Conversion of heart

Dia 2: Conversão do coração

For years St. Teresa lived a tepid religious life until, before an image of the wounded Christ, she wept and surrendered herself wholly to Him. She teaches us that it is never too late to begin again with greater fervor.

Por anos Santa Teresa viveu uma vida religiosa tíbia, até que, diante de uma imagem de Cristo chagado, chorou e se entregou inteiramente a Ele. Ela nos ensina que nunca é tarde para recomeçar com maior fervor.

Intention

For the grace of a deeper conversion and renewed fervor in our faith.

Pela graça de uma conversão mais profunda e de fervor renovado em nossa fé.

Day 3: The interior castle

Dia 3: O castelo interior

St. Teresa saw the soul as a castle of crystal with many rooms, at whose center the King Himself dwells. She invites us to journey inward through prayer until we reach the chamber where God awaits us.

Santa Teresa viu a alma como um castelo de cristal com muitas moradas, em cujo centro habita o próprio Rei. Ela nos convida a caminhar para dentro por meio da oração até alcançarmos a morada onde Deus nos espera.

Intention

For the grace to seek God in the depths of our own soul.

Pela graça de buscar a Deus nas profundezas da nossa própria alma.

Day 4: The transverberation

Dia 4: A transverberação

St. Teresa recounted how an angel pierced her heart with a golden dart of fire, leaving her aflame with love of God. This wound of love reveals that union with Christ is the deepest joy and the truest treasure of the soul.

Santa Teresa relatou como um anjo lhe traspassou o coração com um dardo de ouro em fogo, deixando-a inflamada de amor a Deus. Essa ferida de amor revela que a união com Cristo é a alegria mais profunda e o tesouro mais verdadeiro da alma.

Intention

For a heart set on fire with love for God above all things.

Por um coração inflamado de amor a Deus acima de todas as coisas.

Day 5: Reformer of Carmel

Dia 5: Reformadora do Carmelo

Against great opposition, St. Teresa founded convents of strict poverty and deep prayer, restoring the original spirit of Carmel. She teaches us that genuine reform begins not with grand plans but with humble, persevering love.

Contra grande oposição, Santa Teresa fundou conventos de estrita pobreza e profunda oração, restaurando o espírito original do Carmelo. Ela nos ensina que a verdadeira reforma não começa com grandes planos, mas com um amor humilde e perseverante.

Intention

For the courage to pursue holiness despite obstacles and opposition.

Pela coragem de buscar a santidade apesar dos obstáculos e da oposição.

Day 6: Determined determination

Dia 6: Uma determinada determinação

St. Teresa urged souls to set out on the path of prayer with a determined determination never to turn back. She reminds us that perseverance, more than feelings, carries the soul to God.

Santa Teresa exortava as almas a empreender o caminho da oração com uma determinada determinação de jamais voltar atrás. Ela nos recorda que a perseverança, mais que os sentimentos, conduz a alma a Deus.

Intention

For the grace of perseverance when prayer feels dry or difficult.

Pela graça da perseverança quando a oração se torna árida ou difícil.

Day 7: Humility and self-knowledge

Dia 7: Humildade e conhecimento próprio

St. Teresa taught that humility is truth, and that we cannot grow in prayer without honest knowledge of ourselves before God. She shows us that the lowly soul is the one most ready to be raised by grace.

Santa Teresa ensinava que a humildade é a verdade, e que não podemos crescer na oração sem o conhecimento sincero de nós mesmos diante de Deus. Ela nos mostra que a alma humilde é a que está mais pronta para ser elevada pela graça.

Intention

For the grace of true humility and honest self-knowledge.

Pela graça da verdadeira humildade e do sincero conhecimento próprio.

Day 8: Let nothing disturb you

Dia 8: Nada te perturbe

On a slip of paper in her breviary St. Teresa left the words, 'Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you; all things pass, God never changes.' She teaches a serene peace rooted in the unchanging faithfulness of God.

Num papel guardado em seu breviário, Santa Teresa deixou as palavras: 'Nada te perturbe, nada te espante; tudo passa, Deus não muda.' Ela nos ensina uma paz serena enraizada na fidelidade imutável de Deus.

Intention

For the grace of unshakable peace amid the trials of life.

Pela graça de uma paz inabalável em meio às provações da vida.

Day 9: God alone suffices

Dia 9: Só Deus basta

St. Teresa concluded that whoever possesses God lacks nothing, for God alone suffices. She invites us to anchor our whole hope in Him and to find in His love the fulfillment of every desire.

Santa Teresa concluiu que quem possui a Deus de nada carece, pois só Deus basta. Ela nos convida a ancorar toda a nossa esperança n'Ele e a encontrar em Seu amor a realização de todo desejo.

Intention

For the grace to find in God alone the fulfillment of every longing.

Pela graça de encontrar só em Deus a realização de todo anseio.

Closing prayer

St. Teresa, whose heart was wounded by the fire of divine love, intercede that nothing may ever disturb the peace of my soul. Keep me steadfast in patience and trust, knowing that God alone suffices and that whoever has God wants for nothing. Amen.

Santa Teresa, cujo coração foi ferido pelo fogo do amor divino, intercedei para que nada jamais perturbe a paz da minha alma. Conservai-me firme na paciência e na confiança, sabendo que só Deus basta e que quem tem a Deus de nada precisa. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="teresa">
Novena
to St. Teresa of Avila

Pray especially beginning on 6 October and ending on 14
October, the eve of the Feast of St. Teresa. This Novena was written by
St. Alphonsus Liguori.

First Day:

O most amiable Lord Jesus Christ! We thank Thee for the
great gift of faith and of devotion to the Holy Sacrament, which Thou
didst grant to Thy beloved Teresa; we pray Thee, by Thy merits and by
those of Thy faithful spouse, to grant us the gift of a lively faith,
and of a fervent devotion toward the most Holy Sacrament of the altar;
where Thou, O infinite Majesty! hast obliged Thyself to abide with us
even to the end of the world, and wherein Thou didst so lovingly give
Thy whole Self to us.

Say one Our Father, Hail
Mary and Glory be

V. St. Teresa, pray for us:

R. That we may become worthy of the promises of Jesus Christ.

Let us pray: Graciously hear us, O God of our salvation! that as we
rejoice in the commemoration of the blessed virgin Teresa, so we may be
nourished by her heavenly doctrine, and draw from thence the fervour of
a tender devotion; through our Lord Jesus Christ, Thy Son, Who liveth
and reigneth with Thee in the unity of the Holy Spirit, God for ever
and ever. Amen.

Second Day:

O most merciful Lord Jesus Christ! we thank Thee for the great gift of
hope which Thou didst grant to Thy beloved Teresa; we pray Thee, by Thy
merits, and by those of Thy holy spouse, to give us a great confidence
in Thy goodness, by reason of Thy Precious Blood, which Thou hast shed
to its last drop for our salvation.

Say one Our Father, Hail
Mary and Glory be

V. St. Teresa, pray for us:

R. That we may become worthy of the promises of Jesus Christ.

Let us pray: Graciously hear us, O God of our salvation! that as we
rejoice in the commemoration of the blessed virgin Teresa, so we may be
nourished by her heavenly doctrine, and draw from thence the fervour of
a tender devotion; through our Lord Jesus Christ, Thy Son, Who liveth
and reigneth with Thee in the unity of the Holy Spirit, God for ever
and ever. Amen.

Third Day:

O most loving Lord Jesus Christ! we thank Thee for the great gift of
love which Thou didst grant to Thy beloved Teresa; we pray Thee, by Thy
merits, and by those of Thy most loving spouse, to give us the great,
the crowning gift of Thy perfect love.

Say one Our Father, Hail
Mary and Glory be

V. St. Teresa, pray for us:

R. That we may become worthy of the promises of Jesus Christ.

Let us pray: Graciously hear us, O God of our salvation! that as we
rejoice in the commemoration of the blessed virgin Teresa, so we may be
nourished by her heavenly doctrine, and draw from thence the fervour of
a tender devotion; through our Lord Jesus Christ, Thy Son, Who liveth
and reigneth with Thee in the unity of the Holy Spirit, God for ever
and ever. Amen.

Fourth Day:

O most sweet Lord Jesus Christ! we thank Thee for the gift of great
desire and resolution which Thou didst grant to Thy beloved Teresa,
that she might love Thee perfectly; we pray Thee, by Thy merits, and by
those of Thy most generous spouse, to give us a true desire, and a true
resolution of pleasing Thee the utmost of our power.

Say one Our Father, Hail
Mary and Glory be

V. St. Teresa, pray for us:

R. That we may become worthy of the promises of Jesus Christ.

Let us pray: Graciously hear us, O God of our salvation! that as we
rejoice in the commemoration of the blessed virgin Teresa, so we may be
nourished by her heavenly doctrine, and draw from thence the fervour of
a tender devotion; through our Lord Jesus Christ, Thy Son, Who liveth
and reigneth with Thee in the unity of the Holy Spirit, God for ever
and ever. Amen.

Fifth Day:

O most kind Lord Jesus Christ! we thank Thee for the great gift of
humility which Thou didst grant to Thy beloved Teresa; we pray Thee, by
Thy merits, and by those of Thy most humble spouse, to grant us the
grace of a tr

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross and a fixed thanksgiving ("O most loving Heavenly Father! We thank you for the great gift you gave us through your beloved St. Teresa of Avila, virgin and doctor of the Church"), with a distinct daily intention, the recurring petition "Open wide the doors to the interior castle of our hearts and souls so that we may know how to pray," and concluding Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be plus "Saint Teresa of Avila, pray for us!" Daily themes: 1 gift of prayer, 2 greater trust, 3 recognizing God's plan, 4 love for Jesus, 5 listening to God, 6 strength in grief, 7 a consistent relationship with God, 8 awareness of God's presence, 9 recognizing answered prayers.

Fidelity note

Ember's novena is its own composition that draws directly on Teresa's writings and life — prayer as friendship with the One who loves us, her conversion before the wounded Christ, the interior castle, the transverberation, the reform of Carmel, "determined determination," humility as truth, "let nothing disturb you," and "God alone suffices." It shares PrayMore's core themes (prayer, trust, conversion, perseverance) and the same Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be conclusion, while grounding each day in a specific Teresian text or episode rather than a generic petition. Wording is original, not paraphrased; the FishEaters source could not be checked.

St. Margaret Mary Alacoque st-margaret-mary-novena

Oct 7–15
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Margaret Mary Alacoque, the humble Visitation nun to whom Christ revealed the riches of His Sacred Heart. Through her example we learn to love His Heart, to make reparation, and to trust His twelve great promises.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Margarida Maria Alacoque, a humilde religiosa da Visitação a quem Cristo revelou as riquezas de Seu Sagrado Coração. Por seu exemplo aprendemos a amar o Seu Coração, a fazer reparação e a confiar em Suas doze grandes promessas.

Margaret Mary Alacoque was born in Burgundy, France, in 1647, and from childhood was drawn to suffering and to the presence of God. At twenty-four she entered the Order of the Visitation at Paray-le-Monial, where between 1673 and 1675 the Lord Jesus appeared to her and revealed His Sacred Heart, burning with love for humanity yet wounded by ingratitude. He entrusted to her the devotion of reparation, the Holy Hour, the Communion of the First Fridays, and the Twelve Promises for those who honor His Heart. Aided by her Jesuit director St. Claude de la Colombière, she spread this devotion despite ridicule and resistance, and died in 1690. She was canonized in 1920, and the feast of the Sacred Heart became one of the great devotions of the universal Church.

Margarida Maria Alacoque nasceu na Borgonha, França, em 1647, e desde a infância sentiu-se atraída pelo sofrimento e pela presença de Deus. Aos vinte e quatro anos entrou na Ordem da Visitação em Paray-le-Monial, onde, entre 1673 e 1675, o Senhor Jesus lhe apareceu e revelou Seu Sagrado Coração, ardendo de amor pela humanidade, mas ferido pela ingratidão. Confiou-lhe a devoção da reparação, a Hora Santa, a Comunhão das Primeiras Sextas-feiras e as Doze Promessas para os que honram Seu Coração. Auxiliada por seu diretor jesuíta São Cláudio de la Colombière, propagou essa devoção apesar do escárnio e da resistência, e morreu em 1690. Foi canonizada em 1920, e a festa do Sagrado Coração tornou-se uma das grandes devoções da Igreja universal.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Margaret Mary, faithful confidante of the Sacred Heart, who beheld the furnace of love burning for sinners, draw me close to that wounded Heart. Teach me to console Him by my love, and obtain for me the grace I implore in this novena. Amen.

Santa Margarida Maria, fiel confidente do Sagrado Coração, que contemplastes a fornalha de amor ardendo pelos pecadores, aproximai-me daquele Coração ferido. Ensinai-me a consolá-lo com o meu amor, e alcançai-me a graça que imploro nesta novena. Amém.

Day 1: The Heart that loves us

Dia 1: O Coração que nos ama

Jesus showed St. Margaret Mary His Heart as a burning furnace of love, surrounded by thorns and crowned with the cross. He revealed that His one desire is to be loved in return by the souls for whom He gave everything.

Jesus mostrou a Santa Margarida Maria Seu Coração como uma fornalha ardente de amor, cercada de espinhos e coroada pela cruz. Ele revelou que Seu único desejo é ser amado em retorno pelas almas por quem deu tudo.

Intention

For the grace to return love to the Heart of Jesus who loves us first.

Pela graça de retribuir amor ao Coração de Jesus que nos ama primeiro.

Day 2: Chosen in humility

Dia 2: Escolhida na humildade

Christ chose a hidden, unlearned nun to be the herald of His Heart, passing over the wise and powerful of the world. St. Margaret Mary teaches us that God delights to accomplish great things through the lowly and humble of heart.

Cristo escolheu uma religiosa escondida e sem instrução para ser a mensageira de Seu Coração, passando por cima dos sábios e poderosos do mundo. Santa Margarida Maria nos ensina que Deus se compraz em realizar grandes coisas por meio dos pequenos e humildes de coração.

Intention

For the humility to let God work through our lowliness.

Pela humildade de deixar Deus agir através da nossa pequenez.

Day 3: The apparitions at Paray-le-Monial

Dia 3: As aparições de Paray-le-Monial

Over several years in the quiet convent of Paray-le-Monial, Jesus poured out His confidences to St. Margaret Mary, asking that devotion to His Heart be established throughout the world. She faithfully received and guarded this great mission for the Church.

Ao longo de vários anos, no quieto convento de Paray-le-Monial, Jesus derramou Suas confidências a Santa Margarida Maria, pedindo que a devoção ao Seu Coração fosse estabelecida no mundo inteiro. Ela recebeu e guardou fielmente essa grande missão para a Igreja.

Intention

For openness to the gentle invitations of Christ in our hearts.

Pela abertura aos suaves convites de Cristo em nossos corações.

Day 4: Reparation for ingratitude

Dia 4: Reparação pela ingratidão

Jesus lamented to St. Margaret Mary the coldness and ingratitude His love so often receives, and asked her to make reparation. She teaches us to console the Sacred Heart by offering our love in place of the world's indifference.

Jesus lamentou a Santa Margarida Maria a frieza e a ingratidão que Seu amor tantas vezes recebe, e pediu-lhe que fizesse reparação. Ela nos ensina a consolar o Sagrado Coração oferecendo o nosso amor em lugar da indiferença do mundo.

Intention

For a spirit of reparation for the offenses against the love of God.

Por um espírito de reparação pelas ofensas contra o amor de Deus.

Day 5: The Holy Hour

Dia 5: A Hora Santa

Jesus asked St. Margaret Mary to keep watch with Him in prayer, sharing in the agony He suffered in Gethsemane. The Holy Hour she received teaches us to remain with the Lord and to comfort Him in His sorrow.

Jesus pediu a Santa Margarida Maria que velasse com Ele em oração, partilhando a agonia que sofreu no Getsêmani. A Hora Santa que ela recebeu nos ensina a permanecer com o Senhor e a consolá-lo em Sua dor.

Intention

For the grace to keep faithful watch with Jesus in prayer.

Pela graça de velar fielmente com Jesus em oração.

Day 6: The First Fridays

Dia 6: As Primeiras Sextas-feiras

Through St. Margaret Mary, Jesus promised the grace of final repentance to those who receive Communion on nine consecutive First Fridays in His honor. This great promise reveals the boundless patience of His Heart for sinners.

Por meio de Santa Margarida Maria, Jesus prometeu a graça do arrependimento final aos que comungarem em nove Primeiras Sextas-feiras consecutivas em Sua honra. Essa grande promessa revela a infinita paciência de Seu Coração para com os pecadores.

Intention

For perseverance in the devotion of the Nine First Fridays.

Pela perseverança na devoção das Nove Primeiras Sextas-feiras.

Day 7: The Twelve Promises

Dia 7: As Doze Promessas

Jesus made twelve great promises to those devoted to His Heart, among them peace in their families and comfort in their trials. St. Margaret Mary invites us to take these promises to heart with humble and grateful trust.

Jesus fez doze grandes promessas aos devotos de Seu Coração, entre elas a paz nas famílias e o consolo nas provações. Santa Margarida Maria nos convida a acolher essas promessas com confiança humilde e agradecida.

Intention

For confidence in the promises of the Sacred Heart for our families.

Pela confiança nas promessas do Sagrado Coração para nossas famílias.

Day 8: Patience under trial

Dia 8: Paciência na provação

St. Margaret Mary endured years of misunderstanding, mockery, and suspicion from those around her before her message was believed. She teaches us to bear contradiction in silence, uniting our trials to the wounded Heart of Christ.

Santa Margarida Maria suportou anos de incompreensão, zombaria e desconfiança dos que a cercavam antes que sua mensagem fosse acreditada. Ela nos ensina a suportar a contradição em silêncio, unindo as nossas provações ao Coração ferido de Cristo.

Intention

For patience and trust when we are misunderstood or rejected.

Pela paciência e confiança quando somos incompreendidos ou rejeitados.

Day 9: Refuge in the Sacred Heart

Dia 9: Refúgio no Sagrado Coração

St. Margaret Mary found in the Heart of Jesus a sure refuge for every weakness and every sorrow. She calls us to take shelter there, certain that the Heart pierced for our sake will never turn away a contrite soul.

Santa Margarida Maria encontrou no Coração de Jesus um refúgio seguro para toda fraqueza e toda dor. Ela nos chama a abrigar-nos ali, certos de que o Coração traspassado por nós jamais rejeitará uma alma contrita.

Intention

For the grace to make the Sacred Heart our refuge in life and death.

Pela graça de fazer do Sagrado Coração o nosso refúgio na vida e na morte.

Closing prayer

St. Margaret Mary, apostle of the Sacred Heart and model of reparation, intercede that I may take refuge in the Heart of Jesus all the days of my life. May I love Him who has so loved us, and trust the promises He revealed through you for every faithful soul. Amen.

Santa Margarida Maria, apóstola do Sagrado Coração e modelo de reparação, intercedei para que eu me refugie no Coração de Jesus todos os dias da minha vida. Que eu ame Aquele que tanto nos amou e confie nas promessas que Ele revelou por vós a toda alma fiel. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

With no FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas counterpart, there is nothing to measure verbal fidelity against. The content is doctrinally sound and faithful to St. Margaret Mary's life and the Sacred Heart revelations at Paray-le-Monial (1673–1675): the Heart as a furnace of love crowned with thorns, her humble hidden vocation, the call to reparation for ingratitude, the Holy Hour, the Communion of the Nine First Fridays, the Twelve Promises, and her years of being misunderstood. Themes track the established Sacred Heart devotion closely. Treat as an original, sourced-by-spirituality composition rather than a transcription.

St. Gerard Majella st-gerard-majella-novena

Oct 7–15
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Gerard Majella, the Redemptorist lay brother and wonderworker beloved as the patron of expectant mothers. Through his example we learn obedience, hidden holiness, and total abandonment to the will of God.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Geraldo Majela, o irmão leigo redentorista e taumaturgo amado como patrono das gestantes. Por seu exemplo aprendemos a obediência, a santidade escondida e o total abandono à vontade de Deus.

Gerard Majella was born in 1726 in Muro Lucano, Italy, the youngest child of a poor tailor's family, and worked as a tailor and servant before entering the Congregation of the Most Holy Redeemer as a lay brother. Though entrusted only with humble tasks as sacristan, porter, and infirmarian, he became renowned for extraordinary gifts: miracles, ecstasies, bilocation, and a profound discernment of souls. He is best known as the patron of expectant mothers from a beloved tradition in which he gave a handkerchief to a young woman, telling her she would one day need it, and years later, in danger during childbirth, she was delivered through it. Worn out by penance and tuberculosis, he died in 1755 at the age of twenty-nine; he was canonized by Pope St. Pius X in 1904, and his feast is kept on October 16.

Geraldo Majela nasceu em 1726 em Muro Lucano, Itália, o filho mais novo de uma pobre família de alfaiate, e trabalhou como alfaiate e criado antes de entrar na Congregação do Santíssimo Redentor como irmão leigo. Embora lhe fossem confiadas apenas tarefas humildes de sacristão, porteiro e enfermeiro, tornou-se célebre por dons extraordinários: milagres, êxtases, bilocação e profundo discernimento das almas. É conhecido sobretudo como patrono das gestantes a partir de uma querida tradição, segundo a qual deu um lenço a uma jovem, dizendo-lhe que um dia precisaria dele, e anos mais tarde, em perigo durante o parto, ela foi salva por meio dele. Esgotado pela penitência e pela tuberculose, morreu em 1755, aos vinte e nove anos; foi canonizado pelo Papa São Pio X em 1904, e sua festa é celebrada em 16 de outubro.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Gerard Majella, faithful servant of God who hid great holiness beneath the humblest of duties, look kindly upon me. Teach me to love the will of God as you did, with simple and joyful surrender, and obtain for me the grace I ask in this novena. Amen.

São Geraldo Majela, fiel servo de Deus que escondestes grande santidade sob os mais humildes deveres, olhai com bondade para mim. Ensinai-me a amar a vontade de Deus como vós a amastes, com entrega simples e alegre, e alcançai-me a graça que peço nesta novena. Amém.

Day 1: Abandonment to God's will

Dia 1: Abandono à vontade de Deus

St. Gerard surrendered himself so completely to God that he longed only to do the divine will in all things. He teaches us that true peace is found in trusting God's plan even when it is hidden from our eyes.

São Geraldo entregou-se tão completamente a Deus que desejava apenas cumprir a vontade divina em tudo. Ele nos ensina que a verdadeira paz se encontra em confiar no plano de Deus, mesmo quando ele permanece oculto aos nossos olhos.

Intention

For the grace to abandon myself wholly to the will of God.

Pela graça de abandonar-me inteiramente à vontade de Deus.

Day 2: Hidden holiness

Dia 2: Santidade escondida

As a lay brother St. Gerard served quietly as porter, sacristan, and infirmarian, content to be unnoticed by the world. He shows us that the smallest task done for love of God can become a path to great sanctity.

Como irmão leigo, São Geraldo servia em silêncio como porteiro, sacristão e enfermeiro, contente em passar despercebido aos olhos do mundo. Ele nos mostra que a menor tarefa feita por amor de Deus pode tornar-se caminho de grande santidade.

Intention

For the grace to seek holiness in humble and hidden service.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade no serviço humilde e escondido.

Day 3: Heroic obedience

Dia 3: Obediência heroica

St. Gerard obeyed his superiors so promptly that he saw in their commands the very voice of God. Even when falsely accused, he submitted in silence rather than defend himself against his superiors.

São Geraldo obedecia aos superiores tão prontamente que via em suas ordens a própria voz de Deus. Mesmo quando falsamente acusado, submeteu-se em silêncio em vez de defender-se diante de seus superiores.

Intention

For the grace of humble obedience to God and lawful authority.

Pela graça da humilde obediência a Deus e à autoridade legítima.

Day 4: Patron of mothers

Dia 4: Patrono das mães

Tradition recalls how St. Gerard left a handkerchief with a young woman, saying she would one day need it, and through it a mother was later saved in childbirth. He has become the loving protector of mothers and the children they carry.

A tradição recorda como São Geraldo deixou um lenço com uma jovem, dizendo que um dia ela precisaria dele, e por meio dele uma mãe foi mais tarde salva no parto. Ele se tornou o amoroso protetor das mães e dos filhos que carregam.

Intention

For the safety of expectant mothers and the children in their wombs.

Pela proteção das gestantes e dos filhos em seus ventres.

Day 5: Friend of the poor

Dia 5: Amigo dos pobres

St. Gerard gave generously to the poor, often emptying the convent stores in his confidence that God would provide. His charity reveals a heart that loved Christ in every needy and suffering person.

São Geraldo dava generosamente aos pobres, esvaziando muitas vezes os depósitos do convento, confiante de que Deus proveria. Sua caridade revela um coração que amava a Cristo em cada pessoa necessitada e sofredora.

Intention

For a generous heart toward the poor and suffering around us.

Por um coração generoso para com os pobres e sofredores ao nosso redor.

Day 6: Love of the Eucharist

Dia 6: Amor à Eucaristia

St. Gerard was often found rapt in adoration before the Blessed Sacrament, drawn by an ardent love for the hidden Christ. From the Eucharist he drew the strength for his penances and his tireless charity.

São Geraldo era muitas vezes encontrado arrebatado em adoração diante do Santíssimo Sacramento, atraído por um amor ardente ao Cristo escondido. Da Eucaristia haurava a força para suas penitências e sua caridade incansável.

Intention

For a deeper love of Jesus present in the Holy Eucharist.

Por um amor mais profundo a Jesus presente na Santíssima Eucaristia.

Day 7: Innocence vindicated

Dia 7: Inocência defendida

When falsely accused of grave sin, St. Gerard bore the disgrace without complaint, trusting God to defend his honor in His own time. His innocence was later revealed, and he had won great merit by his silence.

Quando falsamente acusado de pecado grave, São Geraldo suportou a desonra sem queixa, confiando que Deus defenderia sua honra em Seu próprio tempo. Sua inocência foi mais tarde revelada, e ele havia ganhado grande mérito por seu silêncio.

Intention

For the grace to bear injustice in silence, trusting God to defend us.

Pela graça de suportar a injustiça em silêncio, confiando que Deus nos defenderá.

Day 8: Penance and purity

Dia 8: Penitência e pureza

St. Gerard embraced severe penances and guarded the purity of his heart with great care, that nothing might come between him and God. He teaches us that self-denial frees the soul to love the Lord without division.

São Geraldo abraçou severas penitências e guardou a pureza do coração com grande cuidado, para que nada se interpusesse entre ele e Deus. Ele nos ensina que a abnegação liberta a alma para amar o Senhor sem divisão.

Intention

For the grace of self-denial and purity of heart.

Pela graça da abnegação e da pureza de coração.

Day 9: A holy death

Dia 9: Uma santa morte

St. Gerard died at twenty-nine, asking only that God's will be done, with a note on his door reading that here the will of God was carried out. He teaches us to meet death in peace, surrendered to the loving plan of our Father.

São Geraldo morreu aos vinte e nove anos, pedindo apenas que a vontade de Deus fosse cumprida, com um bilhete em sua porta que dizia que ali se fazia a vontade de Deus. Ele nos ensina a enfrentar a morte em paz, entregues ao plano amoroso de nosso Pai.

Intention

For the grace of a holy death, surrendered to the will of God.

Pela graça de uma santa morte, entregue à vontade de Deus.

Closing prayer

St. Gerard, wonderworker and tender protector of mothers and the helpless, intercede for me before the throne of God. Obtain for me a heart obedient and abandoned to Providence, so that in all things I may say with you, 'Lord, let it be done as You will.' Amen.

São Geraldo, taumaturgo e terno protetor das mães e dos desamparados, intercedei por mim diante do trono de Deus. Alcançai-me um coração obediente e abandonado à Providência, para que em tudo eu diga convosco: 'Senhor, faça-se como Vós quereis.' Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="gerard">
Novena
to St. Gerard

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 7
October and ending on 15 October, the eve of the Feast of St. Gerard.

First Day:

Saint Gerard, ever full of faith, obtain for me that, believing firmly
all that the Church of God proposes to my belief, I may strive to
secure through a holy life the joys of eternal happiness.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

O Almighty and Everlasting God, who didst draw to thyself Saint Gerard,
even from his tenderest years, making him conformable to the Image of
Thy Crucified Son, grant we beseech Thee, that imitating his example,
we may be made like unto the same Divine Image, through Jesus Christ
Our Lord. Amen.

Second Day:

Saint Gerard, most generous saint, who from thy tenderest years didst
care so little for the goods of earth, grant that I may place all my
confidence in Jesus Christ alone, my true Treasure, who alone can make
me happy in time and in eternity.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Third Day:

Saint Gerard, bright seraph of love, who despising all earthly love,
didst consecrate thy life to the service of God and thy neighbor,
promoting God’s glory in thy lowly state, and ever ready to assist the
distressed and console the sorrowful, obtain for me, I beseech thee,
that loving God the only God and my neighbor for His sake, I may be
hereafter united to Him for ever in glory.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Fourth Day:

Saint Gerard, spotless lily of purity, by the angelic virtue and thy
wonderful innocence of life thou didst receive from the Infant Jesus
and His Immaculate Mother, sweet pledges of tenderest love, grant, I
beseech thee, that I may ever strive in my life-long fight, and thus
win the crown that awaits the brave and the true.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Fifth Day:

Saint Gerard, model of holy obedience, who through thy life didst
heroically submit the judgment to those who represent Jesus Christ to
thee, thus sanctifying thy lowliest actions, obtain for me from God
cheerful admission to His Holy Will and the virtue of perfect
obedience, that I may be made comfortable to Jesus, my Model, Who was
obedient even to death.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Sixth Day:

Saint Gerard, most perfect imitator of Jesus Christ our Redeemer, do
thou whose greatest glory was to be humble and lowly, obtain that I
too, knowing my littleness in God’s sight, may be found worthy to enter
the kingdom that is promised to the humble and lowly of heart.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Seventh Day:

Saint Gerard, unconquered hero, most patient in suffering, do thou who
didst glory in infirmity, and under slander and most cruel ignominy
didst rejoice to suffer with Christ, obtain for me patience and
resignation in my sorrows, that I may bravely bear the cross that is to
gain for me the crown of everlasting glory.

Say nine Hail Marys

V. Pray for us, O Saint Gerard.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Eighth Day:

Saint Gerard, true lover of Jesus in the Blessed Sacrament of the
Altar, do thou who didst kneel long hours before the Tabernacle, and
there didst taste the joys of the Paradise. Obtain for me, I beseech
thee, the spirit of prayer and an undying love for the Most Holy
Sacrament, that thus receiving frequently the Body and Blood of Jesus,
I may daily grow in His holy love and merit the priceless grace of
loving Him even to the end.

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Nine days, each with an identical structural frame and a distinct daily petition. Each day: "In the name of the Father..." → a day-specific opening prayer naming one virtue → Nine Hail Marys → V. "Pray for us, O Saint Gerard." R. "That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ." → closing Sign of the Cross. Daily virtues: (1) Faith — "believing firmly all that the Church of God proposes"; (2) Detachment — "place all my confidence in Jesus Christ alone"; (3) Charity; (4) Purity; (5) Obedience — "cheerful admission to His Holy Will"; (6) Humility; (7) Patience in suffering — "bravely bear the cross"; (8) Devotion to the Eucharist; (9) Devotion to Mary.

Fidelity note

Ember and PrayMoreNovenas share the nine-day-with-distinct-daily-theme structure and overlap heavily on themes (obedience/abandonment, humility/hidden holiness, the Eucharist, purity, patience in suffering, Marian/Providence trust). But the wording is independent: Ember writes a fresh narrative meditation on Gerard's life and gifts for each day plus a one-line intention, whereas PrayMoreNovenas uses a classic virtue-petition formula with fixed responsorial elements (nine Hail Marys, the "Pray for us" versicle). Ember also foregrounds Gerard's hallmark patronage of expectant mothers (Day 4), which the PrayMoreNovenas virtue list does not isolate. Overall: faithful in spirit and recognizable as the same devotion, but a distinct composition rather than a reproduction of the FishEaters/PMN text.

St. Ignatius of Antioch st-ignatius-antioch-novena

Oct 8–16
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Ignatius of Antioch, Apostolic Father, bishop, and martyr, who longed to be ground like wheat into the bread of Christ.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Inácio de Antioquia, Padre Apostólico, bispo e mártir, que desejou ser moído como o trigo no pão de Cristo.

St. Ignatius of Antioch (died c. 107) was bishop of Antioch and a disciple of the Apostle John. Condemned to die in the Roman arena, he wrote seven letters along the road to martyrdom, begging the Christians of Rome not to prevent his death. His words 'I am the wheat of God, and I am ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ' have echoed through the Church for nineteen centuries.

Santo Inácio de Antioquia (falecido por volta de 107) foi bispo de Antioquia e discípulo do apóstolo João. Condenado a morrer na arena romana, escreveu sete cartas ao longo do caminho para o martírio, suplicando aos cristãos de Roma que não impedissem sua morte. Suas palavras — 'Sou o trigo de Deus, e serei moído pelos dentes das feras, para tornar-me o puro pão de Cristo' — ecoam na Igreja há dezenove séculos.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O God, who crowned the courage of St. Ignatius of Antioch with the palm of martyrdom, grant that we who honor his witness may share his ardent love for Your Son. Through his intercession, kindle in us the same longing to belong wholly to Christ. Amen.

Ó Deus, que coroastes a coragem de Santo Inácio de Antioquia com a palma do martírio, concedei-nos, que honramos seu testemunho, partilhar de seu ardente amor por vosso Filho. Por sua intercessão, acendei em nós o mesmo desejo de pertencer inteiramente a Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: The wheat of God

Dia 1: O trigo de Deus

On the road to Rome, Ignatius wrote that he was God's wheat, to be ground by the teeth of beasts into the pure bread of Christ. He shows us that suffering offered in love is never wasted, but transformed into something that nourishes the whole Body.

A caminho de Roma, Inácio escreveu que era o trigo de Deus, para ser moído pelos dentes das feras no puro pão de Cristo. Ele nos mostra que o sofrimento oferecido com amor nunca se perde, mas é transformado em algo que alimenta o Corpo inteiro.

Intention

For the grace to offer our sufferings in union with Christ, that they may bear fruit for souls.

Pela graça de oferecer nossos sofrimentos em união com Cristo, para que deem fruto em favor das almas.

Day 2: Longing for union with Christ

Dia 2: Desejo de união com Cristo

Ignatius wrote, 'My love has been crucified, and there is no fire in me desiring earthly things.' His whole heart strained toward Jesus, so that even life itself seemed small beside the joy of belonging to Him.

Inácio escreveu: 'Meu amor foi crucificado, e não há em mim fogo que deseje as coisas terrenas.' Todo o seu coração se voltava para Jesus, de modo que a própria vida parecia pequena diante da alegria de pertencer a Ele.

Intention

For the grace to desire Christ above every earthly comfort.

Pela graça de desejar a Cristo acima de todo conforto terreno.

Day 3: A bishop's faithful care

Dia 3: O zelo fiel de um bispo

As bishop of Antioch for nearly forty years, Ignatius shepherded his flock through persecution with vigilance and tenderness. Even in chains, his thoughts turned to the churches he loved, urging their pastors to guard the faith and feed the weak.

Como bispo de Antioquia por quase quarenta anos, Inácio pastoreou seu rebanho em meio à perseguição com vigilância e ternura. Mesmo acorrentado, seus pensamentos se voltavam às igrejas que amava, exortando seus pastores a guardar a fé e alimentar os fracos.

Intention

For our bishops and priests, that they may shepherd God's people with courage and love.

Pelos nossos bispos e sacerdotes, para que pastoreiem o povo de Deus com coragem e amor.

Day 4: The Eucharist, medicine of immortality

Dia 4: A Eucaristia, remédio da imortalidade

Ignatius called the Eucharist 'the medicine of immortality, the antidote against death,' and the flesh of Jesus Christ. He bears witness that from the very beginning the Church adored the Lord truly present in the breaking of the bread.

Inácio chamou a Eucaristia de 'remédio da imortalidade, antídoto contra a morte' e carne de Jesus Cristo. Ele atesta que, desde o princípio, a Igreja adorou o Senhor verdadeiramente presente na fração do pão.

Intention

For a deeper reverence and love for Jesus truly present in the Holy Eucharist.

Por uma reverência e um amor mais profundos a Jesus verdadeiramente presente na Santa Eucaristia.

Day 5: The unity of the Church

Dia 5: A unidade da Igreja

Again and again Ignatius pleaded for the unity of the faithful around their bishop, 'as the strings to a harp.' He saw division as the great wound of the Body and prayed that all might be one in Christ.

Repetidas vezes Inácio suplicou pela unidade dos fiéis em torno de seu bispo, 'como as cordas de uma harpa'. Ele via a divisão como a grande ferida do Corpo e orava para que todos fossem um em Cristo.

Intention

For the unity of all Christians and the healing of every division in the Church.

Pela unidade de todos os cristãos e pela cura de toda divisão na Igreja.

Day 6: Imitating the Master in suffering

Dia 6: Imitar o Mestre no sofrimento

Ignatius asked the Romans, 'Allow me to be an imitator of the passion of my God.' He understood that the disciple is not above his Master, and that the cross is the truest school of love.

Inácio pediu aos romanos: 'Deixai-me ser imitador da paixão do meu Deus.' Ele compreendeu que o discípulo não está acima do Mestre, e que a cruz é a mais verdadeira escola do amor.

Intention

For the grace to carry our daily crosses in imitation of the suffering Christ.

Pela graça de carregar nossas cruzes diárias em imitação a Cristo sofredor.

Day 7: Boldness in confessing the faith

Dia 7: Ousadia em confessar a fé

Tradition holds that Ignatius confessed Christ before the emperor Trajan without flinching, choosing chains over compromise. He reminds us that the name of Jesus is worth more than the favor of any earthly power.

A tradição conta que Inácio confessou a Cristo diante do imperador Trajano sem vacilar, preferindo as correntes ao acordo com o erro. Ele nos lembra que o nome de Jesus vale mais do que o favor de qualquer poder terreno.

Intention

For the courage to confess our faith openly, whatever it may cost.

Pela coragem de confessar abertamente nossa fé, custe o que custar.

Day 8: Joy on the way to God

Dia 8: Alegria no caminho para Deus

Far from dreading his end, Ignatius wrote that only when he reached the beasts would he truly begin to be a disciple. His joy in the face of death reveals a soul already living in the presence of the Lord.

Longe de temer seu fim, Inácio escreveu que somente ao chegar diante das feras começaria de fato a ser discípulo. Sua alegria diante da morte revela uma alma que já vivia na presença do Senhor.

Intention

For the gift of supernatural joy that no trial can extinguish.

Pelo dom da alegria sobrenatural que nenhuma provação pode extinguir.

Day 9: To live in Christ alone

Dia 9: Viver somente em Cristo

Ignatius declared that he sought the One who died for him and rose again, desiring nothing of this passing world. His final witness teaches us that Christ alone is the treasure worth every loss.

Inácio declarou que buscava Aquele que morreu por ele e ressuscitou, nada desejando deste mundo passageiro. Seu testemunho final nos ensina que somente Cristo é o tesouro que vale toda perda.

Intention

For the grace to make Christ the center and treasure of our whole life.

Pela graça de fazer de Cristo o centro e o tesouro de toda a nossa vida.

Closing prayer

Glorious St. Ignatius, you ran to martyrdom as to a wedding feast, that you might be united forever with the Lord you loved. Obtain for us hearts undivided, a love stronger than fear, and a Church bound together in charity and truth. Amen.

Glorioso Santo Inácio, vós que correstes ao martírio como a um banquete nupcial, para serdes unido para sempre ao Senhor que amastes, alcançai-nos um coração sem divisão, um amor mais forte que o medo, e uma Igreja unida na caridade e na verdade. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Nine days built on one repeated core prayer with a single changing petition line. Each day: "In the name of the Father..." → "Blessed holy servant of God, St. Ignatius of Antioch, we pray today for protection of the faithful throughout the world." (acknowledging his martyrdom and asking for spiritual growth) → a day-specific "Pray for us, that..." petition → "Glory be to the Father..." Daily petitions: (1) that persecution not bring hopelessness; (2) faith strengthened when tested; (3) disease not take away our joy; (4) courage to witness Christ's love; (5) offer suffering in union with Christ; (6) glorify God through trials; (7) hope of everlasting life; (8) hearts filled with tranquility and peace; (9) spiritual protection through faith in Jesus.

Fidelity note

Both are nine-day novenas to the same martyr, but the resemblance is thematic rather than textual. Ember composes a distinct life-and-letters meditation for each day, anchored in Ignatius's own famous words ("wheat of God," "medicine of immortality," "imitator of the passion of my God," union around the bishop "as strings to a harp") — material the PrayMoreNovenas version does not draw on; PMN instead repeats a single generic core prayer with a rotating one-line petition. Ember's content is markedly richer and more historically grounded in Ignatius's seven letters, and is plainly an original composition rather than a reworking of the PMN text. The shared notes are the martyr's joy in suffering and the call to belong wholly to Christ.

St. Peter of Alcantara st-peter-of-alcantara-novena

Oct 10–18
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Peter of Alcantara, Franciscan reformer, mystic, and penitent, who guided St. Teresa of Ávila in the ways of prayer.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Pedro de Alcântara, reformador franciscano, místico e penitente, que guiou Santa Teresa de Ávila nos caminhos da oração.

St. Peter of Alcantara (1499–1562) was born in Extremadura, Spain, and entered the Franciscans as a young man. He launched a strict reform of the Order, leading his friars in extreme poverty, fasting, and contemplation. As confessor and friend of St. Teresa of Ávila, he encouraged her Carmelite reform and confirmed her interior life; the Church remembers him as a master of prayer and a man who treated his body with heroic severity for the love of God.

São Pedro de Alcântara (1499–1562) nasceu na Estremadura, Espanha, e entrou para os franciscanos ainda jovem. Empreendeu uma reforma rigorosa da Ordem, conduzindo seus frades em extrema pobreza, jejum e contemplação. Como confessor e amigo de Santa Teresa de Ávila, encorajou sua reforma carmelita e confirmou sua vida interior; a Igreja o recorda como mestre de oração e como homem que tratou o próprio corpo com heroica severidade pelo amor de Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O God, who adorned St. Peter of Alcantara with the spirit of penance and the gift of deep prayer, grant us through his intercession to seek You above all things. Teach us to die to ourselves that we may live more fully in You. Amen.

Ó Deus, que ornastes São Pedro de Alcântara com o espírito de penitência e o dom da oração profunda, concedei-nos, por sua intercessão, buscar-Vos acima de todas as coisas. Ensinai-nos a morrer para nós mesmos, a fim de vivermos mais plenamente em Vós. Amém.

Day 1: Renouncing all for Christ

Dia 1: Renunciar a tudo por Cristo

As a young man, Peter left behind a noble household to embrace the poverty of St. Francis. He teaches us that the soul is free only when it has let go of what the world prizes, in order to grasp the one thing necessary.

Ainda jovem, Pedro deixou uma casa nobre para abraçar a pobreza de São Francisco. Ele nos ensina que a alma só é livre quando se desprende daquilo que o mundo estima, a fim de alcançar o único necessário.

Intention

For the grace to let go of whatever keeps us from giving ourselves fully to God.

Pela graça de abandonar tudo aquilo que nos impede de nos entregar plenamente a Deus.

Day 2: The love of poverty

Dia 2: O amor à pobreza

Peter's friars built cells so small a man could scarcely lie down, owning nothing and begging their bread. His radical poverty was not gloom but joy, the freedom of a heart that had found its treasure in God alone.

Os frades de Pedro construíam celas tão pequenas que mal cabia um homem deitado, nada possuindo e mendigando o próprio pão. Sua pobreza radical não era tristeza, mas alegria — a liberdade de um coração que encontrou seu tesouro somente em Deus.

Intention

For a spirit of simplicity and contentment with little.

Por um espírito de simplicidade e de contentamento com o pouco.

Day 3: Heroic penance

Dia 3: Penitência heroica

Peter slept barely an hour and a half each night, often upright, and fasted with astonishing severity. He confessed to St. Teresa that such mortification cost him dearly, showing us that true penance is offered not for its own sake, but out of love.

Pedro dormia apenas uma hora e meia por noite, muitas vezes sentado, e jejuava com severidade espantosa. Confessou a Santa Teresa que tal mortificação lhe custava muito, mostrando-nos que a verdadeira penitência não se oferece por si mesma, mas por amor.

Intention

For the grace to embrace small self-denials with a loving heart.

Pela graça de abraçar pequenas renúncias com coração amoroso.

Day 4: A master of prayer

Dia 4: Mestre de oração

Peter wrote a treatise on prayer and meditation that has guided souls for centuries. He taught that prayer is the breath of the spiritual life, and that without it the soul withers however busy it may be.

Pedro escreveu um tratado sobre a oração e a meditação que guia almas há séculos. Ensinou que a oração é a respiração da vida espiritual, e que sem ela a alma definha, por mais ocupada que esteja.

Intention

For perseverance and fervor in our daily prayer.

Pela perseverança e pelo fervor em nossa oração diária.

Day 5: Guiding St. Teresa of Ávila

Dia 5: Guiar Santa Teresa de Ávila

When St. Teresa was troubled and doubted, Peter recognized the work of God in her soul and confirmed her with calm certainty. He shows us how one holy friendship can strengthen a soul to do great things for the Lord.

Quando Santa Teresa estava angustiada e duvidava, Pedro reconheceu a obra de Deus em sua alma e a confirmou com serena certeza. Ele nos mostra como uma só amizade santa pode fortalecer uma alma a realizar grandes coisas pelo Senhor.

Intention

For holy friendships that draw us closer to God.

Por amizades santas que nos aproximem de Deus.

Day 6: Contemplation and silence

Dia 6: Contemplação e silêncio

Peter spent long hours in solitude, absorbed in the love of God, sometimes rapt in ecstasy. He reminds us that beneath the noise of life there is a quiet place where the soul meets its Maker.

Pedro passava longas horas na solidão, absorto no amor de Deus, por vezes arrebatado em êxtase. Ele nos lembra que, sob o ruído da vida, há um lugar silencioso onde a alma encontra o seu Criador.

Intention

For the grace to seek silence and recollection amid our daily noise.

Pela graça de buscar o silêncio e o recolhimento em meio ao ruído de cada dia.

Day 7: Reforming with patience

Dia 7: Reformar com paciência

Peter's reform met resistance and misunderstanding, yet he advanced with patience and trust in Providence. He teaches us that the renewal God desires is built slowly, in humility, and never by force or bitterness.

A reforma de Pedro encontrou resistência e incompreensão, mas ele avançou com paciência e confiança na Providência. Ele nos ensina que a renovação que Deus deseja se constrói lentamente, na humildade, e nunca pela força ou pela amargura.

Intention

For patience and trust in God when our efforts to do good are opposed.

Pela paciência e confiança em Deus quando nossos esforços pelo bem encontram oposição.

Day 8: Mastery over the body

Dia 8: Domínio sobre o corpo

Peter treated his body as a willing servant rather than a master, training his senses to obey the spirit. His example calls us to a wise discipline that frees us to love God with our whole strength.

Pedro tratava o corpo como servo dócil, e não como senhor, educando os sentidos a obedecer ao espírito. Seu exemplo nos chama a uma disciplina sábia que nos liberta para amar a Deus com todas as nossas forças.

Intention

For temperance and self-mastery in all things.

Pela temperança e pelo domínio de si em todas as coisas.

Day 9: A holy death and the vision of God

Dia 9: Uma santa morte e a visão de Deus

Peter died kneeling in prayer, rejoicing to be going home, and St. Teresa later recounted that he appeared to her in glory. His passing assures us that a life poured out for God ends not in loss, but in the joy of His presence.

Pedro morreu ajoelhado em oração, alegrando-se por voltar para casa, e Santa Teresa relatou mais tarde que ele lhe apareceu na glória. Sua partida nos assegura que uma vida derramada por Deus não termina em perda, mas na alegria de Sua presença.

Intention

For the grace of a holy death and the hope of seeing God face to face.

Pela graça de uma santa morte e pela esperança de ver a Deus face a face.

Closing prayer

Glorious St. Peter of Alcantara, lover of poverty and silence, you found God in the cell of your own heart. Obtain for us a spirit of holy detachment, perseverance in prayer, and the courage to seek nothing but the will of God. Amen.

Glorioso São Pedro de Alcântara, amante da pobreza e do silêncio, vós que encontrastes a Deus na cela do próprio coração, alcançai-nos um espírito de santo desapego, perseverança na oração e a coragem de buscar somente a vontade de Deus. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

With no FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas text to compare against, this stands as an original composition. The content is nevertheless faithful to the historical St. Peter of Alcantara: it accurately reflects his Franciscan reform and extreme poverty, his famous austerities (the tiny cells, the minimal sleep), his treatise on prayer and meditation, and his documented spiritual direction of St. Teresa of Ávila — including her testimony that he appeared to her after death. The day themes form a coherent arc from renunciation through prayer and self-mastery to a holy death, hagiographically sound and well-suited to a nine-day novena.

St. John Paul II st-john-paul-ii-novena

Oct 13–21
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. John Paul II, the pope who told the world 'Be not afraid!' and gave himself wholly to Christ through Mary.

Uma novena de nove dias a São João Paulo II, o papa que disse ao mundo 'Não tenhais medo!' e se entregou inteiramente a Cristo por meio de Maria.
