Karol Wojtyła (1920–2005) was born in Poland, survived Nazi occupation and Communist rule, and became Archbishop of Kraków before his election as Pope John Paul II in 1978. Over twenty-six years he traveled the world, defended the dignity of every human life, taught the Theology of the Body, entrusted himself to Mary with the motto 'Totus Tuus,' and bore his final illness in the sight of all. Canonized in 2014, he is remembered as a tireless herald of Christ and a father to the young.

Karol Wojtyła (1920–2005) nasceu na Polônia, sobreviveu à ocupação nazista e ao regime comunista, e tornou-se arcebispo de Cracóvia antes de ser eleito Papa João Paulo II em 1978. Por vinte e seis anos percorreu o mundo, defendeu a dignidade de toda vida humana, ensinou a Teologia do Corpo, entregou-se a Maria com o lema 'Totus Tuus' e suportou sua enfermidade final à vista de todos. Canonizado em 2014, é recordado como incansável arauto de Cristo e pai dos jovens.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Loving Father, who gave St. John Paul II to Your Church as a fearless witness to Christ, grant us through his intercession the courage to open wide the doors of our hearts to the Redeemer. Help us, like him, to belong entirely to Jesus through Mary. Amen.

Pai amoroso, que destes São João Paulo II à vossa Igreja como destemido testemunho de Cristo, concedei-nos, por sua intercessão, a coragem de abrir de par em par as portas do nosso coração ao Redentor. Ajudai-nos, como ele, a pertencer inteiramente a Jesus por meio de Maria. Amém.

Day 1: Be not afraid!

Dia 1: Não tenhais medo!

At the beginning of his pontificate, John Paul II cried out to a fearful world, 'Be not afraid! Open wide the doors to Christ!' He teaches us that fear loses its power over a heart that has welcomed the Redeemer.

No início de seu pontificado, João Paulo II bradou a um mundo temeroso: 'Não tenhais medo! Abri de par em par as portas a Cristo!' Ele nos ensina que o medo perde seu poder sobre o coração que acolheu o Redentor.

Intention

For the courage to open every part of our lives to Christ.

Pela coragem de abrir a Cristo cada parte da nossa vida.

Day 2: Totus Tuus — wholly Mary's

Dia 2: Totus Tuus — inteiramente de Maria

John Paul II took as his motto 'Totus Tuus' — 'wholly Yours, Mary.' He shows us that consecration to Our Lady does not lessen our love for Christ, but carries us straight to her Son.

João Paulo II tomou como lema 'Totus Tuus' — 'inteiramente vosso, ó Maria'. Ele nos mostra que a consagração a Nossa Senhora não diminui o nosso amor a Cristo, mas nos conduz diretamente ao seu Filho.

Intention

For the grace to entrust ourselves entirely to Mary.

Pela graça de nos entregarmos inteiramente a Maria.

Day 3: Defender of every human life

Dia 3: Defensor de toda vida humana

In an age that grew cold to the weakest, John Paul II proclaimed the Gospel of Life, defending the child in the womb and the dying alike. He reminds us that every person, from conception to natural death, bears the image of God.

Numa época que se tornava fria com os mais frágeis, João Paulo II proclamou o Evangelho da Vida, defendendo tanto a criança no ventre quanto o moribundo. Ele nos lembra que toda pessoa, da concepção à morte natural, traz a imagem de Deus.

Intention

For the protection of human life at every stage and a renewed culture of life.

Pela proteção da vida humana em todas as suas etapas e por uma renovada cultura da vida.

Day 4: The beauty of married love

Dia 4: A beleza do amor conjugal

Through the Theology of the Body, John Paul II revealed that the human body itself speaks of God's love and the gift of self. He restored to marriage and chastity their true splendor as paths to holiness.

Por meio da Teologia do Corpo, João Paulo II revelou que o próprio corpo humano fala do amor de Deus e do dom de si. Ele devolveu ao matrimônio e à castidade o seu verdadeiro esplendor como caminhos de santidade.

Intention

For the strengthening of marriages and families in self-giving love.

Pelo fortalecimento dos matrimônios e das famílias no amor que se doa.

Day 5: Apostle of Divine Mercy

Dia 5: Apóstolo da Divina Misericórdia

John Paul II canonized St. Faustina and gave the world Divine Mercy Sunday, urging souls to trust in God's boundless forgiveness. Fittingly, he himself died on its vigil, commending the Church to the mercy he had preached.

João Paulo II canonizou Santa Faustina e deu ao mundo o Domingo da Divina Misericórdia, exortando as almas a confiar no perdão sem limites de Deus. De modo significativo, ele mesmo faleceu em sua véspera, confiando a Igreja à misericórdia que pregara.

Intention

For deep trust in God's mercy and the grace to extend mercy to others.

Por uma profunda confiança na misericórdia de Deus e pela graça de sermos misericordiosos com os outros.

Day 6: A father to the young

Dia 6: Pai dos jovens

John Paul II loved the young and gathered millions of them at World Youth Days, challenging them to be saints. He believed in the goodness hidden in every young heart and called them to settle for nothing less than greatness in God.

João Paulo II amava os jovens e reuniu milhões deles nas Jornadas Mundiais da Juventude, desafiando-os a serem santos. Ele acreditava no bem escondido em cada coração jovem e os chamava a não se contentarem com menos do que a grandeza em Deus.

Intention

For young people, that they may discover their call to holiness.

Pelos jovens, para que descubram seu chamado à santidade.

Day 7: Forgiveness and peace

Dia 7: Perdão e paz

After surviving an assassin's bullet, John Paul II went to the prison to forgive the man who shot him. He showed the world that mercy is stronger than vengeance and that peace begins in a reconciled heart.

Após sobreviver à bala de um assassino, João Paulo II foi à prisão perdoar o homem que atirou nele. Ele mostrou ao mundo que a misericórdia é mais forte que a vingança e que a paz começa num coração reconciliado.

Intention

For the grace to forgive those who have wronged us and to be peacemakers.

Pela graça de perdoar quem nos feriu e de sermos construtores da paz.

Day 8: Suffering offered to God

Dia 8: O sofrimento oferecido a Deus

In his final years, the once-vigorous pope grew frail and bore his illness before the eyes of the whole world. He turned weakness into a pulpit, teaching that suffering united to Christ has redeeming power.

Em seus últimos anos, o papa outrora vigoroso tornou-se frágil e carregou sua enfermidade diante dos olhos do mundo inteiro. Ele transformou a fraqueza em púlpito, ensinando que o sofrimento unido a Cristo tem poder redentor.

Intention

For the grace to unite our sufferings to the cross of Christ.

Pela graça de unir nossos sofrimentos à cruz de Cristo.

Day 9: Let me go to the Father's house

Dia 9: Deixai-me ir para a casa do Pai

As death drew near, John Paul II whispered his desire to go to the house of the Father, serene in the hope he had always preached. His holy passing crowned a life spent entirely for Christ and His Church.

Ao aproximar-se a morte, João Paulo II murmurou seu desejo de ir para a casa do Pai, sereno na esperança que sempre pregara. Sua santa partida coroou uma vida inteiramente gasta por Cristo e por Sua Igreja.

Intention

For the grace to live and die in joyful hope of eternal life.

Pela graça de viver e morrer na alegre esperança da vida eterna.

Closing prayer

St. John Paul II, friend of the young and defender of life, you taught us that man cannot live without love. Obtain for us a heart wholly given to Christ, unwavering hope in His mercy, and the strength to proclaim the Gospel without fear. Amen.

São João Paulo II, amigo dos jovens e defensor da vida, vós que nos ensinastes que o homem não pode viver sem amor, alcançai-nos um coração inteiramente entregue a Cristo, esperança firme em Sua misericórdia e a força de anunciar o Evangelho sem medo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Nine days on one repeated core prayer with a single changing petition. Each day: "In the name of the Father..." → "Most holy servant of God, Pope St. John Paul II, we pray today for the youth and for World Youth Day." → "We ask you to grant us your blessing from heaven!" → "Most holy St. John Paul II, you were a witness of Jesus Christ for the whole world." → a day-specific "Pray for us, that..." petition → "Hail Mary… Glory be… In the name of the Father..." Daily petitions: (1) the unhappy filled with joy; (2) convey forgiveness to those who have done wrong; (3) guided/protected on difficult paths; (4) grace to see goodness in our experiences; (5) bear witness to God's mercy; (6) share faith with those in doubt; (7) give hope to the discouraged; (8) love those who feel indifferent; (9) the love sparked in us transforms hearts.

Fidelity note

Both are nine-day novenas to JPII, but they are independent compositions. PrayMoreNovenas repeats one short core prayer (heavily centered on youth / World Youth Day) with a rotating petition line. Ember instead builds a distinct biographical meditation for each day that traces the actual landmarks of his life and teaching — "Be not afraid," Totus Tuus, the Gospel of Life, the Theology of the Body, Divine Mercy (and his death on its vigil), World Youth Days, his forgiveness of his would-be assassin, his final illness, and his death. Ember's version is substantially fuller and more historically specific; the only real overlap with PMN is the shared devotion to the young (Ember's Day 6) and the general theme of mercy. Faithful in spirit, clearly an original work rather than a reproduction.

St. Anthony of Sant'Anna Galvão st-anthony-galvao-novena

Oct 16–24
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Anthony of Sant'Anna Galvão, the first Brazilian-born saint, Franciscan friar of São Paulo and herald of Christ's peace.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Antônio de Sant'Ana Galvão, o primeiro santo nascido no Brasil, frade franciscano de São Paulo e arauto da paz de Cristo.

St. Anthony of Sant'Anna Galvão (1739–1822) was born in Guaratinguetá, São Paulo, and entered the Franciscan Order, being ordained a priest in 1762. In São Paulo he served as preacher, confessor, and porter, founded the Recolhimento de Nossa Senhora da Luz, and became famous for the little 'pílulas' — tiny paper pills bearing a Marian invocation — through which countless healings were reported. A man of peace, humility, and trust in Providence, he was canonized by Pope Benedict XVI in 2007, the first saint born in Brazil.

Santo Antônio de Sant'Ana Galvão (1739–1822) nasceu em Guaratinguetá, São Paulo, e entrou para a Ordem Franciscana, sendo ordenado sacerdote em 1762. Em São Paulo serviu como pregador, confessor e porteiro, fundou o Recolhimento de Nossa Senhora da Luz e tornou-se célebre pelas 'pílulas' — pequenas pílulas de papel com uma invocação mariana — pelas quais se relatam inúmeras curas. Homem de paz, humildade e confiança na Providência, foi canonizado pelo Papa Bento XVI em 2007, o primeiro santo nascido no Brasil.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O God, who raised up St. Anthony Galvão as a herald of Your peace among the people of Brazil, grant us through his intercession a humble and trusting heart. Through Our Lady, in whom he placed all his confidence, lead us closer to Your Son. Amen.

Ó Deus, que suscitastes Santo Antônio Galvão como arauto de vossa paz entre o povo do Brasil, concedei-nos, por sua intercessão, um coração humilde e confiante. Por Nossa Senhora, em quem ele depositou toda a sua confiança, conduzi-nos para mais perto de vosso Filho. Amém.

Day 1: Leaving all to follow Christ

Dia 1: Deixar tudo para seguir a Cristo

Born into a prominent family, Anthony renounced wealth and standing to clothe himself in the poor habit of St. Francis. He teaches us that the heart finds its true riches only when it gives itself to God.

Nascido em família de destaque, Antônio renunciou às riquezas e à posição social para vestir o pobre hábito de São Francisco. Ele nos ensina que o coração só encontra suas verdadeiras riquezas quando se entrega a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to seek God above worldly honor and comfort.

Pela graça de buscar a Deus acima da honra e do conforto do mundo.

Day 2: A heart consecrated to Mary

Dia 2: Um coração consagrado a Maria

Anthony bound himself to Our Lady as her devoted son and signed himself her slave of love. His tender Marian devotion reminds us that the surest path to Jesus passes through the heart of His Mother.

Antônio ligou-se a Nossa Senhora como filho dedicado e assinou-se escravo de amor da Virgem. Sua terna devoção mariana nos lembra que o caminho mais seguro para Jesus passa pelo coração de sua Mãe.

Intention

For a deeper love and confidence in the Blessed Virgin Mary.

Por um amor e uma confiança mais profundos na Santíssima Virgem Maria.

Day 3: The healing pills of faith

Dia 3: As pílulas de fé

When a man came to him gravely ill, Anthony gave him a tiny paper pill inscribed with a prayer to Our Lady, and the sick were restored. His famous 'pílulas' teach us that God works wonders through humble means and confident faith.

Quando um homem gravemente enfermo o procurou, Antônio lhe deu uma pequena pílula de papel com uma oração a Nossa Senhora, e os doentes recuperaram a saúde. Suas célebres 'pílulas' nos ensinam que Deus opera maravilhas por meios humildes e pela fé confiante.

Intention

For the healing of all who are sick in body or soul.

Pela cura de todos os que estão enfermos no corpo ou na alma.

Day 4: The patient confessor

Dia 4: O confessor paciente

For long hours Anthony sat in the confessional, drawing souls back to God with gentleness and patience. He shows us that there is no greater work of mercy than to lead a sinner to the peace of forgiveness.

Por longas horas Antônio permanecia no confessionário, reconduzindo as almas a Deus com brandura e paciência. Ele nos mostra que não há obra de misericórdia maior do que conduzir um pecador à paz do perdão.

Intention

For sincere repentance and a grateful love of the sacrament of Confession.

Por um arrependimento sincero e por um amor agradecido ao sacramento da Confissão.

Day 5: Founder of the Recolhimento da Luz

Dia 5: Fundador do Recolhimento da Luz

Anthony founded the Recolhimento de Nossa Senhora da Luz so that women might live consecrated to God in prayer and labor. He poured himself into this work, often laboring with his own hands, trusting Providence to supply what was needed.

Antônio fundou o Recolhimento de Nossa Senhora da Luz para que mulheres pudessem viver consagradas a Deus na oração e no trabalho. Entregou-se a essa obra, muitas vezes trabalhando com as próprias mãos, confiando à Providência o que faltava.

Intention

For all who consecrate their lives to God in religious life.

Por todos os que consagram a vida a Deus na vida religiosa.

Day 6: Trust in divine Providence

Dia 6: Confiança na divina Providência

Though his works often lacked money and material, Anthony never wavered in trusting that God would provide. His serene confidence teaches us to lay our worries before the Father who feeds the birds of the air.

Embora suas obras carecessem muitas vezes de recursos e materiais, Antônio jamais vacilou em confiar que Deus proveria. Sua serena confiança nos ensina a depor nossas preocupações diante do Pai que alimenta as aves do céu.

Intention

For the grace to trust God's providence amid our worries and needs.

Pela graça de confiar na providência de Deus em meio às nossas preocupações e necessidades.

Day 7: A man of peace

Dia 7: Um homem de paz

Known as a herald of Christ's peace, Anthony reconciled enemies and calmed troubled hearts wherever he went. He reminds us that peace is born of humility, and that the meek truly inherit the earth.

Conhecido como arauto da paz de Cristo, Antônio reconciliava inimigos e acalmava corações inquietos por onde passava. Ele nos lembra que a paz nasce da humildade, e que os mansos verdadeiramente herdam a terra.

Intention

For peace in our homes, our communities, and our nation.

Pela paz em nossos lares, em nossas comunidades e em nossa nação.

Day 8: Humble service of his brothers

Dia 8: Humilde serviço aos irmãos

Anthony gladly served as porter, preacher, and helper of the poor, never seeking honor for himself. His humility shows us that true greatness is found in serving others for the love of God.

Antônio serviu de bom grado como porteiro, pregador e auxílio dos pobres, sem jamais buscar honra para si. Sua humildade nos mostra que a verdadeira grandeza está em servir aos outros pelo amor de Deus.

Intention

For a humble and generous spirit in serving those around us.

Por um espírito humilde e generoso no serviço aos que nos cercam.

Day 9: Faithful to the end

Dia 9: Fiel até o fim

Anthony labored for souls until his old age and died beloved by the people of São Paulo, who proclaimed him a saint long before the Church did. His holy life assures us that quiet, faithful service is never forgotten by God.

Antônio trabalhou pelas almas até a velhice e morreu amado pelo povo de São Paulo, que o proclamou santo muito antes da Igreja. Sua vida santa nos assegura que o serviço fiel e discreto nunca é esquecido por Deus.

Intention

For the grace of perseverance in serving God faithfully all our days.

Pela graça da perseverança em servir a Deus com fidelidade por todos os nossos dias.

Closing prayer

St. Anthony Galvão, friend of the poor and instrument of healing, you spent yourself in serving the souls God placed before you. Obtain for us healing of body and spirit, peace in our trials, and an unshakable trust in the providence of God. Amen.

Santo Antônio Galvão, amigo dos pobres e instrumento de cura, vós que vos consumistes a serviço das almas que Deus vos confiou, alcançai-nos a cura do corpo e do espírito, a paz nas provações e uma confiança inabalável na providência de Deus. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

As Brazil's first native-born saint, St. Anthony Galvão has no FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas novena to compare against, so this is an original composition. It is faithful to his documented life: the renunciation of a prominent family for the Franciscans, his ardent Marian devotion (signing himself Mary's "slave"), the celebrated "pílulas" (paper pills with a Marian invocation linked to healings), his long hours as confessor and porter, his founding of the Recolhimento de Nossa Senhora da Luz in São Paulo, his trust in Providence, and his renown as a peacemaker beloved by the people. The themes are well chosen and historically accurate; the closing prayer's appeal for healing fittingly echoes the devotion's strongest popular association.

All Saints all-saints-novena

Oct 23–31
description & history

A nine-day novena for the feast of All Saints, honoring the great multitude before the Lamb and the universal call to holiness.

Uma novena de nove dias para a festa de Todos os Santos, honrando a grande multidão diante do Cordeiro e o chamado universal à santidade.

The Solemnity of All Saints, kept on November 1, celebrates the whole company of the blessed in heaven — the known and the unknown, martyrs and confessors, virgins and ordinary faithful who reached glory. Scripture shows them as a great multitude no one could count, standing before the Lamb in white robes (Revelation 7). The feast reminds the Church that holiness is not for a few, but the destiny to which God calls every soul.

A Solenidade de Todos os Santos, celebrada em 1º de novembro, festeja toda a multidão dos bem-aventurados no céu — os conhecidos e os desconhecidos, mártires e confessores, virgens e fiéis comuns que alcançaram a glória. A Escritura os mostra como uma grande multidão que ninguém podia contar, de pé diante do Cordeiro com vestes brancas (Apocalipse 7). A festa recorda à Igreja que a santidade não é para poucos, mas o destino ao qual Deus chama toda alma.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Almighty and eternal God, who gladden us by the merits and intercession of all Your saints, grant that we who honor them on earth may one day share their joy in heaven. Make us, by Your grace, members of that great multitude that praises You forever. Amen.

Deus todo-poderoso e eterno, que nos alegrais com os méritos e a intercessão de todos os vossos santos, concedei que nós, que os honramos na terra, possamos um dia partilhar de sua alegria no céu. Fazei-nos, por vossa graça, membros daquela grande multidão que Vos louva eternamente. Amém.

Day 1: The universal call to holiness

Dia 1: O chamado universal à santidade

The saints were not a separate breed of men, but sinners who let God's grace transform them. Their lives proclaim that holiness is the vocation of every baptized soul, including our own.

Os santos não foram uma raça distinta de homens, mas pecadores que deixaram a graça de Deus transformá-los. Suas vidas proclamam que a santidade é a vocação de toda alma batizada, inclusive a nossa.

Intention

For the grace to answer God's call to holiness in our own state of life.

Pela graça de responder ao chamado de Deus à santidade no nosso próprio estado de vida.

Day 2: The great multitude before the Lamb

Dia 2: A grande multidão diante do Cordeiro

St. John saw a multitude beyond counting, from every nation and tongue, robed in white before the throne. This vision assures us that heaven is full and welcoming, and that there is room there for us.

São João viu uma multidão incontável, de toda nação e língua, vestida de branco diante do trono. Essa visão nos assegura que o céu está cheio e acolhedor, e que há lugar ali também para nós.

Intention

For a living hope of heaven that shapes how we live each day.

Por uma esperança viva do céu que molde o nosso viver de cada dia.

Day 3: Poor in spirit

Dia 3: Pobres em espírito

The saints lived the first Beatitude, holding loosely to the things of earth so as to be filled with God. Their poverty of spirit opened their hands to receive the kingdom of heaven.

Os santos viveram a primeira bem-aventurança, soltando-se das coisas da terra para serem cheios de Deus. Sua pobreza de espírito abriu suas mãos para receber o reino dos céus.

Intention

For a detached heart that finds its treasure in God alone.

Por um coração desapegado que encontra seu tesouro somente em Deus.

Day 4: Meek and merciful

Dia 4: Mansos e misericordiosos

The Beatitudes promise mercy to the merciful and the earth to the meek, and the saints made these promises their way of life. They overcame evil not with force but with gentleness and forgiveness.

As bem-aventuranças prometem misericórdia aos misericordiosos e a terra aos mansos, e os santos fizeram dessas promessas o seu modo de viver. Venceram o mal não com a força, mas com a brandura e o perdão.

Intention

For the grace to be gentle and merciful toward everyone we meet.

Pela graça de sermos mansos e misericordiosos com todos os que encontramos.

Day 5: Pure of heart

Dia 5: Puros de coração

'Blessed are the pure of heart, for they shall see God.' The saints guarded their hearts from divided loves so that nothing might cloud their vision of the One they sought.

'Bem-aventurados os puros de coração, porque verão a Deus.' Os santos guardaram o coração dos amores divididos, para que nada turvasse a visão Daquele que buscavam.

Intention

For purity of heart and singleness of love for God.

Pela pureza de coração e por um amor sem divisão a Deus.

Day 6: The witness of the martyrs

Dia 6: O testemunho dos mártires

Countless saints poured out their blood rather than deny Christ, washing their robes white in the blood of the Lamb. Their courage shows us that no earthly loss can outweigh the gift of remaining faithful.

Incontáveis santos derramaram o próprio sangue em vez de negar a Cristo, lavando suas vestes no sangue do Cordeiro. Sua coragem nos mostra que nenhuma perda terrena supera o dom de permanecer fiel.

Intention

For courage to remain faithful to Christ in every trial.

Pela coragem de permanecer fiéis a Cristo em toda provação.

Day 7: The hidden saints

Dia 7: Os santos ocultos

Most of the blessed are unknown to the world — mothers and laborers, the poor and the forgotten, who loved God faithfully in ordinary days. Their hidden holiness teaches us that no quiet act of love is ever lost.

A maioria dos bem-aventurados é desconhecida do mundo — mães e trabalhadores, os pobres e os esquecidos, que amaram a Deus fielmente nos dias comuns. Sua santidade oculta nos ensina que nenhum ato silencioso de amor se perde.

Intention

For the grace to grow holy in the ordinary duties of each day.

Pela graça de crescer em santidade nos deveres ordinários de cada dia.

Day 8: The communion of saints

Dia 8: A comunhão dos santos

The saints in glory are not distant strangers but our family, bound to us in one Body and praying for us still. We are never alone on the journey, for heaven is full of friends who long to help us home.

Os santos na glória não são estranhos distantes, mas nossa família, unidos a nós num só Corpo e ainda intercedendo por nós. Nunca estamos sós na caminhada, pois o céu está cheio de amigos que anseiam por nos conduzir à casa do Pai.

Intention

For confidence in the prayers and friendship of the saints.

Pela confiança nas orações e na amizade dos santos.

Day 9: Pressing on toward the prize

Dia 9: Avançar rumo ao prêmio

Surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, we are urged to run our race with perseverance, keeping our eyes on Jesus. The saints who finished the course now cheer us on toward the same crown of life.

Cercados por tão grande nuvem de testemunhas, somos exortados a correr com perseverança a nossa corrida, com os olhos fixos em Jesus. Os santos que completaram a corrida agora nos animam rumo à mesma coroa da vida.

Intention

For perseverance to the end and the grace of a holy death.

Pela perseverança até o fim e pela graça de uma santa morte.

Closing prayer

All you holy men and women, citizens of heaven and our elder brothers and sisters in the faith, pray for us who still walk the pilgrim road. Obtain for us perseverance in grace, hunger for holiness, and the hope of one day joining your unending song before the throne of God. Amen.

Ó todos os santos e santas, cidadãos do céu e nossos irmãos mais velhos na fé, rogai por nós que ainda caminhamos como peregrinos. Alcançai-nos a perseverança na graça, a fome de santidade e a esperança de um dia nos unirmos ao vosso canto sem fim diante do trono de Deus. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="allsaints">
All
Saints Novena

Pray especially starting on October 23 and
ending on
October 31.

Let us begin, In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the
Holy Spirit. Amen.

The Antiphon: O All ye Saints of God, vouchsafe to intercede for our
salvation, and for that of all persons.

V. Be glad in our Lord, and rejoice ye just.

R. And Glory all ye right of Heart.

Let us Pray. Protect Thy people, O Lord: And, as we confide in the
patronage of Thy Apostles, Peter and Paul, and of Thy other Apostles,
so preserve and defend us continually. May all Thy Saints, we beseech
Thee, O Lord, everywhere assist us; that whiles we celebrate their
merits, we may find their patronage. Grant us Thy peace in our time,
and drive away all wickedness from Thy Church. Direct our ways, actions
and desires, and those of all Thy servants, in the prosperity of Thy
saving conduct: Grant to our benefactors eternal blessings, and to the
faithful departed, everlasting rest, through our Lord Jesus Thy Son.
Amen.

V. O Lord, hear my Prayer.

R. And let my Cry come unto Thee.

V. Bless us our Lord.

R. Thanks be to God.

V. May the souls of the faithful departed, through the Mercy of God,
rest in peace.

R. Amen.

O Blessed Saints of Paradise, the glorious Citizens of Heaven, as I
render most humble thanks to God for all the good He hath done you; so
I beseech you to remember me in your prayers, and obtain for me the
entire remission of my sins, the amendment of my life, and imitation of
your good spirit, and holy graces, that I may be perfectly reconciled
to my Savior, and always please Him, but especially I recommend to you
the hour of my death, that by your holy intercession my soul may depart
this world in the grace of God, and immediately come to Life
Everlasting. Amen.

(Mention your request here)

Recite one Our
Father, one Hail Mary and one Glory be <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

— not on PrayMoreNovenas

Fidelity note

The FishEaters All Saints novena text could not be extracted, so a line-by-line comparison was not possible; what follows assesses Ember on its own merits. Ember's version is a coherent, theologically sound nine-day reflection on the feast, weaving together the universal call to holiness, the Revelation 7 vision of the great multitude, the Beatitudes (Days 3–5), the martyrs and the hidden saints, the communion of saints, and the Hebrews 12 "cloud of witnesses." It reads as an original composition; absent the FishEaters source text, its closeness to that source cannot be confirmed, but it is faithful to the spirit and Scripture of the solemnity.

Holy Souls holy-souls-novena

Oct 25–Nov 2
description & history

A nine-day novena for the Holy Souls in purgatory — the Church Suffering — imploring God's mercy, the cleansing of their sins, and their swift entrance into eternal light.

Uma novena de nove dias pelas Almas do Purgatório — a Igreja Padecente — implorando a misericórdia de Deus, a purificação de seus pecados e sua pronta entrada na luz eterna.

From the earliest centuries the Church has prayed for her departed children, offering the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass for their relief. This devotion gathers the ancient cry of the De Profundis — 'Out of the depths I have cried to Thee, O Lord' — into nine days of intercession for the souls who are saved yet still being purified in love.

Desde os primeiros séculos a Igreja reza por seus filhos defuntos, oferecendo o Santo Sacrifício da Missa para seu alívio. Esta devoção recolhe o antigo clamor do De Profundis — 'Das profundezas clamo a Vós, Senhor' — em nove dias de intercessão pelas almas que, já salvas, ainda são purificadas no amor.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O God, the Creator and Redeemer of all the faithful, I come before You for the souls of the faithful departed who suffer in purgatory. By the Precious Blood of Your Son, wash away whatever still stains them, and lead them quickly into the light of Your presence. Amen.

Ó Deus, Criador e Redentor de todos os fiéis, eu me apresento diante de Vós pelas almas dos fiéis defuntos que padecem no purgatório. Pelo Preciosíssimo Sangue de Vosso Filho, lavai tudo o que ainda as mancha e conduzi-as prontamente à luz de Vossa presença. Amém.

Day 1: Out of the depths

Dia 1: Das profundezas

With the psalmist the Holy Souls cry from the depths, 'O Lord, hear my voice.' They can no longer help themselves, and they wait upon our prayers as a parched land waits for rain.

Com o salmista, as Almas clamam das profundezas: 'Senhor, ouvi a minha voz.' Já não podem ajudar a si mesmas e aguardam nossas orações como a terra ressequida aguarda a chuva.

Intention

For all the souls who have no one on earth to remember them.

Por todas as almas que não têm ninguém na terra que se lembre delas.

Day 2: The mercy of God

Dia 2: A misericórdia de Deus

'If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand?' Yet with the Lord there is mercy and plentiful redemption. The fire of purgatory is the mercy of God completing what His grace began.

'Se observardes as iniquidades, Senhor, quem poderá subsistir?' Mas em Vós há misericórdia e abundante redenção. O fogo do purgatório é a misericórdia de Deus que completa o que Sua graça começou.

Intention

For the souls who trusted in God's mercy and now await its fullness.

Pelas almas que confiaram na misericórdia de Deus e agora aguardam sua plenitude.

Day 3: A purifying love

Dia 3: Um amor que purifica

Purgatory is not punishment without hope but a love that burns away every imperfection. The souls there suffer gladly, for each pang draws them nearer to the God they already love.

O purgatório não é castigo sem esperança, mas um amor que consome toda imperfeição. As almas ali padecem de bom grado, pois cada dor as aproxima do Deus que já amam.

Intention

For souls being purified of the last attachments that keep them from God.

Pelas almas que se purificam dos últimos apegos que as afastam de Deus.

Day 4: The Holy Sacrifice of the Mass

Dia 4: O Santo Sacrifício da Missa

No gift relieves the dead like the Holy Mass, where the same Victim of Calvary is offered anew. To have a Mass said for a departed soul is to place the Blood of Christ between them and their debt.

Nenhum dom alivia os mortos como a Santa Missa, em que a mesma Vítima do Calvário é oferecida de novo. Mandar celebrar uma Missa por uma alma é colocar o Sangue de Cristo entre ela e sua dívida.

Intention

That many Masses may be offered for the relief of the Holy Souls.

Para que muitas Missas sejam oferecidas pelo alívio das Almas do Purgatório.

Day 5: Works of mercy for the dead

Dia 5: Obras de misericórdia pelos mortos

To pray for the living and the dead is a work of mercy the Church has never set aside. Our almsgiving, fasting, and indulgences are spiritual coin we may freely spend on souls who can no longer earn it themselves.

Rezar pelos vivos e pelos mortos é uma obra de misericórdia que a Igreja nunca abandonou. Nossas esmolas, jejuns e indulgências são moeda espiritual que podemos gastar livremente em favor de almas que já não podem merecê-la por si.

Intention

For the grace to offer my penances and good works for the faithful departed.

Pela graça de oferecer minhas penitências e boas obras pelos fiéis defuntos.

Day 6: My own departed

Dia 6: Meus próprios entes queridos

We owe a debt of love to our parents, friends, and benefactors who have gone before us. They taught us, fed us, and prayed for us; now they look to us in their need.

Devemos uma dívida de amor a nossos pais, amigos e benfeitores que partiram antes de nós. Eles nos ensinaram, nos sustentaram e rezaram por nós; agora voltam-se para nós em sua necessidade.

Intention

For the souls of my own family, friends, and benefactors.

Pelas almas de minha própria família, amigos e benfeitores.

Day 7: The communion of saints

Dia 7: A comunhão dos santos

The Church Militant on earth, the Church Suffering in purgatory, and the Church Triumphant in heaven are one body in Christ. Our prayers cross that threshold, and the souls we help will one day pray for us before the throne of God.

A Igreja Militante na terra, a Igreja Padecente no purgatório e a Igreja Triunfante no céu são um só corpo em Cristo. Nossas orações atravessam esse limiar, e as almas que ajudarmos um dia rogarão por nós diante do trono de Deus.

Intention

That the bond of charity between the living and the dead may grow strong.

Para que o vínculo de caridade entre os vivos e os mortos se fortaleça.

Day 8: The hope of the resurrection

Dia 8: A esperança da ressurreição

We do not grieve as those who have no hope, for Christ has conquered death and promised to raise up the bodies of the faithful. Every soul we pray for is destined for the glory of the resurrection.

Não nos entristecemos como os que não têm esperança, pois Cristo venceu a morte e prometeu ressuscitar os corpos dos fiéis. Cada alma por quem rezamos está destinada à glória da ressurreição.

Intention

For a deepened hope in the resurrection of the body and life everlasting.

Por uma esperança mais firme na ressurreição da carne e na vida eterna.

Day 9: Into eternal light

Dia 9: Para a luz eterna

At last the purified soul is summoned into the unveiled light of God, where mourning is ended and every tear is wiped away. May our prayers hasten that hour for the souls we have carried these nine days.

Enfim, a alma purificada é chamada à luz desvelada de Deus, onde cessa o pranto e toda lágrima é enxugada. Que nossas orações apressem essa hora para as almas que carregamos nestes nove dias.

Intention

That the Holy Souls may soon behold the face of God in glory.

Para que as Almas do Purgatório contemplem em breve a face de Deus na glória.

Closing prayer

Eternal rest grant unto them, O Lord, and let perpetual light shine upon them. May they rest in peace, and may the gates of heaven open to receive them into the company of the saints. Amen.

Dai-lhes, Senhor, o eterno descanso, e que a luz perpétua os ilumine. Que descansem em paz, e que as portas do céu se abram para recebê-los na companhia dos santos. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="allsouls">
All
Souls Novena

Say once a day for nine days, starting on 24 October
until the eve of All Souls Day.

O God, the Creater and Redeemer of all the faithful, grant to the souls
of Thy servants and handmaids departed, the remission of all their
sins; that through pious supplications they may obtain the pardon they
have always desired. Who livest and reignest with God the Father in the
unity of the Holy Ghost, God, world without end. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Nine days on a repeating framework with thematically varying daily petitions. Each day: "In the name of the Father..." → a shared opening: "Dear Lord, we trust in Your infinite goodness. We praise Your justice and implore Your mercy..." → a day-specific group/theme and petition → a personal-intention insertion point ("I especially ask in this novena...") → closing: "Lord, hear our prayers! In the name of the Father..." Daily themes target different groups of the dead: deceased loved ones (Days 1–2), priests (Day 3), religious (Day 4), those who died suddenly (Day 5), the repentant (Day 6), the holy (Day 7), the regretful (Day 8), and the forgotten (Day 9).

Fidelity note

The FishEaters text was not retrievable, so the comparison rests on PrayMoreNovenas. Both share the Holy Souls devotion and several anchors — trust in God's mercy/justice, the forgotten souls (Ember Day 1 / PMN Day 9), and intercession for one's own departed. But the wording is independent and the organizing principle differs: PMN sorts the nine days by category of the dead (priests, religious, the sudden, the repentant, etc.), whereas Ember builds a doctrinal/devotional arc — De Profundis, the nature of purgatory as purifying love, the Mass, works of mercy, the communion of saints, the resurrection, entrance into light. Ember leans on Psalm 130 and the traditional "Eternal rest" prayer for its frame; it is faithful to Catholic teaching on purgatory and clearly an original composition rather than a reproduction of either source.

Christ the King christ-the-king-novena

9 days before Christ the King
description & history

A nine-day novena acknowledging the universal kingship of Jesus Christ over every heart, family, and nation, and begging the coming of His reign of truth, life, holiness, justice, love, and peace.

Uma novena de nove dias que reconhece a realeza universal de Jesus Cristo sobre todo coração, família e nação, e implora a vinda de Seu reino de verdade, vida, santidade, justiça, amor e paz.

In 1925, amid the rise of secular powers that sought to banish God from public life, Pope Pius XI instituted the Feast of Christ the King in his encyclical Quas Primas. He proclaimed that Christ reigns not only over the faithful but over all creation, and that His kingdom is one 'of truth and life, of holiness and grace, of justice, love, and peace.'

Em 1925, em meio à ascensão de poderes seculares que buscavam banir Deus da vida pública, o Papa Pio XI instituiu a Festa de Cristo Rei em sua encíclica Quas Primas. Ele proclamou que Cristo reina não só sobre os fiéis, mas sobre toda a criação, e que Seu reino é um reino 'de verdade e de vida, de santidade e de graça, de justiça, de amor e de paz.'

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Lord Jesus Christ, King of kings and Lord of lords, I acknowledge You as the sovereign of all creation and the rightful ruler of my soul. Reign in my heart, my home, and my whole life, and let nothing in me resist Your gentle and saving rule. Amen.

Senhor Jesus Cristo, Rei dos reis e Senhor dos senhores, eu Vos reconheço como soberano de toda a criação e legítimo senhor da minha alma. Reinai em meu coração, em meu lar e em toda a minha vida, e que nada em mim resista ao Vosso domínio suave e salvador. Amém.

Day 1: King of all creation

Dia 1: Rei de toda a criação

All things were made through Christ and for Him, and in Him all things hold together. His kingship is not borrowed from men but flows from His very nature as the eternal Word and as our Redeemer.

Todas as coisas foram feitas por Cristo e para Ele, e n'Ele tudo subsiste. Sua realeza não é emprestada dos homens, mas brota de Sua própria natureza como Verbo eterno e como nosso Redentor.

Intention

For the grace to recognize Christ as Lord over all that exists.

Pela graça de reconhecer Cristo como Senhor de tudo o que existe.

Day 2: King of hearts

Dia 2: Rei dos corações

Christ does not reign by force but by love, seeking the free surrender of each heart. He stands at the door and knocks, and asks only that we open and let Him enter as Lord.

Cristo não reina pela força, mas pelo amor, buscando a entrega livre de cada coração. Ele está à porta e bate, e pede apenas que abramos e O deixemos entrar como Senhor.

Intention

That I may give Christ the throne of my own heart without reserve.

Para que eu entregue a Cristo o trono do meu próprio coração sem reservas.

Day 3: King of families

Dia 3: Rei das famílias

When Christ reigns in a home, He guards its peace and binds its members in charity. To enthrone Him in the family is to invite the King who blessed the household of Nazareth.

Quando Cristo reina num lar, Ele guarda sua paz e une seus membros na caridade. Entronizá-Lo na família é convidar o Rei que abençoou a casa de Nazaré.

Intention

That Christ may reign in every Christian family.

Para que Cristo reine em toda família cristã.

Day 4: King of nations

Dia 4: Rei das nações

Pius XI taught that rulers and assemblies are bound to give public honor to Christ the King, for nations that exile God invite ruin upon themselves. The peace of peoples is found in submission to His just rule.

Pio XI ensinou que governantes e assembleias são obrigados a prestar honra pública a Cristo Rei, pois as nações que exilam Deus atraem sobre si a ruína. A paz dos povos se encontra na submissão ao Seu domínio justo.

Intention

That rulers and nations may submit their laws to the law of Christ.

Para que governantes e nações submetam suas leis à lei de Cristo.

Day 5: A reign of truth and life

Dia 5: Um reino de verdade e de vida

Christ told Pilate that He came into the world to bear witness to the truth, and He is Himself the Way, the Truth, and the Life. His kingdom drives out the lies that enslave and bestows the life that never ends.

Cristo disse a Pilatos que viera ao mundo para dar testemunho da verdade, e Ele é Ele mesmo o Caminho, a Verdade e a Vida. Seu reino expulsa as mentiras que escravizam e concede a vida que não acaba.

Intention

That the truth of Christ may dispel error in the world.

Para que a verdade de Cristo dissipe o erro no mundo.

Day 6: A reign of holiness and grace

Dia 6: Um reino de santidade e de graça

The King who reigns from the Cross pours out grace to make His subjects holy. To belong to His kingdom is to be drawn, day by day, into the very holiness of God.

O Rei que reina da Cruz derrama a graça para tornar santos os Seus súditos. Pertencer ao Seu reino é ser atraído, dia após dia, à própria santidade de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to grow in holiness as a true subject of the King.

Pela graça de crescer em santidade como verdadeiro súdito do Rei.

Day 7: A reign of justice

Dia 7: Um reino de justiça

Christ the King gives to each his due and lifts up the lowly whom the world overlooks. Where He reigns, the poor are not forgotten and right ordering returns to a wounded world.

Cristo Rei dá a cada um o que lhe é devido e levanta os humildes que o mundo despreza. Onde Ele reina, os pobres não são esquecidos e a reta ordem volta a um mundo ferido.

Intention

That the justice of Christ may heal the wounds of society.

Para que a justiça de Cristo cure as feridas da sociedade.

Day 8: A reign of love

Dia 8: Um reino de amor

The scepter of this King is His pierced Heart, and the law of His kingdom is love. He conquers not by crushing His enemies but by laying down His life for them.

O cetro deste Rei é o Seu Coração trespassado, e a lei do Seu reino é o amor. Ele vence não esmagando os inimigos, mas dando a vida por eles.

Intention

That the love of Christ may rule all my dealings with others.

Para que o amor de Cristo governe todo o meu trato com os outros.

Day 9: A reign of peace

Dia 9: Um reino de paz

Christ is our peace, who reconciles all things to the Father by the Blood of His Cross. Only when He reigns within us and among us does true and lasting peace come to the world.

Cristo é a nossa paz, que reconcilia todas as coisas com o Pai pelo Sangue de Sua Cruz. Somente quando Ele reina em nós e entre nós é que a paz verdadeira e duradoura chega ao mundo.

Intention

That the peace of Christ may reign in hearts, families, and nations.

Para que a paz de Cristo reine nos corações, nas famílias e nas nações.

Closing prayer

Christ Jesus, King of all nations, may Your kingdom come and Your will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Subject all hearts to Your reign of truth and peace, that one day every knee may bend before You and every tongue confess that You are Lord. Amen.

Cristo Jesus, Rei de todas as nações, venha a nós o Vosso reino e seja feita a Vossa vontade assim na terra como no céu. Submetei todos os corações ao Vosso reino de verdade e de paz, para que um dia todo joelho se dobre diante de Vós e toda língua confesse que sois o Senhor. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="king">
Novena
to Christ the King

Pray once a day for 9 days, especially beginning 10 days
before the Feast of Christ the King (the last Sunday in October).

Say one Our Father, one
Hail Mary, and one Glory Be before praying the
following prayer:

O Lord our God, Thou alone art the Most Holy King and Ruler of all
nations.

We pray to Thee, Lord, in the great expectation of receiving from Thee,
O Divine King, mercy, peace, justice and all good things. Protect, O
Lord our King, our families and the land of our birth.

Guard us we pray, Most Faithful One. Protect us from our enemies and
from Thy just judgment. Forgive us, O Sovereign King, our sins against
Thee.

Jesus, Thou art a King of Mercy. We have deserved Thy just judgment.
Have mercy on us, Lord, and forgive us. We trust in Thy Great Mercy.

O most awe-inspiring King, We bow before Thee and pray: may Thy reign,
Thy Kingdom, be recognized on earth. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

— not on PrayMoreNovenas

Fidelity note

Ember shares the source's central theme — the universal kingship of Christ over creation, hearts, families, and nations, grounded in Pius XI's *Quas Primas* — and its nine-day frame with Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be. But the structures diverge: FishEaters repeats one fixed petition-prayer daily, whereas Ember is a fully composed nine-day cycle with distinct daily titles and meditations (notably the six attributes of Christ's reign — truth, life, holiness, justice, love, peace — drawn straight from the *Quas Primas* preface). The opening and closing prayers are Ember's own wording, not the FishEaters text. This is best read as a faithful original elaboration on the same devotion rather than a transcription.

Presentation of Mary presentation-of-mary-novena

Nov 12–20
description & history

A nine-day novena honoring the Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary in the temple, contemplating her total consecration to God from her earliest years and learning from her hidden self-offering.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra da Apresentação da Bem-Aventurada Virgem Maria no templo, contemplando sua total consagração a Deus desde os primeiros anos e aprendendo com sua oferenda oculta.

An ancient tradition, recalled in the East from the sixth century and kept in the West on November 21, tells how the child Mary was brought by her parents Joachim and Anne to be presented in the temple of Jerusalem. The feast celebrates not so much a historical detail as a truth: that Mary belonged wholly to God from her first dawning, the living temple of the Holy Spirit.

Uma antiga tradição, recordada no Oriente desde o século VI e guardada no Ocidente em 21 de novembro, narra como a menina Maria foi levada por seus pais Joaquim e Ana para ser apresentada no templo de Jerusalém. A festa celebra não tanto um detalhe histórico, mas uma verdade: que Maria pertenceu inteiramente a Deus desde sua primeira aurora, templo vivo do Espírito Santo.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Mary, presented in the temple as a little child and given wholly to God, draw me into your spirit of total consecration. Teach me to belong to the Lord without reserve, as you did from your earliest years. Amen.

Ó Maria, apresentada no templo ainda menina e entregue inteiramente a Deus, atraí-me ao vosso espírito de total consagração. Ensinai-me a pertencer ao Senhor sem reservas, como vós desde a mais tenra idade. Amém.

Day 1: The promised child

Dia 1: A menina prometida

Joachim and Anne, long childless, received Mary as the fruit of persevering prayer, and in gratitude they gave her back to God. Her whole life began as a gift returned to the Giver.

Joaquim e Ana, por muito tempo sem filhos, receberam Maria como fruto de oração perseverante e, em gratidão, devolveram-na a Deus. Toda a sua vida começou como um dom restituído àquele que o dera.

Intention

For parents who consecrate their children to God.

Pelos pais que consagram seus filhos a Deus.

Day 2: Offered to God

Dia 2: Oferecida a Deus

While still a small child Mary was brought to the temple and placed in the service of the Lord. She teaches us that no soul is too young, and no hour too early, to give itself entirely to God.

Ainda pequena, Maria foi levada ao templo e posta a serviço do Senhor. Ela nos ensina que nenhuma alma é jovem demais, nem hora alguma cedo demais, para entregar-se inteiramente a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to give myself to God without holding anything back.

Pela graça de entregar-me a Deus sem reter coisa alguma.

Day 3: The living temple

Dia 3: O templo vivo

Mary entered a temple of stone, yet she herself was the truer temple, conceived without sin and filled with grace. In her the Holy Spirit found a dwelling more glorious than any sanctuary built by hands.

Maria entrou num templo de pedra, mas ela mesma era o templo mais verdadeiro, concebida sem pecado e cheia de graça. N'ela o Espírito Santo encontrou uma morada mais gloriosa que qualquer santuário feito por mãos humanas.

Intention

That my soul may become a fitting temple of the Holy Spirit.

Para que minha alma se torne um templo digno do Espírito Santo.

Day 4: Consecration from the first moment

Dia 4: Consagração desde o primeiro instante

From the very first instant of her existence Mary was wholly the Lord's, untouched by sin and turned entirely toward Him. Her example invites us to make every moment, from rising to rest, an offering to God.

Desde o primeiríssimo instante de sua existência Maria foi toda do Senhor, intocada pelo pecado e voltada inteiramente para Ele. Seu exemplo nos convida a fazer de cada momento, do despertar ao repouso, uma oferta a Deus.

Intention

For the grace to renew my consecration to God each day.

Pela graça de renovar a cada dia minha consagração a Deus.

Day 5: The hidden years

Dia 5: Os anos ocultos

Tradition pictures Mary in the temple growing in silence, prayer, and humble work, unknown to the world. She shows that holiness is forged not in display but in faithfulness to the hidden duties of each day.

A tradição imagina Maria no templo crescendo em silêncio, oração e trabalho humilde, desconhecida do mundo. Ela mostra que a santidade se forja não na ostentação, mas na fidelidade aos deveres ocultos de cada dia.

Intention

For the grace to love the hidden, ordinary duties of my life.

Pela graça de amar os deveres ocultos e comuns da minha vida.

Day 6: A pure and undivided heart

Dia 6: Um coração puro e indiviso

Mary kept her heart wholly for God, undivided by any rival love. Her purity was not mere absence of stain but a single, burning desire for the One she served.

Maria guardou o coração inteiramente para Deus, indiviso por qualquer amor rival. Sua pureza não era mera ausência de mancha, mas um único e ardente desejo por Aquele a quem servia.

Intention

That my heart may be cleansed of every divided affection.

Para que meu coração seja purificado de toda afeição dividida.

Day 7: Self-offering in the everyday

Dia 7: A oferenda de si no cotidiano

Mary made of her whole life a quiet, continual gift, offering even small acts with great love. We too can lay our work, our trials, and our joys upon the altar as an unbroken offering.

Maria fez de toda a sua vida um dom silencioso e contínuo, oferecendo mesmo os pequenos atos com grande amor. Também nós podemos depositar no altar nosso trabalho, nossas provações e nossas alegrias como oferenda ininterrupta.

Intention

For the grace to offer each task of the day with love.

Pela graça de oferecer com amor cada tarefa do dia.

Day 8: Awaiting the Lord

Dia 8: À espera do Senhor

In the temple Mary waited upon God with watchful, expectant love, her whole being a 'yes' before the Annunciation was ever spoken. Her hidden readiness prepared her to receive the Word made flesh.

No templo Maria esperava por Deus com amor vigilante e cheio de expectativa, todo o seu ser um 'sim' antes mesmo que a Anunciação fosse pronunciada. Sua prontidão oculta a preparou para receber o Verbo feito carne.

Intention

For a heart ever ready to answer God's call.

Por um coração sempre pronto a responder ao chamado de Deus.

Day 9: Wholly the Lord's

Dia 9: Toda do Senhor

All that began at Mary's presentation reached its flower in her fiat and her place beneath the Cross. She who gave herself completely as a child gave herself completely to the end, and so became Mother of all the living.

Tudo o que começou na apresentação de Maria floresceu em seu fiat e em sua presença ao pé da Cruz. Ela que se entregou por inteiro quando menina entregou-se por inteiro até o fim, e assim tornou-se Mãe de todos os viventes.

Intention

That I may belong to the Lord completely, like Mary, all my days.

Para que eu pertença inteiramente ao Senhor, como Maria, todos os meus dias.

Closing prayer

Holy Virgin, living temple of the Most High, obtain for me the grace to offer myself daily to God as you offered yourself in the temple. Keep my heart pure and undivided, that I too may be a dwelling place worthy of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

Santa Virgem, templo vivo do Altíssimo, alcançai-me a graça de oferecer-me a Deus cada dia como vós vos oferecestes no templo. Conservai meu coração puro e indiviso, para que também eu seja morada digna do Espírito Santo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Each of the nine days opens with "In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen." and closes with personal intentions and thanks for Mary's faithfulness. Day-specific content:
- Day 1: Mary's presentation; "help me grow in desire to serve God. Help me to say yes" to His will even when fearful.
- Day 2: St. Anne, who "had just suffered through a period of infertility"; blessings on children worldwide; "Guide their parents to dedicate their children's lives" to God.
- Day 3: For those struggling with infertility; guidance toward "medical help" and openness to "adoption, spiritual parenthood, or some other ministry."
- Day 4: For priests; "Crush the devil's head" and "Defend your holy priests."
- Day 5: Scripture study; help to "better understand the Bible" and be "more dedicated to spending time reading God's word."
- Day 6: "We do not worship you" — clarifies Mary's intercessory role.
- Day 7: Temptation and sin; "help me to resist temptation."
- Day 8: The dying and suffering; "be with all who are ill" and grant "a peaceful death."
- Day 9: Mary's lifelong "yes"; help in trusting God's will and dedicating oneself to Him.

Fidelity note

Ember and PrayMoreNovenas share the core devotion and a couple of anchoring motifs — Mary as a child given wholly to God, the role of Joachim and Anne, and the lifelong "yes" culminating in the fiat (Ember's Days 1 and 9 echo PMN's Days 1–2 and 9). Beyond that the daily content diverges substantially: PMN builds its days around modern intercessory topics (infertility, priests, Bible study, the dying), while Ember pursues a contemplative arc on consecration, the living temple, the hidden years, and self-offering. The opening and closing prayers are Ember's own composition, not PMN's wording. Faithful to the feast and spirit, but an independent meditation set rather than a port of the PMN text.

St. Catherine of Alexandria st-catherine-alexandria-novena

Nov 16–24
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Catherine of Alexandria, virgin and martyr, whose God-given wisdom confounded the pagan philosophers and whose courage broke even the wheel of torture — patroness of students, scholars, and all who seek the truth.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Catarina de Alexandria, virgem e mártir, cuja sabedoria recebida de Deus confundiu os filósofos pagãos e cuja coragem quebrou até a roda do suplício — padroeira dos estudantes, dos sábios e de todos os que buscam a verdade.

According to ancient tradition, Catherine was a noble and learned maiden of Alexandria in the early fourth century who confronted the emperor Maxentius for persecuting Christians. When fifty pagan philosophers were summoned to refute her, she instead converted them by her wisdom. Condemned to die on a spiked wheel, she touched it and it shattered; she was then beheaded, and is honored among the Fourteen Holy Helpers.

Segundo a antiga tradição, Catarina era uma jovem nobre e instruída de Alexandria, no início do século IV, que enfrentou o imperador Maxêncio por perseguir os cristãos. Quando cinquenta filósofos pagãos foram convocados para refutá-la, ela ao contrário os converteu por sua sabedoria. Condenada a morrer numa roda cravejada, ela a tocou e a roda se despedaçou; foi então decapitada, e é honrada entre os Catorze Santos Auxiliadores.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O glorious St. Catherine, virgin and martyr, who confessed Christ before kings and confounded the wise of this world, intercede for me before the throne of God. Obtain for me a share in your wisdom, your purity, and your unshakable courage. Amen.

Ó gloriosa Santa Catarina, virgem e mártir, que confessastes a Cristo diante dos reis e confundistes os sábios deste mundo, intercedei por mim diante do trono de Deus. Alcançai-me uma parte de vossa sabedoria, de vossa pureza e de vossa coragem inabalável. Amém.

Day 1: A heart seeking the truth

Dia 1: Um coração que busca a verdade

Catherine devoted her early years to the study of philosophy and the sciences, but her restless mind found peace only when it found Christ. She teaches that all true learning leads at last to the One who is Truth itself.

Catarina dedicou seus primeiros anos ao estudo da filosofia e das ciências, mas sua mente inquieta só encontrou paz quando encontrou Cristo. Ela ensina que todo verdadeiro saber conduz por fim Àquele que é a própria Verdade.

Intention

For all who seek truth in their studies, that they may find Christ.

Por todos os que buscam a verdade em seus estudos, para que encontrem Cristo.

Day 2: Wisdom from above

Dia 2: Sabedoria do alto

Summoned before fifty learned philosophers, Catherine spoke with a wisdom that was not her own but the gift of the Holy Spirit. She fulfilled the Lord's promise to give His witnesses words their adversaries could not resist.

Convocada diante de cinquenta filósofos eruditos, Catarina falou com uma sabedoria que não era sua, mas dom do Espírito Santo. Ela cumpriu a promessa do Senhor de dar às Suas testemunhas palavras às quais os adversários não poderiam resistir.

Intention

For the gift of wisdom to know and defend the truth of the faith.

Pelo dom da sabedoria para conhecer e defender a verdade da fé.

Day 3: Confounding the wise

Dia 3: Confundindo os sábios

The philosophers who came to silence Catherine were instead converted by her arguments and embraced the faith they had been called to attack. God chooses the lowly to shame the strong and the simple to confound the proud.

Os filósofos que vieram silenciar Catarina foram, ao contrário, convertidos por seus argumentos e abraçaram a fé que tinham sido chamados a atacar. Deus escolhe os humildes para confundir os fortes e os simples para envergonhar os soberbos.

Intention

That intellectuals and scholars may be led from error to faith.

Para que intelectuais e estudiosos sejam conduzidos do erro à fé.

Day 4: Courage before the powerful

Dia 4: Coragem diante dos poderosos

Catherine did not flatter the emperor but rebuked his cruelty to her face, fearing God more than any earthly throne. She gives courage to all who must speak the truth before those who hold power.

Catarina não adulou o imperador, mas censurou sua crueldade face a face, temendo a Deus mais do que qualquer trono terreno. Ela dá coragem a todos os que devem dizer a verdade diante dos que detêm o poder.

Intention

For the courage to confess my faith without fear of the world.

Pela coragem de confessar minha fé sem temor do mundo.

Day 5: Purity for the King of heaven

Dia 5: Pureza para o Rei do céu

Offered marriage and honors if she would renounce Christ, Catherine refused, having given her whole heart to her heavenly Bridegroom. Her purity was the freedom of a soul that loved God above every passing reward.

Quando lhe ofereceram casamento e honrarias se renunciasse a Cristo, Catarina recusou, pois dera o coração inteiro ao seu Esposo celeste. Sua pureza era a liberdade de uma alma que amava a Deus acima de toda recompensa passageira.

Intention

For the grace of purity and undivided love of God.

Pela graça da pureza e do amor indiviso a Deus.

Day 6: The breaking wheel

Dia 6: A roda que se quebra

Condemned to be torn apart on a spiked wheel, Catherine prayed, and the instrument of her torture shattered at her touch. God showed that no power of this world can crush the soul that rests in Him.

Condenada a ser dilacerada numa roda cravejada, Catarina rezou, e o instrumento de seu suplício se despedaçou ao seu toque. Deus mostrou que nenhum poder deste mundo pode esmagar a alma que descansa n'Ele.

Intention

For trust in God's protection amid suffering and trial.

Pela confiança na proteção de Deus em meio ao sofrimento e à provação.

Day 7: Faithful unto death

Dia 7: Fiel até a morte

When the wheel failed, Catherine was beheaded, sealing her witness with her blood. She received the martyr's crown for being faithful to the end, choosing death rather than the slightest betrayal of Christ.

Quando a roda falhou, Catarina foi decapitada, selando seu testemunho com o próprio sangue. Ela recebeu a coroa do martírio por ser fiel até o fim, escolhendo a morte em vez da menor traição a Cristo.

Intention

For perseverance in the faith until my final breath.

Pela perseverança na fé até o meu último suspiro.

Day 8: Patroness of students and scholars

Dia 8: Padroeira dos estudantes e dos sábios

Because she joined deep learning to deeper holiness, Catherine became the patroness of students, teachers, and philosophers. She reminds the learned that knowledge without love of God can puff up, but wisdom with humility saves.

Por ter unido o saber profundo a uma santidade ainda mais profunda, Catarina tornou-se padroeira dos estudantes, dos mestres e dos filósofos. Ela lembra aos sábios que o conhecimento sem o amor de Deus pode ensoberbecer, mas a sabedoria com humildade salva.

Intention

For students and teachers, that their learning may serve God.

Pelos estudantes e mestres, para que seu saber sirva a Deus.

Day 9: One of the Holy Helpers

Dia 9: Uma dos Santos Auxiliadores

Among the Fourteen Holy Helpers, Catherine is invoked by the faithful in their needs and especially at the hour of death. She who broke the wheel by prayer is a powerful intercessor for all who call upon her.

Entre os Catorze Santos Auxiliadores, Catarina é invocada pelos fiéis em suas necessidades e especialmente na hora da morte. Ela, que quebrou a roda pela oração, é poderosa intercessora por todos os que a invocam.

Intention

For St. Catherine's intercession in all my needs and at my death.

Pela intercessão de Santa Catarina em todas as minhas necessidades e na minha morte.

Closing prayer

St. Catherine of Alexandria, faithful unto death and crowned with glory, lead me along the path of truth and keep me steadfast in the faith. By your powerful intercession, help me to love wisdom that comes from above and to remain Christ's to the end. Amen.

Santa Catarina de Alexandria, fiel até a morte e coroada de glória, conduzi-me pelo caminho da verdade e conservai-me firme na fé. Por vossa poderosa intercessão, ajudai-me a amar a sabedoria que vem do alto e a permanecer de Cristo até o fim. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="catherineofalexandria">
Novena
to St. Catherine of Alexandria

Pray once a day for nine days, especially from November
16 and ending on November 24, the eve of St. Catherine's Feast

Almighty and eternal God! With lively faith and reverently worshipping
Thy Divine Majesty, I prostrate myself before Thee and invoke with
filial trust Thy supreme bounty and mercy. Illumine the darkness of my
intellect with a ray of Thy heavenly light and inflame my heart with
the fire of Thy Divine love, that I may contemplate the great virtues
and merits of Saint Catherine of Alexandria in whose honor I make this
novena,
and following her example imitate, like her, the life of Thy Divine
Son.

Moreover, I beseech Thee to grant graciously, through the merits and
intercession of this powerful Helper, the petition which through him I
humbly place before Thee, devoutly saying, “Thy will be done on earth
as it is in heaven.” Vouchsafe graciously to hear it, if it redounds to
Thy greater glory and to the salvation of my soul. Amen.

(Mention your intentions here)

O God, Who didst distinguish Thy holy virgin and martyr
Catherine by the gift of great wisdom and virtue, and a victorious
combat with the enemies of the Faith; grant us, we beseech Thee,
through her intercession, constancy in the Faith and the wisdom of the
saints, that we may devote all the powers of our mind and heart to Thy
service. Through Christ our Lord. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Each of the nine days opens with "In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.", gives thanks to God for St. Catherine as an example with a day-specific focus, addresses a petition to her, and closes with "St. Catherine of Alexandria, pray for us!" plus space for personal intentions ("Please also pray for (mention your intentions here)"). Daily themes: Day 1 — active choice to love God despite persecution; Day 2 — bold defense of others / confronting injustice; Day 3 — courage facing torture and death; Day 4 — eloquent defense of the faith; Day 5 — fortitude amid suffering; Day 6 — faithfulness amid deprivation; Day 7 — inspiring conversions through suffering; Day 8 — rejecting worldly power for devotion; Day 9 — courageously facing martyrdom.

Fidelity note

Ember tracks the same hagiographic arc as the PrayMoreNovenas version — Catherine's learning, her confrontation with the emperor, the conversion of the philosophers, her purity, the shattered wheel, and her martyrdom — and both attach a daily petition and personal intention. The ordering differs (Ember places wisdom/conversion early and martyrdom late, while PMN front-loads courage and ends on martyrdom), and Ember adds two distinctively traditional days PMN does not stress: patroness of students/scholars and her place among the Fourteen Holy Helpers. Wording is Ember's own throughout; the opening and closing prayers do not reproduce PMN's text. The FishEaters source could not be verified. Overall a faithful retelling of the same saint's narrative in original prose.

Immaculate Conception immaculate-conception-novena

Nov 29–Dec 7
description & history

A nine-day novena in honor of the Immaculate Conception of the Blessed Virgin Mary, conceived without sin and full of grace — the all-beautiful one, the dawn of our redemption.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra da Imaculada Conceição da Bem-Aventurada Virgem Maria, concebida sem pecado e cheia de graça — a toda formosa, a aurora da nossa redenção.

On December 8, 1854, Pope Pius IX defined the dogma that Mary, from the first instant of her conception, was preserved free from all stain of original sin by a singular grace and in view of the merits of Christ. Four years later, at Lourdes, the Lady confirmed it to St. Bernadette: 'I am the Immaculate Conception.' The Church sings to her in the words of the Tota Pulchra: 'You are all beautiful, O Mary, and the original stain is not in you.'

Em 8 de dezembro de 1854, o Papa Pio IX definiu o dogma de que Maria, desde o primeiro instante de sua concepção, foi preservada livre de toda mancha do pecado original, por singular graça e em vista dos méritos de Cristo. Quatro anos depois, em Lourdes, a Senhora confirmou-o a Santa Bernadette: 'Eu sou a Imaculada Conceição.' A Igreja a ela canta nas palavras do Tota Pulchra: 'Sois toda formosa, ó Maria, e em vós não há a mancha original.'

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O Mary conceived without sin, all-beautiful and full of grace, I come to you who were never for a moment a slave of the enemy. Draw me into your purity, and obtain for me the grace to hate sin and to love God as you do. Amen.

Ó Maria concebida sem pecado, toda formosa e cheia de graça, eu venho a vós, que nunca por um só instante fostes escrava do inimigo. Atraí-me à vossa pureza e alcançai-me a graça de odiar o pecado e de amar a Deus como vós O amais. Amém.

Day 1: Conceived without sin

Dia 1: Concebida sem pecado

From the first instant of her conception Mary was preserved free from all stain of original sin, a privilege granted to no other creature. God prepared a worthy dwelling for His Son before the world ever knew her name.

Desde o primeiro instante de sua concepção Maria foi preservada livre de toda mancha do pecado original, privilégio concedido a nenhuma outra criatura. Deus preparou uma morada digna para Seu Filho antes mesmo que o mundo soubesse seu nome.

Intention

For a deeper gratitude for the gift of Mary's Immaculate Conception.

Por uma gratidão mais profunda pelo dom da Imaculada Conceição de Maria.

Day 2: Full of grace

Dia 2: Cheia de graça

The angel greeted Mary as 'full of grace,' a vessel so filled with God that no room remained for sin. Her soul was a garden enclosed where the Lord delighted to dwell.

O anjo saudou Maria como 'cheia de graça', um vaso tão repleto de Deus que não restava lugar para o pecado. Sua alma era um jardim fechado onde o Senhor se comprazia em habitar.

Intention

That God's grace may fill and transform my soul.

Para que a graça de Deus encha e transforme minha alma.

Day 3: You are all beautiful

Dia 3: Sois toda formosa

'Tota pulchra es, Maria' — you are all beautiful, and the original stain is not in you. Mary's beauty is the radiance of a soul wholly given to God, undimmed by any shadow.

'Tota pulchra es, Maria' — sois toda formosa, e em vós não há a mancha original. A beleza de Maria é o esplendor de uma alma inteiramente dada a Deus, sem o obscurecimento de sombra alguma.

Intention

For the beauty of holiness to grow in my own soul.

Para que a beleza da santidade cresça em minha própria alma.

Day 4: The new Eve

Dia 4: A nova Eva

Where the first Eve fell to the serpent, Mary crushed his head by her obedience and her sinless 'yes.' She is the woman foretold in Genesis, at enmity with the devil from the very beginning.

Onde a primeira Eva caiu diante da serpente, Maria esmagou-lhe a cabeça por sua obediência e por seu 'sim' sem pecado. Ela é a mulher anunciada no Gênesis, em inimizade com o demônio desde o princípio.

Intention

For Mary's protection against the temptations of the evil one.

Pela proteção de Maria contra as tentações do maligno.

Day 5: The dawn of redemption

Dia 5: A aurora da redenção

Mary's Immaculate Conception is the first light of the coming Redeemer, the dawn that heralds the rising Sun. In her preservation from sin we already see the saving power of the Cross at work.

A Imaculada Conceição de Maria é a primeira luz do Redentor que vem, a aurora que anuncia o Sol nascente. Em sua preservação do pecado já vemos atuar o poder salvador da Cruz.

Intention

That my heart may prepare for the coming of Christ.

Para que meu coração se prepare para a vinda de Cristo.

Day 6: Saved by Christ's merits

Dia 6: Salva pelos méritos de Cristo

Mary was redeemed in the most perfect way, preserved from sin rather than rescued from it, in view of her Son's future merits. She is the first and greatest fruit of His redemption, and proof of all He longs to do in us.

Maria foi redimida do modo mais perfeito, preservada do pecado em vez de resgatada dele, em vista dos méritos futuros de seu Filho. Ela é o primeiro e maior fruto de Sua redenção, e prova de tudo o que Ele deseja realizar em nós.

Intention

For confidence in the redeeming power of Christ.

Pela confiança no poder redentor de Cristo.

Day 7: I am the Immaculate Conception

Dia 7: Eu sou a Imaculada Conceição

At Lourdes the Lady told Bernadette her name: 'I am the Immaculate Conception,' confirming from heaven what the Church had defined. To a poor and unlettered child she revealed the mystery the learned had long contemplated.

Em Lourdes a Senhora disse a Bernadette o seu nome: 'Eu sou a Imaculada Conceição', confirmando do céu o que a Igreja havia definido. A uma criança pobre e sem instrução ela revelou o mistério que os sábios por muito tempo contemplaram.

Intention

For childlike trust in the words of our heavenly Mother.

Pela confiança de criança nas palavras de nossa Mãe celeste.

Day 8: Refuge of sinners

Dia 8: Refúgio dos pecadores

Though she never knew sin, Mary is the tender refuge of sinners, drawing us out of the mire by her prayers. The sinless Mother is most moved by the misery of her wandering children.

Embora nunca tenha conhecido o pecado, Maria é o terno refúgio dos pecadores, que nos arranca do lodo por suas orações. A Mãe sem pecado é a que mais se comove com a miséria de seus filhos desgarrados.

Intention

For all sinners, that they may turn to Mary and be converted.

Por todos os pecadores, para que se voltem a Maria e se convertam.

Day 9: Patroness and Mother

Dia 9: Padroeira e Mãe

Under the title of the Immaculate Conception, Mary is the patroness of many nations and the Mother of all the Church. She who is wholly God's takes us as her own and leads us to her Son.

Sob o título da Imaculada Conceição, Maria é padroeira de muitas nações e Mãe de toda a Igreja. Ela, que é inteiramente de Deus, toma-nos como seus e nos conduz ao seu Filho.

Intention

That I may entrust myself wholly to the Immaculate Mother.

Para que eu me entregue inteiramente à Mãe Imaculada.

Closing prayer

O Mary conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to you. By your Immaculate Conception, purify my heart, defend me from the snares of the evil one, and lead me safely to your Son. Amen.

Ó Maria concebida sem pecado, rogai por nós que recorremos a vós. Por vossa Imaculada Conceição, purificai o meu coração, defendei-me das ciladas do maligno e conduzi-me com segurança ao vosso Filho. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="ic">
Novena
to the Immaculate Conception

Say once a day for nine days, especially starting on
November 29 and ending on December 7, the Vigil of the Feast of the
Immaculate Conception.

Immaculate Virgin! Mary, conceived without sin! Remember, thou wert
miraculously preserved from even the shadow of sin, because thou wert
destined to become not only the Mother of God, but also the mother, the
refuge, and the advocate of man; penetrated therefore, with the most
lively confidence in thy never-failing intercession, we most humbly
implore thee to look with favor upon the intentions of this novena, and
to obtain for us the graces and the favors we request. Thou knowest, O
Mary, how often our hearts are the sanctuaries of God, Who abhors
iniquity. Obtain for us, then, than angelic purity which was thy
favorite virtue, that purity of heart which will attach us to God
alone, and that purity of intention which will consecrate every
thought, word, and action to His greater glory. Obtain also for us a
constant spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we many recover by
penance that innocence which we have lost by sin, and at length attain
safety to that blessed abode of the saints, where nothing defiled can
enter.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.

V. Thou are all fair, O Mary.

R. Thou art all fair, O Mary.

V. And the original stain is not in thee.

R. And the original stain is not in thee.

V. Thou art the glory of Jerusalem.

R. Thou art the joy of Israel

V. Thou art the honor of our people.

R. Thou art the advocate of sinners.

V. O Mary.

R. O Mary.

V. Virgin, most prudent.

R. Mother, most tender.

V. Pray for us.

R. Intercede for us with Jesus our Lord.

V. In thy conception, Holy Virgin, thou wast immaculate.

R. Pray for us to the Father Whose Son thou didst bring forth.

V. O Lady! aid my prayer.

R. And let my cry come unto thee.

Let us pray

Holy Mary, Queen of Heaven, Mother of Our Lord Jesus Christ, and
mistress of the world, who forsakest no one, and despisest no one, look
upon me, O Lady! with an eye of pity, and entreat for me of thy beloved
Son the forgiveness of all my sins; that, as I now celebrate, with
devout affection, thy holy and immaculate conception, so, hereafter I
may receive the prize of eternal blessedness, by the grace of Him whom
thou, in virginity, didst bring forth, Jesus Christ Our Lord: Who, with
the Father and the Holy Ghost, liveth and reigneth, in perfect Trinity,
God, world without end. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Each day opens "In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen." The main prayer, repeated identically all nine days, begins "O most pure Virgin Mary conceived without sin, from the very first instant, you were entirely immaculate" and continues with requests for grace to imitate Mary's holiness and submission to God's will; closes with space to mention intentions, three petitions for increased faith, hope, and love in Jesus, and a concluding Hail Mary.

Fidelity note

The theology is fully aligned: both sources and Ember center on Mary preserved free from all stain of original sin from the first instant of her conception, her role as refuge/advocate of sinners, and the call to imitate her purity — and Ember's dating (Nov 29–Dec 7) matches FishEaters exactly.
