At Nazareth, Joseph lived in constant union with Jesus and Mary. We entrust our whole life to his care, asking to persevere faithfully until the end.

Em Nazaré, José viveu em constante união com Jesus e Maria. Confiamos toda a nossa vida ao seu cuidado, pedindo a graça da perseverança fiel até o fim.

Intention

For final perseverance and lifelong fidelity to Christ in communion with Mary and Joseph.

Pela perseverança final e fidelidade a Cristo por toda a vida, em comunhão com Maria e José.

Closing prayer—

FishEaters traditional

name="joseph">
Novena
to St. Joseph

Say once a day for nine days, especially beginning on 10
March and ending on 18 March, the eve of the Feast of St. Joseph

O glorious descendant of the kings of Judah, Inheritor of the virtues
of all the patriarchs. Just and happy St. Joseph, listen to my prayer.
Thou art my glorious protector, and shall ever be, after Jesus and Mary
the object of my most profound veneration and confidence. Thou art the
most hidden, though the greatest Saint, and art particularly the patron
of those who serve God with the greatest purity and fervor. In union
with all those who have ever been most devoted to thee I now dedicate
myself to thy service; beseeching thee, for the sake of Jesus Christ,
who vouchsafed to love and obey thee as a son, to become a father to
me; and to obtain for me the filial respect, confidence and love of a
child towards thee.

O powerful advocate of all Christians, whose intercession, as St.
Theresa assures us, has never been found to fail, deign to intercede
for me now, and to implore for me the particular intention of this
Novena.

(Mention your intentions here)

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be

Present me O great Saint to the adorable Trinity, with Whom thou hadst
so glorious and so intimate a correspondence. Obtain that I may never
efface by sin the Sacred Image according to the likeness of which, I
was created. Beg for me that my divine Redeemer would enkindle in my
heart and in all hearts, the fire of His Love, and infuse therein the
virtues of His adorable infancy, His purity, simplicity, obedience, and
humility.

Obtain for me likewise a lively devotion to thy virgin spouse, and
protect me so powerfully in life and death, that I may have the
happiness of dying as thou didst, in the friendship of my Creator, and
under the immediate protection of the Mother of God. Amen. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Fetched from https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-joseph-novena. The PrayMoreNovenas novena uses a fixed daily structure: an identical opening invocation ("Saint Joseph, you are the faithful protector and intercessor of all who love and venerate you…"), a personal-intention section that is the same every day, a doxology (Glory Be), and a per-day litany-style title for St. Joseph (Day 1 "St. Joseph Most Just," Day 2 "Most Prudent," Day 3 "Most Loving Husband," etc.). The day-to-day variation is essentially the changing title/invocation rather than a fresh meditation.

Fidelity note

Ember and PrayMoreNovenas share the same devotional object and the same nine-day Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be backbone, but their daily content differs in kind: PrayMoreNovenas keeps a single repeated petition and varies only St. Joseph's title each day, whereas Ember supplies nine distinct themed meditations (obedience, chastity, family, work, fatherhood, trials, the Church, a happy death, perseverance) drawn from the traditional spheres of Josephine patronage. Ember is faithful to the saint's recognized patronages and to the standard novena form, but its meditations are an original composition rather than a transcription of either source. This is a reasonable, doctrinally sound adaptation; it is not a verbatim rendering of the PrayMoreNovenas text.

St. Anthony of Padua st-anthony-novena

May 31–Jun 12
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Anthony of Padua, asking for deeper faith, charity, and trust in God's providence.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santo Antônio de Pádua, pedindo fé mais profunda, caridade e confiança na providência de Deus.

St. Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), Franciscan priest and Doctor of the Church, is loved for his Gospel preaching, care for the poor, and powerful intercession. Over time, Catholics also invoked him in lost causes and lost things, entrusting difficult needs to his prayer.

Santo Antônio de Pádua (1195–1231), sacerdote franciscano e Doutor da Igreja, é amado por sua pregação evangélica, cuidado com os pobres e poderosa intercessão. Com o tempo, os católicos também passaram a invocá-lo nas causas e coisas perdidas, confiando-lhe necessidades difíceis.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Anthony of Padua, faithful disciple of Christ and servant of the poor, intercede for us before God. Teach us to love the Gospel, seek holiness in ordinary life, and trust in divine providence in every need. Amen.

Santo Antônio de Pádua, fiel discípulo de Cristo e servo dos pobres, intercedei por nós diante de Deus. Ensinai-nos a amar o Evangelho, buscar a santidade na vida comum e confiar na providência divina em toda necessidade. Amém.

Day 1: Called to follow Christ in simplicity

Dia 1: Chamado a seguir Cristo na simplicidade

St. Anthony left honors and comforts to follow Christ in the Franciscan path of poverty and joy. He teaches us to seek God above possessions and to live with a free and trusting heart.

Santo Antônio deixou honras e comodidades para seguir Cristo no caminho franciscano de pobreza e alegria. Ele nos ensina a buscar Deus acima dos bens e a viver com coração livre e confiante.

Intention

For freedom from attachment and a deeper desire to follow Jesus.

Pela liberdade dos apegos e por um desejo mais profundo de seguir Jesus.

Day 2: Love for Sacred Scripture and truth

Dia 2: Amor à Sagrada Escritura e à verdade

Known as a great preacher and teacher, St. Anthony loved the Word of God and proclaimed it with clarity. He invites us to read Scripture prayerfully and to shape our lives by the truth of the Gospel.

Conhecido como grande pregador e mestre, Santo Antônio amava a Palavra de Deus e a anunciava com clareza. Ele nos convida a ler a Escritura em espírito de oração e a moldar a vida pela verdade do Evangelho.

Intention

For hunger for God's Word and fidelity to Catholic truth.

Pela fome da Palavra de Deus e fidelidade à verdade católica.

Day 3: Humility in service and preaching

Dia 3: Humildade no serviço e na pregação

Though gifted and admired, St. Anthony remained humble, seeking only God's glory. His witness teaches us to serve quietly, speak with charity, and let Christ be seen in all we do.

Mesmo dotado e admirado, Santo Antônio permaneceu humilde, buscando apenas a glória de Deus. Seu testemunho nos ensina a servir em silêncio, falar com caridade e deixar Cristo aparecer em tudo o que fazemos.

Intention

For humility, docility, and purity of intention in our work.

Pela humildade, docilidade e pureza de intenção em nosso trabalho.

Day 4: Charity toward the poor and suffering

Dia 4: Caridade para com os pobres e sofredores

St. Anthony cared for the poor with concrete compassion, seeing Christ in those in need. He calls us to generous mercy, practical help, and a heart attentive to those who suffer.

Santo Antônio cuidava dos pobres com compaixão concreta, reconhecendo Cristo nos necessitados. Ele nos chama à misericórdia generosa, à ajuda prática e a um coração atento aos que sofrem.

Intention

For greater charity toward the poor, sick, and abandoned.

Por maior caridade para com os pobres, doentes e abandonados.

Day 5: Purity of heart and conversion

Dia 5: Pureza de coração e conversão

St. Anthony preached conversion with zeal and tenderness, leading sinners back to God's mercy. Through his intercession, we ask for a clean heart and the courage to repent sincerely.

Santo Antônio pregava a conversão com zelo e ternura, conduzindo os pecadores de volta à misericórdia de Deus. Por sua intercessão, pedimos um coração puro e a coragem de nos arrepender com sinceridade.

Intention

For true repentance and growth in purity and holiness.

Por verdadeiro arrependimento e crescimento na pureza e santidade.

Day 6: Intercession for families and daily work

Dia 6: Intercessão pelas famílias e pelo trabalho diário

In ordinary life, God sanctifies homes, labor, and responsibilities. St. Anthony encourages us to entrust family needs and daily burdens to the Lord with confidence.

Na vida comum, Deus santifica os lares, o trabalho e as responsabilidades. Santo Antônio nos encoraja a confiar as necessidades da família e os fardos diários ao Senhor com confiança.

Intention

For peace in families, honest work, and providence in material needs.

Pela paz nas famílias, pelo trabalho honesto e pela providência nas necessidades materiais.

Day 7: Hope in what is lost and forgotten

Dia 7: Esperança no que está perdido e esquecido

Many invoke St. Anthony when something is lost, but his deepest gift is leading souls back to God. He reminds us that divine grace can restore what seems broken, forgotten, or far away.

Muitos invocam Santo Antônio quando algo se perde, mas seu dom mais profundo é reconduzir as almas a Deus. Ele nos recorda que a graça divina pode restaurar o que parece quebrado, esquecido ou distante.

Intention

For the recovery of what is truly needed and the return of hearts to God.

Pela recuperação do que é realmente necessário e pelo retorno dos corações a Deus.

Day 8: Peace and reconciliation

Dia 8: Paz e reconciliação

St. Anthony worked for justice and reconciliation among divided people. Under his patronage we ask for healed relationships, forgiven offenses, and hearts renewed in peace.

Santo Antônio trabalhou pela justiça e reconciliação entre pessoas divididas. Sob seu patrocínio, pedimos relações curadas, ofensas perdoadas e corações renovados na paz.

Intention

For reconciliation in families, friendships, and communities.

Pela reconciliação nas famílias, amizades e comunidades.

Day 9: Perseverance in holiness and mission

Dia 9: Perseverança na santidade e na missão

As this novena ends, we thank God for every grace received through St. Anthony's intercession. We ask to remain faithful, to love Christ above all, and to serve the Church with joy until the end.

Ao concluir esta novena, agradecemos a Deus por cada graça recebida pela intercessão de Santo Antônio. Pedimos permanecer fiéis, amar Cristo acima de tudo e servir a Igreja com alegria até o fim.

Intention

For final perseverance and a life that gives glory to God.

Pela perseverança final e por uma vida que dê glória a Deus.

Closing prayer

St. Anthony, friend of Jesus and helper of all who call upon you, obtain for us steadfast faith, generous charity, and perseverance in prayer. May we seek first the Kingdom of God and give thanks for every grace received. Amen.

Santo Antônio, amigo de Jesus e auxílio de todos os que vos invocam, alcançai-nos fé firme, caridade generosa e perseverança na oração. Que busquemos em primeiro lugar o Reino de Deus e demos graças por cada graça recebida. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="anthony">
Novena
to St. Anthony of Padua

Say once a day for nine days, especially beginning on 4
June and ending on 12 June, the eve of the Feast of St. Anthony. Some
pray a Novena to St. Anthony on thirteen consecutive Tuesdays, per the
instructions of Pope Leo XIII, or on all Tuesdays.

O White lily of purity, sublime example of poverty,
true mirror of humility, resplendent star of sanctity. O glorious St
Anthony, who didst enjoy the sweet privilege of receiving into thy arms
the Infant Jesus, I beseech thee to take me under thy powerful
protection. Thou in whom the power of working miracles shines forth
among the other gifts of God, have pity upon me and come to my aid in
this my great need.

(Mention your intentions here).

Cleanse my heart from every disorderly affection, obtain for me a true
contrition for my sins and a great love of God and of my neighbour that
serving God faithfully in this life, I may come to praise, enjoy and
bless Him eternally with thee in Paradise. Amen

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Fetched from https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-anthony-of-padua-novena. Opening prayer (daily): "Dear Lord, we thank You and praise You for giving us St. Anthony of Padua as an example of holiness. Please hear the prayers he brings before Your throne on our behalf." The Day 1 intention asks God to inspire more men and women toward religious and priestly vocations and for eagerness to serve God and grow spiritually each day. Closing prayer (daily): "St. Anthony of Padua, pray for us! In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen." Structure is identical opening and closing each day, with only the day-specific virtue/petition changing.

Fidelity note

Both versions are nine-day novenas to the same saint built on the same Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be frame, and both close with the invocation "St. Anthony, pray for us." The wording differs throughout: PrayMoreNovenas addresses its opening to God ("Dear Lord, we thank You…"), while Ember addresses St. Anthony directly, and the daily themes are independently composed (Ember leads with Franciscan poverty and Scripture; PrayMoreNovenas leads with vocations). Ember's meditations stay faithful to St. Anthony's authentic profile — Franciscan preacher, friend of the poor, Doctor of the Church, patron of lost things — so the adaptation is sound, but it is an original text rather than the PrayMoreNovenas prayers verbatim.

Our Lady of Perpetual Help perpetual-help-novena

Jun 18–26
description & history

A nine-day novena to Our Lady of Perpetual Help, entrusting needs, trials, and perseverance in faith to Mary's maternal intercession.

Uma novena de nove dias a Nossa Senhora do Perpétuo Socorro, confiando necessidades, provações e perseverança na fé à intercessão maternal de Maria.

The icon of Our Lady of Perpetual Help is an ancient Byzantine image long venerated in the Church. In 1866, Pope Pius IX entrusted the icon to the Redemptorists with the mission to make Mary known under this title throughout the world. Since then, countless faithful have turned to Our Lady of Perpetual Help for consolation, conversion, and confidence in God's mercy.

O ícone de Nossa Senhora do Perpétuo Socorro é uma antiga imagem bizantina venerada há séculos na Igreja. Em 1866, o Papa Pio IX confiou o ícone aos Redentoristas com a missão de tornar Maria conhecida sob esse título em todo o mundo. Desde então, incontáveis fiéis recorrem a Nossa Senhora do Perpétuo Socorro em busca de consolo, conversão e confiança na misericórdia de Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Mother of Perpetual Help, with childlike confidence I come before your holy icon and place this novena in your hands. Obtain for me the grace to trust Jesus in every need, persevere in prayer, and follow His will with a faithful heart. Amen.

Mãe do Perpétuo Socorro, com confiança filial venho diante de vosso santo ícone e coloco esta novena em vossas mãos. Alcançai-me a graça de confiar em Jesus em toda necessidade, perseverar na oração e seguir Sua vontade com coração fiel. Amém.

Day 1: Mother of Perpetual Help, icon of mercy

Dia 1: Mãe do Perpétuo Socorro, ícone de misericórdia

In the holy icon, Mary points us to Jesus, the true source of every grace. Her gaze invites us to trust God's mercy in every circumstance, especially when we feel weak or afraid.

No santo ícone, Maria nos aponta para Jesus, a verdadeira fonte de toda graça. Seu olhar nos convida a confiar na misericórdia de Deus em toda circunstância, especialmente quando nos sentimos fracos ou com medo.

Intention

For renewed trust in God's mercy and Mary's maternal care.

Por uma confiança renovada na misericórdia de Deus e no cuidado maternal de Maria.

Day 2: Help in anxiety and trials

Dia 2: Socorro nas angústias e provações

Mary is called Perpetual Help because she does not abandon her children in hardship. She teaches us to bring every burden to Christ with perseverance, believing that grace is stronger than fear.

Maria é chamada Perpétuo Socorro porque não abandona seus filhos nas dificuldades. Ela nos ensina a levar todo peso a Cristo com perseverança, crendo que a graça é mais forte que o medo.

Intention

For all who are anxious, burdened, or passing through difficult trials.

Por todos os que estão ansiosos, sobrecarregados ou atravessando provações difíceis.

Day 3: Refuge of sinners and conversion

Dia 3: Refúgio dos pecadores e conversão

At the foot of the Cross, Mary received us as her children. She intercedes for sinners and leads hearts back to Jesus with tenderness and truth.

Aos pés da Cruz, Maria nos recebeu como filhos. Ela intercede pelos pecadores e reconduz os corações a Jesus com ternura e verdade.

Intention

For the conversion of sinners and those far from the Church.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e daqueles que estão afastados da Igreja.

Day 4: Mother of families and homes

Dia 4: Mãe das famílias e dos lares

Mary lived the hidden life of Nazareth with Jesus and Joseph. She understands family joys and wounds, and helps homes become places of prayer, forgiveness, and faithful love.

Maria viveu a vida escondida de Nazaré com Jesus e José. Ela conhece as alegrias e feridas das famílias, e ajuda os lares a se tornarem lugares de oração, perdão e amor fiel.

Intention

For peace, healing, and unity in our families.

Por paz, cura e unidade em nossas famílias.

Day 5: Consolation of the sick and suffering

Dia 5: Consolação dos enfermos e sofredores

The Mother of Perpetual Help is close to those who suffer in body or soul. She carries our pain to Christ and obtains strength, patience, and hope in the midst of affliction.

A Mãe do Perpétuo Socorro está próxima dos que sofrem no corpo ou na alma. Ela leva nossa dor a Cristo e obtém força, paciência e esperança em meio à aflição.

Intention

For the sick, the suffering, and all who care for them.

Pelos enfermos, pelos sofredores e por todos os que cuidam deles.

Day 6: Help of the Church and vocations

Dia 6: Auxílio da Igreja e das vocações

Mary never ceases to intercede for the Church. Under her mantle we ask for holy pastors, generous vocations, and faithful disciples who witness to Christ with courage.

Maria não cessa de interceder pela Igreja. Sob seu manto, pedimos santos pastores, vocações generosas e discípulos fiéis que testemunhem Cristo com coragem.

Intention

For the Church, the Pope, bishops, priests, and new vocations.

Pela Igreja, pelo Papa, bispos, sacerdotes e por novas vocações.

Day 7: Mother of hope for the poor and forgotten

Dia 7: Mãe da esperança dos pobres e esquecidos

Mary's heart is especially near to the poor, abandoned, and oppressed. She teaches us to recognize Christ in those who suffer and to practice concrete charity.

O coração de Maria está especialmente próximo dos pobres, abandonados e oprimidos. Ela nos ensina a reconhecer Cristo nos que sofrem e a praticar uma caridade concreta.

Intention

For the poor, the abandoned, and all who seek dignity and daily bread.

Pelos pobres, pelos abandonados e por todos os que buscam dignidade e pão de cada dia.

Day 8: Guide in fidelity and mission

Dia 8: Guia na fidelidade e na missão

Mary formed the disciples in prayer while they awaited the Holy Spirit. She forms us to remain faithful in daily duties and to serve the Gospel with humility and zeal.

Maria formou os discípulos na oração enquanto aguardavam o Espírito Santo. Ela nos forma para permanecer fiéis nos deveres diários e servir ao Evangelho com humildade e zelo.

Intention

For perseverance in vocation and fruitfulness in Christian mission.

Pela perseverança na vocação e fecundidade na missão cristã.

Day 9: Lead us always to Jesus

Dia 9: Conduzi-nos sempre a Jesus

Every true Marian devotion leads us to Jesus. At the close of this novena, we entrust our whole life to Our Lady of Perpetual Help, asking for final perseverance in grace.

Toda devoção mariana autêntica nos conduz a Jesus. Ao concluir esta novena, entregamos toda a nossa vida a Nossa Senhora do Perpétuo Socorro, pedindo a graça da perseverança final.

Intention

For final perseverance and lifelong fidelity to Jesus Christ.

Pela perseverança final e fidelidade a Jesus Cristo por toda a vida.

Closing prayer

Our Lady of Perpetual Help, pray for us. Help us in every trial, protect our families, strengthen the Church, and lead us always to your Son, Jesus Christ. Amen.

Nossa Senhora do Perpétuo Socorro, rogai por nós. Socorrei-nos em toda provação, protegei nossas famílias, fortalecei a Igreja e conduzi-nos sempre ao vosso Filho, Jesus Cristo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="olph">
Novena
to Our Lady of Perpetual Help

Say once a day for 9 days

O Mother of Perpetual Succour, behold me, a miserable sinner at thy
feet. I have recourse to thee and put my trust in thee. O Mother of
Mercy, have pity upon me, I hear thee called by all, the refuge and the
hope of sinners, be then my refuge and my hope. Succour me for the love
of Jesus Christ; stretch forth thy hand to me, a poor sinner, who
recommend and dedicate myself to thee, as thy perpetual servant. I
bless and thank God, for, having in his Mercy given me this confidence
in Thee, the pledge, as I believe, of my eternal salvation. Alas, too
often in past times have I miserably fallen, because I had not recourse
to thee, I know that with thy help I shall conquer. I know that thou
will help me, if I recommend myself to thee, but I fear lest in the
occasion of falling, I should cease to call upon thee, and, so should
loose my soul. This then is the grace I seek from thee, and I beg of
thee, as far as I know how and can, to obtain it for me, namely, in the
assaults of hell, always to have recourse to thee and to say to thee; O
Mary, help me, Mother of Perpetual Succor, suffer me not to lose my
God. Amen.

<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Fetched from https://www.praymorenovenas.com/our-lady-of-perpetual-help-novena. Opening (daily): "In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen. Dear Lord, we thank You and praise You for giving us Your mother as Our Lady of Perpetual Help." Day 1 intention: that "we and all people may grow in devotion to you." Closing (daily): "Our Lady of Perpetual Help, pray for us! In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen." Same opening/closing each day; the day-specific themes move from growing devotion (Day 1) to vocations (Days 4–5) to trust and love of God (Days 8–9).

Fidelity note

Both are nine-day novenas to the same Redemptorist Marian title, both bracketed by the Sign of the Cross and built on the Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be. Ember's closing invocation "Our Lady of Perpetual Help, pray for us" matches PrayMoreNovenas almost word-for-word, and several themes overlap (vocations, the Church, leading us to Jesus). The opening prayers differ in voice — PrayMoreNovenas addresses God in thanksgiving, Ember addresses Mary before her icon — and Ember's nine meditations are its own composition organized around the icon and Mary's titles. The history (Byzantine icon, 1866, Pius IX, the Redemptorists) is accurate. Faithful in form and devotion; original in wording.

Our Lady of Mt. Carmel mount-carmel-novena

Jul 7–15
description & history

A nine-day novena to Our Lady of Mount Carmel, asking for deeper prayer, fidelity to Christ, and perseverance under Mary's maternal protection.

Uma novena de nove dias a Nossa Senhora do Carmo, pedindo oração mais profunda, fidelidade a Cristo e perseverança sob a proteção maternal de Maria.

Devotion to Our Lady of Mount Carmel is rooted in the Carmelite tradition, which honors Mary as Mother, Patroness, and model of contemplative life. Under this title the faithful entrust themselves to her care, especially through the sign of the Brown Scapular, seeking to live in prayer, purity, and union with Jesus Christ.

A devoção a Nossa Senhora do Carmo tem raízes na tradição carmelitana, que honra Maria como Mãe, Padroeira e modelo de vida contemplativa. Sob esse título, os fiéis se confiam ao seu cuidado, especialmente pelo sinal do Escapulário marrom, buscando viver na oração, na pureza e na união com Jesus Cristo.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O beautiful Flower of Carmel, Mother of God and Mother most loving, I place this novena in your hands. Obtain for me the grace to pray faithfully, love Jesus with an undivided heart, and persevere in holiness under your maternal care. Amen.

Ó formosa Flor do Carmelo, Mãe de Deus e Mãe amorosíssima, coloco esta novena em vossas mãos. Alcançai-me a graça de rezar com fidelidade, amar Jesus com coração indiviso e perseverar na santidade sob vosso cuidado maternal. Amém.

Day 1: Mother and beauty of Carmel

Dia 1: Mãe e beleza do Carmelo

Under the title of Mount Carmel, Mary is honored as the beauty of holiness and the Mother who leads us to Jesus. She invites us to begin this novena with trust and filial love.

Sob o título do Carmo, Maria é honrada como beleza da santidade e Mãe que nos conduz a Jesus. Ela nos convida a iniciar esta novena com confiança e amor filial.

Intention

For a heart open to Mary's maternal guidance.

Por um coração aberto à orientação maternal de Maria.

Day 2: School of prayer and contemplation

Dia 2: Escola de oração e contemplação

Carmel teaches silence, recollection, and attentive listening to God. With Mary we learn to treasure the Word in our hearts and remain faithful in daily prayer.

O Carmelo ensina o silêncio, o recolhimento e a escuta atenta de Deus. Com Maria, aprendemos a guardar a Palavra no coração e permanecer fiéis na oração diária.

Intention

For perseverance in prayer and a contemplative spirit.

Pela perseverança na oração e por um espírito contemplativo.

Day 3: Fidelity to Christ through Mary

Dia 3: Fidelidade a Cristo por Maria

True Marian devotion always leads to deeper union with Jesus. Our Lady of Mount Carmel forms disciples who obey the Gospel with humility and courage.

Toda devoção mariana autêntica conduz a uma união mais profunda com Jesus. Nossa Senhora do Carmo forma discípulos que obedecem ao Evangelho com humildade e coragem.

Intention

For fidelity to Jesus Christ in every state of life.

Pela fidelidade a Jesus Cristo em todo estado de vida.

Day 4: The scapular as sign of consecration

Dia 4: O escapulário como sinal de consagração

The Brown Scapular reminds us to belong to Mary and to live clothed in Christ. It is a call to conversion, sacramental life, and daily imitation of her virtues.

O Escapulário marrom nos recorda que pertencemos a Maria e devemos viver revestidos de Cristo. Ele é um chamado à conversão, à vida sacramental e à imitação diária de suas virtudes.

Intention

For grace to live our Marian consecration with integrity.

Pela graça de viver com integridade nossa consagração mariana.

Day 5: Purity of heart and conversion

Dia 5: Pureza de coração e conversão

Mary's Immaculate Heart reflects total openness to God. She helps us turn away from sin and seek purity in mind, speech, and action.

O Imaculado Coração de Maria reflete total abertura a Deus. Ela nos ajuda a abandonar o pecado e buscar pureza nos pensamentos, palavras e ações.

Intention

For ongoing conversion and purity of life.

Pela conversão constante e pureza de vida.

Day 6: Strength in trials and darkness

Dia 6: Força nas provações e na escuridão

In moments of suffering, Mary remains close and steadfast. Under her mantle we learn to endure trials with hope, trusting that God is present even in the night of faith.

Nos momentos de sofrimento, Maria permanece próxima e firme. Sob seu manto, aprendemos a suportar as provações com esperança, confiando que Deus está presente mesmo na noite da fé.

Intention

For those who suffer, that they may receive hope and endurance.

Pelos que sofrem, para que recebam esperança e perseverança.

Day 7: Charity and service to neighbor

Dia 7: Caridade e serviço ao próximo

Contemplation bears fruit in charity. Our Lady of Mount Carmel teaches us to recognize Christ in others and to serve with humility, gentleness, and generous love.

A contemplação produz fruto na caridade. Nossa Senhora do Carmo nos ensina a reconhecer Cristo nos outros e a servir com humildade, mansidão e amor generoso.

Intention

For growth in charity and concrete works of mercy.

Pelo crescimento na caridade e em obras concretas de misericórdia.

Day 8: Comfort of the dying and hope for souls

Dia 8: Consolo dos agonizantes e esperança pelas almas

The Church invokes Our Lady of Mount Carmel for the dying and for the souls in purgatory. She accompanies her children at life's final hour and leads them toward the mercy of Christ.

A Igreja invoca Nossa Senhora do Carmo pelos agonizantes e pelas almas do purgatório. Ela acompanha seus filhos na hora final da vida e os conduz à misericórdia de Cristo.

Intention

For the dying, the souls in purgatory, and final perseverance.

Pelos agonizantes, pelas almas do purgatório e pela perseverança final.

Day 9: Perseverance in holiness

Dia 9: Perseverança na santidade

We conclude this novena by entrusting our whole life to Mary of Carmel. With her intercession, may we remain faithful to Christ until the end and rejoice forever in God's presence.

Concluímos esta novena entregando toda a nossa vida a Maria do Carmo. Por sua intercessão, que permaneçamos fiéis a Cristo até o fim e nos alegremos para sempre na presença de Deus.

Intention

For final perseverance and eternal union with God.

Pela perseverança final e união eterna com Deus.

Closing prayer

Our Lady of Mount Carmel, pray for us. Cover us with your mantle, protect our families, strengthen the Church, and lead us always to your Son, Jesus Christ. Amen.

Nossa Senhora do Carmo, rogai por nós. Cobri-nos com vosso manto, protegei nossas famílias, fortalecei a Igreja e conduzi-nos sempre ao vosso Filho, Jesus Cristo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="carmel">
Novena
to Our Lady of Mt. Carmel

Say once a day for nine days, especially starting on 7
July and ending on 15 July, the eve of the Feast of Our Lady of Mt.
Carmel

O most beautiful Flower of Mount Carmel, Fruitful Vine, Splendour of
Heaven, Blessed Mother of the Son of God, Immaculate Virgin, assist me
in this my necessity.

( Mention your intention here )

O Star of the Sea, help me and show me in this that thou art my Mother.
O holy Mary, Mother of God, Queen of Heaven and earth, I humbly beseech
thee, from the bottom of my heart, to help me in this necessity; there
are none that can withstand thy power. O show me in this that thou art
my Mother!

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.

Sweet Mother, I place this cause in thy hands.

Sweet Mother, I place this cause in thy hands.

Sweet Mother, I place this cause in thy hands. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross and closes with "Our Lady of Mount Carmel, pray for us!" Rather than one repeated prayer, it gives nine distinct daily themes with their own reflections and a space to state personal intentions: Day 1 scapular wearers' faithfulness; Day 2 Carmelite communities and vocations; Day 3 those in poverty and suffering; Day 4 understanding devotion to Mary; Day 5 comfort for the dying and deceased; Day 6 the holy souls in Purgatory; Day 7 healing for the sick; Day 8 surrendering to God's will; Day 9 drawing closer to Jesus through Mary.

Fidelity note

Ember's version is an original composition rather than a transcription of either source. It does not reproduce the famous "Flower of Carmel"/"Star of the Sea" prayer (the most distinctive textual marker of the traditional FishEaters novena), instead opening with a freshly written invocation that echoes the "Flower of Carmel" image. Structurally it most resembles PrayMoreNovenas' model — nine thematic days each with a meditation and intention — and its day themes overlap meaningfully (scapular/consecration, prayer/contemplation, the suffering and dying, the souls in purgatory, perseverance), though the wording is paraphrased and reorganized. The closing "Our Lady of Mount Carmel, pray for us" matches PMN's refrain. Faithful in spirit and theme; not a verbatim rendering of the historic prayers.

St. Thérèse (Rose Novena) st-therese-novena

Sep 22–30
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Therese of Lisieux, asking for childlike trust, hidden charity, and faithful love in ordinary life.

Uma novena de nove dias a Santa Teresinha de Lisieux, pedindo confiança filial, caridade escondida e amor fiel na vida cotidiana.

St. Therese of Lisieux (1873–1897), a Carmelite nun and Doctor of the Church, taught the 'Little Way' of holiness through trust, humility, and ordinary acts done with great love. This novena follows the Church's tradition of persevering prayer over nine days under her intercession.

Santa Teresinha de Lisieux (1873–1897), carmelita e Doutora da Igreja, ensinou a 'Pequena Via' da santidade por meio da confiança, da humildade e dos atos comuns feitos com grande amor. Esta novena segue a tradição da Igreja de perseverar na oração durante nove dias sob sua intercessão.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Therese, Little Flower of Jesus, teach me your Little Way of trust, humility, and love. Present my intentions to the Lord and help me offer each moment with generous charity. Amen.

Santa Teresinha, Pequena Flor de Jesus, ensinai-me vossa Pequena Via de confiança, humildade e amor. Apresentai minhas intenções ao Senhor e ajudai-me a oferecer cada momento com caridade generosa. Amém.

Day 1: Spiritual childhood and confidence in God

Dia 1: Infância espiritual e confiança em Deus

St. Therese teaches that holiness begins by becoming little before God. Like a child in a father's arms, we entrust ourselves to divine mercy with simplicity and peace.

Santa Teresinha ensina que a santidade começa ao nos fazermos pequenos diante de Deus. Como criança nos braços do Pai, nós nos entregamos à misericórdia divina com simplicidade e paz.

Intention

For childlike trust in God's fatherly love.

Pela confiança filial no amor paterno de Deus.

Day 2: Humility and littleness

Dia 2: Humildade e pequenez

Therese did not seek greatness in the eyes of the world, but the hidden place where God is found. True humility receives everything from the Lord and gives Him all the glory.

Teresinha não buscou grandeza aos olhos do mundo, mas o lugar escondido onde Deus é encontrado. A verdadeira humildade recebe tudo do Senhor e Lhe devolve toda a glória.

Intention

For a humble heart free from pride and self-seeking.

Por um coração humilde, livre do orgulho e da busca de si.

Day 3: Hidden charity in ordinary duties

Dia 3: Caridade escondida nos deveres comuns

In Carmel, Therese sanctified small tasks and quiet sacrifices. She reminds us that ordinary duties become extraordinary when done with patient and generous love.

No Carmelo, Teresinha santificou tarefas pequenas e sacrifícios silenciosos. Ela nos recorda que os deveres comuns se tornam extraordinários quando feitos com amor paciente e generoso.

Intention

For fidelity and charity in daily responsibilities.

Pela fidelidade e caridade nas responsabilidades de cada dia.

Day 4: Love for Jesus in prayer and sacrifice

Dia 4: Amor a Jesus na oração e no sacrifício

Therese offered prayer, suffering, and joy to console the Heart of Jesus. She shows us that every sacrifice united to Christ can become an act of pure love.

Teresinha oferecia oração, sofrimento e alegria para consolar o Coração de Jesus. Ela nos mostra que todo sacrifício unido a Cristo pode tornar-se um ato de amor puro.

Intention

For deeper love of Jesus in prayer and sacrifice.

Por um amor mais profundo a Jesus na oração e no sacrifício.

Day 5: Trust through dryness and suffering

Dia 5: Confiança na aridez e no sofrimento

Even in spiritual darkness and physical suffering, Therese trusted God without reserve. Her witness teaches us to cling to Jesus when we feel weak, dry, or forgotten.

Mesmo na escuridão espiritual e no sofrimento físico, Teresinha confiou em Deus sem reservas. Seu testemunho nos ensina a permanecer com Jesus quando nos sentimos fracos, áridos ou esquecidos.

Intention

For perseverance and trust in every trial.

Pela perseverança e confiança em toda provação.

Day 6: Merciful love and conversion of sinners

Dia 6: Amor misericordioso e conversão dos pecadores

Therese prayed fervently for souls far from God, especially those in danger of despair. She invites us to intercede with confidence in the triumph of divine mercy.

Teresinha rezou com fervor pelas almas afastadas de Deus, especialmente pelas que estavam em perigo de desespero. Ela nos convida a interceder com confiança no triunfo da misericórdia divina.

Intention

For conversion of sinners and return of the fallen away.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e pela volta dos afastados.

Day 7: Zeal for priests, missionaries, and souls

Dia 7: Zelo pelos sacerdotes, missionários e almas

Though hidden in cloister, Therese became Patroness of the Missions through prayer and sacrifice. She teaches us that contemplative love strengthens the Church's evangelizing mission.

Mesmo escondida no claustro, Teresinha tornou-se Padroeira das Missões por meio da oração e do sacrifício. Ela nos ensina que o amor contemplativo fortalece a missão evangelizadora da Igreja.

Intention

For priests, missionaries, and all who labor for the Gospel.

Pelos sacerdotes, missionários e todos os que trabalham pelo Evangelho.

Day 8: Joyful fidelity in daily life

Dia 8: Fidelidade alegre na vida diária

Therese lived with spiritual joy rooted in love, not in comfort. She encourages us to embrace each day with gratitude, turning small acts into offerings pleasing to God.

Teresinha viveu uma alegria espiritual enraizada no amor, e não no conforto. Ela nos encoraja a abraçar cada dia com gratidão, transformando pequenos atos em ofertas agradáveis a Deus.

Intention

For joyful fidelity in family, work, and prayer.

Pela fidelidade alegre na família, no trabalho e na oração.

Day 9: Hopeful perseverance under St. Therese's intercession

Dia 9: Perseverança esperançosa sob a intercessão de Santa Teresinha

At the end of this novena, we entrust our future to God with hope. With St. Therese, we ask for the grace of final perseverance and a life wholly offered in love.

Ao final desta novena, entregamos nosso futuro a Deus com esperança. Com Santa Teresinha, pedimos a graça da perseverança final e de uma vida inteiramente oferecida no amor.

Intention

For final perseverance and holiness of life.

Pela perseverança final e santidade de vida.

Closing prayer

St. Therese of Lisieux, Little Flower, obtain for us the grace to do small things with great love, to trust Jesus in every trial, and to persevere in holiness. Pray for us now and at the hour of our death. Amen.

Santa Teresinha de Lisieux, Pequena Flor, alcançai-nos a graça de fazer as pequenas coisas com grande amor, de confiar em Jesus em toda provação e de perseverar na santidade. Rogai por nós agora e na hora de nossa morte. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross and prays the same core prayer every day: "Dearest Saint Therese of Lisieux, you said that you would spend your time in heaven doing good on earth. Your trust in God was complete. Pray that He may increase my trust in His goodness and mercy as I ask for the following petitions…" — then "(State your intentions)" — then "Pray for me that I, like you, may have great and innocent confidence in the loving promises of our God." Each day adds a short day-specific aspiration: Day 1 "I love you, Lord. Help me to love you more!"; Day 2 "I trust you, Lord. Help me to trust you more!"; Day 3 "I see you, Lord. Help me to see you more!"; Day 4 "I am humble, Lord. Give me more humility!"; Day 5 "I try to forgive, Lord. Help me to forgive 70 times 7 times!"; Day 6 "I accept your will, Lord. Help me to accept your will every day!"; Day 7 "I rely on you, Lord. Help me to rely on you more!"; Day 8 "I reflect you to the world, Lord. Help me to reflect you more clearly!"; Day 9 "I love your people, Lord. Help me to love them more!"

Fidelity note

Ember's St. Thérèse novena is an original composition, not a transcription of PrayMoreNovenas (the only source available; it is not on FishEaters). It does not use PMN's repeated "Dearest Saint Therese… spend your time in heaven doing good on earth" prayer or the daily "Help me to love/trust/forgive…" aspirations. Instead it builds nine themed meditations around authentic Theresian topics — the Little Way, spiritual childhood, humility, hidden charity, trust through dryness, mercy for sinners, zeal for the missions — which are faithful to her spirituality and overlap thematically with PMN (trust, humility, forgiveness/mercy). The result is doctrinally sound and well-aligned with St. Thérèse's "Story of a Soul" themes, but wording-wise it is a fresh paraphrase rather than the familiar Rose Novena text.

Our Lady Aparecida our-lady-aparecida-novena

Oct 3–11
description & history

A nine-day novena honoring Our Lady of Aparecida, entrusting families, the poor, and the Church in Brazil to Mary's maternal intercession.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra a Nossa Senhora Aparecida, confiando as famílias, os pobres e a Igreja no Brasil à intercessão maternal de Maria.

In 1717, fishermen on the Paraíba do Sul River found a small image of the Blessed Virgin Mary in two pieces: first the body, then the head. After they placed it in the boat, their nets filled abundantly. Devotion spread from this sign of providence, and Our Lady of Aparecida became the principal patroness of Brazil.

Em 1717, pescadores no Rio Paraíba do Sul encontraram uma pequena imagem da Santíssima Virgem Maria em duas partes: primeiro o corpo e depois a cabeça. Depois de colocá-la na barca, as redes se encheram abundantemente. A devoção se espalhou a partir desse sinal de providência, e Nossa Senhora Aparecida tornou-se a padroeira principal do Brasil.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Most Holy Virgin, Mother Aparecida, I place myself under your mantle and ask your help in this novena. Present my intentions to Jesus and teach me to trust in God's providence with a humble and faithful heart. Amen.

Virgem Santíssima, Mãe Aparecida, eu me coloco sob o vosso manto e peço vosso auxílio nesta novena. Apresentai minhas intenções a Jesus e ensinai-me a confiar na providência de Deus com coração humilde e fiel. Amém.

Day 1: Mary, found among the humble

Dia 1: Maria, encontrada entre os humildes

The image of Our Lady Aparecida was found by simple fishermen. God often reveals His mercy through what the world considers small. Mary teaches us to welcome grace with humility and grateful faith.

A imagem de Nossa Senhora Aparecida foi encontrada por pescadores simples. Deus muitas vezes revela Sua misericórdia por meio daquilo que o mundo considera pequeno. Maria nos ensina a acolher a graça com humildade e fé agradecida.

Intention

For a humble heart that recognizes God's presence in ordinary life.

Por um coração humilde que reconheça a presença de Deus na vida cotidiana.

Day 2: Mother who gathers what is broken

Dia 2: Mãe que reúne o que está quebrado

The body and head of the image were found separately and made whole again. Under Mary's care, what is divided can be reconciled. She helps us seek unity in our families, communities, and hearts.

O corpo e a cabeça da imagem foram encontrados separadamente e reunidos de novo. Sob o cuidado de Maria, o que está dividido pode ser reconciliado. Ela nos ajuda a buscar unidade nas famílias, comunidades e no próprio coração.

Intention

For reconciliation in families and healing of relationships.

Pela reconciliação nas famílias e pela cura dos relacionamentos.

Day 3: Mother of Divine Providence

Dia 3: Mãe da Divina Providência

After invoking Mary, the fishermen's empty nets became abundant. She points us to God's providence, which never fails those who trust Him. In spiritual and material needs, we learn to depend on the Lord.

Depois de invocarem Maria, as redes vazias dos pescadores se tornaram abundantes. Ela nos aponta para a providência de Deus, que nunca falha com os que n'Ele confiam. Nas necessidades espirituais e materiais, aprendemos a depender do Senhor.

Intention

For all who lack work, bread, or daily necessities.

Por todos os que carecem de trabalho, pão e sustento diário.

Day 4: Mother close to the poor

Dia 4: Mãe próxima dos pobres

Our Lady Aparecida is beloved by the poor and forgotten. Mary stands with those who suffer and reminds the Church to serve with tenderness and justice. In her school we learn charity that is concrete and faithful.

Nossa Senhora Aparecida é amada pelos pobres e esquecidos. Maria permanece com os que sofrem e recorda à Igreja o serviço com ternura e justiça. Em sua escola aprendemos uma caridade concreta e fiel.

Intention

For the poor, the abandoned, and all who seek dignity and hope.

Pelos pobres, abandonados e por todos os que buscam dignidade e esperança.

Day 5: Mother and Patroness of Brazil

Dia 5: Mãe e Padroeira do Brasil

As Patroness of Brazil, Our Lady of Aparecida gathers a diverse people under one maternal mantle. She calls the nation to conversion, peace, and fidelity to Christ. True renewal begins with prayer and holiness.

Como Padroeira do Brasil, Nossa Senhora Aparecida reúne um povo diverso sob um só manto maternal. Ela chama a nação à conversão, à paz e à fidelidade a Cristo. A verdadeira renovação começa na oração e na santidade.

Intention

For Brazil: for peace, justice, and fidelity to the Gospel.

Pelo Brasil: por paz, justiça e fidelidade ao Evangelho.

Day 6: Mother of the Church

Dia 6: Mãe da Igreja

At the foot of the Cross, Jesus gave Mary as Mother to all disciples. She intercedes for the Church so that we may remain united in truth and charity. Through her, we ask for faithful shepherds and holy vocations.

Aos pés da Cruz, Jesus nos deu Maria como Mãe de todos os discípulos. Ela intercede pela Igreja para que permaneçamos unidos na verdade e na caridade. Por sua intercessão, pedimos pastores fiéis e santas vocações.

Intention

For the Church, the Pope, bishops, priests, and all consecrated life.

Pela Igreja, pelo Papa, bispos, sacerdotes e toda a vida consagrada.

Day 7: Mother of families

Dia 7: Mãe das famílias

Mary lived the hidden life of Nazareth with Jesus and Joseph. She understands the joys and trials of home life. Entrusting our households to her, we ask for patience, forgiveness, and persevering love.

Maria viveu a vida escondida de Nazaré com Jesus e José. Ela conhece as alegrias e as provações do lar. Confiando nossas casas a ela, pedimos paciência, perdão e amor perseverante.

Intention

For families, marriages, children, and all who long for a holy home.

Pelas famílias, pelos matrimônios, pelas crianças e por todos os que desejam um lar santo.

Day 8: Mother of consolation and healing

Dia 8: Mãe de consolação e cura

Many faithful turn to Our Lady Aparecida in illness and affliction. Mary does not abandon her children in suffering; she leads them to the Heart of Jesus, source of mercy and healing.

Muitos fiéis recorrem a Nossa Senhora Aparecida na doença e na aflição. Maria não abandona seus filhos no sofrimento; ela os conduz ao Coração de Jesus, fonte de misericórdia e cura.

Intention

For the sick, the grieving, and all who need consolation.

Pelos enfermos, pelos enlutados e por todos os que necessitam de consolo.

Day 9: Mother who leads us to Jesus

Dia 9: Mãe que nos conduz a Jesus

At Cana, Mary said, 'Do whatever He tells you.' Every true Marian devotion ends in deeper discipleship of Christ. At the end of this novena, we entrust our lives to Jesus through Mary's maternal hands.

Em Caná, Maria disse: 'Fazei tudo o que Ele vos disser.' Toda devoção mariana autêntica termina em maior discipulado de Cristo. Ao concluir esta novena, entregamos nossa vida a Jesus pelas mãos maternas de Maria.

Intention

For perseverance in faith and total fidelity to Jesus Christ.

Pela perseverança na fé e fidelidade total a Jesus Cristo.

Closing prayer

Our Lady of Aparecida, Queen and Patroness of Brazil, pray for us. Protect our families, console the afflicted, strengthen the Church, and lead us always to your Son, Jesus Christ. Amen.

Nossa Senhora Aparecida, Rainha e Padroeira do Brasil, rogai por nós. Protegei nossas famílias, consolai os aflitos, fortalecei a Igreja e conduzi-nos sempre ao vosso Filho, Jesus Cristo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

This is an original composition for a Brazilian devotion that neither English source covers. Its historical anchor is sound and well-told — the 1717 finding of the image by fishermen on the Paraíba do Sul (body then head, reunited; the abundant catch) drives Days 1–3, matching the traditional Aparecida narrative. The remaining days develop standard, doctrinally safe Marian themes (poor, Patroness of Brazil, Mother of the Church, families, the sick, leading to Jesus at Cana) appropriate to the title. Since the pt-BR side is the natural primary language for this devotion, the comparison baseline is internal; the text reads as a faithful, catechetically careful modern novena rather than a transcription of any specific published Aparecida novena.

Our Lady of Grace (Miraculous Medal) miraculous-medal-novena

Nov 18–26
description & history

A nine-day novena honoring Our Lady of the Miraculous Medal, based on the apparitions of the Blessed Virgin Mary to St. Catherine Labouré in 1830 at the Rue du Bac chapel in Paris.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra a Nossa Senhora da Medalha Milagrosa, baseada nas aparições da Santíssima Virgem Maria a Santa Catarina Labouré em 1830 na capela da Rue du Bac em Paris.

In 1830, the Blessed Virgin Mary appeared three times to St. Catherine Labouré, a Daughter of Charity novice in Paris. During the November apparition, Mary showed the design of a medal, saying 'Have a medal struck upon this model. Those who wear it will receive great graces.' The medal was first struck in 1832, and the extraordinary favors it brought led the faithful to call it the 'Miraculous Medal.'

Em 1830, a Santíssima Virgem Maria apareceu três vezes a Santa Catarina Labouré, uma noviça das Filhas da Caridade em Paris. Durante a aparição de novembro, Maria mostrou o desenho de uma medalha, dizendo 'Mandai cunhar uma medalha segundo este modelo. As pessoas que a usarem receberão grandes graças.' A medalha foi cunhada pela primeira vez em 1832, e os favores extraordinários que trouxe levaram os fiéis a chamá-la de 'Medalha Milagrosa.'

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer—

Day 1: Mary, Full of Grace

Dia 1: Maria, Cheia de Graça

O Immaculate Virgin Mary, from the first moment of your conception you were preserved from all stain of sin by a singular privilege of God. The angel greeted you as 'full of grace,' for you were chosen to be the spotless vessel of the Incarnation. Your purity shines as a beacon of hope for all who struggle against sin. Help us to imitate your openness to God's grace and to seek the holiness to which we are called.

Ó Virgem Imaculada Maria, desde o primeiro instante da vossa conceição fostes preservada de toda mancha de pecado por um singular privilégio de Deus. O anjo vos saudou como 'cheia de graça,' pois fostes escolhida para ser o vaso imaculado da Encarnação. Vossa pureza brilha como um farol de esperança para todos que lutam contra o pecado. Ajudai-nos a imitar vossa abertura à graça de Deus e a buscar a santidade à qual somos chamados.

Intention

For the grace to grow in purity of heart, mind, and body.

Pela graça de crescer na pureza de coração, mente e corpo.

Day 2: Mary, Mother of God

Dia 2: Maria, Mãe de Deus

O Blessed Mother, the Council of Ephesus proclaimed you Theotokos — the God-Bearer — for in your womb the eternal Word took flesh. You held in your arms the One who holds all creation in being. Your 'yes' at the Annunciation made possible the salvation of the world. As you nurtured the infant Jesus with a mother's tender love, nurture in us a living faith and an ever-deeper love for your Divine Son.

Ó Mãe Santíssima, o Concílio de Éfeso vos proclamou Theotokos — a Mãe de Deus — pois em vosso ventre o Verbo eterno se fez carne. Vós segurastes em vossos braços Aquele que sustenta toda a criação. Vosso 'sim' na Anunciação tornou possível a salvação do mundo. Assim como nutriste o menino Jesus com ternura maternal, nutri em nós uma fé viva e um amor cada vez mais profundo pelo vosso Divino Filho.

Intention

For a deeper devotion to Jesus Christ through his Blessed Mother.

Por uma devoção mais profunda a Jesus Cristo por meio de sua Mãe Santíssima.

Day 3: Mary, Refuge of Sinners

Dia 3: Maria, Refúgio dos Pecadores

O merciful Mother, you never cease to intercede for those who have strayed from the path of grace. At the foot of the Cross, you received us all as your children, and your maternal heart yearns for the conversion of every sinner. No one who has sought your help has ever been left unaided. We entrust to you all who are far from God, that through your prayers they may find the way back to your Son's merciful Heart.

Ó Mãe misericordiosa, vós nunca cessais de interceder por aqueles que se desviaram do caminho da graça. Aos pés da Cruz, recebestes todos nós como vossos filhos, e vosso coração maternal anseia pela conversão de cada pecador. Ninguém que buscou vossa ajuda ficou jamais desamparado. Confiamos a vós todos os que estão longe de Deus, para que por vossas orações encontrem o caminho de volta ao Coração misericordioso de vosso Filho.

Intention

For the conversion of sinners and those who have fallen away from the faith.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e daqueles que se afastaram da fé.

Day 4: Mary, Comfort of the Afflicted

Dia 4: Maria, Consoladora dos Aflitos

O compassionate Mother, you know the depth of human suffering, for you stood beneath the Cross and watched your Son die. Your heart was pierced by a sword of sorrow, as Simeon foretold, and through that suffering you became the consolation of all who weep. Turn your gentle eyes upon those who are burdened with grief, loneliness, and despair. Wrap them in your mantle of comfort and lead them to the peace that only God can give.

Ó Mãe compassiva, vós conheceis a profundidade do sofrimento humano, pois permanecestes ao pé da Cruz e vistes vosso Filho morrer. Vosso coração foi traspassado por uma espada de dor, como Simeão profetizou, e por meio desse sofrimento vos tornastes a consolação de todos os que choram. Voltai vossos olhos bondosos para aqueles que estão sobrecarregados pela tristeza, solidão e desespero. Envolvei-os em vosso manto de consolo e conduzi-os à paz que só Deus pode dar.

Intention

For all who suffer grief, depression, or any affliction of body or soul.

Por todos que sofrem tristeza, depressão ou qualquer aflição de corpo ou alma.

Day 5: Mary, Health of the Sick

Dia 5: Maria, Saúde dos Enfermos

O tender Mother, countless miracles of healing have been granted through your intercession and through the wearing of your Miraculous Medal. You who accompanied your Son during His earthly ministry of healing now continue that work of mercy from heaven. Look with compassion upon all who are ill, upon those who care for them, and upon all who work in the healing arts. Obtain for the sick the grace of patience and, if it be God's will, restoration of health.

Ó terna Mãe, incontáveis milagres de cura foram concedidos por vossa intercessão e pelo uso da vossa Medalha Milagrosa. Vós que acompanhastes vosso Filho durante Seu ministério terreno de cura, continuais agora essa obra de misericórdia do céu. Olhai com compaixão para todos os que estão doentes, para aqueles que cuidam deles e para todos que trabalham nas artes da cura. Obtende para os enfermos a graça da paciência e, se for a vontade de Deus, a restauração da saúde.

Intention

For the healing and comfort of all who are sick or suffering.

Pela cura e consolo de todos os que estão doentes ou sofrendo.

Day 6: Mary, Queen of All Saints

Dia 6: Maria, Rainha de Todos os Santos

O glorious Queen, you stand above all the angels and saints in heaven, crowned with twelve stars, clothed with the sun. Yet your glory comes not from yourself but from the grace of God, who looked upon the lowliness of His handmaid and did great things for you. The saints throughout the ages have looked to you as their model and their Mother. Inspire us to follow their example and yours, that we may one day join the communion of saints in the presence of God.

Ó Rainha gloriosa, vós estais acima de todos os anjos e santos no céu, coroada com doze estrelas, vestida de sol. Porém vossa glória não vem de vós mesma, mas da graça de Deus, que olhou para a humildade de Sua serva e fez grandes coisas por vós. Os santos ao longo dos séculos olharam para vós como seu modelo e sua Mãe. Inspirai-nos a seguir o exemplo deles e o vosso, para que um dia possamos nos juntar à comunhão dos santos na presença de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to pursue holiness and follow the example of the saints.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade e seguir o exemplo dos santos.

Day 7: Mary, Help of Christians

Dia 7: Maria, Auxílio dos Cristãos

O powerful Virgin, throughout the centuries you have come to the aid of your children in times of peril and persecution. At Lepanto, at Vienna, and in countless hidden battles of the soul, your intercession has turned the tide against the forces of evil. You appeared to St. Catherine Labouré with rays of light streaming from your hands — graces poured out upon the world. Protect the Church, defend the faithful, and strengthen all who are persecuted for the name of Christ.

Ó Virgem poderosa, ao longo dos séculos viestes em auxílio de vossos filhos em tempos de perigo e perseguição. Em Lepanto, em Viena e em incontáveis batalhas ocultas da alma, vossa intercessão mudou o rumo contra as forças do mal. Vós aparecestes a Santa Catarina Labouré com raios de luz emanando de vossas mãos — graças derramadas sobre o mundo. Protegei a Igreja, defendei os fiéis e fortalecei todos os que são perseguidos pelo nome de Cristo.

Intention

For the protection of the Church and all persecuted Christians throughout the world.

Pela proteção da Igreja e de todos os cristãos perseguidos pelo mundo.

Day 8: Mary, Gate of Heaven

Dia 8: Maria, Porta do Céu

O blessed Gate of Heaven, through you the eternal Word entered our world, and through your maternal guidance countless souls have found their way to paradise. You show us the path to your Son, for as you said at Cana, 'Do whatever He tells you.' On the Miraculous Medal you stand upon the globe, crushing the serpent beneath your feet, your hands open in blessing. Lead us safely through the trials of this life to the eternal joy that awaits us in your Son's kingdom.

Ó bendita Porta do Céu, por vós o Verbo eterno entrou em nosso mundo, e por vossa orientação maternal incontáveis almas encontraram o caminho para o paraíso. Vós nos mostrais o caminho para vosso Filho, pois como dissestes em Caná: 'Fazei tudo o que Ele vos disser.' Na Medalha Milagrosa, vós estais sobre o globo, esmagando a serpente sob vossos pés, com as mãos abertas em bênção. Conduzi-nos com segurança através das provações desta vida até a alegria eterna que nos espera no reino de vosso Filho.

Intention

For the grace of a holy death and the salvation of souls.

Pela graça de uma morte santa e pela salvação das almas.

Day 9: Mary, Mediatrix of All Graces

Dia 9: Maria, Medianeira de Todas as Graças

O most generous Mother, God has willed that every grace should pass through your hands, as the rays of light streaming from your fingers on the Miraculous Medal beautifully signify. You are the channel through which the treasures of heaven flow to earth, not because God needs you, but because He delights in honoring you. As this novena draws to a close, we place all our needs and intentions into your Immaculate Heart, confident that you will present them to your Son with a mother's loving intercession.

Ó Mãe generosíssima, Deus quis que toda graça passasse por vossas mãos, como os raios de luz emanando de vossos dedos na Medalha Milagrosa belamente significam. Vós sois o canal pelo qual os tesouros do céu fluem para a terra, não porque Deus necessita de vós, mas porque Ele Se deleita em vos honrar. Ao encerrar esta novena, colocamos todas as nossas necessidades e intenções em vosso Imaculado Coração, confiantes de que as apresentareis ao vosso Filho com a amorosa intercessão de Mãe.

Intention

For all the intentions held in my heart during this novena, and for the grace to trust always in Mary's intercession.

Por todas as intenções guardadas no meu coração durante esta novena, e pela graça de confiar sempre na intercessão de Maria.

Closing prayer—

FishEaters traditional

name="grace">
Novena
of Grace (in honor of St. Francis Xavier)

In A.D. 1633, the Jesuit priest, Father Mastrilli, was
at the point of death as a result of an accident when St. Francis
Xavier appeared to him and asked him to devote his life to missionizing
the Indies. Father Mastrilli made a vow before his superior that he
would do so if God spared his life. St. Francis appeared to him again
to ask him to renew the vow, and foretold that Fr. Mastrilli would be
martyred. After this second apparition, the priest was completely
healed. News of the miracle spread, and this novena began to be prayed.
Father Mastrilli kept his vow and was martyred on 17 October, 1637.
This novena can be prayed any time, but is often prayed, even publicly
along with preparatory prayers , a sermon, Benediction, and hymns,
starting on 4 March and ending on 12 March, the date that St. Francis
Xavier and St. Ignatius of Loyola were canonized in 1622.

V: In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.
Amen. O Jesus, answer our petitions as we kneel before Thee.

R: O Heart of Jesus, hear and grant our prayers.

V: Pray for us, Saint Francis Xavier,

R: that we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

V: Let us pray: Come, Holy Ghost, fill the hearts of Thy faithful.
Enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy spirit and they
shall be created,

R: and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

V: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost,

R: as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without
end. Amen.

All: O most lovable and loving Saint Francis Xavier in union with thee
I adore the Divine Majesty. The remembrance of the favors with which
God blessed thee during life and of thy glory after death fills me with
joy; and I unite with thee in offering to Him my humble tribute of
thanksgiving and of praise. I implore thee to secure for me through thy
powerful intercession the inestimable blessing of living and dying in
the state of grace. I also beseech thee to obtain for me the favor I
ask in this novena.

(Mention your intentions here, or simply pause
to allow people to form their intentions silently if this is being
prayed publicly)

But if what I ask is not for the glory of God and
for the good of my soul do thou obtain for me what is more conducive to
both. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and one Glory Be

V: Pray for us, Saint Francis Xavier,

R: That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Hymn to Saint Francis Xavier (may be recited if prayed
privately):

Oh, Father Saint Francis, we kneel at thy feet,

While blessings and favors we beg and entreat,

That thou from thy bright throne in heaven above

Wouldst look on thy clients with pity and love.

Saint Francis Xavier, Oh pray for us!

Saint Francis Xavier, Oh pray for us!

Oh, Father Saint Francis, thy words were once strong

Against Satan's wiles and an infidel throng.

Not less is thy might where in heaven thou art;

Oh, come to our aid, in our battle take part.

Saint Francis Xavier, Oh pray for us!

Saint Francis Xavier, Oh pray for us!

St. Francis Xavier's
Prayer for Unbelievers:

All: Eternal God, Creator of all things remember that the souls of
unbelievers have been created by Thee and formed to Thy own image and
likeness. Behold, O Lord, how to Thy dishonor hell is being filled with
these very souls. Remember that Jesus Christ, Thy Son for their
salvation suffered a most cruel death. Do not permit, O Lord, I beseech
Thee that Thy divine Son be any longer despised by unbelievers, but
rather, being appeased by the prayers of Thy saints and the Church the
most holy spouse of Thy Son deign to be mindful of Thy mercy and
forgetting their idolatry and their unbelief bring them to know Him
Whom Thou didst send Jesus Christ, Thy Son, Our Lord Who is our health,
life, and resurrection through Whom we have been redeemed and sav

PrayMoreNovenas modern

— not on PrayMoreNovenas (no `pmn` URL provided for this entry).

Fidelity note

With no usable source on either site, this is best read as an original composition. Its one fixed, traditional element is fully faithful: the Miraculous Medal invocation "O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee," prayed three times, exactly as struck on the 1830 medal revealed to St. Catherine Labouré. The nine daily meditations are freshly written around classic Litany-of-Loreto-style Marian titles (Full of Grace, Mother of God, Refuge of Sinners, Health of the Sick, Help of Christians, Gate of Heaven, Mediatrix of All Graces) and weave in authentic Miraculous Medal imagery — the rays of grace from Mary's hands, standing on the globe, crushing the serpent, and St. Catherine Labouré herself. Doctrinally careful and devotionally apt; not a transcription of any specific published Miraculous Medal novena. Note also the structural difference from the other novenas: no separate opening/closing prayer, with the day's intention printed inline.

Our Lady of Guadalupe guadalupe-novena

Dec 3–11
description & history

A nine-day novena honoring Our Lady of Guadalupe, entrusting the Church, families, and all the peoples of the Americas to Mary's maternal intercession.

Uma novena de nove dias em honra a Nossa Senhora de Guadalupe, confiando a Igreja, as famílias e todos os povos das Américas à intercessão maternal de Maria.

In December 1531, the Blessed Virgin Mary appeared on Tepeyac Hill near Mexico City to Saint Juan Diego, identifying herself as the Mother of the true God. She asked that a chapel be built and left a miraculous sign: her image imprinted on Juan Diego's tilma. Under the title of Our Lady of Guadalupe, she is venerated as Patroness of the Americas and a mother of consolation, conversion, and hope.

Em dezembro de 1531, a Santíssima Virgem Maria apareceu no monte Tepeyac, perto da Cidade do México, a São Juan Diego, apresentando-se como a Mãe do verdadeiro Deus. Ela pediu a construção de uma capela e deixou um sinal milagroso: sua imagem impressa no tilma de Juan Diego. Sob o título de Nossa Senhora de Guadalupe, é venerada como Padroeira das Américas e mãe de consolação, conversão e esperança.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Most Blessed Virgin of Guadalupe, Mother of the true God and our tender Mother, I place this novena in your hands. Intercede for me before your Son, Jesus Christ, and help me to welcome His will with faith, humility, and trust. Amen.

Virgem Santíssima de Guadalupe, Mãe do verdadeiro Deus e nossa terna Mãe, coloco esta novena em vossas mãos. Intercedei por mim junto de vosso Filho, Jesus Cristo, e ajudai-me a acolher Sua vontade com fé, humildade e confiança. Amém.

Day 1: Mother of the true God

Dia 1: Mãe do verdadeiro Deus

At Tepeyac, Mary revealed herself as the Mother of the true God through whom we live. She invites us to trust the living Lord in every need. Her presence reminds us that God draws close to His children with tenderness.

No Tepeyac, Maria se revelou como a Mãe do verdadeiro Deus por quem vivemos. Ela nos convida a confiar no Senhor vivo em toda necessidade. Sua presença recorda que Deus se aproxima de seus filhos com ternura.

Intention

For deeper faith in God and trust in His providence.

Por uma fé mais profunda em Deus e confiança em Sua providência.

Day 2: Mother who comes to the humble

Dia 2: Mãe que vem aos humildes

Mary chose Saint Juan Diego, a simple and faithful man, to carry her message. God delights in working through little ones. She teaches us to offer ourselves with simplicity and obedience.

Maria escolheu São Juan Diego, um homem simples e fiel, para levar sua mensagem. Deus se compraz em agir pelos pequenos. Ela nos ensina a nos oferecer com simplicidade e obediência.

Intention

For humility, obedience, and fidelity to God's call.

Por humildade, obediência e fidelidade ao chamado de Deus.

Day 3: Mother of consolation

Dia 3: Mãe da consolação

Mary spoke with maternal tenderness to Juan Diego in his sorrow and fear. She does not ignore our pain, but accompanies us in it. Under her mantle we find comfort and peace in trials.

Maria falou com ternura maternal a Juan Diego em sua tristeza e temor. Ela não ignora nossa dor, mas nos acompanha nela. Sob seu manto encontramos consolo e paz nas provações.

Intention

For the grieving, the anxious, and all who need consolation.

Pelos enlutados, pelos aflitos e por todos os que precisam de consolo.

Day 4: Mother who gives signs of hope

Dia 4: Mãe que dá sinais de esperança

In winter, roses bloomed on Tepeyac as a heavenly sign. God can make grace blossom where we expect barrenness. Mary teaches us to hope even when situations seem impossible.

Em pleno inverno, rosas floresceram no Tepeyac como sinal do céu. Deus pode fazer a graça florescer onde esperamos esterilidade. Maria nos ensina a esperar mesmo quando as situações parecem impossíveis.

Intention

For hope in difficult situations and trust in God's timing.

Por esperança nas situações difíceis e confiança no tempo de Deus.

Day 5: Mother whose image calls to conversion

Dia 5: Mãe cuja imagem chama à conversão

The tilma image speaks without words, drawing hearts to Christ. Mary's mission is always to lead us to repentance, faith, and holiness. True devotion bears fruit in conversion of life.

A imagem do tilma fala sem palavras, atraindo os corações para Cristo. A missão de Maria é sempre conduzir ao arrependimento, à fé e à santidade. A verdadeira devoção produz fruto na conversão de vida.

Intention

For conversion of heart and perseverance in confession and prayer.

Pela conversão do coração e perseverança na confissão e na oração.

Day 6: Mother and protectress of families

Dia 6: Mãe e protetora das famílias

Our Lady of Guadalupe gathers families under her mantle and teaches them to pray together. In a wounded world, Christian homes are called to be places of charity and fidelity. Mary strengthens spouses, parents, and children in love.

Nossa Senhora de Guadalupe reúne as famílias sob seu manto e as ensina a rezar juntas. Num mundo ferido, os lares cristãos são chamados a ser lugares de caridade e fidelidade. Maria fortalece esposos, pais e filhos no amor.

Intention

For families, marriages, and healing of domestic wounds.

Pelas famílias, pelos matrimônios e pela cura das feridas do lar.

Day 7: Mother of the vulnerable

Dia 7: Mãe dos vulneráveis

In the Guadalupe image, Mary appears as a mother carrying new life. She teaches us to defend the dignity of every human person, especially the poor, the unborn, and those forgotten by society.

Na imagem de Guadalupe, Maria aparece como mãe que carrega nova vida. Ela nos ensina a defender a dignidade de toda pessoa humana, especialmente os pobres, os nascituros e os esquecidos pela sociedade.

Intention

For respect for life from conception to natural death.

Pelo respeito à vida desde a concepção até a morte natural.

Day 8: Mother of the Church in the Americas

Dia 8: Mãe da Igreja nas Américas

As Patroness of the Americas, Our Lady of Guadalupe calls the Church to unity and missionary zeal. She gathers diverse peoples into one family in Christ and encourages us to witness with charity and truth.

Como Padroeira das Américas, Nossa Senhora de Guadalupe chama a Igreja à unidade e ao ardor missionário. Ela reúne povos diversos numa só família em Cristo e nos encoraja a testemunhar com caridade e verdade.

Intention

For the Church in the Americas, for missionaries, and for new evangelization.

Pela Igreja nas Américas, pelos missionários e pela nova evangelização.

Day 9: Mother who leads us always to Jesus

Dia 9: Mãe que sempre nos conduz a Jesus

Mary's final work in every heart is to bring us to her Son. At Cana she said, 'Do whatever He tells you,' and this remains her maternal counsel. We end this novena by entrusting our lives to Jesus through her intercession.

A obra final de Maria em cada coração é conduzir-nos ao seu Filho. Em Caná ela disse: 'Fazei tudo o que Ele vos disser', e esse continua sendo seu conselho materno. Concluímos esta novena entregando nossa vida a Jesus por sua intercessão.

Intention

For final perseverance in faith and full fidelity to Jesus Christ.

Pela perseverança final na fé e plena fidelidade a Jesus Cristo.

Closing prayer

Our Lady of Guadalupe, Mother of the Americas, pray for us. Protect our families, defend the vulnerable, strengthen the Church, and lead all peoples to your Son, Jesus Christ. Amen.

Nossa Senhora de Guadalupe, Mãe das Américas, rogai por nós. Protegei nossas famílias, defendei os vulneráveis, fortalecei a Igreja e conduzi todos os povos ao vosso Filho, Jesus Cristo. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="guadalupe">
Novena
to Our Lady of Guadalupe

Say once a day for nine days, especially beginning on 3
December and ending on 11 December, the eve of the Feast of Our Lady of
Guadalupeh

Our Lady of Guadalupe, according to thy message in Mexico I venerate
thee as the Virgin Mother of the true God for whom we live, the Creator
of all the world, Maker of heaven and earth. In spirit I kneel before
thy most holy image which thou didst miraculously imprint upon the
cloak of the Indian Juan Diego, and with the faith of the countless
numbers of pilgrims who visit thy shrine, I beg thy for this favor:

(Mention your intentions
here)

Remember, O Immaculate Virgin, the words thou hast spoken to thy devout
client: "I am a merciful mother to thee and to all thy people who love
me and trust in me and invoke my help. I listen to their lamentations
and solace all their sorrows and sufferings." I beg thee to be a
merciful mother to me, because I sincerely love thee and trust in thee
and invoke thy help. I entreat thee, our Lady of Guadalupe, to grant my
request, if this should be the will of God, in order that I may bear
witness to thy love, thy compassion, thy help and protection. Do not
forsake me in my needs.

Our Lady of Guadalupe, pray for us.

Our Lady of Guadalupe, pray for us.

Our Lady of Guadalupe, pray for us.

Pray three Hail Marys
<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross and closes with the Our Father, Hail Mary, and Glory Be. It uses nine per-day petitions to "Dearest Lady of Guadalupe": Day 1 "teach me your ways of gentleness and strength"; Day 2 "a lively faith to do your Son's holy will always"; Day 3 strength to "walk valiantly amid the sharp thorns strewn across my pathway"; Day 4 "a fortified will to imitate your divine Son's charity"; Day 5 "pardon of all my sins, abundant graces to serve your Son more faithfully"; Day 6 multiplication of "priestly vocations and religious houses"; Day 7 that "parents live a holy life and educate their children in a Christian manner"; Day 8 "grace to fulfill the duties of my state in life with faithfulness and constancy"; Day 9 "the happiness of seeing her face to face in heaven."

Fidelity note

Ember's Guadalupe novena is an original composition rather than a transcription of either source, but it is strongly faithful to the Guadalupe event and its theology. The opening prayer deliberately echoes the signature FishEaters/Nican Mopohua phrasing — "Mother of the true God" — and the meditations are built squarely on the apparition narrative (Tepeyac, Juan Diego's humility and sorrow, winter roses, the tilma image, Patroness of the Americas). Its day-themes overlap with PMN's concerns (humility/obedience, conversion and pardon, families and the education of children, fidelity to one's state in life, perseverance to heaven), but wording is paraphrased and reorganized, and it adds a distinctly modern pro-life "Mother of the vulnerable" day grounded in the image's depiction of Mary as expectant. The closing matches the common "Our Lady of Guadalupe, pray for us" refrain. Faithful in event, imagery, and theme; not verbatim.

Pentecost (Holy Spirit) novena-holy-spirit

Friday after Ascension → Pentecost
description & history

The original novena of the Church — nine days of prayer to the Holy Spirit, following the example of the Apostles and Our Lady who gathered in the Upper Room from the Ascension to Pentecost. Each day focuses on a gift or fruit of the Holy Spirit.

A novena original da Igreja — nove dias de oração ao Espírito Santo, seguindo o exemplo dos Apóstolos e de Nossa Senhora que se reuniram no Cenáculo da Ascensão a Pentecostes. Cada dia foca em um dom ou fruto do Espírito Santo.

This is the oldest novena in Christianity, rooted in the nine days the Apostles and the Blessed Virgin Mary spent in prayer between the Ascension and Pentecost (Acts 1:14). Pope Leo XIII in his 1897 encyclical Divinum Illud Munus encouraged the faithful to pray a novena to the Holy Spirit before Pentecost each year. All subsequent novenas in Catholic tradition draw their inspiration from this original nine-day vigil of prayer.

Esta é a novena mais antiga do Cristianismo, enraizada nos nove dias que os Apóstolos e a Santíssima Virgem Maria passaram em oração entre a Ascensão e Pentecostes (At 1,14). O Papa Leão XIII em sua encíclica Divinum Illud Munus de 1897 encorajou os fiéis a rezar uma novena ao Espírito Santo antes de Pentecostes a cada ano. Todas as novenas subsequentes na tradição católica se inspiram nesta vigília original de nove dias de oração.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer—

Day 1: The Gift of Wisdom

Dia 1: O Dom da Sabedoria

Come, O Spirit of Wisdom, and grant us the grace to see all things in the light of eternity. Detach our hearts from the passing vanities of this world and fix them upon the things that are above. Let us taste the sweetness of divine truth and find in God alone our lasting joy, so that we may order our lives according to His holy will.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Sabedoria, e concedei-nos a graça de ver todas as coisas à luz da eternidade. Desapegai nossos corações das vaidades passageiras deste mundo e fixai-os nas coisas do alto. Fazei-nos saborear a doçura da verdade divina e encontrar somente em Deus a nossa alegria duradoura, para que possamos ordenar nossas vidas segundo a Sua santa vontade.

Intention

For the grace to judge all things wisely in the light of God's eternal truth.

Pela graça de julgar todas as coisas sabiamente à luz da verdade eterna de Deus.

Day 2: The Gift of Understanding

Dia 2: O Dom do Entendimento
