Come, O Spirit of Understanding, and enlighten the eyes of our minds. Grant us a penetrating insight into the mysteries of faith, that we may grasp what God has revealed and hold it with unwavering conviction. Dispel the darkness of ignorance and error, and let the radiance of divine truth illuminate our hearts and guide our steps.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Entendimento, e iluminai os olhos de nossas mentes. Concedei-nos uma compreensão penetrante dos mistérios da fé, para que possamos captar o que Deus revelou e mantê-lo com convicção inabalável. Dissipai as trevas da ignorância e do erro, e que a luz da verdade divina ilumine nossos corações e guie nossos passos.

Intention

For a deeper penetration into the truths of our holy faith.

Por uma compreensão mais profunda das verdades da nossa santa fé.

Day 3: The Gift of Counsel

Dia 3: O Dom do Conselho

Come, O Spirit of Counsel, and guide us in all our ways. In moments of doubt and uncertainty, whisper to our hearts the path that leads to God. Preserve us from rash judgments and hasty decisions, and grant us the prudence to discern Your will in all things. Be our guide in every difficulty, that we may never stray from the way of righteousness.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Conselho, e guiai-nos em todos os nossos caminhos. Nos momentos de dúvida e incerteza, sussurrai aos nossos corações o caminho que conduz a Deus. Preservai-nos de julgamentos precipitados e decisões apressadas, e concedei-nos a prudência para discernir a Vossa vontade em todas as coisas. Sede nosso guia em cada dificuldade, para que nunca nos desviemos do caminho da retidão.

Intention

For the light to know God's will and the courage to follow it.

Pela luz para conhecer a vontade de Deus e a coragem para segui-la.

Day 4: The Gift of Fortitude

Dia 4: O Dom da Fortaleza

Come, O Spirit of Fortitude, and strengthen our souls in time of trial. Grant us the holy courage of the martyrs, that we may bear every cross with patience and overcome every temptation with steadfast faith. When the way grows dark and our strength fails, be our rock and fortress, that we may persevere unto the end and never falter in our love for God.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Fortaleza, e fortalecei nossas almas no tempo da provação. Concedei-nos a santa coragem dos mártires, para que possamos carregar cada cruz com paciência e vencer cada tentação com fé inabalável. Quando o caminho se tornar escuro e nossas forças falharem, sede nossa rocha e fortaleza, para que perseveremos até o fim e nunca vacilemos no nosso amor a Deus.

Intention

For strength to bear the crosses of life and remain faithful to God.

Pela força para carregar as cruzes da vida e permanecer fiel a Deus.

Day 5: The Gift of Knowledge

Dia 5: O Dom da Ciência

Come, O Spirit of Knowledge, and teach us to see the hand of God in all created things. Grant us the grace to use the goods of this world as stepping stones to heaven and never as obstacles to our salvation. Help us to recognize the emptiness of earthly treasures and to seek with all our hearts the imperishable riches of the kingdom of God.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Ciência, e ensinai-nos a ver a mão de Deus em todas as coisas criadas. Concedei-nos a graça de usar os bens deste mundo como degraus para o céu e nunca como obstáculos à nossa salvação. Ajudai-nos a reconhecer o vazio dos tesouros terrenos e a buscar com todo o coração as riquezas imperecíveis do reino de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to see God's presence in all of creation and to use His gifts wisely.

Pela graça de ver a presença de Deus em toda a criação e usar Seus dons sabiamente.

Day 6: The Gift of Piety

Dia 6: O Dom da Piedade

Come, O Spirit of Piety, and fill our hearts with a tender devotion to our heavenly Father. Enkindle within us a filial love that turns to God in all things — in joy and in sorrow, in abundance and in want. Grant us reverence for all that is sacred, compassion for our brothers and sisters, and a spirit of worship that transforms our every action into a prayer.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Piedade, e enchei nossos corações com uma terna devoção ao nosso Pai celeste. Acendei em nós um amor filial que se volta a Deus em todas as coisas — na alegria e na tristeza, na abundância e na necessidade. Concedei-nos reverência por tudo o que é sagrado, compaixão pelos nossos irmãos e irmãs, e um espírito de adoração que transforme cada ação nossa em oração.

Intention

For a childlike trust in God and a heart full of devotion.

Por uma confiança filial em Deus e um coração cheio de devoção.

Day 7: The Gift of Fear of the Lord

Dia 7: O Dom do Temor de Deus

Come, O Spirit of Holy Fear, and fill us with a profound reverence for the majesty of God. Grant us not a slavish terror, but the loving awe of children who dread above all else to offend their Father. Let this holy fear be the guardian of our souls, preserving us from sin and drawing us ever closer to the Heart of God, in whom alone we find our peace and security.

Vinde, ó Espírito de Santo Temor, e enchei-nos com uma profunda reverência pela majestade de Deus. Concedei-nos não um terror servil, mas o temor amoroso dos filhos que temem acima de tudo ofender seu Pai. Que este santo temor seja o guardião de nossas almas, preservando-nos do pecado e atraindo-nos sempre mais ao Coração de Deus, no qual somente encontramos nossa paz e segurança.

Intention

For a holy reverence that keeps us from sin and draws us closer to God.

Por uma santa reverência que nos preserve do pecado e nos aproxime de Deus.

Day 8: The Fruits of the Holy Spirit

Dia 8: Os Frutos do Espírito Santo

Come, O Holy Spirit, and bring forth in us the abundant fruits of Your indwelling: love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. As a vine brings forth fruit in due season, so let our souls, grafted onto Christ and animated by Your grace, bear fruit that will last for eternal life. Transform our hearts that others may see in us the living presence of God.

Vinde, ó Espírito Santo, e produzi em nós os frutos abundantes da Vossa presença: amor, alegria, paz, paciência, benignidade, bondade, fidelidade, mansidão e domínio próprio. Como a videira produz fruto na estação devida, assim nossas almas, enxertadas em Cristo e animadas pela Vossa graça, produzam frutos que permaneçam para a vida eterna. Transformai nossos corações para que outros vejam em nós a presença viva de Deus.

Intention

For the grace to bear the fruits of the Holy Spirit in our daily lives.

Pela graça de produzir os frutos do Espírito Santo em nossa vida diária.

Day 9: Come, Holy Spirit — Prayer for a New Pentecost

Dia 9: Vinde, Espírito Santo — Oração por um Novo Pentecostes

Come, O Holy Spirit, descend upon us as You descended upon the Apostles and Our Lady in the Upper Room. Renew the face of the earth and send forth Your fire to enkindle in all hearts the flame of Your divine love. As You gave courage to the first disciples to proclaim the Gospel to all nations, so strengthen Your Church today. Grant us a new Pentecost — a fresh outpouring of grace, zeal, and apostolic boldness.

Vinde, ó Espírito Santo, descei sobre nós como descestes sobre os Apóstolos e Nossa Senhora no Cenáculo. Renovai a face da terra e enviai Vosso fogo para acender em todos os corações a chama do Vosso divino amor. Assim como destes coragem aos primeiros discípulos para proclamar o Evangelho a todas as nações, assim fortalecei Vossa Igreja hoje. Concedei-nos um novo Pentecostes — uma nova efusão de graça, zelo e audácia apostólica.

Intention

For a renewed outpouring of the Holy Spirit upon the Church and the world.

Por uma renovada efusão do Espírito Santo sobre a Igreja e o mundo.

Closing prayer—

FishEaters traditional

name="holyghost">
Novena
to the Holy Ghost

Pray especially starting on the day after Ascension
Thursday, ending on the Pentecost

First Day:

Almighty and eternal God, Who hast vouchsafed to regenerate us by water
and the Holy Ghost, and hast given us forgiveness of all our sins,
vouchsafe to send forth from heaven upon us Thy sevenfold Spirit, the
Spirit of Wisdom and Understanding, the Spirit of Counsel and
Fortitude, the Spirit of Knowledge and Piety, and fill us with the
Spirit of Holy Fear. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Second Day:

Come, O blessed Spirit of Holy Fear, penetrate my inmost heart, that I
may set Thee, my Lord and God, before my face forever; help me to shun
all things that can offend Thee, and make me worthy to appear before
the pure eyes of Thy Divine Majesty in heaven, where Thou livest and
reignest in the unity of the ever Blessed Trinity, God world without
end. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Third Day:

Come, O Blessed Spirit of Piety, possess my heart. Enkindle therein
such a love for God, that I may find satisfaction only in His service,
and for His sake lovingly submit to all legitimate authority. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Fourth Day:

Come, O Blessed Spirit of Fortitude, uphold my soul in times of trouble
and adversity, sustain my efforts after holiness, strengthen my
weakness, give me courage against all the assaults of my enemies, that
I may never be overcome and separated from Thee, my God and greatest
Good. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Fifth Day:

Come, O Blessed Spirit of Knowledge, and grant that I may perceive the
will of the Father; show me the nothingness of earthly things, that I
may realize their vanity and use them only for Thy glory and my own
salvation, looking ever beyond them to Thee, and Thy eternal rewards.
Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Sixth Day:

Come, O Spirit of Understanding, and enlighten our minds, that we may
know and believe all the mysteries of salvation; and may merit at last
to see the eternal light in Thy light; and in the light of glory to
have a clear vision of Thee and the Father and the Son. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Seventh Day:

Come, O Spirit of Counsel, help and guide me in all my ways, that I may
always do Thy holy will. Incline my heart to that which is good; turn
it away from all that is evil, and direct me by the straight path of
Thy commandments to that goal of eternal life for which I long. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Eighth Day:

Come, O Spirit of Wisdom, and reveal to my soul the mysteries of
heavenly things, their exceeding greatness, power and beauty. Teach me
to love them above and beyond all passing joys and satisfactions of the
earth. Help me to attain them and possess them for ever. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's

Ninth Day:

Come, O Divine Spirit, fill my heart with Thy heavenly fruits, Thy
charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, faith, mildness,
and temperance, that I may never weary in the service of God, but by
continued faithful submission to Thy inspiration, may merit to be
united eternally with Thee in the love of the Father and the Son. Amen.

Recite one Our Father,
one Hail Mary, and seven Glory Be's <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross, then a fixed invocation: "Let us bow down in humility at the power and grandeur of the Holy Spirit. Let us worship the Holy Trinity and give glory today to the Paraclete, our Advocate." Its common closing each day is the "Come Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of your faithful… Send forth your Spirit and they shall be created. And You shall renew the face of the earth." Rather than the seven gifts, PMN organizes the nine days around the fruits/virtues: Day 1 Charity, 2 Joy, 3 Peace, 4 Patience, 5 Kindness, 6 Faithfulness, 7 Gentleness, 8 Self-Control, 9 Goodness, each with a "grace of [intention]" petition.

Fidelity note

Ember's Holy Spirit novena is an original composition that follows the older, traditional FishEaters structure much more closely than PMN's. Its daily opening — "Come, Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love" — is the historic versicle (the same line PMN expands in its closing). The core architecture matches the classic seven-gifts novena: Days 1–7 take the gifts one by one and Days 8–9 turn to the fruits and a Pentecost prayer — exactly the FishEaters arc, though Ember orders the gifts Wisdom→Fear (descending) rather than the traditional Fear→Wisdom ascent, and ends each day with one Glory Be rather than seven. The day-prayers themselves ("Come, O Spirit of Wisdom…," etc.) are freshly written paraphrases in the same idiom, not the verbatim "Almighty and eternal God…" text. The fruits enumerated on Day 8 follow the Douay/traditional list (charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, faith, mildness, temperance — rendered here in modern terms). Highly faithful in structure and spirit; paraphrased in wording.

Sacred Heart of Jesus novena-sacred-heart

before the Sacred Heart
description & history

A nine-day prayer of petition and devotion to the Sacred Heart of Jesus, asking for His grace, mercy, and love.

Uma oração de nove dias de petição e devoção ao Sagrado Coração de Jesus, pedindo Sua graça, misericórdia e amor.

Devotion to the Sacred Heart was popularized by St. Margaret Mary Alacoque after her visions at Paray-le-Monial in the 17th century. Jesus revealed His Heart as a symbol of His boundless love and asked for acts of reparation and devotion. The novena tradition grew from the nine days the Apostles spent in prayer between the Ascension and Pentecost.

A devoção ao Sagrado Coração foi popularizada por Santa Margarida Maria Alacoque após suas visões em Paray-le-Monial no século XVII. Jesus revelou Seu Coração como símbolo de Seu amor infinito e pediu atos de reparação e devoção. A tradição da novena cresceu dos nove dias que os Apóstolos passaram em oração entre a Ascensão e Pentecostes.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer—

Day 1: The Goodness of the Sacred Heart

Dia 1: A Bondade do Sagrado Coração

O most Sacred Heart of Jesus, You revealed to St. Margaret Mary Your burning desire to be loved by all. Your Heart is the source of all goodness, overflowing with mercy for sinners and consolation for the afflicted. I come before You, conscious of my unworthiness, yet trusting in Your infinite goodness.

Ó Sacratíssimo Coração de Jesus, Vós revelastes a Santa Margarida Maria Vosso ardente desejo de ser amado por todos. Vosso Coração é a fonte de toda bondade, transbordando de misericórdia para os pecadores e consolação para os aflitos. Venho diante de Vós, consciente da minha indignidade, mas confiando na Vossa infinita bondade.

Intention

For a deeper awareness of God's goodness in my life.

Por uma consciência mais profunda da bondade de Deus na minha vida.

Day 2: The Love of the Sacred Heart

Dia 2: O Amor do Sagrado Coração

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, Your love for us is beyond all understanding. You loved us even unto death on the Cross, and Your Heart was pierced so that we might find refuge within it. Teach me to love as You love — without counting the cost, without seeking reward, with a love that is patient, generous, and self-giving.

Ó Sagrado Coração de Jesus, Vosso amor por nós está além de toda compreensão. Vós nos amastes até a morte na Cruz, e Vosso Coração foi traspassado para que pudéssemos encontrar refúgio nele. Ensinai-me a amar como Vós amais — sem contar o custo, sem buscar recompensa, com um amor paciente, generoso e abnegado.

Intention

For the grace to love God and neighbor more ardently.

Pela graça de amar a Deus e ao próximo mais ardentemente.

Day 3: The Mercy of the Sacred Heart

Dia 3: A Misericórdia do Sagrado Coração

O merciful Heart of Jesus, You never turn away the repentant sinner. You welcomed the woman at the well, the good thief, and Peter after his denial. Your mercy is new every morning, and Your compassion never fails. I bring before You all my sins and failings, trusting that Your mercy is greater than my weakness.

Ó misericordioso Coração de Jesus, Vós nunca rejeitais o pecador arrependido. Vós acolhestes a mulher junto ao poço, o bom ladrão e Pedro após sua negação. Vossa misericórdia se renova a cada manhã, e Vossa compaixão nunca falha. Trago diante de Vós todos os meus pecados e falhas, confiando que Vossa misericórdia é maior que minha fraqueza.

Intention

For the conversion of sinners and the souls in purgatory.

Pela conversão dos pecadores e pelas almas do purgatório.

Day 4: The Humility of the Sacred Heart

Dia 4: A Humildade do Sagrado Coração

O humble Heart of Jesus, You who said 'Learn from Me, for I am meek and humble of heart,' teach me true humility. Though You are Lord of all, You chose to be born in a stable, to wash the feet of Your disciples, and to die the death of a criminal. Free me from pride, vanity, and the desire for human praise.

Ó humilde Coração de Jesus, Vós que dissestes 'Aprendei de Mim, porque sou manso e humilde de coração,' ensinai-me a verdadeira humildade. Embora sejais Senhor de tudo, escolhestes nascer numa estrebaria, lavar os pés dos Vossos discípulos e morrer a morte de um criminoso. Livrai-me do orgulho, da vaidade e do desejo de louvor humano.

Intention

For the grace of humility in all things.

Pela graça da humildade em todas as coisas.

Day 5: The Patience of the Sacred Heart

Dia 5: A Paciência do Sagrado Coração

O patient Heart of Jesus, how long You bore with the unbelief of the Pharisees, the slowness of Your disciples, and the ingratitude of those You healed. You endured the agony in the garden, the scourging, the crowning with thorns, and the way of the Cross — all without complaint. Grant me patience in my trials and sufferings.

Ó paciente Coração de Jesus, quanto tempo Vós suportastes a incredulidade dos fariseus, a lentidão dos Vossos discípulos e a ingratidão daqueles que curastes. Vós suportastes a agonia no jardim, a flagelação, a coroação de espinhos e o caminho da Cruz — tudo sem queixa. Concedei-me paciência nas minhas provações e sofrimentos.

Intention

For patience and perseverance in trials.

Pela paciência e perseverança nas provações.

Day 6: The Generosity of the Sacred Heart

Dia 6: A Generosidade do Sagrado Coração

O generous Heart of Jesus, You gave everything — Your time, Your strength, Your very life — for the salvation of souls. You multiplied loaves for the hungry, gave sight to the blind, and poured out Your Precious Blood for the forgiveness of sins. Inspire me to give generously of myself, my time, and my resources for the good of others.

Ó generoso Coração de Jesus, Vós destes tudo — Vosso tempo, Vossa força, Vossa própria vida — pela salvação das almas. Vós multiplicastes os pães para os famintos, destes vista aos cegos e derramastes Vosso Precioso Sangue para o perdão dos pecados. Inspirai-me a dar generosamente de mim mesmo, do meu tempo e dos meus recursos para o bem dos outros.

Intention

For a generous heart, open to the needs of others.

Por um coração generoso, aberto às necessidades dos outros.

Day 7: The Obedience of the Sacred Heart

Dia 7: A Obediência do Sagrado Coração

O obedient Heart of Jesus, You were obedient to the Father's will in all things, even unto death on a cross. In Gethsemane You prayed, 'Not My will, but Yours be done.' You submitted to the laws of Your own creation, obeying Mary and Joseph in the hidden life at Nazareth. Help me to embrace God's will with joy and trust.

Ó obediente Coração de Jesus, Vós fostes obediente à vontade do Pai em todas as coisas, mesmo até a morte na cruz. No Getsêmani Vós orastes: 'Não a Minha vontade, mas a Vossa seja feita.' Vós Vos submetestes às leis da Vossa própria criação, obedecendo a Maria e José na vida oculta em Nazaré. Ajudai-me a abraçar a vontade de Deus com alegria e confiança.

Intention

For docility to God's will in my life.

Pela docilidade à vontade de Deus na minha vida.

Day 8: The Sacred Heart, Source of Peace

Dia 8: O Sagrado Coração, Fonte de Paz

O peaceful Heart of Jesus, You said to Your disciples, 'Peace I leave with you; My peace I give to you.' In the midst of storms You slept peacefully, and after Your resurrection You greeted Your frightened Apostles with 'Peace be with you.' Grant me that deep interior peace which the world cannot give — peace born of trust in Your providence.

Ó pacífico Coração de Jesus, Vós dissestes aos Vossos discípulos: 'A paz vos deixo, a Minha paz vos dou.' No meio das tempestades Vós dormíeis em paz, e após Vossa ressurreição saudastes Vossos Apóstolos assustados com 'A paz esteja convosco.' Concedei-me aquela profunda paz interior que o mundo não pode dar — paz nascida da confiança na Vossa providência.

Intention

For peace in families, communities, and the world.

Pela paz nas famílias, comunidades e no mundo.

Day 9: The Sacred Heart, Our Refuge

Dia 9: O Sagrado Coração, Nosso Refúgio

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, You are our refuge and our strength, an ever-present help in trouble. Your Heart, pierced by the soldier's lance, opened wide to receive all who come to You. In every sorrow, every temptation, every moment of darkness, I take refuge in Your Sacred Heart. As this novena ends, I entrust to You all my needs and the needs of those I love.

Ó Sagrado Coração de Jesus, Vós sois nosso refúgio e nossa fortaleza, um auxílio sempre presente nas dificuldades. Vosso Coração, traspassado pela lança do soldado, abriu-se amplamente para receber todos que vêm a Vós. Em cada tristeza, cada tentação, cada momento de escuridão, eu me refugio no Vosso Sagrado Coração. Ao terminar esta novena, confio a Vós todas as minhas necessidades e as necessidades daqueles que amo.

Intention

For all the intentions held in my heart during this novena.

Por todas as intenções guardadas no meu coração durante esta novena.

Closing prayer—

FishEaters traditional

name="sacredheart">
Novena
to the Sacred Heart

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on the
Feast of Corpus Christi and ending on the Feast of the Sacred Heart

O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore Thee, I
love Thee and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer Thee this poor
heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure and wholly obedient to Thy
will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in Thee and for Thee. Protect
me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health
of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Thy blessing on all that I
do, and the grace of a holy death. Within Thy Heart I place my every
care. In every need let me come to Thee with humble trust saying, Heart
of Jesus help me. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas opens each day with the Sign of the Cross and prays the same framework all nine days, built on three Gospel-promise petitions: 1) "O my Jesus, you have said: 'Truly I say to you, ask and you will receive…' Behold I knock, I seek and ask for the grace of… [intention]"; 2) "…if you ask anything of the Father in my name, he will give it to you"; 3) "…heaven and earth will pass away but my words will not pass away." After each, the refrain "Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in you." The closing prayer is: "O Sacred Heart of Jesus, for whom it is impossible not to have compassion on the afflicted, have pity on us miserable sinners and grant us the grace which we ask of you, through the Sorrowful and Immaculate Heart of Mary, your tender Mother and ours. Amen."

Fidelity note

Ember's Sacred Heart novena is an original composition that does not reproduce the fixed text of either source — neither the FishEaters "fountain of every blessing… this poor heart of mine" prayer nor PMN's three "Truly I say to you…" Gospel-promise petitions. Instead it builds nine themed meditations on attributes of the Heart of Jesus (goodness, love, mercy, humility, patience, generosity, obedience, peace, refuge), which are authentic to the devotion and notably echo the virtues the FishEaters prayer petitions (humility, patience, conformity to God's will). It is well-grounded in the tradition: it names St. Margaret Mary and the revelation of Christ's "burning desire to be loved," and centers on the pierced Heart as refuge. Its closing aspiration — "O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You" — directly matches PMN's repeated refrain. Faithful in spirit, imagery, and the trademark act of trust; paraphrased rather than verbatim.

Divine Mercy divine-mercy-novena

Good Friday → Divine Mercy Sunday
description & history

A nine-day novena of Divine Mercy rooted in the devotion revealed to St. Faustina, with daily intentions offered together with the Chaplet.

Uma novena de nove dias da Divina Misericórdia enraizada na devoção revelada a Santa Faustina, com intenções diárias oferecidas juntamente com o Terço.

The Divine Mercy Novena is traditionally prayed from Good Friday through Holy Saturday, culminating in Divine Mercy Sunday. It grew from the spirituality of St. Faustina Kowalska and centers on trust in Jesus and intercession for souls most in need of mercy.

A Novena da Divina Misericórdia é tradicionalmente rezada da Sexta-Feira Santa ao Sábado Santo, culminando no Domingo da Divina Misericórdia. Ela cresceu da espiritualidade de Santa Faustina Kowalska e se concentra na confiança em Jesus e na intercessão pelas almas que mais necessitam de misericórdia.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer—

Day 1: For all mankind, especially sinners

Dia 1: Por toda a humanidade, especialmente os pecadores

Jesus opens His Heart to every soul and invites us to trust in His mercy. Today we begin by placing the whole world before Him, especially those most wounded by sin.

Jesus abre Seu Coração a toda alma e nos convida a confiar em Sua misericórdia. Hoje começamos colocando o mundo inteiro diante d'Ele, especialmente os mais feridos pelo pecado.

Intention

For all mankind, especially sinners, that they may receive conversion and mercy.

Por toda a humanidade, especialmente os pecadores, para que recebam conversão e misericórdia.

Day 2: For priests and religious

Dia 2: Pelos sacerdotes e religiosos

The Lord gives shepherds and consecrated souls to nourish and guide His people. We pray that they may be strengthened in holiness, zeal, and fidelity.

O Senhor dá pastores e almas consagradas para alimentar e guiar o Seu povo. Rezamos para que sejam fortalecidos na santidade, no zelo e na fidelidade.

Intention

For priests, deacons, and all religious, that they may be living witnesses of Divine Mercy.

Pelos sacerdotes, diáconos e todos os religiosos, para que sejam testemunhas vivas da Divina Misericórdia.

Day 3: For devout and faithful souls

Dia 3: Pelas almas devotas e fiéis

Faithful souls sustain the Church through hidden prayer and sacrifice. We ask that those who already seek God may persevere and draw many others toward His Heart.

As almas fiéis sustentam a Igreja por meio de oração e sacrifício escondidos. Pedimos que aqueles que já buscam a Deus perseverem e atraiam muitos outros para o Seu Coração.

Intention

For devout and faithful souls, that they may grow in trust, charity, and perseverance.

Pelas almas devotas e fiéis, para que cresçam na confiança, na caridade e na perseverança.

Day 4: For those who do not believe in Christ and those who do not know Him

Dia 4: Por aqueles que não creem em Cristo e não O conhecem

Christ desires that every person know the truth of His love. Today we entrust to Him all who are far from faith, asking for light, healing, and the grace of encounter.

Cristo deseja que toda pessoa conheça a verdade do Seu amor. Hoje confiamos a Ele todos os que estão longe da fé, pedindo luz, cura e a graça do encontro.

Intention

For those who do not believe in Christ and those who have not yet known Him.

Por aqueles que não creem em Cristo e por aqueles que ainda não O conheceram.

Day 5: For separated brethren

Dia 5: Pelos irmãos separados

The Heart of Jesus longs for unity among all who call upon His name. We pray for healing of divisions, mutual charity, and the full communion willed by Christ.

O Coração de Jesus anseia pela unidade de todos os que invocam o Seu nome. Rezamos pela cura das divisões, pela caridade mútua e pela plena comunhão querida por Cristo.

Intention

For separated brethren, that all Christians may be led into deeper unity in truth and love.

Pelos irmãos separados, para que todos os cristãos sejam conduzidos a uma unidade mais profunda na verdade e no amor.

Day 6: For meek and humble souls, and little children

Dia 6: Pelas almas mansas e humildes, e pelas criancinhas

The meek and humble resemble the Heart of Jesus and are precious in His sight. We ask for childlike trust and a gentle spirit for ourselves and for all souls.

Os mansos e humildes se assemelham ao Coração de Jesus e são preciosos aos Seus olhos. Pedimos confiança filial e espírito manso para nós e para todas as almas.

Intention

For meek and humble souls, and for little children, that they may remain under God's loving care.

Pelas almas mansas e humildes, e pelas criancinhas, para que permaneçam sob o amoroso cuidado de Deus.

Day 7: For souls who especially venerate and glorify Divine Mercy

Dia 7: Pelas almas que veneram e glorificam especialmente a Divina Misericórdia

Those who proclaim Divine Mercy become signs of hope in the world. We pray that their witness may spread confidence in Jesus and ignite works of charity.

Aqueles que proclamam a Divina Misericórdia tornam-se sinais de esperança no mundo. Rezamos para que seu testemunho espalhe a confiança em Jesus e desperte obras de caridade.

Intention

For souls devoted to Divine Mercy, that they may be faithful messengers of trust and compassion.

Pelas almas devotas da Divina Misericórdia, para que sejam fiéis mensageiras da confiança e da compaixão.

Day 8: For the souls in purgatory

Dia 8: Pelas almas do purgatório

In charity we remember the faithful departed who await the vision of God. We commend them to the Precious Blood of Christ and to the maternal intercession of the Church.

Na caridade, recordamos os fiéis defuntos que aguardam a visão de Deus. Nós os confiamos ao Preciosíssimo Sangue de Cristo e à intercessão maternal da Igreja.

Intention

For the souls in purgatory, that they may soon enter the joy of heaven.

Pelas almas do purgatório, para que em breve entrem na alegria do céu.

Day 9: For lukewarm souls

Dia 9: Pelas almas tíbias

Lukewarmness weakens love and dulls the desire for holiness. We ask Christ to rekindle in every soul the fire of divine charity and wholehearted conversion.

A tibieza enfraquece o amor e diminui o desejo de santidade. Pedimos a Cristo que reacenda em toda alma o fogo da caridade divina e da conversão sincera.

Intention

For lukewarm souls, that they may be renewed by grace and set ablaze with love for God.

Pelas almas tíbias, para que sejam renovadas pela graça e abrasadas no amor de Deus.

Closing prayer—

FishEaters traditional

name="divinemercy">
Novena
to the Divine Mercy of Jesus Christ

The Novena may be prayed any time, but Jesus asked that
it especially be prayed beginning on Good Friday and ending the day
before Low Sunday (what is now also known as Divine Mercy Sunday -- the
Sunday after Easter).

First Day:

Most Merciful Jesus, whose very nature it is to have compassion on us
and to forgive us, do not look upon our sins, but upon the trust which
we place in Your infinite goodness. Receive us all into the abode of
Your Most Compassionate Heart, and never let us escape from It. We beg
this of You by Your love which unites You to the Father and the Holy
Spirit.

O omnipotence of Divine Mercy, salvation of sinful man, You are a sea
of mercy and compassion. You aid him who entreats You with humility.

Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon all mankind and especially
upon poor sinners, all enfolded in the Most Compassionate Heart of
Jesus. For the sake of His sorrowful Passion, show us Your mercy, that
we may praise the omnipotence of Your mercy forever and ever. Amen.

Recite the Divine Mercy
Chaplet

Second Day:

Most Merciful Jesus, from whom comes all that is good, increase Your
grace in us, that we may perform worthy works of mercy, and that all
who see us may glorify the Father of Mercy who is in heaven.

The fountain of God's love dwells in pure hearts, bathed in the Sea of
Mercy, radiant as stars, bright as the dawn.

Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the company of chosen ones
in Your vineyard upon the souls of priests and religious; and endow
them with the strength of Your blessing. For the love of the Heart of
Your Son, in which they are enfolded, impart to them Your power and
light, that they may be able to guide others in the way of salvation,
and with one voice sing praise to Your boundless mercy for ages without
end. Amen.

Recite the Divine Mercy
Chaplet

Third Day:

Most Merciful Jesus, from the treasury of Your mercy, You impart Your
graces in great abundance to each and all. Receive us into the abode of
Your Most Compassionate Heart and never let us escape from It. We beg
this of You by that most wondrous love for the heavenly Father with
which Your Heart burns so fiercely.

The miracles of mercy are impenetrable. Neither the sinner nor just one
will fathom them. When You cast upon us an eye of pity, You draw us all
closer to Your love.

Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon faithful souls, as upon
the inheritance of Your Son. For the sake of His sorrowful Passion,
grant them Your blessing and surround them with Your constant
protection. Thus may they never fail in love or lose the treasure of
the holy faith, but rather, with all the hosts of Angels and Saints,
may they glorify Your boundless mercy for endless ages. Amen.

Recite the Divine Mercy
Chaplet

Fourth Day:

Most Compassionate Jesus, You are the Light of the whole world. Receive
into the abode of Your Most Compassionate Heart the souls of pagans who
as yet do not know You. Let the rays of Your grace enlighten them that
they, too, together with us, may extol Your wonderful mercy; and do not
let them escape from the abode which is Your Most Compassionate Heart.

May the light of Your love enlighten the souls in darkness; Grant that
these souls will know You and, together with us, praise Your mercy.

Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the souls of pagans and of
those who as yet do not know You, but who are enclosed in the Most
Compassionate Heart of Jesus. Draw them to the light of the Gospel.
These souls do not know what great happiness it is to love You. Grant
that they, too, may extol the generosity of Your mercy for endless
ages. Amen.

Recite the Divine Mercy Chaplet

Fifth Day:

Most Merciful Jesus, Goodness Itself, You do not refuse light to those
who seek it of You. Receive into the abode of Your Most Compassionate
Heart the souls of heretics and sc

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Daily themes: (1) all mankind, especially sinners; (2) souls of priests and religious; (3) all devout and faithful souls; (4) those who don't believe in God or don't know Him; (5) souls separated from the Church; (6) meek, humble souls and little children; (7) souls who venerate and glorify Divine Mercy; (8) souls detained in Purgatory; (9) lukewarm souls. Each day: Trinitarian opening invocation, Jesus's daily intention quoted from St. Faustina's Diary, a prayer to Jesus, a prayer to the Eternal Father, a closing blessing, then the Divine Mercy Chaplet. Sample opening: "Most Merciful Jesus, whose very nature it is to have compassion on us and to forgive us, do not look upon our sins but upon our trust which we place in Your infinite goodness." Source noted as the copyrighted *Diary of St. Maria Faustina Kowalska* © 1987 Marian Fathers.

Fidelity note

The nine daily intentions match the canonical Faustina novena exactly in order and theme (sinners, priests/religious, faithful souls, unbelievers, separated brethren, the meek/children, devotees of mercy, purgatory, lukewarm souls), and the structure faithfully pairs each day with the full Divine Mercy Chaplet, which is the defining element of the devotion. However, the meditations and petition prayers are Ember's own newly composed paraphrases rather than the verbatim prayers from St. Faustina's Diary — a deliberate choice, since the standard texts (e.g., "Today bring to Me all mankind, especially all sinners…") are under copyright by the Marian Fathers. The closing "Eternal God, in whom mercy is endless…" prayer is the one genuinely traditional concluding text and is reproduced accurately. Overall: structurally and thematically faithful, with original (non-copyright) wording standing in for the diary prayers.

St. Jude st-jude-novena

desperate causes
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Jude, asking his intercession in difficult needs and growing in hope, perseverance, and trust in Christ.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Judas Tadeu, pedindo sua intercessão nas causas difíceis e crescendo na esperança, na perseverança e na confiança em Cristo.

St. Jude Thaddeus, one of the Twelve Apostles and author of the Epistle of Jude, has long been invoked in the Church as a powerful intercessor in difficult and desperate circumstances. Popular devotion to his novena spread widely through preaching and parish life, especially as a prayer of steadfast hope in God's mercy.

São Judas Tadeu, um dos Doze Apóstolos e autor da Epístola de Judas, é invocado há muito tempo na Igreja como poderoso intercessor nas causas difíceis e desesperadoras. A devoção à sua novena se difundiu amplamente pela pregação e pela vida paroquial, sobretudo como oração de esperança perseverante na misericórdia de Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, I place this novena before God through your intercession. Obtain for me a heart full of hope, perseverance in prayer, and trust in the mercy of Christ. Amen.

São Judas Tadeu, servo fiel e amigo de Jesus, eu coloco esta novena diante de Deus por vossa intercessão. Alcançai-me um coração cheio de esperança, perseverança na oração e confiança na misericórdia de Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: Called by Christ and chosen as Apostle

Dia 1: Chamado por Cristo e escolhido como Apóstolo

St. Jude was called personally by Jesus and sent to proclaim the Kingdom. His vocation reminds us that every Christian life begins with a divine call and a mission of love.

São Judas Tadeu foi chamado pessoalmente por Jesus e enviado para anunciar o Reino. Sua vocação nos recorda que toda vida cristã começa com um chamado divino e uma missão de amor.

Intention

For the grace to hear Christ's call and respond with generosity.

Pela graça de ouvir o chamado de Cristo e responder com generosidade.

Day 2: Fidelity in hidden service

Dia 2: Fidelidade no serviço escondido

Though less mentioned in the Gospels, St. Jude remained faithful to the Lord. He teaches us that holiness is often forged in hidden, humble, and persevering service.

Mesmo sendo menos mencionado nos Evangelhos, São Judas Tadeu permaneceu fiel ao Senhor. Ele nos ensina que a santidade é muitas vezes forjada no serviço escondido, humilde e perseverante.

Intention

For fidelity in daily duties, especially when unseen or unrecognized.

Pela fidelidade nos deveres diários, especialmente quando não somos vistos ou reconhecidos.

Day 3: Confidence in difficult causes

Dia 3: Confiança nas causas difíceis

The Church invokes St. Jude in difficult needs because he points souls toward unwavering trust in God's power. No situation is beyond divine mercy and providence.

A Igreja invoca São Judas Tadeu nas causas difíceis porque ele conduz as almas à confiança inabalável no poder de Deus. Nenhuma situação está além da misericórdia e da providência divinas.

Intention

For urgent intentions that seem difficult or impossible in human eyes.

Pelas intenções urgentes que parecem difíceis ou impossíveis aos olhos humanos.

Day 4: Perseverance in prayer

Dia 4: Perseverança na oração

St. Jude's intercession encourages us to pray without losing heart. Persevering prayer stretches our trust, purifies our desires, and keeps us close to Christ.

A intercessão de São Judas Tadeu nos encoraja a rezar sem desanimar. A oração perseverante dilata nossa confiança, purifica nossos desejos e nos mantém próximos de Cristo.

Intention

For constancy in prayer and deeper trust in God's timing.

Pela constância na oração e por maior confiança no tempo de Deus.

Day 5: Courage to witness the Gospel

Dia 5: Coragem para testemunhar o Evangelho

As an Apostle, St. Jude preached Christ with courage amid trials. He strengthens us to witness the Gospel with clarity, charity, and steadfast faith.

Como Apóstolo, São Judas Tadeu anunciou Cristo com coragem em meio às provações. Ele nos fortalece para testemunhar o Evangelho com clareza, caridade e fé firme.

Intention

For boldness to live and share the faith in our homes, work, and communities.

Pela coragem de viver e partilhar a fé em nossos lares, trabalho e comunidades.

Day 6: Mercy for sinners and the afflicted

Dia 6: Misericórdia pelos pecadores e aflitos

St. Jude calls us to compassion for those burdened by sin, suffering, and discouragement. In Christ, mercy heals wounds and restores hearts to hope.

São Judas Tadeu nos chama à compaixão por aqueles que estão carregados pelo pecado, sofrimento e desânimo. Em Cristo, a misericórdia cura feridas e devolve esperança aos corações.

Intention

For those in spiritual, emotional, or material distress.

Por aqueles que vivem em aflição espiritual, emocional ou material.

Day 7: Unity and peace in families

Dia 7: Unidade e paz nas famílias

Through the intercession of St. Jude, we ask for reconciliation where there is division. Christ desires homes marked by forgiveness, patience, and mutual charity.

Pela intercessão de São Judas Tadeu, pedimos reconciliação onde há divisão. Cristo deseja lares marcados pelo perdão, pela paciência e pela caridade mútua.

Intention

For peace, healing, and unity in our families and relationships.

Pela paz, cura e unidade em nossas famílias e relacionamentos.

Day 8: Hope for the despairing and abandoned

Dia 8: Esperança para os desesperados e abandonados

St. Jude is often sought by those who feel at the end of their strength. He reminds us that God never abandons His children and always opens a path of grace.

São Judas Tadeu é frequentemente procurado por aqueles que sentem ter chegado ao limite de suas forças. Ele nos recorda que Deus nunca abandona seus filhos e sempre abre um caminho de graça.

Intention

For all who are tempted to despair, that they may receive renewed hope.

Por todos os que são tentados ao desespero, para que recebam esperança renovada.

Day 9: Thanksgiving and mission

Dia 9: Ação de graças e missão

As this novena concludes, we thank God for every grace already given. With St. Jude, we ask for perseverance so that our lives become a witness of gratitude, faith, and charity.

Ao concluir esta novena, damos graças a Deus por cada graça já concedida. Com São Judas Tadeu, pedimos perseverança para que nossa vida se torne testemunho de gratidão, fé e caridade.

Intention

For final perseverance and a life that gives glory to God.

Pela perseverança final e por uma vida que dê glória a Deus.

Closing prayer

St. Jude, Apostle of Christ and helper in difficult needs, pray for us and for all who seek God's mercy. May we remain faithful to Jesus, serve our neighbor with charity, and give thanks for every grace received. Amen.

São Judas Tadeu, Apóstolo de Cristo e socorro nas causas difíceis, rogai por nós e por todos os que buscam a misericórdia de Deus. Que permaneçamos fiéis a Jesus, sirvamos ao próximo com caridade e demos graças por toda graça recebida. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="jude">
Novena
to St. Jude

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on 19
October and ending on 27 October, the eve of the Feast of St. Jude.
When the Novena is finished, you must publicly thank St. Jude for his
prayers for you. This was classicaly done by taking out a small ad in
the
Classifieds section of newspapers, but nowadays a mention on social
media would do as well.

To Saint Jude, Holy Saint Jude, Apostle and Martyr, great in virtue and
rich in miracles, near kinsman of Jesus Christ, faithful intercessor of
all who invoke thy special patronage in time of need. To thee I have
recourse from the depths of my heart and humbly beg to whom God has
given such great power to come to my assistance. Help me in my present
and urgent petition, in return I promise to make thy name known and
cause thee to be invoked. Saint Jude pray for us and all who invoke thy
aid. Amen.

Recite an Our Father,
Hail Mary, and Glory Be three times. Keep your
promise to St. Jude and make his name known. <a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

Opening: Trinitarian formula ("In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen."), then a prayer addressing St. Jude as apostle, martyr, and friend of Jesus, requesting his intercession for stated intentions. Daily themes: (1) general petition for grace and mercy; (2) zeal to preach the Gospel; (3) patience during struggles; (4) help with impossible needs; (5) sacrifice like St. Jude; (6) to know God's will; (7) trust in God's providence; (8) to maintain hope; (9) to preserve faith. Recurring core line: "You are the patron of the impossible. Pray for me and my intentions! O St. Jude, pray that God's grace and mercy will cover my intentions." Each day closes with the Sign of the Cross.

Fidelity note

Ember's novena keeps the same essential identity as the common St. Jude devotion — invoking the apostle as friend of Jesus and patron of difficult/impossible causes, framed around hope, perseverance, and trust in God's mercy — and its day themes overlap meaningfully with PrayMoreNovenas (difficult causes, perseverance in prayer, courage to witness, maintaining hope). The wording is entirely Ember's own composition rather than a copy of either source; it is more catechetical and biographically structured (call, hidden service, witness, thanksgiving) than PrayMoreNovenas' petition-list format. There is no verbatim borrowing, and the recurring "patron of the impossible" refrain from PrayMoreNovenas is not used. The FishEaters text could not be verified due to a truncated fetch, but the Ember version is plainly an original, doctrinally sound treatment rather than a transcription of any single source.
